objective: Create a module that calculates the future value of an investment using the compound interest formula. The module should take the principal amount, annual interest rate (as a decimal), compounding frequency per year, and the number of years, returning the total amount after compounding.
solution:
(module CompoundInterestCalculator
  (defun calculate_future_value (principal annual_rate compounding_frequency years)
    ; A = P * (1 + r/n)^(nt)
    ; annual_rate is expected as a decimal (e.g., 0.05 for 5%)
    ; compounding_frequency: 1 for annually, 4 for quarterly, 12 for monthly, 365 for daily
    (def rate_per_period (/ annual_rate compounding_frequency))
    (def num_periods (* compounding_frequency years))
    (def base (+ 1 rate_per_period))
    (* principal (math_pow base num_periods)))

  ; Test cases:
  ; P = 1000, r = 0.05 (5%), n = 1 (annually), t = 1 year
  ; A = 1000 * (1 + 0.05/1)^(1*1) = 1000 * 1.05 = 1050
  (test annual_1_year (near? (calculate_future_value 1000 0.05 1 1) 1050.0 0.001))

  ; P = 1000, r = 0.05 (5%), n = 4 (quarterly), t = 1 year
  ; A = 1000 * (1 + 0.05/4)^(4*1) = 1000 * (1.0125)^4 = 1050.9453369...
  (test quarterly_1_year (near? (calculate_future_value 1000 0.05 4 1) 1050.9453369 0.001))

  ; P = 1000, r = 0.05 (5%), n = 12 (monthly), t = 1 year
  ; A = 1000 * (1 + 0.05/12)^(12*1) = 1051.1618978...
  (test monthly_1_year (near? (calculate_future_value 1000 0.05 12 1) 1051.1618978 0.001))

  ; P = 5000, r = 0.06 (6%), n = 2 (semi-annually), t = 5 years
  ; A = 5000 * (1 + 0.06/2)^(2*5) = 5000 * (1.03)^10 = 6719.5818967...
  (test semi_annual_5_years (near? (calculate_future_value 5000 0.06 2 5) 6719.5818967 0.001))

  ; P = 100, r = 0.10 (10%), n = 365 (daily), t = 10 years
  ; A = 100 * (1 + 0.10/365)^(365*10) = 271.7912448...
  (test daily_10_years (near? (calculate_future_value 100 0.10 365 10) 271.7912448 0.001))
)

topic: read two env vars and concat
objective: Write a function that constructs a full log file path by reading the `LOG_DIR` and `LOG_FILE_NAME` environment variables. If `LOG_DIR` is empty, default to `/tmp`. If `LOG_FILE_NAME` is empty, default to `app.log`.
solution:
(module LogPathBuilder
  (extern defun env_read (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun get_env_or_default (key default_value)
    (def val (env_read key))
    (if (str_eq val "")
        (return default_value))
    val)

  (defun get_log_file_path ()
    (def log_dir (get_env_or_default "LOG_DIR" "/tmp"))
    (def log_file_name (get_env_or_default "LOG_FILE_NAME" "app.log"))
    (str_concat log_dir (str_concat "/" log_file_name)))

  (test both_present
    (mocks (env.get "LOG_DIR" "/var/log/myapp")
           (env.get "LOG_FILE_NAME" "server.log"))
    (eq? (get_log_file_path) "/var/log/myapp/server.log"))

  (test dir_missing
    (mocks (env.get "LOG_DIR" "")
           (env.get "LOG_FILE_NAME" "audit.log"))
    (eq? (get_log_file_path) "/tmp/audit.log"))

  (test file_missing
    (mocks (env.get "LOG_DIR" "/home/user/logs")
           (env.get "LOG_FILE_NAME" ""))
    (eq? (get_log_file_path) "/home/user/logs/app.log"))

  (test both_missing
    (mocks (env.get "LOG_DIR" "")
           (env.get "LOG_FILE_NAME" ""))
    (eq? (get_log_file_path) "/tmp/app.log"))

  (test root_dir
    (mocks (env.get "LOG_DIR" "/")
           (env.get "LOG_FILE_NAME" "system.log"))
    (eq? (get_log_file_path) "//system.log"))

  (test custom_defaults
    (mocks (env.get "LOG_DIR" "/mnt/data")
           (env.get "LOG_FILE_NAME" "backup.log"))
    (eq? (get_log_file_path) "/mnt/data/backup.log"))
)

objective: Write an AGC function `count_non_empty_lines` that takes a file path. It should read the content of the file and return the number of lines that are not empty strings, after splitting the file content by newline characters. Lines consisting solely of whitespace characters (e.g., " ", "\t") are considered non-empty for this problem.
solution:
(module NonEmptyLineCounter
  (extern defun file_read (path) @capability "file.read")

  (defun count_non_empty_lines (path)
    (def content (file_read path))
    (def lines (str_split content "\n"))
    (def count 0)
    (def i 0)
    (def n (arr_length lines))

    (while (< i n)
      (def line (arr_get lines i))
      ;; A line is considered non-empty if its string length is greater than 0
      ;; after splitting by newline. This includes lines containing only whitespace.
      (if (> (str_length line) 0)
        (set count (+ count 1)))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    count)

  (test empty_file
    (mocks (file.read "/path/empty.txt" ""))
    (eq? (count_non_empty_lines "/path/empty.txt") 0))

  (test one_non_empty_line
    (mocks (file.read "/path/one.txt" "Hello World\n"))
    (eq? (count_non_empty_lines "/path/one.txt") 1))

  (test multiple_lines_with_empty
    (mocks (file.read "/path/multi.txt" "Line1\n\nLine2\n\nLine3\n"))
    (eq? (count_non_empty_lines "/path/multi.txt") 3))

  (test only_empty_lines
    (mocks (file.read "/path/only_empty.txt" "\n\n\n"))
    (eq? (count_non_empty_lines "/path/only_empty.txt") 0))

  (test lines_with_whitespace
    (mocks (file.read "/path/whitespace.txt" "  Line1  \n\t\t\n   Line2\n"))
    (eq? (count_non_empty_lines "/path/whitespace.txt") 3))

  (test no_ending_newline
    (mocks (file.read "/path/no_newline.txt" "Last line without newline"))
    (eq? (count_non_empty_lines "/path/no_newline.txt") 1))

  (test mixed_content
    (mocks (file.read "/path/mixed.txt" "First\n\nSecond\n   \nThird"))
    (eq? (count_non_empty_lines "/path/mixed.txt") 4)))

topic: read two env vars and concat
objective: Create a function that reads the values of two specified environment variables and concatenates them. If an environment variable is not set, its value should be treated as an empty string before concatenation.
solution:
(module EnvVarConcatenator
  (extern defun env_get (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun concat_two_env_vars (key1 key2)
    (def val1 (env_get key1))
    (def val2 (env_get key2))
    (str_concat val1 val2))

  (test both_present
    (mocks
      (env.get "PREFIX" "hello")
      (env.get "SUFFIX" "world"))
    (eq? (concat_two_env_vars "PREFIX" "SUFFIX") "helloworld"))

  (test first_missing
    (mocks
      (env.get "USER" "")
      (env.get "DOMAIN" "@example.com"))
    (eq? (concat_two_env_vars "USER" "DOMAIN") "@example.com"))

  (test second_missing
    (mocks
      (env.get "PATH_PART1" "/usr/local")
      (env.get "PATH_PART2" ""))
    (eq? (concat_two_env_vars "PATH_PART1" "PATH_PART2") "/usr/local"))

  (test both_missing
    (mocks
      (env.get "EMPTY1" "")
      (env.get "EMPTY2" ""))
    (eq? (concat_two_env_vars "EMPTY1" "EMPTY2") ""))

  (test numeric_values_concat
    (mocks
      (env.get "NUM1" "123")
      (env.get "NUM2" "456"))
    (eq? (concat_two_env_vars "NUM1" "NUM2") "123456"))

  (test mixed_values
    (mocks
      (env.get "APP_NAME" "MyWebApp")
      (env.get "VERSION" "-v1.0"))
    (eq? (concat_two_env_vars "APP_NAME" "VERSION") "MyWebApp-v1.0")))

objective: Implement a function `factorial` that calculates the factorial of a given non-negative integer `n`. The factorial of `n` is the product of all positive integers less than or equal to `n`, with `factorial(0)` defined as 1.
solution:
(module Factorial
  (defun factorial (n)
    (if (= n 0) (return 1))
    (def result 1)
    (def i 1)
    (while (<= i n)
      (set result (* result i))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    result)

  (test zero       (eq? (factorial 0) 1))
  (test one        (eq? (factorial 1) 1))
  (test two        (eq? (factorial 2) 2))
  (test three      (eq? (factorial 3) 6))
  (test five       (eq? (factorial 5) 120))
  (test seven      (eq? (factorial 7) 5040)))

objective: Implement a function that takes an array of numbers and a constant, and returns a new array where each element of the original array has the constant subtracted from it.
solution:
(module ArraySubtractConstant
  (defun subtract_constant_from_array (xs constant)
    (def n (arr_length xs))
    (def result_arr (arr_new n))
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i n)
      (def original_val (arr_get xs i))
      (def new_val (- original_val constant))
      (arr_set result_arr i new_val)
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    result_arr)

  (defun make_array (len v0 v1 v2 v3 v4)
    (def arr (arr_new len))
    (if (> len 0) (arr_set arr 0 v0))
    (if (> len 1) (arr_set arr 1 v1))
    (if (> len 2) (arr_set arr 2 v2))
    (if (> len 3) (arr_set arr 3 v3))
    (if (> len 4) (arr_set arr 4 v4))
    arr)

  (test empty_array
    (eq?
      (subtract_constant_from_array (arr_new 0) 5)
      (arr_new 0)))

  (test single_element
    (eq?
      (subtract_constant_from_array (make_array 1 10 0 0 0 0) 3)
      (make_array 1 7 0 0 0 0)))

  (test positive_constant
    (eq?
      (subtract_constant_from_array (make_array 3 10 20 30 0 0) 5)
      (make_array 3 5 15 25 0 0)))

  (test negative_constant
    (eq?
      (subtract_constant_from_array (make_array 4 1 2 3 4 0) -2)
      (make_array 4 3 4 5 6 0)))

  (test zero_constant
    (eq?
      (subtract_constant_from_array (make_array 2 7 -3 0 0 0) 0)
      (make_array 2 7 -3 0 0 0)))

  (test mixed_values
    (eq?
      (subtract_constant_from_array (make_array 5 0 10 -5 20 -15) 5)
      (make_array 5 -5 5 -10 15 -20))))

objective: Calculate the discriminant of a quadratic equation `ax^2 + bx + c = 0`. The function should take the coefficients `a`, `b`, and `c` as input and return the value of `b^2 - 4ac`.
solution:
(module QuadraticDiscriminant
  (defun calculate_discriminant (a b c)
    (- (* b b) (* 4 a c)))

  (test positive_discriminant
    (eq? (calculate_discriminant 1 5 6) 1)) ; b^2 - 4ac = 5^2 - 4*1*6 = 25 - 24 = 1

  (test zero_discriminant
    (eq? (calculate_discriminant 1 4 4) 0)) ; b^2 - 4ac = 4^2 - 4*1*4 = 16 - 16 = 0

  (test negative_discriminant
    (eq? (calculate_discriminant 1 1 1) -3)) ; b^2 - 4ac = 1^2 - 4*1*1 = 1 - 4 = -3

  (test large_coefficients
    (eq? (calculate_discriminant 2 10 8) 36)) ; b^2 - 4ac = 10^2 - 4*2*8 = 100 - 64 = 36

  (test negative_a
    (eq? (calculate_discriminant -1 2 -1) 0)) ; b^2 - 4ac = 2^2 - 4*(-1)*(-1) = 4 - 4 = 0

  (test zero_b
    (eq? (calculate_discriminant 1 0 9) -36)) ; b^2 - 4ac = 0^2 - 4*1*9 = 0 - 36 = -36
)

objective: Calculate the total fare for a ride given a base fare, the distance traveled, and a per-mile rate. The total fare should be rounded to two decimal places.
solution:
(module RideFareCalculator
  ; Helper function to calculate the cost attributed to distance.
  (defun calculate_distance_cost (distance per_mile_rate)
    (* distance per_mile_rate))

  ; Helper function to round a number to two decimal places.
  (defun round_to_two_decimals (amount)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* amount 100) 0.5)) 100))

  ; Calculates the total ride fare, combining a base fare with distance-based cost,
  ; and rounds the result to two decimal places.
  (defun get_ride_fare (base_fare distance per_mile_rate)
    (def distance_cost (calculate_distance_cost distance per_mile_rate))
    (def total_raw_fare (+ base_fare distance_cost))
    (round_to_two_decimals total_raw_fare))

  (test basic_fare
    (near? (get_ride_fare 2.50 5.0 1.75) 11.25 0.01)) ; 2.50 + (5.0 * 1.75) = 2.50 + 8.75 = 11.25

  (test zero_distance
    (near? (get_ride_fare 3.00 0.0 2.00) 3.00 0.01)) ; 3.00 + (0.0 * 2.00) = 3.00

  (test longer_distance_and_fractional_rate
    (near? (get_ride_fare 4.00 15.5 1.50) 27.25 0.01)) ; 4.00 + (15.5 * 1.50) = 4.00 + 23.25 = 27.25

  (test no_base_fare
    (near? (get_ride_fare 0.00 8.0 2.20) 17.60 0.01)) ; 0.00 + (8.0 * 2.20) = 17.60

  (test rounding_up_example
    (near? (get_ride_fare 1.99 3.0 1.006) 5.01 0.01)) ; 1.99 + (3.0 * 1.006) = 1.99 + 3.018 = 5.008 -> 5.01

  (test rounding_down_example
    (near? (get_ride_fare 1.99 3.0 1.004) 5.00 0.01)) ; 1.99 + (3.0 * 1.004) = 1.99 + 3.012 = 5.002 -> 5.00

  (test large_numbers
    (near? (get_ride_fare 10.00 100.0 0.75) 85.00 0.01)) ; 10.00 + (100.0 * 0.75) = 10.00 + 75.00 = 85.00
)

objective: Calculate a person's maximum heart rate using the formula `220 - age`, and then classify a given current heart rate into one of five training zones: Recovery, Warm-up, Fat Burn, Cardio, Anaerobic, or Peak, based on percentages of their maximum heart rate.
solution:
(module HeartRateClassifier
  (defun calculate_max_hr (age)
    (- 220 age))

  (defun classify_hr_zone (age current_hr)
    (def max_hr (calculate_max_hr age))
    (if (< current_hr (* max_hr 0.5)) (return "Recovery"))
    (if (< current_hr (* max_hr 0.6)) (return "Warm-up"))
    (if (< current_hr (* max_hr 0.7)) (return "Fat Burn"))
    (if (< current_hr (* max_hr 0.8)) (return "Cardio"))
    (if (< current_hr (* max_hr 0.9)) (return "Anaerobic"))
    "Peak")

  (test young_recovery
    (eq? (classify_hr_zone 30 80) "Recovery")) ; Max HR: 190, Recovery < 95

  (test young_fat_burn
    (eq? (classify_hr_zone 30 120) "Fat Burn")) ; Max HR: 190, Fat Burn 114-132

  (test young_peak
    (eq? (classify_hr_zone 30 180) "Peak")) ; Max HR: 190, Peak >= 171

  (test middle_cardio
    (eq? (classify_hr_zone 45 125) "Cardio")) ; Max HR: 175, Cardio 122-139

  (test middle_anaerobic
    (eq? (classify_hr_zone 45 150) "Anaerobic")) ; Max HR: 175, Anaerobic 140-157

  (test older_warmup
    (eq? (classify_hr_zone 60 85) "Warm-up")) ; Max HR: 160, Warm-up 80-95

  (test older_peak
    (eq? (classify_hr_zone 60 145) "Peak"))) ; Max HR: 160, Peak >= 144

objective: Write a function `is_feature_enabled` that reads an environment variable specified by a given key. The function should interpret the environment variable's string value as a boolean, returning `true` if the value is "true", "1", "yes", or "on" (case-insensitive), and `false` otherwise (including empty or unrecognized values).
solution:
(module EnvFeatureToggle
  (extern defun env_read (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun is_truthy_string (s)
    (def lower_s (str_lower s))
    (if (str_eq lower_s "true") (return true))
    (if (str_eq lower_s "1") (return true))
    (if (str_eq lower_s "yes") (return true))
    (if (str_eq lower_s "on") (return true))
    false)

  (defun is_feature_enabled (feature_key)
    (def env_val (env_read feature_key))
    (is_truthy_string env_val))

  (test enabled_true
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_A" "true"))
    (eq? (is_feature_enabled "FEATURE_A") true))

  (test enabled_TRUE_case_insensitive
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_B" "TRUE"))
    (eq? (is_feature_enabled "FEATURE_B") true))

  (test enabled_1
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_C" "1"))
    (eq? (is_feature_enabled "FEATURE_C") true))

  (test enabled_yes
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_D" "yes"))
    (eq? (is_feature_enabled "FEATURE_D") true))

  (test disabled_false
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_E" "false"))
    (eq? (is_feature_enabled "FEATURE_E") false))

  (test disabled_empty_string
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_F" ""))
    (eq? (is_feature_enabled "FEATURE_F") false))

  (test disabled_random_string
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_G" "some_other_value"))
    (eq? (is_feature_enabled "FEATURE_G") false)))

objective: Implement a function to calculate the dot product of two vectors (arrays). The function should assume the input vectors are of the same fixed length and contain numeric elements.
solution:
(module VectorDotProduct
  (defun dot_product (v1 v2)
    (def len (arr_length v1)) ; Assumes v1 and v2 have the same length
    (def i 0)
    (def sum_product 0)
    (while (< i len)
      (set sum_product (+ sum_product (* (arr_get v1 i) (arr_get v2 i))))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    sum_product)

  (defun make_vec2 (a b)
    (def vec (arr_new 2))
    (arr_set vec 0 a)
    (arr_set vec 1 b)
    vec)

  (defun make_vec3 (a b c)
    (def vec (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set vec 0 a)
    (arr_set vec 1 b)
    (arr_set vec 2 c)
    vec)

  (defun make_vec4 (a b c d)
    (def vec (arr_new 4))
    (arr_set vec 0 a)
    (arr_set vec 1 b)
    (arr_set vec 2 c)
    (arr_set vec 3 d)
    vec)

  (test empty_vectors
    (eq? (dot_product (arr_new 0) (arr_new 0)) 0))

  (test single_element_vectors
    (def v1 (arr_new 1))
    (arr_set v1 0 5)
    (def v2 (arr_new 1))
    (arr_set v2 0 3)
    (eq? (dot_product v1 v2) 15))

  (test two_element_vectors
    (eq? (dot_product (make_vec2 1 2) (make_vec2 3 4)) 11)) ; 1*3 + 2*4 = 3 + 8 = 11

  (test three_element_positive_numbers
    (eq? (dot_product (make_vec3 1 2 3) (make_vec3 4 5 6)) 32)) ; 1*4 + 2*5 + 3*6 = 4 + 10 + 18 = 32

  (test three_element_mixed_signs
    (eq? (dot_product (make_vec3 1 -2 3) (make_vec3 4 5 -6)) -24)) ; 1*4 + (-2)*5 + 3*(-6) = 4 - 10 - 18 = -24

  (test three_element_with_zeros
    (eq? (dot_product (make_vec3 1 0 3) (make_vec3 4 5 0)) 4)) ; 1*4 + 0*5 + 3*0 = 4 + 0 + 0 = 4

  (test four_element_vectors
    (eq? (dot_product (make_vec4 1 2 3 4) (make_vec4 5 6 7 8)) 70))) ; 1*5 + 2*6 + 3*7 + 4*8 = 5 + 12 + 21 + 32 = 70

objective: Given an array of integers, create a new array where each element represents the parity of the corresponding number in the input array. Use 0 to represent an even number and 1 to represent an odd number.
solution:
(module ClassifyParity
  (defun is_odd (n)
    ;; Returns 1 if n is odd, 0 if n is even. Handles negative numbers by taking absolute value.
    (if (= (math_mod (math_abs n) 2) 1)
        1
        0))

  (defun classify_parity (numbers)
    (def n (arr_length numbers))
    (def result (arr_new n))
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i n)
      (def num (arr_get numbers i))
      (arr_set result i (is_odd num))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    result)

  (test empty_array
    (eq?
      (classify_parity (arr_new 0))
      (arr_new 0)))

  (test all_even
    (def input_even (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set input_even 0 2)
    (arr_set input_even 1 4)
    (arr_set input_even 2 6)
    (def expected_even (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set expected_even 0 0)
    (arr_set expected_even 1 0)
    (arr_set expected_even 2 0)
    (eq?
      (classify_parity input_even)
      expected_even))

  (test all_odd
    (def input_odd (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set input_odd 0 1)
    (arr_set input_odd 1 3)
    (arr_set input_odd 2 5)
    (def expected_odd (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set expected_odd 0 1)
    (arr_set expected_odd 1 1)
    (arr_set expected_odd 2 1)
    (eq?
      (classify_parity input_odd)
      expected_odd))

  (test mixed_positive
    (def input_mixed (arr_new 5))
    (arr_set input_mixed 0 1)
    (arr_set input_mixed 1 2)
    (arr_set input_mixed 2 3)
    (arr_set input_mixed 3 4)
    (arr_set input_mixed 4 5)
    (def expected_mixed (arr_new 5))
    (arr_set expected_mixed 0 1)
    (arr_set expected_mixed 1 0)
    (arr_set expected_mixed 2 1)
    (arr_set expected_mixed 3 0)
    (arr_set expected_mixed 4 1)
    (eq?
      (classify_parity input_mixed)
      expected_mixed))

  (test mixed_negative_and_zero
    (def input_neg_zero (arr_new 4))
    (arr_set input_neg_zero 0 0)
    (arr_set input_neg_zero 1 -3)
    (arr_set input_neg_zero 2 7)
    (arr_set input_neg_zero 3 -8)
    (def expected_neg_zero (arr_new 4))
    (arr_set expected_neg_zero 0 0)
    (arr_set expected_neg_zero 1 1)
    (arr_set expected_neg_zero 2 1)
    (arr_set expected_neg_zero 3 0)
    (eq?
      (classify_parity input_neg_zero)
      expected_neg_zero))

  (test single_positive
    (def input_single (arr_new 1))
    (arr_set input_single 0 99)
    (def expected_single (arr_new 1))
    (arr_set expected_single 0 1)
    (eq?
      (classify_parity input_single)
      expected_single))
)

objective: Create a program that computes the total monthly bill for a rental property, including a base rent amount fetched from an environment variable and various utility costs provided as arguments.
solution:
(module MonthlyRentCalculator
  ;; Declares an external capability to retrieve environment variables.
  (extern defun env_get (key) @capability "env.get")

  ;; Helper function to parse a string from an environment variable into a number.
  ;; Assumes that values retrieved via env_get for numeric keys will always be valid numeric strings.
  (defun parse_numeric_env_var (key)
    (str_to_num (env_get key)))

  ;; Calculates the total monthly bill by summing base rent (from an environment variable)
  ;; and individual utility costs.
  ;; base_rent_key: The string key to fetch the base rent from the environment.
  ;; electricity_cost: The cost of electricity for the month.
  ;; water_cost: The cost of water for the month.
  ;; gas_cost: The cost of gas for the month.
  ;; internet_cost: The cost of internet for the month.
  (defun calculate_monthly_bill (base_rent_key electricity_cost water_cost gas_cost internet_cost)
    (def base_rent (parse_numeric_env_var base_rent_key))
    (+ base_rent electricity_cost water_cost gas_cost internet_cost))

  ;; --- Test Cases ---

  ;; Test with standard positive costs for all components.
  (test basic_bill_all_utilities
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "BASE_RENT_UNIT_1" "1200"))
    (eq? (calculate_monthly_bill "BASE_RENT_UNIT_1" 50.00 30.50 20.00 60.00) 1360.50))

  ;; Test with some utility costs being zero.
  (test bill_with_some_zero_utilities
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "RENT_APT_B" "850.75"))
    (eq? (calculate_monthly_bill "RENT_APT_B" 0.00 45.00 0.00 35.25) 931.00))

  ;; Test with higher base rent and higher utility costs.
  (test higher_rent_and_utilities
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "HOUSE_RENT_PROPERTY_C" "2500.00"))
    (eq? (calculate_monthly_bill "HOUSE_RENT_PROPERTY_C" 120.75 80.25 70.00 95.00) 2866.00))

  ;; Test calculating only the base rent when no utilities are incurred.
  (test only_base_rent_no_utilities
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "STUDIO_RENT_FLAT_D" "600.00"))
    (eq? (calculate_monthly_bill "STUDIO_RENT_FLAT_D" 0.00 0.00 0.00 0.00) 600.00))

  ;; Test with different types of numeric values (integers and floats).
  (test mixed_numeric_types
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "CONDO_RENT_UNIT_E" "1550"))
    (eq? (calculate_monthly_bill "CONDO_RENT_UNIT_E" 75 40.5 25 55.75) 1746.25))
)

objective: Implement a function that takes an array of numbers and returns a new array with the elements in reverse order. The original array should remain unchanged.
solution:
(module ArrayReverse
  (defun reverse_array (arr)
    (def len (arr_length arr))
    (def reversed_arr (arr_new len))
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i len)
      (arr_set reversed_arr i (arr_get arr (- (- len 1) i)))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    reversed_arr)

  (test empty_array
    (eq? (reverse_array (arr_new 0)) (arr_new 0)))

  (test single_element_array
    (def input (arr_new 1))
    (arr_set input 0 42)
    (def expected (arr_new 1))
    (arr_set expected 0 42)
    (eq? (reverse_array input) expected))

  (test two_elements_array
    (def input (arr_new 2))
    (arr_set input 0 10)
    (arr_set input 1 20)
    (def expected (arr_new 2))
    (arr_set expected 0 20)
    (arr_set expected 1 10)
    (eq? (reverse_array input) expected))

  (test odd_length_array
    (def input (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set input 0 1)
    (arr_set input 1 2)
    (arr_set input 2 3)
    (def expected (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set expected 0 3)
    (arr_set expected 1 2)
    (arr_set expected 2 1)
    (eq? (reverse_array input) expected))

  (test even_length_array
    (def input (arr_new 4))
    (arr_set input 0 100)
    (arr_set input 1 200)
    (arr_set input 2 300)
    (arr_set input 3 400)
    (def expected (arr_new 4))
    (arr_set expected 0 400)
    (arr_set expected 1 300)
    (arr_set expected 2 200)
    (arr_set expected 3 100)
    (eq? (reverse_array input) expected))

  (test array_with_duplicates
    (def input (arr_new 5))
    (arr_set input 0 1)
    (arr_set input 1 2)
    (arr_set input 2 1)
    (arr_set input 3 3)
    (arr_set input 4 2)
    (def expected (arr_new 5))
    (arr_set expected 0 2)
    (arr_set expected 1 3)
    (arr_set expected 2 1)
    (arr_set expected 3 2)
    (arr_set expected 4 1)
    (eq? (reverse_array input) expected)))

objective: Implement the Euclidean algorithm to find the greatest common divisor (GCD) of two non-negative integers. The function should take two integers, `a` and `b`, and return their GCD.
solution:
(module EuclideanGCD
  (defun gcd (a b)
    (while (> b 0)
      (def temp b)
      (set b (math_mod a b))
      (set a temp))
    a)

  (test standard_case
    (eq? (gcd 48 18) 6))

  (test one_is_zero
    (eq? (gcd 25 0) 25))

  (test zero_is_first
    (eq? (gcd 0 10) 10))

  (test prime_coprime
    (eq? (gcd 7 5) 1))

  (test one_is_divisor
    (eq? (gcd 14 7) 7))

  (test large_numbers
    (eq? (gcd 1071 462) 21))

  (test equal_numbers
    (eq? (gcd 12 12) 12)))

objective: Calculate the minimum payment required for a credit card based on the current balance, annual percentage rate (APR), a minimum payment percentage, and a fixed minimum payment. The calculation should first add monthly interest to the balance, then determine the greater of a percentage-based payment or a fixed minimum payment, returning zero if the balance is zero or negative.
solution:
(module CreditCardMinimumPayment
  (defun calculate_monthly_interest (balance apr)
    ;; Calculates the monthly interest. APR is annual, so divide by 12, then by 100 to convert percentage to decimal.
    (* balance (/ apr 1200.0)))

  (defun calculate_balance_after_interest (current_balance monthly_interest)
    ;; Calculates the new balance after adding the monthly interest.
    (+ current_balance monthly_interest))

  (defun determine_minimum_payment (balance apr min_payment_percentage fixed_minimum_payment)
    ;; If the balance is zero or negative, the minimum payment is zero.
    (if (<= balance 0.0) (return 0.0))

    ;; Calculate monthly interest and the new balance.
    (def monthly_interest (calculate_monthly_interest balance apr))
    (def balance_with_interest (calculate_balance_after_interest balance monthly_interest))

    ;; Calculate the payment based on a percentage of the new balance.
    (def percentage_based_payment (* balance_with_interest (/ min_payment_percentage 100.0)))

    ;; The minimum payment is the greater of the percentage-based payment or the fixed minimum.
    (math_max percentage_based_payment fixed_minimum_payment))

  (test min_payment_fixed_applies
    ;; Balance is low, fixed minimum payment applies.
    (near? (determine_minimum_payment 100.0 18.0 2.0 25.0) 25.0 0.01))

  (test min_payment_percentage_applies
    ;; Balance is high, percentage-based payment applies.
    (near? (determine_minimum_payment 2000.0 24.0 3.0 25.0) 61.2 0.01))

  (test zero_balance
    ;; Zero balance should result in zero minimum payment.
    (near? (determine_minimum_payment 0.0 20.0 2.0 25.0) 0.0 0.01))

  (test negative_balance
    ;; Negative balance should result in zero minimum payment.
    (near? (determine_minimum_payment -50.0 15.0 2.5 30.0) 0.0 0.01))

  (test high_interest_high_balance
    ;; Test with higher balance and APR where percentage-based payment applies.
    (near? (determine_minimum_payment 5000.0 29.99 4.0 35.0) 205.00 0.01))

  (test low_percentage_fixed_applies
    ;; Test where a very low percentage still makes the fixed minimum apply.
    (near? (determine_minimum_payment 1000.0 12.0 0.5 25.0) 25.0 0.01))
)

objective: Write a function that calculates the maximum absolute difference between any two numbers within a given array. If the array contains fewer than two elements, the function should return 0.
solution:
(module MaxAbsDifference
  (defun max_absolute_difference (numbers)
    (def n (arr_length numbers))
    (if (< n 2)
      (return 0)) ; Handle empty or single-element arrays

    (def min_val (arr_get numbers 0))
    (def max_val (arr_get numbers 0))
    (def i 1)

    (while (< i n)
      (def current_val (arr_get numbers i))
      (set min_val (math_min min_val current_val))
      (set max_val (math_max max_val current_val))
      (set i (+ i 1)))

    (math_abs (- max_val min_val)))

  (test empty_array
    (eq? (max_absolute_difference (arr_new 0)) 0))

  (test single_element_array
    (def arr1 (arr_new 1))
    (arr_set arr1 0 42)
    (eq? (max_absolute_difference arr1) 0))

  (test positive_numbers
    (def arr_pos (arr_new 5))
    (arr_set arr_pos 0 1)
    (arr_set arr_pos 1 5)
    (arr_set arr_pos 2 2)
    (arr_set arr_pos 3 8)
    (arr_set arr_pos 4 3)
    (eq? (max_absolute_difference arr_pos) 7)) ; max: 8, min: 1, diff: 7

  (test negative_numbers
    (def arr_neg (arr_new 5))
    (arr_set arr_neg 0 -1)
    (arr_set arr_neg 1 -5)
    (arr_set arr_neg 2 -2)
    (arr_set arr_neg 3 -8)
    (arr_set arr_neg 4 -3)
    (eq? (max_absolute_difference arr_neg) 7)) ; max: -1, min: -8, diff: 7

  (test mixed_numbers
    (def arr_mix (arr_new 5))
    (arr_set arr_mix 0 -1)
    (arr_set arr_mix 1 5)
    (arr_set arr_mix 2 0)
    (arr_set arr_mix 3 -8)
    (arr_set arr_mix 4 3)
    (eq? (max_absolute_difference arr_mix) 13)) ; max: 5, min: -8, diff: 13

  (test all_same_numbers
    (def arr_same (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set arr_same 0 7)
    (arr_set arr_same 1 7)
    (arr_set arr_same 2 7)
    (eq? (max_absolute_difference arr_same) 0))

  (test two_elements
    (def arr_two (arr_new 2))
    (arr_set arr_two 0 10)
    (arr_set arr_two 1 2)
    (eq? (max_absolute_difference arr_two) 8)))

objective: Implement a function `integer_pow` that calculates the result of raising an integer `base` to a non-negative integer `exponent`. The function should return `base` raised to the power of `exponent`.
solution:
(module IntegerPower
  (defun integer_pow (base exponent)
    (if (< exponent 0)
      ; For this problem, we assume non-negative exponents.
      ; A negative exponent would typically result in a float,
      ; which is outside the scope of integer power.
      (return 0)) ; Or throw error, but 0 is a safe placeholder for invalid input

    (if (= exponent 0)
      (return 1))

    (def result 1)
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i exponent)
      (set result (* result base))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    result)

  (test base_two_exp_three (eq? (integer_pow 2 3) 8))
  (test base_five_exp_two (eq? (integer_pow 5 2) 25))
  (test base_ten_exp_zero (eq? (integer_pow 10 0) 1))
  (test base_negative_two_exp_four (eq? (integer_pow -2 4) 16))
  (test base_negative_three_exp_three (eq? (integer_pow -3 3) -27))
  (test base_seven_exp_one (eq? (integer_pow 7 1) 7))
  (test base_one_exp_ten (eq? (integer_pow 1 10) 1)))

objective: Write a function that takes three numbers and returns their median. The median of three numbers is the middle value when the numbers are sorted.
solution:
(module MedianOfThree
  (defun median_of_three (a b c)
    (def min_val (math_min a (math_min b c)))
    (def max_val (math_max a (math_max b c)))
    (- (+ a b c) min_val max_val))

  (test sorted_ascending (eq? (median_of_three 1 2 3) 2))
  (test sorted_descending (eq? (median_of_three 3 2 1) 2))
  (test mixed_order (eq? (median_of_three 5 1 3) 3))
  (test two_equal_smallest (eq? (median_of_three 1 5 1) 1))
  (test two_equal_largest (eq? (median_of_three 5 1 5) 5))
  (test all_equal (eq? (median_of_three 7 7 7) 7))
  (test negative_numbers (eq? (median_of_three -1 -5 -3) -3))
  (test mixed_signs (eq? (median_of_three -10 0 10) 0)))

objective: Write a function `sum_absolute_differences` that takes two arrays of numbers. It should calculate the sum of the absolute differences between corresponding elements, up to the length of the shorter array. If either array is empty, or both, the sum is zero.
solution:
(module ArrayAbsoluteDifferences
  (defun sum_absolute_differences (arr1 arr2)
    (def total_diff 0)
    (def len1 (arr_length arr1))
    (def len2 (arr_length arr2))
    (def i 0)

    (def loop_limit (math_min len1 len2))

    (while (< i loop_limit)
      (def val1 (arr_get arr1 i))
      (def val2 (arr_get arr2 i))
      (set total_diff (+ total_diff (math_abs (- val1 val2))))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    total_diff)

  (defun make_arr_2 (a b)
    (def xs (arr_new 2))
    (arr_set xs 0 a)
    (arr_set xs 1 b)
    xs)

  (defun make_arr_3 (a b c)
    (def xs (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set xs 0 a)
    (arr_set xs 1 b)
    (arr_set xs 2 c)
    xs)

  (test empty_arrays
    (eq? (sum_absolute_differences (arr_new 0) (arr_new 0)) 0))

  (test equal_arrays
    (eq? (sum_absolute_differences (make_arr_3 1 2 3) (make_arr_3 1 2 3)) 0))

  (test different_same_length
    (eq? (sum_absolute_differences (make_arr_3 1 2 3) (make_arr_3 4 5 6)) 9))

  (test different_lengths
    (eq? (sum_absolute_differences (make_arr_2 10 20) (make_arr_3 10 15 30)) 5))

  (test negative_numbers
    (eq? (sum_absolute_differences (make_arr_2 -1 -5) (make_arr_2 1 -2)) 5))

  (test one_empty_first
    (eq? (sum_absolute_differences (arr_new 0) (make_arr_2 1 2)) 0))

  (test one_empty_second
    (eq? (sum_absolute_differences (make_arr_2 1 2) (arr_new 0)) 0))
)

objective: Create a system to calculate parking costs where the first hour is free. After the first hour, the parking duration is charged per hour, rounded up to the nearest full hour.
solution:
(module ParkingCostCalculator
  ; Calculates the number of minutes that are subject to charge, after the first hour free.
  (defun get_chargeable_minutes (total_minutes)
    (if (<= total_minutes 60)
      (return 0))
    (- total_minutes 60))

  ; Calculates the number of chargeable hours, rounding up to the nearest whole hour.
  (defun get_chargeable_hours (chargeable_minutes)
    (if (<= chargeable_minutes 0)
      (return 0))
    ; Round up minutes to full hours for charging
    (math_ceil (/ chargeable_minutes 60.0)))

  ; Calculates the total parking cost based on total duration and hourly rate,
  ; with the first hour of parking being free. Chargeable time is rounded up to the nearest hour.
  (defun calculate_total_cost (total_minutes hourly_rate)
    (def cm (get_chargeable_minutes total_minutes))
    (def ch (get_chargeable_hours cm))
    (* ch hourly_rate))

  ; Test cases
  ; Parking within the first hour (30 minutes) should be free.
  (test under_one_hour_free
    (eq? (calculate_total_cost 30 2.50) 0.0))

  ; Parking for exactly one hour (60 minutes) should be free.
  (test exactly_one_hour_free
    (eq? (calculate_total_cost 60 2.50) 0.0))

  ; Parking for 75 minutes (1 hour 15 minutes) means 15 chargeable minutes, rounded up to 1 chargeable hour.
  (test just_over_one_hour
    (eq? (calculate_total_cost 75 2.50) 2.50))

  ; Parking for exactly two hours (120 minutes) means 60 chargeable minutes, rounded up to 1 chargeable hour.
  (test exactly_two_hours
    (eq? (calculate_total_cost 120 2.50) 2.50))

  ; Parking for 121 minutes (2 hours 1 minute) means 61 chargeable minutes, rounded up to 2 chargeable hours.
  (test just_over_two_hours
    (eq? (calculate_total_cost 121 2.50) 5.00))

  ; Parking for 195 minutes (3 hours 15 minutes) means 135 chargeable minutes, rounded up to 3 chargeable hours.
  (test longer_duration
    (eq? (calculate_total_cost 195 2.50) 7.50))

  ; Parking for 301 minutes (5 hours 1 minute) with a rate of 3.00, means 241 chargeable minutes, rounded up to 5 chargeable hours.
  (test very_long_duration
    (eq? (calculate_total_cost 301 3.00) 15.00)))

topic: shipping cost by weight and zone
objective: Write a function `calculate_shipping_cost` that determines the shipping charge for a package based on its weight and destination zone. Zone 'A' costs $1.50/kg, 'B' costs $2.00/kg, and 'C' costs $2.75/kg, with a minimum charge of $5.00 per shipment. Additionally, any package weighing over 20kg incurs a flat $10.00 heavy item surcharge.
solution:
(module ShippingCalculator
  (defun calculate_shipping_cost (weight zone)
    (def rate 0.0)

    ;; Determine base rate per kg based on zone
    (if (str_eq zone "A") (set rate 1.50))
    (if (str_eq zone "B") (set rate 2.00))
    (if (str_eq zone "C") (set rate 2.75))

    ;; Calculate initial cost
    (def cost (* weight rate))

    ;; Apply minimum charge if necessary
    (if (< cost 5.0) (set cost 5.0))

    ;; Apply heavy item surcharge if weight exceeds 20kg
    (if (> weight 20.0) (set cost (+ cost 10.0)))

    cost)

  (test light_zone_A_min_cost
    (near? (calculate_shipping_cost 2.0 "A") 5.0 0.001)) ; 2kg * 1.5 = 3.0, minimum 5.0 applies

  (test medium_zone_B
    (near? (calculate_shipping_cost 10.0 "B") 20.0 0.001)) ; 10kg * 2.0 = 20.0

  (test heavy_zone_C_surcharge
    (near? (calculate_shipping_cost 25.0 "C") 78.75 0.001)) ; 25kg * 2.75 = 68.75, +10.0 surcharge = 78.75

  (test medium_zone_A_no_special
    (near? (calculate_shipping_cost 15.0 "A") 22.5 0.001)) ; 15kg * 1.5 = 22.5

  (test exactly_20kg_zone_B
    (near? (calculate_shipping_cost 20.0 "B") 40.0 0.001)) ; 20kg * 2.0 = 40.0, no surcharge because not > 20.0

  (test light_zone_C_min_cost
    (near? (calculate_shipping_cost 1.0 "C") 5.0 0.001))) ; 1kg * 2.75 = 2.75, minimum 5.0 applies

  (defun calculate_final_grade (quiz_score quiz_weight hw_score hw_weight midterm_score midterm_weight final_exam_score final_exam_weight)
    (def weighted_quiz (calculate_weighted_component quiz_score quiz_weight))
    (def weighted_hw (calculate_weighted_component hw_score hw_weight))
    (def weighted_midterm (calculate_weighted_component midterm_score midterm_weight))
    (def weighted_final_exam (calculate_weighted_component final_exam_score final_exam_weight))
    (+ weighted_quiz weighted_hw weighted_midterm weighted_final_exam))

objective: Calculate the total cost of a hotel stay, factoring in a base nightly rate and a percentage-based surcharge for weekend nights. The calculation should account for a specified number of weekday and weekend nights, and round the final cost to two decimal places.
solution:
(module HotelBooking
  ;; Helper to calculate the absolute surcharge amount for one night
  (defun calculate_surcharge_amount (base_rate surcharge_pct)
    (* base_rate (/ surcharge_pct 100.0)))

  ;; Helper to calculate the rate for a single weekend night, including surcharge
  (defun calculate_weekend_night_rate (base_rate surcharge_pct)
    (+ base_rate (calculate_surcharge_amount base_rate surcharge_pct)))

  ;; Helper to round a number to two decimal places, suitable for currency
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100.0) 0.5)) 100.0))

  ;; Calculates the total cost for a hotel stay based on base rate, weekend surcharge,
  ;; and the number of weekday and weekend nights.
  (defun calculate_total_hotel_cost (base_rate surcharge_pct num_weekdays num_weekend_nights)
    (def weekday_total (* base_rate num_weekdays))
    (def weekend_night_rate (calculate_weekend_night_rate base_rate surcharge_pct))
    (def weekend_total (* weekend_night_rate num_weekend_nights))
    (round2 (+ weekday_total weekend_total)))

  (test one_weekday_no_surcharge
    (near? (calculate_total_hotel_cost 100 0 1 0) 100.00 0.01))

  (test two_weekend_nights_with_surcharge
    (near? (calculate_total_hotel_cost 100 15 0 2) 230.00 0.01))

  (test mixed_stay
    (near? (calculate_total_hotel_cost 120 20 3 2) 648.00 0.01))

  (test zero_nights
    (near? (calculate_total_hotel_cost 150 10 0 0) 0.00 0.01))

  (test fractional_surcharge_and_rate
    (near? (calculate_total_hotel_cost 85.50 12.5 4 1) 438.19 0.01))

  (test high_surcharge_long_stay
    (near? (calculate_total_hotel_cost 200 50 5 3) 1900.00 0.01)))

(module EnvIntReader
  (extern defun env_read (key) @capability "env.get")

  ; Helper to safely parse an integer string, returning a default if empty.
  ; This function assumes that if the input string 's' is not empty,
  ; it represents a valid integer that can be parsed by str_to_num.
  (defun parse_int_or_default (s default_val)
    (if (str_eq s "")
      (return default_val))
    (str_to_num s))

  ; Reads an environment variable identified by 'key' and attempts to parse it as an integer.
  ; Returns 'default_value' if the environment variable is not found or is an empty string.
  (defun get_env_integer (key default_value)
    (def env_val_str (env_read key))
    (parse_int_or_default env_val_str default_value))

  (test value_present
    (mocks (env.get "SERVER_PORT" "8080"))
    (eq? (get_env_integer "SERVER_PORT" 5000) 8080))

    (eq? (get_env_integer "MAX_CONNECTIONS" 10) 100))

  (test zero_value

    (eq? (get_env_integer "DEBUG_LEVEL" 1) 0))

  (test negative_value

    (eq? (get_env_integer "CACHE_EXPIRATION_MINUTES" 60) -1))

objective: Create a program that classifies a person's current heart rate into standard training zones based on their age and current heart rate. The maximum heart rate (MHR) should be calculated as 220 minus age, and the zones are determined by the percentage of MHR.
solution:
(module HeartRateZoneClassifier
  ;; Calculates Maximum Heart Rate (MHR) using the formula 220 - age.
  (defun calculate_mhr (age)
    (- 220 age))

  ;; Calculates the current heart rate as a percentage of the Maximum Heart Rate (MHR).
  (defun get_zone_percentage (mhr current_hr)
    (/ (* current_hr 100.0) mhr))

  ;; Classifies the heart rate percentage into a specific zone.
  ;; Zone 1: Very Light (50-60%)
  ;; Zone 2: Light (60-70%)
  ;; Zone 3: Moderate (70-80%)
  ;; Zone 4: Hard (80-90%)
  ;; Zone 5: Maximum (90-100%)
  ;; Below 50% is "Below Zone 1", above 100% is "Over Maximum".
  (defun classify_zone (percentage)
    (if (< percentage 50.0) "Below Zone 1"
      (if (< percentage 60.0) "Zone 1: Very Light"
        (if (< percentage 70.0) "Zone 2: Light"
          (if (< percentage 80.0) "Zone 3: Moderate"
            (if (< percentage 90.0) "Zone 4: Hard"
              (if (<= percentage 100.0) "Zone 5: Maximum"
                "Over Maximum")))))))

  ;; Main function to determine the heart rate zone given age and current heart rate.
  (defun get_heart_rate_zone (age current_hr)
    (def mhr (calculate_mhr age))
    (def percentage (get_zone_percentage mhr current_hr))
    (classify_zone percentage))

  (test young_low_hr
    (eq? (get_heart_rate_zone 30 80) "Below Zone 1"))

  (test young_moderate_hr
    (eq? (get_heart_rate_zone 30 120) "Zone 2: Light"))

  (test older_moderate_hr
    (eq? (get_heart_rate_zone 50 120) "Zone 3: Moderate"))

  (test older_hard_hr
    (eq? (get_heart_rate_zone 40 155) "Zone 4: Hard"))

  (test max_zone_hr
    (eq? (get_heart_rate_zone 25 190) "Zone 5: Maximum"))

  (test edge_50_percent
    (eq? (get_heart_rate_zone 40 90) "Zone 1: Very Light")) ; 90 is exactly 50% of (220-40=180)

  (test edge_over_max
    (eq? (get_heart_rate_zone 40 185) "Over Maximum"))) ; 185 is over 100% of (220-40=180)

topic: read file and count char occurrences
objective: Create a program that reads the content of a specified file and counts the occurrences of a given character within that content. The function should take the file path and the target character as input and return the total count.
solution:
(module CharacterCounter
  (extern defun file_read (path) @capability "file.read")

  (defun count_char_occurrences (file_path target_char)
    (def content (file_read file_path))
    (def content_length (str_length content))
    (def count 0)
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i content_length)
      (if (str_eq (str_substring content i 1) target_char)
        (set count (+ count 1)))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    count)

  (test empty_file
    (mocks (file.read "/empty.txt" ""))
    (eq? (count_char_occurrences "/empty.txt" "a") 0))

  (test no_occurrences
    (mocks (file.read "/text.txt" "hello world"))
    (eq? (count_char_occurrences "/text.txt" "z") 0))

  (test single_occurrence
    (mocks (file.read "/single.txt" "apple"))
    (eq? (count_char_occurrences "/single.txt" "p") 2))

  (test multiple_occurrences
    (mocks (file.read "/data.txt" "banana rama llama"))
    (eq? (count_char_occurrences "/data.txt" "a") 7))

  (test case_sensitive
    (mocks (file.read "/case.txt" "Hello World"))
    (eq? (count_char_occurrences "/case.txt" "o") 2))

  (test whitespace_char
    (mocks (file.read "/spaces.txt" "one two three"))
    (eq? (count_char_occurrences "/spaces.txt" " ") 2))

  (test full_match
    (mocks (file.read "/char.txt" "x"))
    (eq? (count_char_occurrences "/char.txt" "x") 1))
)

objective: Given an environment variable key, read its string value and convert it to a boolean. The value should be considered `true` if it's "true", "yes", or "1" (case-insensitive); otherwise, it should be `false`.
solution:
(module EnvVarToBool
  (extern defun env_get (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun get_env_bool (key)
    (def val_str (env_get key))
    (def lower_val (str_lower val_str))
    (if (str_eq lower_val "true") (return true))
    (if (str_eq lower_val "yes") (return true))
    (if (str_eq lower_val "1") (return true))
    false)

  (test true_string

objective: Implement a function that takes a string and a non-negative integer `n`, and returns a new string consisting of the original string repeated `n` times. If `n` is zero, return an empty string.
solution:
(module StringRepeater
  (defun repeat_string (s n)
    (def result "")
    (def i 0)
    (if (< n 0)
      (return "")) ; Handle negative n by returning empty string

    (while (< i n)
      (set result (str_concat result s))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    result)

  (test repeat_three_times
    (eq? (repeat_string "abc" 3) "abcabcabc"))

  (test repeat_once
    (eq? (repeat_string "hello" 1) "hello"))

  (test repeat_zero_times
    (eq? (repeat_string "world" 0) ""))

  (test repeat_empty_string
    (eq? (repeat_string "" 5) ""))

  (test repeat_long_string_twice
    (eq? (repeat_string "long_string_example" 2) "long_string_examplelong_string_example"))

  (test repeat_with_negative_n
    (eq? (repeat_string "test" -2) "")))

objective: Implement a pricing system for an online store that applies bulk discounts based on the quantity of items purchased. Calculate the final price for a given unit price and quantity, applying a 5% discount for quantities between 10 and 19 (inclusive) and a 10% discount for quantities of 20 or more.
solution:
(module BulkPricing
  (defun round2 (x) (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))

  (defun get_discount_percentage (qty)
    (if (< qty 10)
      (return 0))
    (if (< qty 20) ; Implies qty >= 10
      (return 5))
    ; Implies qty >= 20
    10)

  (defun apply_discount (amount pct)
    (- amount (/ (* amount pct) 100.0)))

  (defun calculate_bulk_price (unit_price qty)
    (def base_total (* unit_price qty))
    (def discount_pct (get_discount_percentage qty))
    (def final_total (apply_discount base_total discount_pct))
    (round2 final_total))

  (test no_discount (near? (calculate_bulk_price 10 5) 50.00 0.01))
  (test tier1_exact (near? (calculate_bulk_price 10 10) 95.00 0.01))
  (test tier1_middle (near? (calculate_bulk_price 10 15) 142.50 0.01))
  (test tier2_exact (near? (calculate_bulk_price 10 20) 180.00 0.01))
  (test tier2_middle (near? (calculate_bulk_price 10 25) 225.00 0.01))
  (test mixed_price_tier1 (near? (calculate_bulk_price 2.75 12) 31.35 0.01))
  (test mixed_price_tier2 (near? (calculate_bulk_price 5.20 30) 140.40 0.01)))

objective: Implement a function to calculate the minimum payment for a credit card. The minimum payment is determined as the greater of a flat fee or a percentage of the current balance plus the accrued monthly interest, but never more than the outstanding balance itself.
solution:
(module CreditCardMinimumPayment

  ;; Calculates the monthly interest amount based on the balance and annual APR.
  ;; annual_apr is a percentage (e.g., 20 for 20%).
  (defun calculate_monthly_interest (balance annual_apr)
    (* balance (/ annual_apr 1200.0))) ; Divide by 100 for percentage, then by 12 for monthly

  ;; Calculates the base percentage portion of the payment from the balance.
  ;; min_percentage is a percentage (e.g., 2 for 2%).
  (defun calculate_base_percentage_amount (balance min_percentage)
    (* balance (/ min_percentage 100.0)))

  ;; Calculates the final credit card minimum payment.
  ;; balance: Current outstanding balance.
  ;; annual_apr: Annual Percentage Rate (e.g., 20 for 20%).
  ;; min_percentage: Minimum payment percentage of balance (e.g., 2 for 2%).
  ;; min_flat_fee: Flat minimum payment fee (e.g., 25).
  (defun calculate_minimum_payment (balance annual_apr min_percentage min_flat_fee)
    (if (<= balance 0.0)
      (return 0.0))

    (def monthly_interest (calculate_monthly_interest balance annual_apr))
    (def percentage_part (calculate_base_percentage_amount balance min_percentage))
    
    ;; Option A: Percentage of balance + monthly interest
    (def payment_option_A (+ percentage_part monthly_interest))
    
    ;; Compare Option A with the flat fee, take the greater
    (def calculated_min_payment (math_max payment_option_A min_flat_fee))
    
    ;; Ensure minimum payment does not exceed the outstanding balance
    (if (< balance calculated_min_payment)
      balance
      calculated_min_payment))

  (test high_balance_percentage_wins
    (near? (calculate_minimum_payment 1000.0 20.0 2.0 25.0) 36.666 0.001)) ; 1000*0.02 + 1000*0.20/12 = 20 + 16.666 = 36.666 (greater than 25)

  (test high_balance_flat_fee_wins
    (near? (calculate_minimum_payment 500.0 20.0 2.0 25.0) 25.0 0.001)) ; 500*0.02 + 500*0.20/12 = 10 + 8.333 = 18.333 (less than 25, so 25 wins)

  (test zero_balance
    (near? (calculate_minimum_payment 0.0 18.0 3.0 30.0) 0.0 0.001))

  (test negative_balance
    (near? (calculate_minimum_payment -50.0 15.0 2.5 20.0) 0.0 0.001))

  (test small_balance_less_than_min_payment
    (near? (calculate_minimum_payment 10.0 20.0 2.0 25.0) 10.0 0.001)) ; 10*0.02 + 10*0.20/12 = 0.2 + 0.166 = 0.366 (less than 25, but balance is 10, so 10 wins)

  (test balance_equals_flat_fee
    (near? (calculate_minimum_payment 25.0 10.0 1.0 25.0) 25.0 0.001)) ; 25*0.01 + 25*0.10/12 = 0.25 + 0.208 = 0.458 (less than 25, so 25 wins)
)

objective: Implement a Body Mass Index (BMI) calculator and classifier. The program should take a person's weight in kilograms and height in meters, calculate their BMI, and return a string classifying their BMI into categories: "Underweight", "Normal weight", "Overweight", or "Obesity".
solution:
(module BmiClassifier
  (defun calculate_bmi (weight_kg height_m)
    (/ weight_kg (* height_m height_m)))

  (defun classify_bmi_category (bmi)
    (if (< bmi 18.5) (return "Underweight"))
    (if (< bmi 25.0) (return "Normal weight"))
    (if (< bmi 30.0) (return "Overweight"))
    "Obesity")

  (defun get_bmi_classification (weight_kg height_m)
    (def bmi_val (calculate_bmi weight_kg height_m))
    (classify_bmi_category bmi_val))

  (test underweight_example
    (eq? (get_bmi_classification 50 1.7) "Underweight")) ; BMI = 50 / (1.7 * 1.7) = 17.30...

  (test normal_weight_example
    (eq? (get_bmi_classification 70 1.75) "Normal weight")) ; BMI = 70 / (1.75 * 1.75) = 22.85...

  (test overweight_example
    (eq? (get_bmi_classification 85 1.7) "Overweight")) ; BMI = 85 / (1.7 * 1.7) = 29.41...

  (test obesity_example
    (eq? (get_bmi_classification 100 1.7) "Obesity")) ; BMI = 100 / (1.7 * 1.7) = 34.60...

  (test edge_case_normal_lower
    (eq? (get_bmi_classification 53.465 1.7) "Normal weight")) ; BMI = 18.5 exactly

  (test edge_case_overweight_lower
    (eq? (get_bmi_classification 72.25 1.7) "Overweight")) ; BMI = 25.0 exactly

  (test edge_case_obesity_lower
    (eq? (get_bmi_classification 86.7 1.7) "Obesity"))) ; BMI = 30.0 exactly

objective: Implement a function that determines whether a given year is a leap year according to the Gregorian calendar rules. A year is a leap year if it is divisible by 4, unless it is divisible by 100 but not by 400.
solution:
(module LeapYearChecker
  (defun is_leap_year (year)
    (if (= (math_mod year 400) 0)
      (return 1))
    (if (= (math_mod year 100) 0)
      (return 0))
    (if (= (math_mod year 4) 0)
      (return 1))
    0)

  (test year_2000_is_leap (eq? (is_leap_year 2000) 1))
  (test year_1900_not_leap (eq? (is_leap_year 1900) 0))
  (test year_2024_is_leap (eq? (is_leap_year 2024) 1))
  (test year_2023_not_leap (eq? (is_leap_year 2023) 0))
  (test year_1600_is_leap (eq? (is_leap_year 1600) 1))
  (test year_2100_not_leap (eq? (is_leap_year 2100) 0))
  (test year_1996_is_leap (eq? (is_leap_year 1996) 1)))

objective: Write a function that calculates the digital root of a non-negative integer. The digital root is the single-digit value obtained by an iterative process of summing digits, repeating until a single-digit number is reached.
solution:
(module DigitalRootCalculator
  (defun digital_root (n)
    (if (= n 0) (return 0))
    (def remainder (math_mod n 9))
    (if (= remainder 0) (return 9))
    remainder)

  (test zero          (eq? (digital_root 0) 0))
  (test single_digit  (eq? (digital_root 5) 5))
  (test nine          (eq? (digital_root 9) 9))
  (test tens          (eq? (digital_root 10) 1))
  (test hundreds      (eq? (digital_root 123) 6))
  (test multiple_nine (eq? (digital_root 18) 9))
  (test large_number  (eq? (digital_root 12345) 6)))

objective: Implement a function `find_min` that takes an array of numbers and returns the smallest number within that array. If the array is empty, it should return a predefined large sentinel value (999999999).
solution:
(module MinElement
  (defun find_min (numbers)
    (def n (arr_length numbers))
    (if (= n 0)
      (return 999999999)) ; Sentinel for an empty array

    (def min_val (arr_get numbers 0))
    (def i 1)
    (while (< i n)
      (def current_val (arr_get numbers i))
      (if (< current_val min_val)
        (set min_val current_val))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    min_val)

  ; Helper function to create an array with 5 elements for tests
  (defun make_arr_5 (a b c d e)
    (def xs (arr_new 5))
    (arr_set xs 0 a)
    (arr_set xs 1 b)
    (arr_set xs 2 c)
    (arr_set xs 3 d)
    (arr_set xs 4 e)
    xs)

  ; Helper function to create an array with 1 element for tests
  (defun make_arr_1 (a)
    (def xs (arr_new 1))
    (arr_set xs 0 a)
    xs)

  (test basic_positives (eq? (find_min (make_arr_5 5 2 8 1 9)) 1))
  (test all_negatives (eq? (find_min (make_arr_5 -5 -2 -8 -1 -9)) -9))
  (test mixed_numbers (eq? (find_min (make_arr_5 10 -3 7 0 -5)) -5))
  (test duplicates (eq? (find_min (make_arr_5 4 2 8 2 5)) 2))
  (test single_element (eq? (find_min (make_arr_1 7)) 7))
  (test empty_array (eq? (find_min (arr_new 0)) 999999999))
  (test zero_is_min (eq? (find_min (make_arr_5 5 2 0 1 9)) 0))
)

objective: Calculate the Least Common Multiple (LCM) of two integers. The function should handle zero inputs by returning 0, and always return a non-negative result.
solution:
(module LeastCommonMultiple
  (defun gcd (a b)
    (set a (math_abs a))
    (set b (math_abs b))
    (while (> b 0)
      (def temp b)
      (set b (math_mod a b))
      (set a temp))
    a)

  (defun lcm (a b)
    (if (or (= a 0) (= b 0))
      (return 0))
    (def abs_a (math_abs a))
    (def abs_b (math_abs b))
    (def common_divisor (gcd abs_a abs_b))
    (/ (* abs_a abs_b) common_divisor))

  (test lcm_of_4_and_6 (eq? (lcm 4 6) 12))
  (test lcm_of_7_and_5_coprime (eq? (lcm 7 5) 35))
  (test lcm_with_zero_first (eq? (lcm 0 10) 0))
  (test lcm_with_zero_second (eq? (lcm 10 0) 0))
  (test lcm_of_12_and_18 (eq? (lcm 12 18) 36))
  (test lcm_with_negative_input (eq? (lcm -4 6) 12))
  (test lcm_of_same_number (eq? (lcm 5 5) 5)))

topic: read two env vars and concat
objective: Create a function that reads the `APP_NAME` and `APP_VERSION` environment variables. It should return a formatted string "Application: [name] (Version: [version])", using "Unknown Application" if `APP_NAME` is empty and "N/A" if `APP_VERSION` is empty.
solution:
(module AppInfo
  (extern defun env_read (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun get_app_description ()
    (def app_name_raw (env_read "APP_NAME"))
    (def app_version_raw (env_read "APP_VERSION"))

    (def final_app_name app_name_raw)
    (if (str_eq app_name_raw "")
      (set final_app_name "Unknown Application"))

    (def final_app_version app_version_raw)
    (if (str_eq app_version_raw "")
      (set final_app_version "N/A"))

    (str_concat "Application: "
                (str_concat final_app_name
                            (str_concat " (Version: "
                                        (str_concat final_app_version ")")))))

  (test both_present
    (mocks (env.get "APP_NAME" "MyWebApp")
           (env.get "APP_VERSION" "1.0.5"))
    (eq? (get_app_description) "Application: MyWebApp (Version: 1.0.5)"))

  (test name_missing
    (mocks (env.get "APP_NAME" "")
           (env.get "APP_VERSION" "2.1.0"))
    (eq? (get_app_description) "Application: Unknown Application (Version: 2.1.0)"))

  (test version_missing
    (mocks (env.get "APP_NAME" "DataService")
           (env.get "APP_VERSION" ""))
    (eq? (get_app_description) "Application: DataService (Version: N/A)"))

  (test both_missing
    (mocks (env.get "APP_NAME" "")
           (env.get "APP_VERSION" ""))
    (eq? (get_app_description) "Application: Unknown Application (Version: N/A)"))

  (test custom_defaults

           (env.get "APP_VERSION" "beta-1"))

)

objective: Calculate the parking cost based on the duration in minutes and an hourly rate. The first 60 minutes of parking are free. Any time beyond the first hour is charged at the given hourly rate, with partial hours rounded up to the nearest full hour.
solution:
(module ParkingCostCalculator
  ;; Helper to calculate the number of billable hours, rounded up.
  ;; Assumes the input `effective_minutes` is the duration *after* the free period.
  (defun calculate_rounded_up_hours (effective_minutes)
    (if (<= effective_minutes 0)
        (return 0))
    (math_ceil (/ effective_minutes 60.0)))

  ;; Calculates the total parking cost given duration in minutes and an hourly rate.
  ;; The first 60 minutes are free. Subsequent time is charged per hour, rounded up.
  (defun calculate_parking_cost (duration_minutes hourly_rate)
    (if (<= duration_minutes 60)
        (return 0))
    (def billable_minutes (- duration_minutes 60))
    (def billable_hours (calculate_rounded_up_hours billable_minutes))
    (* billable_hours hourly_rate))

  ;; Tests
  (test free_parking_short
    (eq? (calculate_parking_cost 30 10.0) 0.0))

  (test free_parking_exact_hour
    (eq? (calculate_parking_cost 60 15.0) 0.0))

  (test one_minute_over_free
    (eq? (calculate_parking_cost 61 20.0) 20.0)) ; 1 minute over -> 1 billable hour

  (test partial_second_hour
    (eq? (calculate_parking_cost 90 10.0) 10.0)) ; 30 minutes over -> 1 billable hour

  (test exact_second_hour
    (eq? (calculate_parking_cost 120 25.0) 25.0)) ; 60 minutes over -> 1 billable hour

  (test one_minute_into_third_hour
    (eq? (calculate_parking_cost 121 30.0) 60.0)) ; 61 minutes over -> 2 billable hours

  (test exact_three_hours
    (eq? (calculate_parking_cost 180 12.0) 24.0)) ; 120 minutes over -> 2 billable hours
)

objective: Implement a function that calculates the total price for a given quantity of an item, applying a tiered bulk discount. Discounts are 0% for quantities less than 10, 5% for quantities between 10 and 49, and 10% for quantities 50 or more. The final price should be rounded to two decimal places.
solution:
(module BulkPriceCalculator
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))

  (defun get_discount_rate (quantity)
    (if (>= quantity 50)
      (return 0.10)) ; 10% discount for 50 or more
    (if (>= quantity 10)
      (return 0.05)) ; 5% discount for 10-49
    0.0)             ; No discount for less than 10

  (defun calculate_total_price (unit_price quantity)
    (def original_total (* unit_price quantity))
    (def discount_rate (get_discount_rate quantity))
    (def discounted_amount (* original_total discount_rate))
    (def final_price (- original_total discounted_amount))
    (round2 final_price))

  (test no_discount_single_item
    (near? (calculate_total_price 10.00 1) 10.00 0.01))

  (test no_discount_multiple_items_below_tier1
    (near? (calculate_total_price 15.50 5) 77.50 0.01))

  (test tier1_discount_min_quantity
    (near? (calculate_total_price 20.00 10) 190.00 0.01)) ; 20 * 10 = 200, 5% off (10) = 190

  (test tier1_discount_mid_quantity
    (near? (calculate_total_price 5.00 25) 118.75 0.01)) ; 5 * 25 = 125, 5% off (6.25) = 118.75

  (test tier2_discount_min_quantity
    (near? (calculate_total_price 100.00 50) 4500.00 0.01)) ; 100 * 50 = 5000, 10% off (500) = 4500

  (test tier2_discount_large_quantity
    (near? (calculate_total_price 2.50 100) 225.00 0.01)) ; 2.50 * 100 = 250, 10% off (25) = 225
)

objective: Implement a function to calculate a mobile phone bill, which includes a base monthly fee and an additional charge for minutes used beyond a specified allowance.
solution:
(module PhonePlanCalculator
  ; Calculates the number of minutes exceeding the included allowance.
  ; Returns 0 if actual usage is within or equal to the included minutes.
  (defun calculate_overage_minutes (actual_minutes_used included_minutes)
    (if (> actual_minutes_used included_minutes)
      (- actual_minutes_used included_minutes)
      0))

  ; Calculates the cost incurred by overage minutes at a given rate.
  (defun calculate_overage_charge (overage_minutes overage_rate_per_minute)
    (* overage_minutes overage_rate_per_minute))

  ; Calculates the total monthly phone bill, summing the base fee and any overage charges.
  (defun calculate_monthly_bill (base_fee included_minutes overage_rate_per_minute actual_minutes_used)
    (def overage_mins (calculate_overage_minutes actual_minutes_used included_minutes))
    (def overage_cost (calculate_overage_charge overage_mins overage_rate_per_minute))
    (+ base_fee overage_cost))

  (test no_overage
    (eq? (calculate_monthly_bill 30 500 0.10 450) 30))

  (test exact_limit
    (eq? (calculate_monthly_bill 30 500 0.10 500) 30))

  (test some_overage
    (eq? (calculate_monthly_bill 30 500 0.10 550) 35)) ; 50 over * 0.10 = 5.00 + 30 = 35

  (test significant_overage
    (eq? (calculate_monthly_bill 30 500 0.10 700) 50)) ; 200 over * 0.10 = 20.00 + 30 = 50

  (test zero_usage
    (eq? (calculate_monthly_bill 30 500 0.10 0) 30))

  (test different_plan
    (eq? (calculate_monthly_bill 50 1000 0.05 1100) 55)) ; 100 over * 0.05 = 5.00 + 50 = 55

  (test high_overage_rate
    (eq? (calculate_monthly_bill 20 200 0.25 250) 32.5)) ; 50 over * 0.25 = 12.50 + 20 = 32.5
)

objective: Develop a program that calculates the total monthly rent, including base rent and various utility costs. The calculation should account for electricity based on usage, water based on the number of occupants, a fixed internet charge, and shared utility costs split among occupants.
solution:
(module MonthlyRentCalculator

  ;; Calculates the cost of electricity based on usage and rate.
  (defun calculate_electricity_cost (kwh_usage rate_per_kwh)
    (* kwh_usage rate_per_kwh))

  ;; Calculates the cost of water based on the number of occupants and per-person cost.
  (defun calculate_water_cost (num_occupants water_cost_per_person)
    (* num_occupants water_cost_per_person))

  ;; Calculates each occupant's share of total shared utility costs.
  ;; Assumes num_occupants is always greater than 0.
  (defun calculate_shared_utility_share (total_shared_cost num_occupants)
    (/ total_shared_cost num_occupants))

  ;; Calculates the total monthly rent including base rent and all utilities.
  (defun calculate_total_monthly_rent
    (base_rent
     kwh_usage
     rate_per_kwh
     num_occupants
     water_cost_per_person
     internet_cost
     total_shared_cost)
    (def electricity (calculate_electricity_cost kwh_usage rate_per_kwh))
    (def water (calculate_water_cost num_occupants water_cost_per_person))
    (def shared_utilities (calculate_shared_utility_share total_shared_cost num_occupants))
    (+ base_rent electricity water internet_cost shared_utilities))

  (test basic_rent_single_occupant
    (near?
      (calculate_total_monthly_rent 1000 100 0.15 1 40 50 20)
      (+ 1000 (* 100 0.15) (* 1 40) 50 (/ 20 1))
      0.01))

  (test rent_multiple_occupants_higher_usage
    (near?
      (calculate_total_monthly_rent 1500 250 0.18 3 35 65 120)
      (+ 1500 (* 250 0.18) (* 3 35) 65 (/ 120 3))
      0.01))

  (test rent_zero_shared_cost
    (near?
      (calculate_total_monthly_rent 800 50 0.12 2 30 45 0)
      (+ 800 (* 50 0.12) (* 2 30) 45 (/ 0 2))
      0.01))

  (test rent_no_electricity_usage
    (near?
      (calculate_total_monthly_rent 950 0 0.10 1 30 50 30)
      (+ 950 (* 0 0.10) (* 1 30) 50 (/ 30 1))
      0.01))

  (test rent_decimal_rates
    (near?
      (calculate_total_monthly_rent 1200 180 0.165 2 32.5 55.5 85)
      (+ 1200 (* 180 0.165) (* 2 32.5) 55.5 (/ 85 2))
      0.01))
)

objective: Create a function that reads the content of a file and returns only its first line. The first line is defined as the text before the first newline character, or the entire file content if no newline character is present.
solution:
(module FileFirstLine
  (extern defun file_read (path) @capability "file.read")

  (defun get_first_line (path)
    (def content (file_read path))
    (def newline_idx (str_index_of content "\n"))
    (if (= newline_idx -1)
      content
      (str_substring content 0 newline_idx)))

  (test empty_file
    (mocks (file.read "/tmp/empty.txt" ""))
    (eq? (get_first_line "/tmp/empty.txt") ""))

  (test single_line_no_newline
    (mocks (file.read "/tmp/single.txt" "Hello World"))
    (eq? (get_first_line "/tmp/single.txt") "Hello World"))

  (test single_line_with_newline

    (eq? (get_first_line "/tmp/single_nl.txt") "First line"))

  (test multiple_lines
    (mocks (file.read "/tmp/multi.txt" "Line one\nLine two\nLine three"))
    (eq? (get_first_line "/tmp/multi.txt") "Line one"))

  (test starts_with_newline
    (mocks (file.read "/tmp/starts_nl.txt" "\nSecond line"))
    (eq? (get_first_line "/tmp/starts_nl.txt") ""))

  (test only_newline
    (mocks (file.read "/tmp/only_nl.txt" "\n"))
    (eq? (get_first_line "/tmp/only_nl.txt") ""))

  (test long_first_line
    (mocks (file.read "/tmp/long_line.txt" "This is a very long line that should be returned completely as the first line because there are no newlines in it."))
    (eq? (get_first_line "/tmp/long_line.txt") "This is a very long line that should be returned completely as the first line because there are no newlines in it.")))

objective: Implement a function `parse_bool_env` that reads an environment variable specified by a key. It should interpret common string representations of true ("true", "1") and false ("false", "0") as boolean values, case-insensitively. If the environment variable is not set or its value is not recognized, the function should return a provided default boolean value.
solution:
(module EnvVarBool
  (extern defun env_read (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun parse_bool_env (key default_value)
    (def raw_value (env_read key))
    (if (str_eq raw_value "") (return default_value))

    (def lower_value (str_lower raw_value))

    (if (str_eq lower_value "true") (return true))
    (if (str_eq lower_value "1") (return true))

    (if (str_eq lower_value "false") (return false))
    (if (str_eq lower_value "0") (return false))

    ; If none of the above, return the default_value
    default_value)

  (test missing_var_returns_default_true
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_FLAG" ""))
    (eq? (parse_bool_env "FEATURE_FLAG" true) true))

  (test missing_var_returns_default_false
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_FLAG" ""))
    (eq? (parse_bool_env "FEATURE_FLAG" false) false))

  (test explicit_true_string
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_FLAG" "true"))
    (eq? (parse_bool_env "FEATURE_FLAG" false) true))

  (test explicit_false_string
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_FLAG" "false"))
    (eq? (parse_bool_env "FEATURE_FLAG" true) false))

  (test one_as_true
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_FLAG" "1"))
    (eq? (parse_bool_env "FEATURE_FLAG" false) true))

  (test zero_as_false
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_FLAG" "0"))
    (eq? (parse_bool_env "FEATURE_FLAG" true) false))

  (test case_insensitive_true
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_FLAG" "TRUE"))
    (eq? (parse_bool_env "FEATURE_FLAG" false) true))

  (test invalid_string_returns_default
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_FLAG" "yEs"))
    (eq? (parse_bool_env "FEATURE_FLAG" false) false))
)

    result)

objective: The objective is to implement a function that takes an array of numbers and a single constant number, and returns a *new* array where each element is the corresponding element from the input array minus the constant. The original array should remain unchanged.
solution:
(module SubtractConstantFromArray
  (defun subtract_constant_from_array (arr constant)
    (def len (arr_length arr))
    (def new_arr (arr_new len))
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i len)
      (def original_val (arr_get arr i))
      (def subtracted_val (- original_val constant))
      (arr_set new_arr i subtracted_val)
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    new_arr)

  (test empty_array
    (eq? (subtract_constant_from_array (arr_new 0) 5) (arr_new 0)))

  (test single_element
    (eq? (subtract_constant_from_array (arr_new 1 10) 3) (arr_new 1 7)))

  (test positive_results
    (eq? (subtract_constant_from_array (arr_new 3 10 20 30) 5) (arr_new 3 5 15 25)))

  (test zero_results
    (eq? (subtract_constant_from_array (arr_new 3 5 5 5) 5) (arr_new 3 0 0 0)))

  (test negative_results
    (eq? (subtract_constant_from_array (arr_new 3 1 2 3) 5) (arr_new 3 -4 -3 -2)))

  (test mixed_results
    (eq? (subtract_constant_from_array (arr_new 4 10 5 0 -5) 5) (arr_new 4 5 0 -5 -10))))

objective: Implement a function that uses an external HTTP fetch capability to retrieve a web resource. The function should parse the raw string response from the capability to extract and return only the HTTP status code as a string.
solution:
(module HttpGetStatus
  (extern defun http_fetch (url) @capability "http.fetch")

  (defun get_http_status_code (url)
    ; Fetch the raw response string from the given URL
    (def response_string (http_fetch url))
    ; Split the response string by the newline character to get a list of lines.
    ; We assume the first line contains the status in the format "STATUS:XXX".
    (def lines (str_split response_string "\n"))
    ; Get the first line, which should be the status line (e.g., "STATUS:200")
    (def status_line (arr_get lines 0))
    ; Split the status line by the colon to separate the "STATUS" prefix from the code
    (def status_parts (str_split status_line ":"))
    ; The actual status code is the second element (index 1) in the split parts
    (arr_get status_parts 1))

  (test success_200
    (mocks
      ("http.fetch" "http://example.com/success" "STATUS:200\nBODY:Operation successful"))
    (eq? (get_http_status_code "http://example.com/success") "200"))

  (test not_found_404
    (mocks
      ("http.fetch" "http://example.com/missing" "STATUS:404\nBODY:Resource not found"))
    (eq? (get_http_status_code "http://example.com/missing") "404"))

  (test server_error_500
    (mocks
      ("http.fetch" "http://example.com/error" "STATUS:500\nBODY:Internal server error"))
    (eq? (get_http_status_code "http://example.com/error") "500"))

  (test redirect_301
    (mocks
      ("http.fetch" "http://old.domain.com" "STATUS:301\nBODY:Moved Permanently"))
    (eq? (get_http_status_code "http://old.domain.com") "301"))

  (test another_200_with_json_body
    (mocks
      ("http.fetch" "http://api.service.com/data" "STATUS:200\nBODY:{\"id\":1,\"name\":\"test\"}"))
    (eq? (get_http_status_code "http://api.service.com/data") "200")))
