objective: Write a function `median_of_three` that takes three numbers as input and returns their median. The median is the middle value when the numbers are sorted.
solution:
(module MedianOfThree
  (defun median_of_three (a b c)
    (if (< a b) ; Case 1: a < b
      (if (< b c) ; Subcase 1.1: a < b < c, so b is median
        b
        (if (< a c) ; Subcase 1.2: a < c < b, so c is median
          c
          a)) ; Subcase 1.3: c < a < b, so a is median
      ; Else (>= a b)
      (if (< a c) ; Subcase 2.1: b <= a < c, so a is median
        a
        (if (< b c) ; Subcase 2.2: b < c <= a, so c is median
          c
          b)))) ; Subcase 2.3: c <= b <= a, so b is median

  (test sorted (eq? (median_of_three 1 2 3) 2))
  (test reverse_sorted (eq? (median_of_three 3 2 1) 2))
  (test mixed_order_1 (eq? (median_of_three 1 3 2) 2))
  (test mixed_order_2 (eq? (median_of_three 2 1 3) 2))
  (test all_equal (eq? (median_of_three 5 5 5) 5))
  (test two_equal_lower (eq? (median_of_three 1 5 5) 5))
  (test two_equal_higher (eq? (median_of_three 5 5 1) 5)))

objective: Calculate the average of all numeric values in an array of numbers. If the array is empty, the average should be 0.
solution:
(module ArrayAverage
  (defun calculate_average (numbers)
    (def n (arr_length numbers))
    (if (= n 0) (return 0))
    (def sum 0)
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i n)
      (set sum (+ sum (arr_get numbers i)))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    (/ sum n))

  (defun make_array (a b c)
    (def xs (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set xs 0 a)
    (arr_set xs 1 b)
    (arr_set xs 2 c)
    xs)

  (defun make_array_single (a)
    (def xs (arr_new 1))
    (arr_set xs 0 a)
    xs)

  (test empty_array
    (eq? (calculate_average (arr_new 0)) 0))

  (test single_element
    (eq? (calculate_average (make_array_single 10)) 10))

  (test positive_numbers
    (eq? (calculate_average (make_array 1 2 3)) 2))

  (test mixed_numbers
    (near? (calculate_average (make_array 10 -5 15)) 6.666666666666667 0.000000000000001))

  (test zero_sum
    (eq? (calculate_average (make_array 5 -5 0)) 0))

  (test float_numbers
    (near? (calculate_average (make_array 1.5 2.5 3.0)) 2.3333333333333335 0.000000000000001)))

objective: Calculate a person's daily water intake goal in ounces based on their body weight in pounds. The base recommendation is half an ounce per pound of body weight, with an additional 12 ounces if they exercise for 30 minutes or more. The final goal should be rounded to the nearest integer.

    (def final_ounces base_ounces)

    (math_floor (+ final_ounces 0.5)))

objective: Create a function that reads an environment variable and interprets its string value as a boolean. The function should return `true` if the variable is "true" (case-insensitive), `false` if it is "false" (case-insensitive), and a provided default boolean otherwise, including when the variable is missing or empty.
solution:
(module EnvVarToBool
  (extern defun env_read (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun parse_bool_env (key default_value)
    (def val (env_read key))
    (if (str_eq val "") (return default_value))
    (def lower_val (str_lower val))
    (if (str_eq lower_val "true") (return true))
    (if (str_eq lower_val "false") (return false))
    default_value)

  (test missing_var_returns_default
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_ENABLED" ""))
    (eq? (parse_bool_env "FEATURE_ENABLED" false) false))

  (test true_string_returns_true
    (mocks (env.get "DEBUG_MODE" "true"))
    (eq? (parse_bool_env "DEBUG_MODE" false) true))

  (test false_string_returns_false
    (mocks (env.get "DEV_MODE" "false"))
    (eq? (parse_bool_env "DEV_MODE" true) false))

  (test mixed_case_true_returns_true
    (mocks (env.get "VERBOSE_LOGS" "TRuE"))
    (eq? (parse_bool_env "VERBOSE_LOGS" false) true))

  (test mixed_case_false_returns_false
    (mocks (env.get "BETA_FLAG" "fAlSe"))
    (eq? (parse_bool_env "BETA_FLAG" true) false))

  (test invalid_string_returns_default
    (mocks (env.get "IS_ADMIN" "yes"))
    (eq? (parse_bool_env "IS_ADMIN" false) false))

  (test invalid_string_with_true_default_returns_default
    (mocks (env.get "IS_ACTIVE" "1"))
    (eq? (parse_bool_env "IS_ACTIVE" true) true)))

objective: Implement functions to calculate the total fuel cost for a trip given the distance, vehicle's fuel efficiency, and the price of fuel per unit. The final cost should be rounded to two decimal places.
solution:
(module TripFuelEstimator
  ; Calculates the total number of fuel units (e.g., gallons) required for a trip.
  (defun calculate_fuel_needed (distance efficiency)
    (/ distance efficiency))

  ; Calculates the total cost based on fuel units needed and price per unit.
  (defun calculate_total_cost (fuel_units price_per_unit)
    (* fuel_units price_per_unit))

  ; Rounds a given amount to two decimal places, suitable for currency.
  (defun round_to_two_decimals (amount)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* amount 100) 0.5)) 100))

  ; Estimates the total fuel cost for a trip, combining distance, efficiency, and fuel price.
  (defun estimate_trip_fuel_cost (distance efficiency price_per_unit)
    (def needed_fuel (calculate_fuel_needed distance efficiency))
    (def raw_cost (calculate_total_cost needed_fuel price_per_unit))
    (round_to_two_decimals raw_cost))

  (test standard_trip_cost
    (near? (estimate_trip_fuel_cost 300 30 3.50) 35.00 0.01)) ; 300 miles / 30 MPG = 10 gallons * $3.50/gallon = $35.00

  (test short_trip_high_efficiency
    (near? (estimate_trip_fuel_cost 100 50 4.00) 8.00 0.01)) ; 100 miles / 50 MPG = 2 gallons * $4.00/gallon = $8.00

  (test long_trip_low_efficiency
    (near? (estimate_trip_fuel_cost 500 20 3.25) 81.25 0.01)) ; 500 miles / 20 MPG = 25 gallons * $3.25/gallon = $81.25

  (test zero_distance_trip
    (near? (estimate_trip_fuel_cost 0 40 3.75) 0.00 0.01))

  (test fractional_values_rounding
    (near? (estimate_trip_fuel_cost 175.5 28.3 3.99) 24.73 0.01)) ; (175.5 / 28.3) * 3.99 = 6.2014... * 3.99 = 24.733... -> 24.73
)

objective: Calculate the estimated fuel cost for a trip given the total distance, the vehicle's fuel efficiency, and the current price per unit of fuel. The final cost should be rounded to two decimal places, representing currency.
solution:
(module TripFuelCost
  (defun calculate_gallons_needed (distance efficiency)
    ;; Calculates the amount of fuel (in gallons) required for a trip.
    ;; Assumes positive distance and efficiency.
    (/ distance efficiency))

  (defun calculate_total_fuel_cost (gallons_needed price_per_gallon)
    ;; Calculates the total monetary cost of the fuel.
    ;; Assumes non-negative gallons_needed and price_per_gallon.
    (* gallons_needed price_per_gallon))

  (defun round_to_cents (amount)
    ;; Rounds a numerical amount to two decimal places, suitable for currency.
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* amount 100) 0.5)) 100))

  (defun estimate_trip_fuel_cost (distance efficiency price_per_gallon)
    ;; Estimates the total fuel cost for a trip given distance, fuel efficiency (MPG),
    ;; and the price per gallon. Returns 0 if any input is non-positive.
    (if (or (<= distance 0) (<= efficiency 0) (<= price_per_gallon 0))
      (return 0))

    (def gallons (calculate_gallons_needed distance efficiency))
    (def cost (calculate_total_fuel_cost gallons price_per_gallon))
    (round_to_cents cost))

  (test standard_trip
    (near? (estimate_trip_fuel_cost 300 30 3.50) 35.00 0.01)) ; 300 miles / 30 MPG = 10 gallons * $3.50 = $35.00

  (test short_trip
    (near? (estimate_trip_fuel_cost 50 25 4.00) 8.00 0.01))   ; 50 miles / 25 MPG = 2 gallons * $4.00 = $8.00

  (test long_trip_with_rounding
    (near? (estimate_trip_fuel_cost 450 28 3.75) 60.27 0.01)) ; 450/28 = 16.0714... gallons * 3.75 = 60.2678... -> 60.27

  (test zero_distance
    (eq? (estimate_trip_fuel_cost 0 25 4.00) 0))

  (test zero_efficiency
    (eq? (estimate_trip_fuel_cost 100 0 4.00) 0))

  (test zero_price
    (eq? (estimate_trip_fuel_cost 200 20 0) 0))

  (test negative_inputs
    (eq? (estimate_trip_fuel_cost -100 25 4.00) 0))
    (eq? (estimate_trip_fuel_cost 100 -25 4.00) 0))
    (eq? (estimate_trip_fuel_cost 100 25 -4.00) 0)))

objective: Implement a function that takes an array of numbers and a constant, then returns a *new* array where each element of the original array has the constant subtracted from it.
solution:
(module ArraySubtractConstant
  ; Helper function to create an array with 3 elements for testing convenience.
  (defun make_arr3 (a b c)
    (def xs (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set xs 0 a)
    (arr_set xs 1 b)
    (arr_set xs 2 c)
    xs)

  ; Subtracts a constant value from each element of an input array.
  ; Returns a new array with the results.
  (defun subtract_constant_from_array (data_arr constant)
    (def len (arr_length data_arr))
    (def result_arr (arr_new len))
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i len)
      (def original_val (arr_get data_arr i))
      (def new_val (- original_val constant))
      (arr_set result_arr i new_val)
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    result_arr)

  (test empty_array
    (def input_arr (arr_new 0))
    (def expected_arr (arr_new 0))
    (eq? (subtract_constant_from_array input_arr 10) expected_arr))

  (test positive_numbers_positive_constant
    (def input_arr (make_arr3 10 20 30))
    (def expected_arr (make_arr3 5 15 25))
    (eq? (subtract_constant_from_array input_arr 5) expected_arr))

  (test mixed_numbers_positive_constant
    (def input_arr (make_arr3 -5 0 15))
    (def expected_arr (make_arr3 -8 -3 12))
    (eq? (subtract_constant_from_array input_arr 3) expected_arr))

  (test positive_numbers_negative_constant
    (def input_arr (make_arr3 1 2 3))
    (def expected_arr (make_arr3 6 7 8)) ; 1 - (-5) = 1 + 5 = 6
    (eq? (subtract_constant_from_array input_arr -5) expected_arr))

  (test single_element_array
    (def input_arr (arr_new 1))
    (arr_set input_arr 0 100)
    (def expected_arr (arr_new 1))
    (arr_set expected_arr 0 90)
    (eq? (subtract_constant_from_array input_arr 10) expected_arr))

  (test zero_constant
    (def input_arr (make_arr3 10 20 30))
    (def expected_arr (make_arr3 10 20 30))
    (eq? (subtract_constant_from_array input_arr 0) expected_arr))
)

objective: Calculate the total fuel cost for a trip. This involves determining the amount of fuel needed based on distance and vehicle efficiency, then multiplying it by the current fuel price, which is retrieved from an external environment service.
solution:
(module TripFuelCost
  (extern defun get_env_var (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun calculate_fuel_needed (distance efficiency)
    ;; distance: total distance of the trip (e.g., in miles).
    ;; efficiency: vehicle's fuel efficiency (e.g., miles per gallon).
    ;; Returns the total fuel units required.
    (/ distance efficiency))

  (defun calculate_trip_cost (distance efficiency fuel_price_key)
    ;; Calculates the total monetary cost of fuel for a trip.
    ;; distance: total distance of the trip.
    ;; efficiency: vehicle's fuel efficiency.
    ;; fuel_price_key: string key to retrieve the current fuel price from the environment.
    (def fuel_price_str (get_env_var fuel_price_key))
    (def fuel_price (str_to_num fuel_price_str))
    (def fuel_needed (calculate_fuel_needed distance efficiency))
    (* fuel_needed fuel_price))

  (test basic_trip_cost
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "fuel.price.regular" "3.00"))
    (eq? (calculate_trip_cost 100 25 "fuel.price.regular") 12.00)) ; 100 miles / 25 mpg = 4 gallons * $3.00/gal = $12.00

  (test longer_trip_cost
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "fuel.price.premium" "4.25"))
    (eq? (calculate_trip_cost 300 30 "fuel.price.premium") 42.50)) ; 300 miles / 30 mpg = 10 gallons * $4.25/gal = $42.50

  (test short_efficient_trip
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "fuel.price.diesel" "3.80"))
    (eq? (calculate_trip_cost 50 50 "fuel.price.diesel") 3.80)) ; 50 miles / 50 mpg = 1 gallon * $3.80/gal = $3.80

  (test zero_distance_trip
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "fuel.price.regular" "3.00"))
    (eq? (calculate_trip_cost 0 20 "fuel.price.regular") 0.00)) ; 0 miles / 20 mpg = 0 gallons * $3.00/gal = $0.00

  (test decimal_efficiency_and_price
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "fuel.price.e85" "2.85"))
    (near? (calculate_trip_cost 150 28.5 "fuel.price.e85") 15.00 0.001)) ; 150 miles / 28.5 mpg = 5.263157... gallons * $2.85/gal = 15.00

  (test very_long_trip
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "fuel.price.unleaded" "3.15"))
    (near? (calculate_trip_cost 1200 24 "fuel.price.unleaded") 157.50 0.001)) ; 1200 miles / 24 mpg = 50 gallons * $3.15/gal = $157.50
)

objective: Develop a program that classifies a person's current heart rate into a training zone. The classification is based on their age (to calculate maximum heart rate) and their current heart rate, categorizing it into one of five standard zones or "Below Zone 1".
solution:
(module HeartRateZoneClassifier
  ; Calculates the estimated Maximum Heart Rate (MHR) based on age.
  ; Uses the common formula: 220 - Age.
  (defun calculate_mhr (age)
    (- 220 age))

  ; Determines the heart rate training zone based on the current heart rate
  ; as a percentage of Maximum Heart Rate (MHR).
  ; hr_pct should be current_hr / mhr.
  (defun get_zone_classification (hr_pct)
    (if (>= hr_pct 0.9) (return "Zone 5: Maximum")) ; 90-100%+ MHR
    (if (>= hr_pct 0.8) (return "Zone 4: Hard"))    ; 80-89% MHR
    (if (>= hr_pct 0.7) (return "Zone 3: Moderate")) ; 70-79% MHR
    (if (>= hr_pct 0.6) (return "Zone 2: Light"))   ; 60-69% MHR
    (if (>= hr_pct 0.5) (return "Zone 1: Very Light")) ; 50-59% MHR
    "Below Zone 1: Resting") ; Less than 50% MHR

  ; Main function to classify a person's current heart rate into a training zone
  ; given their age and current heart rate.
  (defun classify_heart_rate (age current_hr)
    (def mhr (calculate_mhr age))
    ; Ensure MHR is a positive value to prevent division by zero or negative MHR.
    (if (<= mhr 0) (return "Error: Invalid Age for MHR Calculation"))
    (def hr_pct (/ current_hr mhr))
    (get_zone_classification hr_pct))

  ; --- Tests ---

  ; Test 1: A young adult in the moderate zone.
  ; Age 30 -> MHR = 190. Current HR 135 -> 135/190 = 0.7105... (Zone 3)
  (test young_moderate_zone
    (eq? (classify_heart_rate 30 135) "Zone 3: Moderate"))

  ; Test 2: An older individual in the light zone.
  ; Age 60 -> MHR = 160. Current HR 100 -> 100/160 = 0.625 (Zone 2)
  (test older_light_zone
    (eq? (classify_heart_rate 60 100) "Zone 2: Light"))

  ; Test 3: High intensity, maximum zone.
  ; Age 25 -> MHR = 195. Current HR 180 -> 180/195 = 0.923... (Zone 5)
  (test young_maximum_zone
    (eq? (classify_heart_rate 25 180) "Zone 5: Maximum"))

  ; Test 4: Heart rate below the lowest training zone.
  ; Age 40 -> MHR = 180. Current HR 80 -> 80/180 = 0.444... (Below Zone 1)
  (test below_zone_one
    (eq? (classify_heart_rate 40 80) "Below Zone 1: Resting"))

  ; Test 5: Edge case - exactly 60% MHR, should be Zone 2.
  ; Age 30 -> MHR = 190. Current HR 114 -> 114/190 = 0.6 (Zone 2)
  (test edge_60_percent_zone
    (eq? (classify_heart_rate 30 114) "Zone 2: Light"))

  ; Test 6: Edge case - exactly 50% MHR, should be Zone 1.
  ; Age 30 -> MHR = 190. Current HR 95 -> 95/190 = 0.5 (Zone 1)
  (test edge_50_percent_zone
    (eq? (classify_heart_rate 30 95) "Zone 1: Very Light"))

  ; Test 7: Invalid age leading to non-positive MHR (e.g., age 220 or more).
  (test invalid_age_input
    (eq? (classify_heart_rate 220 100) "Error: Invalid Age for MHR Calculation"))
)

(module AppNameBuilder

topic: paycheck net from hours and rate
objective: Create a program that calculates an employee's net paycheck, accounting for regular and overtime hours, a percentage-based tax deduction, and a fixed deduction for benefits.
solution:
(module PaycheckCalculator
  (defun calculate_gross_pay (hours rate overtime_threshold overtime_multiplier)
    (if (<= hours overtime_threshold)
      (* hours rate)
      (+ (* overtime_threshold rate)
         (* (- hours overtime_threshold) rate overtime_multiplier))))

  (defun calculate_total_deductions (gross_pay tax_rate fixed_deduction)
    (+ (* gross_pay tax_rate) fixed_deduction))

  (defun calculate_net_pay (hours rate overtime_threshold overtime_multiplier tax_rate fixed_deduction)
    (def gross (calculate_gross_pay hours rate overtime_threshold overtime_multiplier))
    (def deductions (calculate_total_deductions gross tax_rate fixed_deduction))
    (- gross deductions))

  (test regular_hours_no_deductions
    (near? (calculate_net_pay 40 15 40 1.5 0 0) 600 0.01))

  (test regular_hours_with_deductions
    (near? (calculate_net_pay 40 15 40 1.5 0.10 25) 515 0.01)) ; Gross: 600, Tax: 60, Fixed: 25, Net: 600 - 85 = 515

  (test overtime_hours_no_deductions
    (near? (calculate_net_pay 45 20 40 1.5 0 0) 950 0.01)) ; Gross: (40*20) + (5*20*1.5) = 800 + 150 = 950

  (test overtime_hours_with_deductions
    (near? (calculate_net_pay 45 20 40 1.5 0.15 50) 757.5 0.01)) ; Gross: 950, Tax: 950*0.15=142.5, Fixed: 50, Net: 950 - (142.5+50) = 950 - 192.5 = 757.5

  (test zero_hours
    (near? (calculate_net_pay 0 20 40 1.5 0.10 0) 0 0.01))

  (test zero_rate
    (near? (calculate_net_pay 40 0 40 1.5 0.10 0) 0 0.01))
)

topic: shipping cost by weight and zone
objective: Implement a shipping cost calculator that determines the total cost based on package weight and the shipping zone. The cost structure includes a base fee per zone and an additional per-unit weight charge, with the final cost rounded to two decimal places.
solution:
(module ShippingCalculator
  (defun get_zone_base_cost (zone)
    (if (str_eq zone "ZoneA") 5.0
      (if (str_eq zone "ZoneB") 10.0
        (if (str_eq zone "ZoneC") 15.0
          0.0))))

  (defun get_zone_per_unit_cost (zone)
    (if (str_eq zone "ZoneA") 0.5
      (if (str_eq zone "ZoneB") 0.75

objective: Implement a function to calculate the Least Common Multiple (LCM) of two non-negative integers. You will also need a helper function to compute the Greatest Common Divisor (GCD) using the Euclidean algorithm.
solution:
(module LeastCommonMultiple
  (defun gcd (a b)
    (while (> b 0)
      (def temp b)
      (set b (math_mod a b))
      (set a temp))
    a)

  (defun lcm (a b)
    (if (= a 0) (return 0))
    (if (= b 0) (return 0))
    (def common_divisor (gcd a b))
    (* (/ a common_divisor) b))

  (test lcm_4_6 (eq? (lcm 4 6) 12))
  (test lcm_7_5 (eq? (lcm 7 5) 35))
  (test lcm_12_18 (eq? (lcm 12 18) 36))
  (test lcm_zero_first (eq? (lcm 0 5) 0))
  (test lcm_zero_second (eq? (lcm 5 0) 0))
  (test lcm_one_ten (eq? (lcm 1 10) 10))
  (test lcm_same_numbers (eq? (lcm 6 6) 6)))

objective: Implement a function that counts the number of lines in a text file. The function should take a file path, read its content using a capability, and return the line count, properly handling empty files and trailing newlines.
solution:
(module FileLines
  (extern defun file_read (path) @capability "file.read")

  (defun count_lines_in_content (content)
    (if (str_eq content "") (return 0))
    (def lines (str_split content "\n"))
    (def num_lines (arr_length lines))
    (if (and (> num_lines 0) (str_eq (arr_get lines (- num_lines 1)) ""))
        (return (- num_lines 1)))
    num_lines)

  (defun count_file_lines (path)
    (def file_content (file_read path))
    (count_lines_in_content file_content))

  (test empty_file
    (mocks (file.read "empty.txt" ""))
    (eq? (count_file_lines "empty.txt") 0))

  (test single_line_no_newline

    (eq? (count_file_lines "single.txt") 1))

  (test single_line_with_newline

    (eq? (count_file_lines "single_nl.txt") 1))

  (test multiple_lines_no_newline

    (eq? (count_file_lines "multi.txt") 3))

  (test multiple_lines_with_newline
    (mocks (file.read "multi_nl.txt" "Line A\nLine B\nLine C\n"))
    (eq? (count_file_lines "multi_nl.txt") 3))

  (test only_newline_char

    (eq? (count_file_lines "only_nl.txt") 1))

  (test two_empty_lines

topic: shipping cost by weight and zone
objective: Implement a function that calculates the shipping cost for a package based on its weight and destination zone. Different zones have different base rates and per-kilogram costs, with an additional flat rate for unknown zones.
solution:
(module ShippingCostCalculator
  ; Helper function to round a number to two decimal places.
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))

  ; Calculates the total shipping cost based on weight and zone.
  ; Zone A: $5.00 base + $1.00/kg
  ; Zone B: $7.50 base + $1.25/kg
  ; Zone C: $10.00 base + $1.50/kg
  ; Any other zone: $15.00 flat rate
  (defun calculate_shipping_cost (weight zone)
    (def cost 0.0)
    (if (str_eq zone "A")
      (set cost (round2 (+ 5.0 (* weight 1.0))))
      (if (str_eq zone "B")
        (set cost (round2 (+ 7.5 (* weight 1.25))))
        (if (str_eq zone "C")
          (set cost (round2 (+ 10.0 (* weight 1.5))))
          ; Default for unknown zones
          (set cost 15.0))))
    cost)

  (test zone_a_2kg
    (near? (calculate_shipping_cost 2.0 "A") 7.00 0.01))

  (test zone_b_5kg
    (near? (calculate_shipping_cost 5.0 "B") 13.75 0.01))

  (test zone_c_1_5kg
    (near? (calculate_shipping_cost 1.5 "C") 12.25 0.01))

  (test unknown_zone_10kg
    (near? (calculate_shipping_cost 10.0 "D") 15.00 0.01))

  (test zone_a_0_5kg
    (near? (calculate_shipping_cost 0.5 "A") 5.50 0.01))

  (test zone_b_0_1kg
    (near? (calculate_shipping_cost 0.1 "B") 7.63 0.01)) ; 7.5 + (0.1 * 1.25) = 7.5 + 0.125 = 7.625 -> 7.63

  (test zone_c_3_7kg
    (near? (calculate_shipping_cost 3.7 "C") 15.55 0.01)) ; 10.0 + (3.7 * 1.5) = 10.0 + 5.55 = 15.55
)

objective: Implement a function that reads an environment variable and interprets its string value as a boolean. The function should return `true` for case-insensitive "true", "1", or "yes", and `false` for "false", "0", or "no". If the environment variable is not set, or its value is not one of the recognized boolean strings, it should return a specified default boolean value.
solution:
(module EnvBoolVar
  (extern defun env_read (key) @capability "env.get")

  ;; Helper to convert a string to a boolean, with case-insensitivity and a default.
  (defun _str_to_bool (s default_val)
    (def lower_s (str_lower s))
    (if (str_eq lower_s "true") (return true))
    (if (str_eq lower_s "1") (return true))
    (if (str_eq lower_s "yes") (return true))
    (if (str_eq lower_s "false") (return false))
    (if (str_eq lower_s "0") (return false))
    (if (str_eq lower_s "no") (return false))
    ;; If not recognized, return the default value
    default_val)

  ;; Reads an environment variable and interprets it as a boolean.
  ;; If the variable is not set or unrecognized, `default_val` is returned.
  (defun get_env_bool (key default_val)
    (def raw_val (env_read key))
    (_str_to_bool raw_val default_val))

  (test true_value
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_X" "true"))
    (eq? (get_env_bool "FEATURE_X" false) true))

  (test uppercase_true_value

    (eq? (get_env_bool "FEATURE_Y" false) true))

  (test one_value
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_Z" "1"))
    (eq? (get_env_bool "FEATURE_Z" false) true))

  (test yes_value
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_A" "yes"))
    (eq? (get_env_bool "FEATURE_A" false) true))

  (test false_value
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_B" "false"))
    (eq? (get_env_bool "FEATURE_B" true) false))

  (test zero_value
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_C" "0"))
    (eq? (get_env_bool "FEATURE_C" true) false))

  (test no_value
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_D" "no"))
    (eq? (get_env_bool "FEATURE_D" true) false))

  (test missing_var_default_false
    (mocks (env.get "MISSING_VAR_1" ""))
    (eq? (get_env_bool "MISSING_VAR_1" false) false))

  (test missing_var_default_true
    (mocks (env.get "MISSING_VAR_2" ""))
    (eq? (get_env_bool "MISSING_VAR_2" true) true))

  (test unrecognized_value_default_false
    (mocks (env.get "UNRECOGNIZED_1" "maybe"))
    (eq? (get_env_bool "UNRECOGNIZED_1" false) false))

  (test unrecognized_value_default_true
    (mocks (env.get "UNRECOGNIZED_2" "some_text"))
    (eq? (get_env_bool "UNRECOGNIZED_2" true) true))
)

topic: read file and count char occurrences
objective: Write an AGC module that reads the content of a specified file using a capability and counts the occurrences of a given character within that file. If the file is empty or not found, it should return 0.
solution:
(module CharCounter
  (extern defun file_read (path) @capability "file.read")

  ;; Helper function to count character occurrences in a given string
  (defun count_char_occurrences (content target_char)
    (def count 0)
    (def i 0)
    (def len (str_length content))
    (while (< i len)
      (def current_char (str_substring content i 1))
      (if (str_eq current_char target_char)
        (set count (+ count 1)))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    count)

  ;; Main function to read a file and count a character
  (defun analyze_file_for_char (filename target_char)
    (def file_content (file_read filename))
    ;; If file_read returns an empty string, assume file is empty or not found.
    ;; In this case, there are 0 occurrences.
    (if (str_eq file_content "")
      (return 0))
    (count_char_occurrences file_content target_char))

  (test char_present_multiple_times
    (mocks (file.read "document.txt" "hello world, hello AGC!"))
    (eq? (analyze_file_for_char "document.txt" "l") 5))

  (test char_present_once
    (mocks (file.read "single.txt" "just one 'x' here."))
    (eq? (analyze_file_for_char "single.txt" "x") 1))

  (test char_not_present
    (mocks (file.read "alpha.txt" "abcde"))
    (eq? (analyze_file_for_char "alpha.txt" "z") 0))

  (test file_empty
    (mocks (file.read "empty_file.txt" ""))
    (eq? (analyze_file_for_char "empty_file.txt" "a") 0))

  (test case_sensitivity
    (mocks (file.read "case.txt" "Hello World"))
    (eq? (analyze_file_for_char "case.txt" "h") 0))

  (test mixed_content
    (mocks (file.read "data.txt" "123a123A123"))
    (eq? (analyze_file_for_char "data.txt" "a") 1))
)

objective: Write a function `count_consonants` that takes a string and returns the number of consonants within it. A consonant is defined as any letter from the English alphabet that is not a vowel (a, e, i, o, u), ignoring case and any non-alphabetic characters.
solution:
(module CountConsonants
  (defun is_vowel_lower (c)
    (if (str_eq c "a") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq c "e") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq c "i") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq c "o") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq c "u") (return 1))
    0)

  (defun is_letter_lower (c)
    (if (str_eq c "a") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq c "b") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq c "c") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq c "d") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq c "e") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq c "f") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq c "g") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq c "h") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq c "i") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq c "j") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq c "k") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq c "l") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq c "m") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq c "n") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq c "o") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq c "p") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq c "q") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq c "r") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq c "s") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq c "t") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq c "u") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq c "v") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq c "w") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq c "x") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq c "y") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq c "z") (return 1))
    0)

  (defun count_consonants (s)
    (def count 0)
    (def i 0)
    (def len (str_length s))
    (while (< i len)
      (def char_str (str_substring s i 1))
      (def lower_char_str (str_lower char_str))
      (if (= (is_letter_lower lower_char_str) 1)
        (if (= (is_vowel_lower lower_char_str) 0) ; if it's a letter AND not a vowel
          (set count (+ count 1))))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    count)

  (test test_empty_string
    (eq? (count_consonants "") 0))

  (test test_simple_word
    (eq? (count_consonants "hello") 3)) ; h, l, l

  (test test_mixed_case
    (eq? (count_consonants "HeLlO WoRlD") 7)) ; H,l,l,W,r,l,d

  (test test_with_punctuation_and_numbers
    (eq? (count_consonants "123 Counting Consonants!") 12)) ; C,n,t,n,g,C,n,s,n,n,t,s

  (test test_all_vowels
    (eq? (count_consonants "aeiouAEIOU") 0))

  (test test_all_consonants
    (eq? (count_consonants "bcdfghjklmnpqrstvwxyzBCDFGHJKLMNPQRSTVWXYZ") 42))

  (test test_only_non_letters
    (eq? (count_consonants "!@#$%^&*()_+-=") 0)))

objective: Calculate a user's daily water intake goal in liters based on their body weight in pounds. The recommendation is to drink half an ounce of water per pound of body weight, with the final result rounded to two decimal places.
solution:
(module WaterIntakeGoal
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))

  (defun calculate_ounces_from_weight (weight_lbs)
    (* weight_lbs 0.5))

(module BulkPricing

    (- amount (* amount (/ percentage 100.0))))

  (defun calculate_bulk_price (price_per_item quantity)
    (def base_price (* price_per_item quantity))
    (if (>= quantity 20)
      (apply_discount base_price 10) ; 10% discount for 20 or more
      (if (>= quantity 10)

        base_price))) ; No discount for less than 10

objective: Implement a function that manually removes leading and trailing space characters from a given string. Internal spaces should be preserved.
solution:
(module StringTrim
  ; Helper function to check if a single-character string is a whitespace character.
  ; For this problem, only the space character ' ' is considered whitespace.
  (defun is_whitespace (char_str)
    (str_eq char_str " "))

  ; Removes leading and trailing space characters from a string.
  (defun trim_whitespace (s)
    (def len (str_length s))
    (if (= len 0) (return ""))

    ; Find the start_idx: first non-whitespace character from the beginning.
    (def start_idx 0)
    (def found_start 0)
    (while (and (< start_idx len) (= found_start 0))
      (if (is_whitespace (str_substring s start_idx 1))
          (set start_idx (+ start_idx 1))
          (set found_start 1)))

    ; If start_idx reached len, the entire string was whitespace or empty.
    (if (= start_idx len) (return ""))

    ; Find the end_idx: last non-whitespace character from the end.
    (def end_idx (- len 1))
    (def found_end 0)
    (while (and (>= end_idx 0) (= found_end 0))
      (if (is_whitespace (str_substring s end_idx 1))
          (set end_idx (- end_idx 1))
          (set found_end 1)))

    ; Extract the substring from start_idx to end_idx (inclusive).
    ; Length of substring is (end_idx - start_idx + 1).
    (str_substring s start_idx (+ (- end_idx start_idx) 1)))

  (test empty_string          (eq? (trim_whitespace "") ""))
  (test only_whitespace       (eq? (trim_whitespace "   ") ""))
  (test no_whitespace         (eq? (trim_whitespace "hello") "hello"))
  (test leading_whitespace    (eq? (trim_whitespace "  hello") "hello"))
  (test trailing_whitespace   (eq? (trim_whitespace "hello  ") "hello"))
  (test both_whitespace       (eq? (trim_whitespace "  hello world  ") "hello world"))
  (test mixed_non_space_whitespace (eq? (trim_whitespace " \t\n hello \r\f ") "\t\n hello \r\f")))

objective: Write a function `count_lines` that takes a file path as input. It should read the content of the file using the `file.read` capability and return the total number of lines in the file. An empty file should return 0 lines; otherwise, each newline character (`\n`) signifies the end of a line, potentially creating an empty line if consecutive or at the end of the file.
solution:
(module CountFileLines
  (extern defun file_read (path) @capability "file.read")

  (defun count_lines (filepath)
    (def content (file_read filepath))
    (if (str_eq content "") (return 0))
    (def lines (str_split content "\n"))
    (arr_length lines))

  (test empty_file
    (mocks (file.read "empty.txt" ""))
    (eq? (count_lines "empty.txt") 0))

  (test single_line_no_newline
    (mocks (file.read "single.txt" "Hello World"))
    (eq? (count_lines "single.txt") 1))

  (test multiple_lines_no_trailing
    (mocks (file.read "multi.txt" "Line1\nLine2\nLine3"))
    (eq? (count_lines "multi.txt") 3))

  (test multiple_lines_with_trailing
    (mocks (file.read "multi_trailing.txt" "Line1\nLine2\nLine3\n"))
    (eq? (count_lines "multi_trailing.txt") 4))

  (test only_newline_char
    (mocks (file.read "newline.txt" "\n"))
    (eq? (count_lines "newline.txt") 2))

  (test blank_lines
    (mocks (file.read "blank.txt" " \n\t\n"))
    (eq? (count_lines "blank.txt") 3))

  (test complex_content_crlf
    (mocks (file.read "complex.txt" "1st line\n2nd line\r\n3rd line\n\n5th line"))
    (eq? (count_lines "complex.txt") 5))
)

(module GymMembershipCost

    (def total_raw_monthly_cost (* monthly_fee num_months))
    (if (>= num_months discount_threshold)
        (return (- total_raw_monthly_cost (* total_raw_monthly_cost discount_rate))))
    total_raw_monthly_cost)

    (def monthly_charges (calculate_discounted_monthly_charges monthly_fee num_months discount_threshold discount_rate))

objective: Implement a function `get_log_level` that reads the `LOG_LEVEL` environment variable. If the environment variable is not set (returns an empty string) or is an invalid non-numeric string, the function should return a default log level of 1. Otherwise, it should parse the environment variable's string value into an integer and return it.
solution:
(module LogLevelReader
  (extern defun env_read (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun get_log_level ()
    (def level_str (env_read "LOG_LEVEL"))
    (def default_level 1)
    (if (str_eq level_str "")
      (return default_level))
    ;; For safety, we assume mocked values are either empty string or valid numeric strings.
    ;; In a real scenario, more robust validation (e.g., regex for is-numeric) would be needed
    ;; but is not available in base AGC or required for this problem's scope given mocks.
    (str_to_num level_str))

    (eq? (get_log_level) 1))

    (eq? (get_log_level) 3))

  (test returns_valid_level_0

    (eq? (get_log_level) 0))

  (test returns_valid_level_7

    (eq? (get_log_level) 7)))

objective: Write a function that takes an array of numbers and a scalar value, and returns a new array where each element from the original array has been multiplied by the scalar. The original array should remain unchanged.
solution:
(module ScalarMultiplyArray
  (defun scalar_multiply (arr scalar)
    (def n (arr_length arr))
    (def result_arr (arr_new n))
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i n)
      (def val (arr_get arr i))
      (arr_set result_arr i (* val scalar))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    result_arr)

  (test empty_array
    (eq? (arr_length (scalar_multiply (arr_new 0) 5)) 0))

  (test positive_elements
    (def xs (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set xs 0 1)
    (arr_set xs 1 2)
    (arr_set xs 2 3)
    (def result (scalar_multiply xs 2))
    (eq? (arr_get result 0) 2)
    (eq? (arr_get result 1) 4)
    (eq? (arr_get result 2) 6))

  (test negative_elements
    (def xs (arr_new 2))
    (arr_set xs 0 -1)
    (arr_set xs 1 -5)
    (def result (scalar_multiply xs 3))
    (eq? (arr_get result 0) -3)
    (eq? (arr_get result 1) -15))

  (test zero_scalar
    (def xs (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set xs 0 10)
    (arr_set xs 1 20)
    (arr_set xs 2 30)
    (def result (scalar_multiply xs 0))
    (eq? (arr_get result 0) 0)
    (eq? (arr_get result 1) 0)
    (eq? (arr_get result 2) 0))

  (test one_scalar
    (def xs (arr_new 2))
    (arr_set xs 0 7)
    (arr_set xs 1 11)
    (def result (scalar_multiply xs 1))
    (eq? (arr_get result 0) 7)
    (eq? (arr_get result 1) 11))

  (test mixed_elements_negative_scalar
    (def xs (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set xs 0 5)
    (arr_set xs 1 -10)
    (arr_set xs 2 0)
    (def result (scalar_multiply xs -2))
    (eq? (arr_get result 0) -10)
    (eq? (arr_get result 1) 20)
    (eq? (arr_get result 2) 0))
)

objective: Develop a module that calculates currency exchange, applying a percentage fee to the initial amount before conversion. The final converted amount should be rounded to two decimal places.
solution:
(module CurrencyExchanger
  ;; Rounds a number to two decimal places.
  (defun round_2_decimal (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100.0) 0.5)) 100.0))

  ;; Deducts a percentage fee from an amount.
  (defun deduct_fee (amount fee_percent)
    (* amount (- 1.0 (/ fee_percent 100.0))))

  ;; Converts an amount from source to target currency using a given rate.
  (defun convert (amount exchange_rate)
    (* amount exchange_rate))

  ;; Calculates the final amount after applying a fee and converting currency.
  (defun exchange_with_fee (initial_amount fee_percent exchange_rate)
    (def amount_after_fee (deduct_fee initial_amount fee_percent))
    (def converted_amount (convert amount_after_fee exchange_rate))
    (round_2_decimal converted_amount))

  (test usd_to_eur_no_fee
    (near? (exchange_with_fee 100.0 0.0 0.90) 90.00 0.01)) ; 100 USD -> 90 EUR

  (test usd_to_eur_with_fee
    (near? (exchange_with_fee 100.0 2.0 0.90) 88.20 0.01)) ; 100 USD - 2% fee = 98 USD -> 88.2 EUR

  (test gbp_to_usd_with_fee
    (near? (exchange_with_fee 50.0 1.5 1.25) 61.56 0.01)) ; 50 GBP - 1.5% fee = 49.25 GBP -> 49.25 * 1.25 = 61.5625 USD -> 61.56 USD

  (test jpy_to_aud_large_amount
    (near? (exchange_with_fee 100000.0 0.5 0.012) 1194.00 0.01)) ; 100000 JPY - 0.5% fee = 99500 JPY -> 99500 * 0.012 = 1194 AUD

  (test zero_amount
    (near? (exchange_with_fee 0.0 5.0 1.1) 0.00 0.01))

  (test high_fee
    (near? (exchange_with_fee 200.0 50.0 0.75) 75.00 0.01)) ; 200 - 50% = 100 -> 100 * 0.75 = 75
)

objective: Calculate an electricity bill based on a progressive tiered pricing structure. The first 100 kWh are charged at one rate, the next 200 kWh (up to 300 kWh total) at a second rate, and any consumption above 300 kWh at a third rate.
solution:
(module ElectricityBillCalculator
  ; Helper function to round a number to two decimal places
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))

  (defun calculate_electricity_bill (kwh_consumed)
    (def total_cost 0.0)
    (def kwh_remaining kwh_consumed)

    ; Tier 1: First 100 kWh at $0.10/kWh
    (def tier1_limit 100)
    (def tier1_rate 0.10)
    (def kwh_in_tier1 (math_min kwh_remaining tier1_limit))
    (set total_cost (+ total_cost (* kwh_in_tier1 tier1_rate)))
    (set kwh_remaining (- kwh_remaining kwh_in_tier1))

    ; Tier 2: Next 200 kWh (from 101 up to 300 kWh total) at $0.15/kWh
    (if (> kwh_remaining 0)
      (do
        (def tier2_limit 200) ; This tier covers 200 kWh
        (def tier2_rate 0.15)
        (def kwh_in_tier2 (math_min kwh_remaining tier2_limit))
        (set total_cost (+ total_cost (* kwh_in_tier2 tier2_rate)))
        (set kwh_remaining (- kwh_remaining kwh_in_tier2))))

    ; Tier 3: Any kWh above 300 at $0.20/kWh
    (if (> kwh_remaining 0)
      (do
        (def tier3_rate 0.20)
        (set total_cost (+ total_cost (* kwh_remaining tier3_rate)))))

    (round2 total_cost))

  ; Test Cases
  ; Case 1: Consumption within Tier 1 (50 kWh)
  ; 50 * 0.10 = 5.00
  (test tier1_only (near? (calculate_electricity_bill 50) 5.00 0.01))

  ; Case 2: Consumption exactly at Tier 1 limit (100 kWh)
  ; 100 * 0.10 = 10.00
  (test exactly_tier1 (near? (calculate_electricity_bill 100) 10.00 0.01))

  ; Case 3: Consumption spanning into Tier 2 (150 kWh)
  ; 100 * 0.10 = 10.00
  ; 50 * 0.15 = 7.50
  ; Total = 17.50
  (test into_tier2 (near? (calculate_electricity_bill 150) 17.50 0.01))

  ; Case 4: Consumption exactly at Tier 2 limit (300 kWh)
  ; 100 * 0.10 = 10.00
  ; 200 * 0.15 = 30.00
  ; Total = 40.00
  (test exactly_tier2 (near? (calculate_electricity_bill 300) 40.00 0.01))

  ; Case 5: Consumption spanning into Tier 3 (350 kWh)
  ; 100 * 0.10 = 10.00
  ; 200 * 0.15 = 30.00
  ; 50 * 0.20 = 10.00
  ; Total = 50.00
  (test into_tier3 (near? (calculate_electricity_bill 350) 50.00 0.01))

  ; Case 6: Zero consumption
  ; Total = 0.00
  (test zero_consumption (near? (calculate_electricity_bill 0) 0.00 0.01))

  ; Case 7: Larger consumption (500 kWh)
  ; 100 * 0.10 = 10.00
  ; 200 * 0.15 = 30.00
  ; 200 * 0.20 = 40.00
  ; Total = 80.00
  (test large_consumption (near? (calculate_electricity_bill 500) 80.00 0.01))
)

objective: Write a function that determines if a given string is a palindrome. A string is a palindrome if it reads the same forwards and backwards, considering case and all characters.
solution:
(module PalindromeChecker
  (defun is_palindrome (s)
    (def len (str_length s))
    (if (<= len 1)
      (return true)) ; Empty or single character strings are palindromes

    (def i 0)
    (def j (- len 1))
    (while (< i j)
      (def char_i (str_substring s i 1))
      (def char_j (str_substring s j 1))
      (if (not (str_eq char_i char_j))
        (return false))
      (set i (+ i 1))
      (set j (- j 1)))
    true)

  (test empty_string
    (eq? (is_palindrome "") true))

  (test single_char_string
    (eq? (is_palindrome "a") true))

  (test simple_palindrome_even
    (eq? (is_palindrome "abba") true))

  (test simple_palindrome_odd
    (eq? (is_palindrome "madam") true))

  (test not_palindrome_even
    (eq? (is_palindrome "abc") false))

  (test not_palindrome_odd
    (eq? (is_palindrome "hello") false))

  (test case_sensitive
    (eq? (is_palindrome "Racecar") false)))

(module ParkingFeeCalculator
  ;; Helper function to determine the number of billable hours,
  ;; considering the first 60 minutes are free and rounding up any partial hour.
  (defun calculate_billable_hours (duration_minutes)
    (def chargeable_minutes (- duration_minutes 60))
    (if (<= chargeable_minutes 0)
      (return 0))
    ;; Convert chargeable minutes to hours, rounding up
    (math_ceil (/ chargeable_minutes 60.0)))

  ;; Main function to calculate the total parking fee based on duration and hourly rate.
  (defun calculate_parking_fee (duration_minutes rate_per_hour)
    (def hours_to_bill (calculate_billable_hours duration_minutes))
    (* hours_to_bill rate_per_hour))

  ;; Tests

    (eq? (calculate_parking_fee 30 5.0) 0.0))

)

objective: Implement a function `get_log_level` that retrieves the desired logging level from the `LOG_LEVEL` environment variable. If the variable is not set or is empty, the function should default to "INFO". Otherwise, it should return the uppercase version of the environment variable's value.
solution:
(module LogLevelReader
  (extern defun env_read (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun get_log_level ()
    (def level_str (env_read "LOG_LEVEL"))
    (if (str_eq level_str "")
      (return "INFO"))
    (str_upper level_str))

  (test default_level
    (mocks (env.get "LOG_LEVEL" ""))
    (eq? (get_log_level) "INFO"))

  (test custom_lowercase
    (mocks (env.get "LOG_LEVEL" "debug"))
    (eq? (get_log_level) "DEBUG"))

  (test custom_mixedcase
    (mocks (env.get "LOG_LEVEL" "WaRnInG"))
    (eq? (get_log_level) "WARNING"))

  (test custom_uppercase
    (mocks (env.get "LOG_LEVEL" "ERROR"))

objective: Calculate the estimated fuel cost for a given trip distance, retrieving the fuel price per gallon and the vehicle's miles per gallon (MPG) from external environment settings. The function should convert these settings from strings to numbers and compute the total cost.
solution:
(module TripFuelCalculator
  (extern defun env_get (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun calculate_gallons (distance mpg)
    (/ distance mpg))

  (defun calculate_cost (gallons price_per_gallon)
    (* gallons price_per_gallon))

  (defun estimate_trip_fuel_cost (distance)
    (def fuel_price_str (env_get "FUEL_PRICE_PER_GALLON"))
    (def vehicle_mpg_str (env_get "VEHICLE_MPG"))

    (def fuel_price (str_to_num fuel_price_str))
    (def vehicle_mpg (str_to_num vehicle_mpg_str))

    (def gallons_needed (calculate_gallons distance vehicle_mpg))
    (calculate_cost gallons_needed fuel_price))

  (test basic_trip
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "FUEL_PRICE_PER_GALLON" "3.50")
      ("env.get" "VEHICLE_MPG" "25"))
    (near? (estimate_trip_fuel_cost 100) 14.00 0.01))

  (test longer_trip
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "FUEL_PRICE_PER_GALLON" "3.50")
      ("env.get" "VEHICLE_MPG" "25"))
    (near? (estimate_trip_fuel_cost 250) 35.00 0.01))

  (test different_mpg_and_price
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "FUEL_PRICE_PER_GALLON" "4.00")
      ("env.get" "VEHICLE_MPG" "30"))
    (near? (estimate_trip_fuel_cost 180) 24.00 0.01))

  (test short_trip_high_efficiency
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "FUEL_PRICE_PER_GALLON" "3.20")
      ("env.get" "VEHICLE_MPG" "40"))
    (near? (estimate_trip_fuel_cost 50) 4.00 0.01))

  (test zero_distance_trip
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "FUEL_PRICE_PER_GALLON" "3.00")
      ("env.get" "VEHICLE_MPG" "20"))
    (near? (estimate_trip_fuel_cost 0) 0.00 0.01))
)

topic: read two env vars and concat
objective: Write a function that reads a user's `FIRST_NAME` and `LAST_NAME` from environment variables. It should return the full name as "FirstName LastName". If either environment variable is not set (i.e., returns an empty string), the function should return "Guest User" as a fallback.
solution:
(module UserFullName
  (extern defun env_get (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun get_full_name ()
    (def first_name (env_get "FIRST_NAME"))
    (def last_name (env_get "LAST_NAME"))
    (if (str_eq first_name "") (return "Guest User"))
    (if (str_eq last_name "") (return "Guest User"))
    (str_concat first_name (str_concat " " last_name)))

  (test both_present
    (mocks
      (env.get "FIRST_NAME" "John")
      (env.get "LAST_NAME" "Doe"))
    (eq? (get_full_name) "John Doe"))

  (test first_name_missing
    (mocks
      (env.get "FIRST_NAME" "")
      (env.get "LAST_NAME" "Doe"))
    (eq? (get_full_name) "Guest User"))

  (test last_name_missing
    (mocks
      (env.get "FIRST_NAME" "Jane")
      (env.get "LAST_NAME" ""))
    (eq? (get_full_name) "Guest User"))

  (test both_names_missing
    (mocks
      (env.get "FIRST_NAME" "")
      (env.get "LAST_NAME" ""))
    (eq? (get_full_name) "Guest User"))

  (test names_with_special_chars
    (mocks
      (env.get "FIRST_NAME" "Jean-Luc")
      (env.get "LAST_NAME" "Picard"))
    (eq? (get_full_name) "Jean-Luc Picard"))
)

objective: The objective is to write a function that fetches content from a given URL using an `http.fetch` capability and returns the length of the HTTP response body as an integer.

objective: Implement a function `median_of_three` that takes three numbers and returns their median. The median is the middle value when the numbers are sorted.
solution:
(module MedianOfThree
  (defun median_of_three (a b c)
    (def min_val (math_min a (math_min b c)))
    (def max_val (math_max a (math_max b c)))
    (- (+ a b c) min_val max_val))

  (test sorted_ascending  (eq? (median_of_three 1 2 3) 2))
  (test sorted_descending (eq? (median_of_three 3 2 1) 2))
  (test mixed_order       (eq? (median_of_three 10 5 15) 10))
  (test one_duplicate     (eq? (median_of_three 7 2 7) 7))
  (test all_duplicates    (eq? (median_of_three 4 4 4) 4))
  (test with_zero         (eq? (median_of_three -5 0 5) 0))
  (test all_negative      (eq? (median_of_three -10 -20 -5) -10)))

objective: Write a program to calculate the total fuel cost for a trip. The calculation should consider the total distance of the trip, the vehicle's fuel efficiency in miles per gallon, and the current price of fuel per gallon, rounding the final cost to two decimal places.
solution:
(module TripFuelCost
  (defun calculate_gallons_needed (distance_miles mpg)
    (/ distance_miles mpg))

  (defun calculate_raw_cost (gallons price_per_gallon)
    (* gallons price_per_gallon))

  (defun round_to_cents (amount)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* amount 100) 0.5)) 100))

  (defun get_trip_fuel_cost (distance_miles mpg price_per_gallon)
    (def gallons (calculate_gallons_needed distance_miles mpg))
    (def raw_cost (calculate_raw_cost gallons price_per_gallon))
    (round_to_cents raw_cost))

  (test simple_trip_cost
    (near? (get_trip_fuel_cost 100 25 3.00) 12.00 0.001))

  (test long_trip_high_cost
    (near? (get_trip_fuel_cost 500 20 4.50) 112.50 0.001))

  (test fractional_gallons_rounding
    (near? (get_trip_fuel_cost 75 22 3.25) 11.08 0.001))

  (test zero_distance
    (near? (get_trip_fuel_cost 0 30 3.50) 0.00 0.001))

  (test different_efficiency_and_price
    (near? (get_trip_fuel_cost 150 35 2.89) 12.39 0.001)) ; 150/35 = 4.2857... gallons * 2.89 = 12.3908... -> 12.39
)

objective: Implement a function that converts a temperature given in Kelvin to its equivalent values in Celsius and Fahrenheit. The function should return an array containing the Celsius value at index 0 and the Fahrenheit value at index 1.
solution:
(module KelvinConverter
  (defun convert_kelvin (k)
    (def celsius (- k 273.15))
    (def fahrenheit (+ (* celsius (/ 9.0 5.0)) 32.0))
    (def result (arr_new 2))
    (arr_set result 0 celsius)
    (arr_set result 1 fahrenheit)
    result)

  (test absolute_zero
    (def result (convert_kelvin 0.0))
    (near? (arr_get result 0) -273.15 0.001)
    (near? (arr_get result 1) -459.67 0.001))

  (test freezing_point_of_water
    (def result (convert_kelvin 273.15))
    (near? (arr_get result 0) 0.0 0.001)
    (near? (arr_get result 1) 32.0 0.001))

  (test boiling_point_of_water
    (def result (convert_kelvin 373.15))
    (near? (arr_get result 0) 100.0 0.001)
    (near? (arr_get result 1) 212.0 0.001))

  (test room_temperature
    (def result (convert_kelvin 293.15)) ; 20C
    (near? (arr_get result 0) 20.0 0.001)
    (near? (arr_get result 1) 68.0 0.001))

  (test high_temperature
    (def result (convert_kelvin 1000.0))
    (near? (arr_get result 0) 726.85 0.001)
    (near? (arr_get result 1) 1340.33 0.001)))

objective: Implement a function that clamps a given number to a specified range [min, max]. If the number is less than the minimum, return the minimum. If it's greater than the maximum, return the maximum. Otherwise, return the number itself.
solution:
(module ClampToRange
  (defun clamp (value min_val max_val)
    (if (< value min_val)
      (return min_val))
    (if (> value max_val)
      (return max_val))
    value)

  (test value_in_range
    (eq? (clamp 5 1 10) 5))

  (test value_below_min
    (eq? (clamp 0 1 10) 1))

  (test value_above_max
    (eq? (clamp 15 1 10) 10))

  (test value_equals_min
    (eq? (clamp 1 1 10) 1))

  (test value_equals_max
    (eq? (clamp 10 1 10) 10))

  (test negative_range
    (eq? (clamp -5 -10 -2) -5))

  (test negative_value_below_min
    (eq? (clamp -15 -10 -2) -10)))

objective: Implement a function that reads an environment variable and interprets its value as a boolean. The function should return `true` if the variable's value is "true" (case-insensitive), and `false` if it is "false" (case-insensitive), not set, or any other invalid string.
solution:
(module EnvBooleanReader
  (extern defun env_read (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun get_env_boolean (key)
    (def val (env_read key))
    (def lower_val (str_lower val))

    (if (str_eq lower_val "true") (return true))
    ; If it's "false" (case-insensitive), return false
    (if (str_eq lower_val "false") (return false))

    ; For empty string or any other value, default to false
    false)

  (test true_lowercase
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_FLAG" "true"))
    (eq? (get_env_boolean "FEATURE_FLAG") true))

  (test true_uppercase
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_FLAG" "TRUE"))
    (eq? (get_env_boolean "FEATURE_FLAG") true))

  (test false_mixedcase
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_FLAG" "False"))
    (eq? (get_env_boolean "FEATURE_FLAG") false))

  (test unset_variable
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_FLAG" ""))
    (eq? (get_env_boolean "FEATURE_FLAG") false))

  (test invalid_numeric_value
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_FLAG" "1"))
    (eq? (get_env_boolean "FEATURE_FLAG") false))

  (test invalid_text_value
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_FLAG" "yes"))
    (eq? (get_env_boolean "FEATURE_FLAG") false))
)

objective: Implement a function that calculates the absolute difference between two numbers, and then use it to find the smallest absolute difference among all possible pairs of three given numbers.
solution:
(module SmallestPairDifference
  (defun abs_diff (a b)
    (math_abs (- a b)))

  (defun smallest_pair_diff (x y z)
    (def diff_xy (abs_diff x y))
    (def diff_xz (abs_diff x z))
    (def diff_yz (abs_diff y z))
    (math_min diff_xy (math_min diff_xz diff_yz)))

  (test positive_numbers
    (eq? (smallest_pair_diff 1 5 3) 2)) ; abs(1-3)=2, abs(1-5)=4, abs(3-5)=2 -> min is 2
  
  (test negative_numbers
    (eq? (smallest_pair_diff -10 -5 -7) 2)) ; abs(-10 - -7)=3, abs(-10 - -5)=5, abs(-5 - -7)=2 -> min is 2

  (test mixed_numbers
    (eq? (smallest_pair_diff -2 0 3) 2)) ; abs(-2-0)=2, abs(-2-3)=5, abs(0-3)=3 -> min is 2

  (test equal_pair
    (eq? (smallest_pair_diff 7 7 12) 0)) ; abs(7-7)=0, abs(7-12)=5 -> min is 0

  (test all_equal
    (eq? (smallest_pair_diff 42 42 42) 0)) ; all differences are 0

  (test larger_range
    (eq? (smallest_pair_diff 100 150 120) 20))) ; abs(100-120)=20, abs(100-150)=50, abs(150-120)=30 -> min is 20

objective: This module calculates the total monthly rent, combining a fixed base rent with variable utility costs for electricity and water. The base rent and per-unit utility rates are retrieved from environment variables, while the monthly usage for electricity (kWh) and water (gallons) are provided as parameters.
solution:
(module MonthlyRentCalculator
  (extern defun env_get (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun parse_float_from_env (key)
    (str_to_num (env_get key)))

  (defun get_base_rent ()
    (parse_float_from_env "BASE_RENT"))

  (defun get_electricity_rate ()
    (parse_float_from_env "ELECTRICITY_RATE_PER_KWH"))

  (defun get_water_rate ()
    (parse_float_from_env "WATER_RATE_PER_GALLON"))

  (defun calculate_electricity_cost (kwh_usage)
    (* kwh_usage (get_electricity_rate)))

  (defun calculate_water_cost (gallons_usage)
    (* gallons_usage (get_water_rate)))

  (defun compute_monthly_rent (kwh_usage gallons_usage)
    (def base_rent (get_base_rent))
    (def electricity_cost (calculate_electricity_cost kwh_usage))
    (def water_cost (calculate_water_cost gallons_usage))
    (+ base_rent electricity_cost water_cost))

  (test basic_case
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "BASE_RENT" "1000.0")
      ("env.get" "ELECTRICITY_RATE_PER_KWH" "0.15")
      ("env.get" "WATER_RATE_PER_GALLON" "0.005"))
    (near? (compute_monthly_rent 200 5000) (+ 1000 (* 200 0.15) (* 5000 0.005)) 0.001))

  (test zero_usage
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "BASE_RENT" "1200.0")
      ("env.get" "ELECTRICITY_RATE_PER_KWH" "0.20")
      ("env.get" "WATER_RATE_PER_GALLON" "0.003"))
    (near? (compute_monthly_rent 0 0) 1200.0 0.001))

  (test high_usage
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "BASE_RENT" "800.0")
      ("env.get" "ELECTRICITY_RATE_PER_KWH" "0.10")
      ("env.get" "WATER_RATE_PER_GALLON" "0.002"))
    (near? (compute_monthly_rent 500 15000) (+ 800 (* 500 0.10) (* 15000 0.002)) 0.001))

  (test only_electricity_usage
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "BASE_RENT" "950.0")
      ("env.get" "ELECTRICITY_RATE_PER_KWH" "0.18")
      ("env.get" "WATER_RATE_PER_GALLON" "0.004"))
    (near? (compute_monthly_rent 150 0) (+ 950 (* 150 0.18) 0) 0.001))

  (test only_water_usage
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "BASE_RENT" "1100.0")
      ("env.get" "ELECTRICITY_RATE_PER_KWH" "0.12")
      ("env.get" "WATER_RATE_PER_GALLON" "0.006"))
    (near? (compute_monthly_rent 0 8000) (+ 1100 0 (* 8000 0.006)) 0.001))

  (test different_rates
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "BASE_RENT" "750.0")
      ("env.get" "ELECTRICITY_RATE_PER_KWH" "0.25")
      ("env.get" "WATER_RATE_PER_GALLON" "0.001"))
    (near? (compute_monthly_rent 300 20000) (+ 750 (* 300 0.25) (* 20000 0.001)) 0.001))
)

objective: Write a program that calculates the final daily hotel room rate, applying a weekend surcharge if the specified day of the week is a Saturday or Sunday. The surcharge is given as a percentage of the base daily rate.
solution:
(module HotelRateCalculator
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))

  (defun is_weekend (day_name)
    (if (str_eq day_name "Saturday")
      (return true))
    (if (str_eq day_name "Sunday")
      (return true))
    false)

  (defun calculate_surcharged_rate (base_rate surcharge_pct)
    (* base_rate (+ 1 (/ surcharge_pct 100))))

  (defun get_final_daily_rate (base_rate day_of_week weekend_surcharge_pct)
    (if (is_weekend day_of_week)
      (round2 (calculate_surcharged_rate base_rate weekend_surcharge_pct))
      (round2 base_rate)))

  (test weekday_no_surcharge
    (eq? (get_final_daily_rate 150 "Monday" 10) 150.00))

  (test weekend_surcharge_applied
    (eq? (get_final_daily_rate 200 "Saturday" 20) 240.00))

  (test weekend_surcharge_applied_sunday
    (eq? (get_final_daily_rate 120 "Sunday" 15) 138.00))

  (test weekday_other_day
    (eq? (get_final_daily_rate 180 "Wednesday" 5) 180.00))

  (test zero_surcharge_weekend
    (eq? (get_final_daily_rate 100 "Saturday" 0) 100.00))

  (test decimal_surcharge_and_base
    (eq? (get_final_daily_rate 175.50 "Sunday" 12.5) 197.44))
)

The function must then concatenate the resolved values of both environment variables into a single string.

(module EnvVarConcatenator
  (extern defun env_read (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun concat_env_with_defaults (key1 default1 key2 default2)
    (def val1 (env_read key1))
    (def final_val1 (if (str_eq val1 "") default1 val1))

    (def val2 (env_read key2))
    (def final_val2 (if (str_eq val2 "") default2 val2))

    (str_concat final_val1 final_val2))

  (test both_present

      (env.get "APP_PREFIX" "Super")
      (env.get "APP_SUFFIX" "Service"))

      (env.get "APP_PREFIX" "") ; Mocking missing/empty
      (env.get "APP_SUFFIX" "Service"))
    (eq? (concat_env_with_defaults "APP_PREFIX" "My" "APP_SUFFIX" "App") "MyService"))

  (test first_present_second_missing

      (env.get "APP_PREFIX" "Super")
      (env.get "APP_SUFFIX" "")) ; Mocking missing/empty
    (eq? (concat_env_with_defaults "APP_PREFIX" "My" "APP_SUFFIX" "App") "SuperApp"))

  (test both_missing

      (env.get "APP_PREFIX" "")
      (env.get "APP_SUFFIX" ""))

  (test with_hyphens_and_defaults

      (env.get "VERSION_TAG" "v1.2-")
      (env.get "BUILD_ID" "")) ; BUILD_ID is missing
    (eq? (concat_env_with_defaults "VERSION_TAG" "dev-" "BUILD_ID" "001") "v1.2-001"))

  (test empty_string_from_env_for_first
    (mocks
      (env.get "ENV_VAR_A" "") ; ENV_VAR_A is explicitly empty from env
      (env.get "ENV_VAR_B" "ValueB"))
    (eq? (concat_env_with_defaults "ENV_VAR_A" "DefaultA" "ENV_VAR_B" "DefaultB") "DefaultAValueB"))
)

objective: The objective is to calculate an individual's Body Mass Index (BMI) given their weight in kilograms and height in meters, and then classify this BMI into one of four categories: "Underweight", "Normal", "Overweight", or "Obese".
solution:
(module BmiClassifier
  (defun calculate_bmi (weight height)
    (/ weight (* height height)))

      (if (< bmi 25.0)
        "Normal"
        (if (< bmi 30.0)
          "Overweight"
          "Obese"))))

  (defun get_bmi_classification (weight height)
    (def bmi_value (calculate_bmi weight height))
    (classify_bmi bmi_value))

objective: Implement a function that calculates the total price for an item given its unit price and quantity, applying a bulk discount based on predefined quantity thresholds. The function should support two discount tiers and round the final price to two decimal places.
solution:
(module BulkPriceCalculator
  ;; Helper function to round a number to two decimal places
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))

  ;; Helper function to apply a percentage discount to an amount
  (defun apply_discount (amount discount_pct)
    (* amount (- 1 (/ discount_pct 100.0)))) ; Use 100.0 for float division

  ;; Calculates the total price for a given quantity, applying tiered bulk discounts.
  ;; discount_pct values are expected as percentages (e.g., 10 for 10%).
  (defun calculate_total_price (unit_price quantity threshold1 discount1_pct threshold2 discount2_pct)
    (def base_price (* unit_price quantity))
    (round2
      (if (>= quantity threshold2)
        (apply_discount base_price discount2_pct)
        (if (>= quantity threshold1)
          (apply_discount base_price discount1_pct)
          base_price))))

  ;; Test cases
  ;; No discount applied (quantity below first threshold)
  (test no_discount
    (near? (calculate_total_price 10.00 2 3 10 5 20) 20.00 0.01))

  ;; First tier discount applied (quantity at threshold1)
  (test first_tier_at_threshold
    (near? (calculate_total_price 10.00 3 3 10 5 20) 27.00 0.01)) ; 3 * 10 * (1 - 0.10) = 27

  ;; First tier discount applied (quantity between thresholds)
  (test first_tier_between_thresholds
    (near? (calculate_total_price 10.00 4 3 10 5 20) 36.00 0.01)) ; 4 * 10 * (1 - 0.10) = 36

  ;; Second tier discount applied (quantity at threshold2)
  (test second_tier_at_threshold
    (near? (calculate_total_price 10.00 5 3 10 5 20) 40.00 0.01)) ; 5 * 10 * (1 - 0.20) = 40

  ;; Second tier discount applied (quantity above second threshold)
  (test second_tier_above_threshold
    (near? (calculate_total_price 10.00 10 3 10 5 20) 80.00 0.01)) ; 10 * 10 * (1 - 0.20) = 80

  ;; Test with different unit price and large quantities, second tier discount
  (test large_quantity_different_price
    (near? (calculate_total_price 12.50 100 10 5 50 15) 1062.50 0.01)) ; 100 * 12.50 * (1 - 0.15) = 1062.50

  ;; Test with decimal unit price and quantity, first tier discount, requires rounding
  (test decimal_price_rounding
    (near? (calculate_total_price 5.99 7 5 5 10 10) 39.83 0.01)) ; 7 * 5.99 * (1 - 0.05) = 39.8335 -> 39.83
)

(module EnvVarBoolean
  (extern defun env_read (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun _is_truthy_string (value_str)
    "Helper to determine if a string represents a truthy boolean."
    (if (str_eq value_str "true") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq value_str "TRUE") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq value_str "1") (return 1))
    0)

    "Reads an environment variable and returns its boolean interpretation (1 for true, 0 for false)."

objective: Implement a function `median_of_three` that takes three numbers as input and returns the middle value among them. This means if the three numbers were sorted, the function should return the number in the second position.
solution:
(module MedianOfThree
  (defun median_of_three (a b c)
    ;; Check if 'a' is the median
    (if (or (and (<= b a) (<= a c))
            (and (<= c a) (<= a b)))
      a
      ;; If not 'a', check if 'b' is the median
      (if (or (and (<= a b) (<= b c))
              (and (<= c b) (<= b a)))
        b
        ;; Otherwise, 'c' must be the median
        c)))

  (test distinct_ascending (eq? (median_of_three 1 2 3) 2))
  (test distinct_descending (eq? (median_of_three 3 2 1) 2))
  (test distinct_mixed (eq? (median_of_three 10 5 15) 10))
  (test distinct_mixed_neg (eq? (median_of_three -5 0 -10) -5))
  (test two_equal_first_two (eq? (median_of_three 7 7 3) 7))
  (test two_equal_last_two (eq? (median_of_three 3 7 7) 7))
  (test all_equal (eq? (median_of_three 100 100 100) 100)))

(module MonthlyRentCalculator

  (defun calculate_utility_sum (electricity water gas internet)
    (+ electricity water gas internet))

  (defun calculate_total_monthly_bill (base_rent electricity water gas internet)
    (def utilities (calculate_utility_sum electricity water gas internet))
    (def total_before_fees (+ base_rent utilities))
    (def maintenance_fee 0)

    (+ total_before_fees maintenance_fee))

objective: Implement a function that takes a string `s` and a non-negative integer `n`, and returns a new string consisting of `s` repeated `n` times. If `n` is negative, return an empty string.
solution:
(module StringRepeater
  (defun repeat_string (s n)
    (if (< n 0)
      (return ""))
    (def result "")
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i n)
      (set result (str_concat result s))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    result)

  (test repeat_three_times (eq? (repeat_string "abc" 3) "abcabcabc"))
  (test repeat_once         (eq? (repeat_string "hello" 1) "hello"))
  (test repeat_zero_times   (eq? (repeat_string "world" 0) ""))
  (test empty_string_input  (eq? (repeat_string "" 5) ""))
  (test negative_count      (eq? (repeat_string "test" -2) ""))
  (test complex_string      (eq? (repeat_string "123-" 2) "123-123-")))

objective: Implement a function that reads an environment variable and interprets its value as a boolean. The function should return `true` if the variable's value (case-insensitive) is "true", `false` if it's "false", and a specified default boolean value for any other input, including an empty or missing variable.
solution:
(module EnvVarBoolParser
  (extern defun env_read (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun parse_env_bool (key default_value)
    (def raw_val (env_read key))
    (def lower_val (str_lower raw_val))
    (if (str_eq lower_val "true") (return true))
    (if (str_eq lower_val "false") (return false))
    default_value)

  (test true_case_insensitive
    (mocks (env.get "VERBOSE" "TRUE"))
    (eq? (parse_env_bool "VERBOSE" false) true))

  (test false_case_insensitive
    (mocks (env.get "DEBUG_MODE" "false"))
    (eq? (parse_env_bool "DEBUG_MODE" true) false))

  (test default_on_empty
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_X" ""))
    (eq? (parse_env_bool "FEATURE_X" false) false))

  (test default_on_missing
    (mocks (env.get "ANALYTICS_ON" "")) ;; env.get returns "" for missing keys
    (eq? (parse_env_bool "ANALYTICS_ON" true) true))

  (test default_on_invalid_string
    (mocks (env.get "LOG_LEVEL" "debug"))
    (eq? (parse_env_bool "LOG_LEVEL" false) false))

  (test default_override
    (mocks (env.get "CACHE_ENABLED" "yes"))
    (eq? (parse_env_bool "CACHE_ENABLED" true) true)))

objective: The objective is to implement a function that calculates the digital root of a non-negative integer. The digital root is the single-digit value obtained by an iterative process of summing digits, on each iteration using the result from the previous iteration as the input, until a single-digit number is reached.
solution:
(module DigitalRoot
  (defun sum_digits (n)
    (def total 0)
    (def current_n n)
    (while (> current_n 0)
      (set total (+ total (math_mod current_n 10)))
      (set current_n (math_floor (/ current_n 10))))
    total)

  (defun digital_root (n)
    (if (< n 0) (return -1)) ; Digital root is typically for non-negative integers.
    (def current_num n)
    (while (>= current_num 10)
      (set current_num (sum_digits current_num)))
    current_num)

  (test zero
    (eq? (digital_root 0) 0))

  (test single_digit
    (eq? (digital_root 5) 5))

  (test ten
    (eq? (digital_root 10) 1)) ; 1 + 0 = 1

  (test two_digits
    (eq? (digital_root 42) 6)) ; 4 + 2 = 6

  (test multiple_iterations
    (eq? (digital_root 12345) 6)) ; 1+2+3+4+5 = 15; 1+5 = 6

  (test large_number
    (eq? (digital_root 987654321) 9)) ; 9+8+7+6+5+4+3+2+1 = 45; 4+5 = 9

  (test negative_input
    (eq? (digital_root -12) -1)))

objective: Implement a function `get_timeout_seconds` that retrieves a timeout duration from the "APP_TIMEOUT" environment variable. If the variable is not set or is an empty string, it should return a default timeout of 60 seconds. Otherwise, it converts the variable's string value to an integer and returns it, assuming the value is always a valid numeric string.
solution:
(module AppTimeout
  (extern defun env_read (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun get_timeout_seconds ()
    (def timeout_str (env_read "APP_TIMEOUT"))
    (if (str_eq timeout_str "")
      (return 60))
    (str_to_num timeout_str))

  (test default_timeout_if_missing
    (mocks (env.get "APP_TIMEOUT" ""))
    (eq? (get_timeout_seconds) 60))

  (test custom_timeout_present
    (mocks (env.get "APP_TIMEOUT" "180"))
    (eq? (get_timeout_seconds) 180))

  (test zero_timeout_present
    (mocks (env.get "APP_TIMEOUT" "0"))
    (eq? (get_timeout_seconds) 0))

  (test another_custom_timeout
    (mocks (env.get "APP_TIMEOUT" "30"))
    (eq? (get_timeout_seconds) 30)))

objective: This module calculates the scaled quantity of a recipe ingredient based on changes in the number of servings. It takes the original ingredient amount, the original number of servings, and the desired target number of servings. The result is rounded to two decimal places.
solution:
(module RecipeScaler
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))

  (defun scale_recipe_quantity (original_qty original_servings target_servings)
    (def per_serving_qty (/ original_qty original_servings))
    (def scaled_raw_qty (* per_serving_qty target_servings))
    (round2 scaled_raw_qty))

  (test double_servings
    (near? (scale_recipe_quantity 100.0 2 4) 200.0 0.01))

  (test half_servings
    (near? (scale_recipe_quantity 50.0 4 2) 25.0 0.01))

  (test fractional_scaling_up
    (near? (scale_recipe_quantity 75.0 3 5) 125.0 0.01))

  (test fractional_scaling_down
    (near? (scale_recipe_quantity 120.0 6 1) 20.0 0.01))

  (test decimal_ingredient_amount
    (near? (scale_recipe_quantity 3.5 2 3) 5.25 0.01))

  (test same_servings_no_change
    (near? (scale_recipe_quantity 250.0 10 10) 250.0 0.01))

  (test large_numbers
    (near? (scale_recipe_quantity 1500.0 50 120) 3600.0 0.01))
)

objective: Calculate the total fuel cost for a trip. Given the total distance to be traveled, the vehicle's fuel efficiency (distance per unit of fuel), and the price per unit of fuel, determine the overall expense.
solution:
(module TripFuelCostCalculator
  ;; Calculates the amount of fuel required for a trip.
  ;; distance: total distance to be traveled (e.g., miles, km)
  ;; efficiency: vehicle's fuel efficiency (e.g., miles per gallon, km per liter)
  (defun calculate_fuel_required (distance efficiency)
    (/ distance efficiency))

  ;; Calculates the total cost of a given amount of fuel.
  ;; fuel_amount: total fuel required (e.g., gallons, liters)
  ;; price_per_unit: cost per unit of fuel (e.g., dollars per gallon, euros per liter)
  (defun calculate_total_cost (fuel_amount price_per_unit)
    (* fuel_amount price_per_unit))

  ;; Main function to calculate the total fuel cost for a trip.
  ;; Combines fuel requirement and cost calculation.
  (defun get_trip_fuel_cost (distance efficiency price_per_unit)
    (def fuel_needed (calculate_fuel_required distance efficiency))
    (calculate_total_cost fuel_needed price_per_unit))

  (test simple_trip
    (near? (get_trip_fuel_cost 100 25 4.00) 16.00 0.001)) ; 100 miles, 25 MPG, $4/gal -> 4 gal * $4 = $16

  (test long_trip_high_efficiency
    (near? (get_trip_fuel_cost 500 40 3.50) 43.75 0.001)) ; 500 miles, 40 MPG, $3.50/gal -> 12.5 gal * $3.50 = $43.75

  (test short_trip_low_efficiency
    (near? (get_trip_fuel_cost 50 10 5.25) 26.25 0.001)) ; 50 km, 10 KPL, $5.25/L -> 5 L * $5.25 = $26.25

  (test decimal_efficiency
    (near? (get_trip_fuel_cost 150 22.5 4.10) 27.333 0.001)) ; 150 miles, 22.5 MPG, $4.10/gal -> 6.666... gal * $4.10 = $27.333...

  (test zero_distance
    (near? (get_trip_fuel_cost 0 30 3.80) 0.00 0.001))
)

objective: Write a function that fetches a given URL and extracts its HTTP status code from the response string. The `http.fetch` capability returns a string like "200 OK" or "404 Not Found", and your function should return only the numeric part of this string (e.g., "200", "404").
solution:
(module HttpStatusFetcher
  (extern defun http_fetch (url) @capability "http.fetch")

  (defun get_http_status_code (url)
    (def status_message (http_fetch url))
    (def parts (str_split status_message " "))
    (arr_get parts 0))

  (test success_200_ok
    (mocks (http.fetch "https://example.com/data" "200 OK"))
    (eq? (get_http_status_code "https://example.com/data") "200"))

  (test client_error_404_not_found
    (mocks (http.fetch "https://example.com/nonexistent" "404 Not Found"))
    (eq? (get_http_status_code "https://example.com/nonexistent") "404"))

  (test server_error_500_internal_error
    (mocks (http.fetch "https://example.com/faulty_service" "500 Internal Server Error"))
    (eq? (get_http_status_code "https://example.com/faulty_service") "500"))

  (test created_201
    (mocks (http.fetch "https://example.com/create_resource" "201 Created"))
    (eq? (get_http_status_code "https://example.com/create_resource") "201"))

  (test unauthorized_401
    (mocks (http.fetch "https://example.com/protected" "401 Unauthorized"))
    (eq? (get_http_status_code "https://example.com/protected") "401"))

  (test no_content_204
    (mocks (http.fetch "https://example.com/empty_response" "204 No Content"))
    (eq? (get_http_status_code "https://example.com/empty_response") "204")))

objective: Implement a function `smallest_prime_factor` that takes an integer `n` (assumed to be `n >= 2`). The function should return the smallest prime factor of `n`. If `n` is a prime number, the function should return `n` itself.
solution:
(module SmallestPrimeFactor
  (defun smallest_prime_factor (n)
    (if (= (math_mod n 2) 0) (return 2)) ; Handle even numbers first
    (def i 3)
    (def bound (math_floor (math_sqrt n)))
    (while (<= i bound)
      (if (= (math_mod n i) 0) (return i))
      (set i (+ i 2)))
    n) ; If no factors found up to sqrt(n), n is prime

  (test two       (eq? (smallest_prime_factor 2) 2))
  (test four      (eq? (smallest_prime_factor 4) 2))
  (test nine      (eq? (smallest_prime_factor 9) 3))
  (test seventeen (eq? (smallest_prime_factor 17) 17))
  (test composite (eq? (smallest_prime_factor 100) 2))
  (test large_prime (eq? (smallest_prime_factor 97) 97))
  (test sixty_three (eq? (smallest_prime_factor 63) 3)))
