objective: Create a module to calculate speed in kilometers per hour (km/h) given distance and time values, which may be provided in various units. The module should handle conversions for meters to kilometers and seconds/minutes to hours before computing the final speed.
solution:
(module SpeedCalculator
  (defun convert_time_to_hours (value unit_str)
    (if (str_eq unit_str "seconds")
      (/ value 3600.0)
      (if (str_eq unit_str "minutes")
        (/ value 60.0)
        ; Default to hours if unit_str is "hours" or unrecognized
        value)))

  (defun convert_distance_to_km (value unit_str)
    (if (str_eq unit_str "meters")
      (/ value 1000.0)
      ; Default to kilometers if unit_str is "kilometers" or unrecognized
      value))

  (defun calculate_speed_kmph (distance_val distance_unit time_val time_unit)
    (def d_km (convert_distance_to_km distance_val distance_unit))
    (def t_hours (convert_time_to_hours time_val time_unit))
    ; Assumes t_hours will not be zero for valid inputs
    (/ d_km t_hours))

  (test basic_km_h
    (near? (calculate_speed_kmph 100 "kilometers" 2 "hours") 50.0 0.001))

  (test meters_minutes_conversion
    ; 10000 meters = 10 km
    ; 30 minutes = 0.5 hours
    ; Speed = 10 km / 0.5 h = 20 km/h
    (near? (calculate_speed_kmph 10000 "meters" 30 "minutes") 20.0 0.001))

  (test meters_seconds_conversion
    ; 36000 meters = 36 km
    ; 7200 seconds = 2 hours
    ; Speed = 36 km / 2 h = 18 km/h
    (near? (calculate_speed_kmph 36000 "meters" 7200 "seconds") 18.0 0.001))

  (test km_minutes_conversion
    ; 50 kilometers
    ; 15 minutes = 0.25 hours
    ; Speed = 50 km / 0.25 h = 200 km/h
    (near? (calculate_speed_kmph 50 "kilometers" 15 "minutes") 200.0 0.001))

  (test km_seconds_conversion
    ; 10 kilometers
    ; 1800 seconds = 0.5 hours
    ; Speed = 10 km / 0.5 h = 20 km/h
    (near? (calculate_speed_kmph 10 "kilometers" 1800 "seconds") 20.0 0.001))

  (test mixed_units_complex
    ; 25000 meters = 25 km
    ; 45 minutes = 0.75 hours
    ; Speed = 25 km / 0.75 h = 33.333... km/h
    (near? (calculate_speed_kmph 25000 "meters" 45 "minutes") 33.333333333333336 0.001))
)

topic: read file and count char occurrences
objective: Implement a function that reads the content of a specified file and counts how many times a particular character appears within that content.
solution:
(module CharacterCounter
  (extern defun file_read (path) @capability "file.read")

  (defun count_char_occurrences (file_path target_char)
    (def content (file_read file_path))
    (def content_len (str_length content))
    (def count 0)
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i content_len)
      (def current_char (str_substring content i 1))
      (if (str_eq current_char target_char)
        (set count (+ count 1)))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    count)

  (test empty_file
    (mocks (file.read "/path/to/empty.txt" ""))
    (eq? (count_char_occurrences "/path/to/empty.txt" "a") 0))

  (test no_occurrences
    (mocks (file.read "/path/to/text.txt" "hello world"))
    (eq? (count_char_occurrences "/path/to/text.txt" "x") 0))

  (test single_occurrence
    (mocks (file.read "/path/to/text.txt" "apple"))
    (eq? (count_char_occurrences "/path/to/text.txt" "a") 1))

  (test multiple_occurrences
    (mocks (file.read "/path/to/text.txt" "banana rama"))
    (eq? (count_char_occurrences "/path/to/text.txt" "a") 5))

  (test case_sensitive
    (mocks (file.read "/path/to/case.txt" "Apple banana"))
    (eq? (count_char_occurrences "/path/to/case.txt" "a") 3))

  (test target_char_is_space
    (mocks (file.read "/path/to/spaces.txt" "hello world again"))
    (eq? (count_char_occurrences "/path/to/spaces.txt" " ") 2))

  (test target_char_is_digit
    (mocks (file.read "/path/to/digits.txt" "12345123"))
    (eq? (count_char_occurrences "/path/to/digits.txt" "1") 2))
)

objective: Implement a function for linear interpolation (LERP). Given two numbers, `start` and `end`, and a `t` value between 0 and 1, the function should return a value proportionally between `start` and `end`. If `t` is outside this range, the function should extrapolate.
solution:
(module LinearInterpolation
  (defun lerp (start end t)
    (+ start (* t (- end start))))

  (test lerp_at_start (near? (lerp 0 10 0) 0 0.0001))
  (test lerp_at_end (near? (lerp 0 10 1) 10 0.0001))
  (test lerp_midpoint (near? (lerp 0 10 0.5) 5 0.0001))
  (test lerp_quarter (near? (lerp 10 20 0.25) 12.5 0.0001))
  (test lerp_reverse_direction (near? (lerp 5 -5 0.75) -2.5 0.0001))
  (test lerp_extrapolate_up (near? (lerp 0 10 1.5) 15 0.0001))
  (test lerp_extrapolate_down (near? (lerp 0 10 -0.5) -5 0.0001))
  (test lerp_negatives (near? (lerp -10 -20 0.5) -15 0.0001)))

objective: Create a function that retrieves an integer value from an environment variable. If the variable is unset (empty string) or explicitly set to "N/A", it should return a provided default integer. Otherwise, it converts the variable's string value to an integer.
solution:
(module EnvIntReader
  (extern defun env_read (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun get_env_int_with_default (key default_val)
    (def val_str (env_read key))
    (if (str_eq val_str "")
      (return default_val))
    (if (str_eq val_str "N/A")
      (return default_val))
    (str_to_num val_str))

  (test present_positive_int
    (mocks (env.get "PORT" "8080"))
    (eq? (get_env_int_with_default "PORT" 3000) 8080))

  (test present_negative_int
    (mocks (env.get "TIMEOUT" "-100"))
    (eq? (get_env_int_with_default "TIMEOUT" 60) -100))

  (test present_zero_int
    (mocks (env.get "COUNT" "0"))
    (eq? (get_env_int_with_default "COUNT" 5) 0))

  (test missing_env_var
    (mocks (env.get "THREADS" ""))
    (eq? (get_env_int_with_default "THREADS" 4) 4))

  (test specific_non_numeric_env_var_N_A
    (mocks (env.get "MAX_CONN" "N/A"))
    (eq? (get_env_int_with_default "MAX_CONN" 100) 100))
)

topic: paycheck net from hours and rate
objective: Calculate an employee's net paycheck. Given the hours worked, hourly rate, and a tax rate percentage, compute the gross pay (with overtime at 1.5x for hours over 40), then deduct taxes, and round the final net pay to two decimal places.
solution:
(module PaycheckCalculator
  ; Rounds a number to two decimal places (cents)
  (defun round_to_cents (amount)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* amount 100) 0.5)) 100))

  ; Calculates gross pay, including 1.5x overtime for hours over 40
  (defun calculate_gross_pay (hours rate)
    (if (> hours 40)
      (+ (* 40 rate) (* (- hours 40) (* rate 1.5)))
      (* hours rate)))

  ; Calculates the tax deduction amount
  (defun calculate_tax_deduction (gross_pay tax_rate_pct)
    (* gross_pay (/ tax_rate_pct 100)))

  ; Calculates the net pay after gross pay and tax deduction, rounded to cents
  (defun calculate_net_pay (hours rate tax_rate_pct)
    (def gross (calculate_gross_pay hours rate))
    (def tax_deduction (calculate_tax_deduction gross tax_rate_pct))
    (round_to_cents (- gross tax_deduction)))

  (test standard_no_tax
    (near? (calculate_net_pay 30 15 0) 450.00 0.01))

  (test standard_with_tax
    (near? (calculate_net_pay 30 15 10) 405.00 0.01))

  (test exactly_40_hours_with_tax
    (near? (calculate_net_pay 40 20 15) 680.00 0.01))

  (test overtime_with_tax
    (near? (calculate_net_pay 45 20 20) 760.00 0.01)) ; Gross: (40*20) + (5*20*1.5) = 800 + 150 = 950; Tax: 950*0.20 = 190; Net: 950 - 190 = 760

  (test more_overtime_with_higher_tax
    (near? (calculate_net_pay 50 25 25) 1031.25 0.01)) ; Gross: (40*25) + (10*25*1.5) = 1000 + 375 = 1375; Tax: 1375*0.25 = 343.75; Net: 1375 - 343.75 = 1031.25

  (test zero_hours
    (near? (calculate_net_pay 0 100 10) 0.00 0.01))
)

objective: Create a program to calculate the total monthly rent, including base rent, usage-based utilities (electricity, gas), fixed-fee utilities (water, internet), and an optional early payment discount.
solution:
(module MonthlyRentCalculator

  (defun calculate_usage_cost (rate usage)
    (* rate usage))

  (defun apply_early_pay_discount (total_cost discount_pct)
    (if (> discount_pct 0)
        (- total_cost (* total_cost (/ discount_pct 100)))
        total_cost))

  (defun round_to_two_decimals (amount)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* amount 100) 0.5)) 100))

  (defun compute_monthly_rent (base_rent elec_rate elec_usage water_fee gas_rate gas_usage internet_fee early_pay_discount_pct)
    (def elec_cost (calculate_usage_cost elec_rate elec_usage))
    (def gas_cost (calculate_usage_cost gas_rate gas_usage))
    (def subtotal (+ base_rent elec_cost water_fee gas_cost internet_fee))
    (def final_cost (apply_early_pay_discount subtotal early_pay_discount_pct))
    (round_to_two_decimals final_cost))

  (test no_utilities_no_discount
    (near? (compute_monthly_rent 1000 0 0 0 0 0 0 0) 1000.00 0.01))

  (test all_fixed_no_discount
    (near? (compute_monthly_rent 1000 0 0 50 0 0 70 0) 1120.00 0.01))

  (test all_usage_no_discount
    (near? (compute_monthly_rent 1000 0.15 200 50 0.8 30 70 0) 1174.00 0.01))

  (test all_usage_with_discount
    (near? (compute_monthly_rent 1000 0.15 200 50 0.8 30 70 5) 1115.30 0.01))

  (test only_base_and_discount
    (near? (compute_monthly_rent 1500 0 0 0 0 0 0 10) 1350.00 0.01))

  (test complex_scenario_with_discount
    (near? (compute_monthly_rent 1200 0.12 150 65 0.75 40 80 2.5) 1358.18 0.01))

  (test zero_discount_percentage
    (near? (compute_monthly_rent 1000 0.1 100 20 0.5 50 60 0) 1115.00 0.01))
)

objective: Create a module that calculates the total fuel cost for a trip. The calculation should consider the total distance, the vehicle's fuel efficiency (miles per gallon), and the current price of fuel per gallon. The final cost should be rounded to two decimal places.
solution:
(module TripFuelCost
  ; Helper function to round a number to two decimal places
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100.0) 0.5)) 100.0))

  ; Calculates the total amount of fuel required for a trip
  ; distance: total distance to be traveled (e.g., in miles)
  ; efficiency: vehicle's fuel efficiency (e.g., miles per gallon)
  (defun calculate_fuel_needed (distance efficiency)
    (/ distance efficiency))

  ; Calculates the total monetary cost based on fuel quantity and price
  ; fuel_amount: total quantity of fuel needed (e.g., in gallons)
  ; price_per_unit: cost of one unit of fuel (e.g., dollars per gallon)
  (defun calculate_total_cost (fuel_amount price_per_unit)
    (* fuel_amount price_per_unit))

  ; Main function to estimate the total fuel cost for a trip
  ; distance: total distance to be traveled
  ; efficiency: vehicle's fuel efficiency
  ; price_per_gallon: cost of one gallon of fuel
  (defun estimate_trip_cost (distance efficiency price_per_gallon)
    (def fuel (calculate_fuel_needed distance efficiency))
    (def cost (calculate_total_cost fuel price_per_gallon))
    (round2 cost))

  (test basic_scenario
    (near? (estimate_trip_cost 300 25 3.00) 36.00 0.01)) ; 300 miles / 25 MPG = 12 gallons; 12 * $3.00 = $36.00

  (test decimal_fuel_and_cost
    (near? (estimate_trip_cost 350 20 3.50) 61.25 0.01)) ; 350 miles / 20 MPG = 17.5 gallons; 17.5 * $3.50 = $61.25

  (test long_distance_low_efficiency
    (near? (estimate_trip_cost 1000 15 4.25) 283.33 0.01)) ; 1000 / 15 = 66.666... gal; 66.666... * 4.25 = 283.333... -> $283.33

  (test short_distance_high_efficiency
    (near? (estimate_trip_cost 50 40 2.80) 3.50 0.01)) ; 50 / 40 = 1.25 gal; 1.25 * 2.80 = $3.50

  (test fractional_price_per_gallon
    (near? (estimate_trip_cost 200 30 3.29) 21.93 0.01)) ; 200 / 30 = 6.666... gal; 6.666... * 3.29 = 21.933... -> $21.93
)

objective: Calculate the factorial of a given non-negative integer. The factorial of a non-negative integer `n`, denoted as `n!`, is the product of all positive integers less than or equal to `n`. Handle the base cases where 0! = 1 and 1! = 1.
solution:
(module Factorial
  (defun factorial (n)
    (if (< n 0) (return 0)) ;; Or throw error, but 0 is an acceptable "invalid" return for some contexts.
    (if (<= n 1) (return 1))
    (def result 1)
    (def i 2)
    (while (<= i n)
      (set result (* result i))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    result)

  (test factorial_zero  (eq? (factorial 0) 1))
  (test factorial_one   (eq? (factorial 1) 1))
  (test factorial_five  (eq? (factorial 5) 120))
  (test factorial_seven (eq? (factorial 7) 5040))
  (test factorial_negative (eq? (factorial -3) 0)))

objective: Implement a currency converter that converts a source amount to a target currency using an exchange rate, then applies a transaction fee. The fee is calculated as a percentage of the converted amount, but must always be at least a specified minimum fee and never more than a specified maximum fee, with the final result rounded to two decimal places.
solution:
(module CurrencyConverter

  (defun round_to_cents (amount)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* amount 100) 0.5)) 100))

  (defun calculate_converted_amount (source_amount exchange_rate)
    (* source_amount exchange_rate))

  (defun calculate_exchange_fee (converted_amount fee_percentage min_fee max_fee)
    (def pct_fee (/ (* converted_amount fee_percentage) 100))
    (def actual_fee (math_max pct_fee min_fee))
    (math_min actual_fee max_fee))

  (defun perform_exchange (source_amount exchange_rate fee_percentage min_fee max_fee)
    (def converted (calculate_converted_amount source_amount exchange_rate))
    (def fee (calculate_exchange_fee converted fee_percentage min_fee max_fee))
    (round_to_cents (- converted fee)))

  (test simple_case
    (near? (perform_exchange 100 0.9 2 1 5) 88.2 0.01)) ;; 100*0.9 = 90. Fee 2% of 90 = 1.8. 90-1.8 = 88.2

  (test min_fee_triggered
    (near? (perform_exchange 10 0.9 2 1 5) 8 0.01)) ;; 10*0.9 = 9. Fee 2% of 9 = 0.18. Min fee 1. So fee is 1. 9-1 = 8

  (test max_fee_triggered
    (near? (perform_exchange 1000 0.9 2 1 5) 895 0.01)) ;; 1000*0.9 = 900. Fee 2% of 900 = 18. Max fee 5. So fee is 5. 900-5 = 895

  (test zero_fee_percentage
    (near? (perform_exchange 50 1.2 0 1 5) 59 0.01)) ;; 50*1.2 = 60. Fee 0% of 60 = 0. Min fee 1. So fee is 1. 60-1 = 59

  (test zero_source_amount
    (near? (perform_exchange 0 0.9 2 1 5) -1 0.01)) ;; 0*0.9 = 0. Fee 2% of 0 = 0. Min fee 1. So fee is 1. 0-1 = -1

  (test different_rates_and_fees
    (near? (perform_exchange 250 1.15 1.5 2 10) 283.19 0.01)) ;; 250 * 1.15 = 287.5. Fee 1.5% of 287.5 = 4.3125. Max(4.3125, 2) = 4.3125. Min(4.3125, 10) = 4.3125. 287.5 - 4.3125 = 283.1875. Rounded to 283.19

  (test exact_min_fee
    (near? (perform_exchange 50 1 2 1 5) 49 0.01)) ;; 50*1 = 50. Fee 2% of 50 = 1. Min fee 1. So fee is 1. 50-1 = 49
)

objective: Create a function that reads an integer from an environment variable, applies a default value if the variable is not set or empty, and then ensures the resulting integer, whether from the environment or the default, is clamped within a specified minimum and maximum range.
solution:
(module ClampedEnvInt
  (extern defun env_get (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun get_clamped_env_int (key default_val min_val max_val)
    (def val_str (env_get key))
    (def initial_val
      (if (str_eq val_str "")
        default_val
        (str_to_num val_str))) ; Convert to number (assuming valid due to test constraints and mocks)

    (if (< initial_val min_val) (return min_val)) ; Clamp to min
    (if (> initial_val max_val) (return max_val)) ; Clamp to max

    initial_val) ; Return the value if it's within range

  (test "missing variable uses default, which is then clamped"
    (mocks (env.get "WORKERS" ""))
    (eq? (get_clamped_env_int "WORKERS" 5 1 10) 5))

  (test "value within range is returned as is"
    (mocks (env.get "WORKERS" "7"))
    (eq? (get_clamped_env_int "WORKERS" 5 1 10) 7))

  (test "value below min is clamped to min"
    (mocks (env.get "WORKERS" "-2"))
    (eq? (get_clamped_env_int "WORKERS" 5 1 10) 1))

  (test "value above max is clamped to max"
    (mocks (env.get "WORKERS" "15"))
    (eq? (get_clamped_env_int "WORKERS" 5 1 10) 10))

  (test "value at min boundary"
    (mocks (env.get "WORKERS" "1"))
    (eq? (get_clamped_env_int "WORKERS" 5 1 10) 1))

  (test "value at max boundary"
    (mocks (env.get "WORKERS" "10"))
    (eq? (get_clamped_env_int "WORKERS" 5 1 10) 10))

  (test "default value itself is clamped if outside range"
    (mocks (env.get "THRESHOLD" ""))
    (eq? (get_clamped_env_int "THRESHOLD" -10 0 100) 0)) ; Default -10 is below min 0, so it should be clamped to 0
)

objective: Create a function that reads the `RETRY_COUNT` environment variable. If the variable is not set or is an empty string, it should return a default value of 3. If it's set to a numeric value, parse it as an integer. If the parsed integer is negative, return 0; otherwise, return the parsed integer.
solution:
(module RetryConfig
  (extern defun env_read (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun get_retry_limit ()
    (def retry_str (env_read "RETRY_COUNT"))
    (if (str_eq retry_str "")
        (return 3)) ; Default value if not set or empty

    (def parsed_retries (str_to_num retry_str)) ; Mocks ensure numeric strings, preventing str_to_num errors
    (if (< parsed_retries 0)
        (return 0)) ; Retries cannot be negative, clamp to 0
    parsed_retries)

  (test default_if_empty
    (mocks (env.get "RETRY_COUNT" ""))
    (eq? (get_retry_limit) 3))

  (test positive_retry_count
    (mocks (env.get "RETRY_COUNT" "5"))
    (eq? (get_retry_limit) 5))

  (test zero_retry_count
    (mocks (env.get "RETRY_COUNT" "0"))
    (eq? (get_retry_limit) 0))

  (test negative_retry_count_clamped
    (mocks (env.get "RETRY_COUNT" "-2"))
    (eq? (get_retry_limit) 0))

  (test large_retry_count
    (mocks (env.get "RETRY_COUNT" "100"))
    (eq? (get_retry_limit) 100)))

objective: Implement a function `get_feature_flag` that retrieves a feature flag setting from an environment variable. The function should accept an environment variable key and a default boolean value (represented as 0 or 1). It should return 1 if the environment variable is set to "true", "TRUE", or "1", and 0 if it's "false", "FALSE", or "0". If the environment variable is empty or contains any other unrecognized string, the function should return the provided default value.
solution:
(module FeatureFlagReader
  (extern defun env_read (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun get_feature_flag (key default_val)
    (def val (env_read key))
    (if (str_eq val "true") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq val "TRUE") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq val "1") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq val "false") (return 0))
    (if (str_eq val "FALSE") (return 0))
    (if (str_eq val "0") (return 0))
    ; If the value is unrecognized or empty, return the default
    default_val)

  (test missing_default_true
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_A_ENABLED" ""))
    (eq? (get_feature_flag "FEATURE_A_ENABLED" 1) 1))

  (test missing_default_false
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_B_ENABLED" ""))
    (eq? (get_feature_flag "FEATURE_B_ENABLED" 0) 0))

  (test present_true_string
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_C_ENABLED" "true"))
    (eq? (get_feature_flag "FEATURE_C_ENABLED" 0) 1))

  (test present_false_uppercase
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_D_ENABLED" "FALSE"))
    (eq? (get_feature_flag "FEATURE_D_ENABLED" 1) 0))

  (test present_numeric_one
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_E_ENABLED" "1"))
    (eq? (get_feature_flag "FEATURE_E_ENABLED" 0) 1))

  (test present_numeric_zero
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_F_ENABLED" "0"))
    (eq? (get_feature_flag "FEATURE_F_ENABLED" 1) 0))

  (test unrecognized_value
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_G_ENABLED" "invalid_value"))
    (eq? (get_feature_flag "FEATURE_G_ENABLED" 1) 1)))

objective: Create a function that reads a text file and returns its first line. If the file is empty, or the first line itself is empty (e.g., the file starts with a newline), an empty string should be returned.

objective: Implement a function `read_env_bool` that reads a string environment variable and interprets its value as a boolean. If the variable is not set or empty, a provided default boolean value should be returned. Otherwise, the strings 'true', 'yes', or '1' should evaluate to `true`, and all other non-empty strings (including 'false', 'no', '0') should evaluate to `false`.
solution:
(module EnvBool
  (extern defun env_read (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun read_env_bool (key default_val)
    (def val (env_read key))
    (if (str_eq val "") (return default_val)) ; Not set or empty, use default
    (if (str_eq val "true") (return true))
    (if (str_eq val "yes") (return true))
    (if (str_eq val "1") (return true))
    (return false)) ; Any other non-empty string is false

  (test key_missing_default_true
    (mocks (env.get "DEBUG" ""))
    (eq? (read_env_bool "DEBUG" true) true))

  (test key_missing_default_false
    (mocks (env.get "VERBOSE" ""))
    (eq? (read_env_bool "VERBOSE" false) false))

  (test key_is_true
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_X" "true"))
    (eq? (read_env_bool "FEATURE_X" false) true))

  (test key_is_1
    (mocks (env.get "ENABLE_LOGS" "1"))
    (eq? (read_env_bool "ENABLE_LOGS" false) true))

  (test key_is_false
    (mocks (env.get "DRY_RUN" "false"))
    (eq? (read_env_bool "DRY_RUN" true) false))

  (test key_is_no
    (mocks (env.get "FORCE" "no"))
    (eq? (read_env_bool "FORCE" true) false))

  (test key_is_other_string
    (mocks (env.get "EXPERIMENTAL" "maybe"))
    (eq? (read_env_bool "EXPERIMENTAL" true) false)))

topic: shopping cart total with tax and discount
objective: Develop a module to calculate the final cost of a shopping cart item. The calculation should account for the item's base price and quantity, apply a percentage discount, then add a percentage sales tax, and finally include a flat shipping fee, rounding the total to two decimal places.
solution:
(module ShoppingCartCalculator
  (defun round_to_cents (value)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* value 100) 0.5)) 100))

  (defun calculate_discount_amount (subtotal discount_rate_pct)
    (* subtotal (/ discount_rate_pct 100)))

  (defun apply_discount (subtotal discount_rate_pct)
    (- subtotal (calculate_discount_amount subtotal discount_rate_pct)))

  (defun calculate_tax_amount (amount tax_rate_pct)
    (* amount (/ tax_rate_pct 100)))

  (defun apply_tax (amount tax_rate_pct)
    (+ amount (calculate_tax_amount amount tax_rate_pct)))

  (defun get_final_cart_total (item_base_price quantity discount_pct tax_pct shipping_fee)
    (def subtotal (* item_base_price quantity))
    (def discounted_subtotal (apply_discount subtotal discount_pct))
    (def taxed_subtotal (apply_tax discounted_subtotal tax_pct))
    (def total_before_rounding (+ taxed_subtotal shipping_fee))
    (round_to_cents total_before_rounding))

  (test simple_no_changes (near? (get_final_cart_total 10 2 0 0 0) 20.00 0.01))
  (test with_discount_only (near? (get_final_cart_total 10 2 10 0 0) 18.00 0.01))
  (test with_tax_only (near? (get_final_cart_total 10 2 0 5 0) 21.00 0.01))
  (test with_shipping_only (near? (get_final_cart_total 10 2 0 0 5) 25.00 0.01))
  (test all_components_full (near? (get_final_cart_total 100 3 10 20 5) 329.00 0.01))
  (test hundred_percent_discount (near? (get_final_cart_total 50 1 100 10 2) 2.00 0.01))
  (test complex_rounding (near? (get_final_cart_total 7.33 3 5 8 1.50) 24.06 0.01))
)

objective: Create a module that calculates a person's Body Mass Index (BMI) given their weight in kilograms and height in meters, then classifies the BMI into standard categories (Underweight, Normal Weight, Overweight, Obese).
solution:
(module BmiClassifier
  ; Calculates BMI given weight in kilograms and height in meters.
  (defun calculate_bmi (weight_kg height_m)
    (/ weight_kg (* height_m height_m)))

  ; Classifies a given BMI value into standard categories.
  (defun classify_bmi (bmi)
    (if (< bmi 18.5)
      "Underweight"
      (if (< bmi 25.0)
        "Normal Weight"
        (if (< bmi 30.0)
          "Overweight"
          "Obese"))))

  ; Main function to calculate BMI and return its classification.
  (defun get_bmi_classification (weight_kg height_m)
    (def bmi_val (calculate_bmi weight_kg height_m))
    (classify_bmi bmi_val))

  ; Test cases
  (test underweight_example
    (eq? (get_bmi_classification 45 1.70) "Underweight")) ; BMI ~15.57

  (test normal_weight_example
    (eq? (get_bmi_classification 70 1.75) "Normal Weight")) ; BMI ~22.86

  (test overweight_example
    (eq? (get_bmi_classification 80 1.70) "Overweight")) ; BMI ~27.68

  (test obese_example
    (eq? (get_bmi_classification 100 1.70) "Obese")) ; BMI ~34.60

  (test normal_boundary_lower
    (eq? (get_bmi_classification 53.6125 1.70) "Normal Weight")) ; BMI = 18.5

  (test overweight_boundary_lower
    (eq? (get_bmi_classification 72.25 1.70) "Overweight")) ; BMI = 25.0

  (test obese_boundary_lower
    (eq? (get_bmi_classification 86.7 1.70) "Obese"))) ; BMI = 30.0

topic: shipping cost by weight and zone
objective: Create a module to calculate the total shipping cost for a package based on its weight and destination zone. The cost includes a zone-specific base fee, a per-kilogram weight charge, and a flat handling fee.
solution:
(module ShippingCalculator
  ;; Helper to round to 2 decimal places for currency calculations
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))

  ;; Calculates the base shipping cost for a given zone
  (defun get_zone_base_cost (zone)
    (if (str_eq zone "A") 5.0
    (if (str_eq zone "B") 10.0
    (if (str_eq zone "C") 15.0
    20.0)))) ;; Default base cost for any other/unknown zones

  ;; Calculates the cost component based on the package's weight
  (defun calculate_weight_cost (weight_kg)
    (* weight_kg 2.50)) ;; Assumes a rate of 2.50 per kilogram

  ;; Calculates the total shipping cost by summing the base cost, weight cost, and a flat handling fee
  (defun calculate_total_shipping_cost (zone weight_kg)
    (def base_cost (get_zone_base_cost zone))
    (def weight_cost (calculate_weight_cost weight_kg))
    (def handling_fee 1.50)
    (round2 (+ base_cost weight_cost handling_fee)))

  (test zone_a_low_weight
    (near? (calculate_total_shipping_cost "A" 2.0) 11.50 0.01))

  (test zone_b_medium_weight
    (near? (calculate_total_shipping_cost "B" 5.5) 25.25 0.01))

  (test zone_c_high_weight
    (near? (calculate_total_shipping_cost "C" 10.0) 41.50 0.01))

  (test unknown_zone
    (near? (calculate_total_shipping_cost "X" 3.0) 29.00 0.01))

  (test zone_a_zero_weight
    (near? (calculate_total_shipping_cost "A" 0.0) 6.50 0.01))
)

topic: paycheck net from hours and rate
objective: Write a program that calculates an employee's net pay. It should account for standard hours, overtime hours (at 1.5x rate for hours exceeding a threshold), percentage-based federal and state taxes, and a fixed deduction, rounding the final net pay to two decimal places.
solution:
(module PaycheckCalculator
  (defun round_currency (amount)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* amount 100) 0.5)) 100))

  (defun calculate_gross (hours rate overtime_threshold overtime_multiplier)
    (if (<= hours overtime_threshold)
      (* hours rate)
      (+ (* overtime_threshold rate)
         (* (- hours overtime_threshold) rate overtime_multiplier))))

  (defun calculate_percentage_deduction (amount percentage)
    (* amount (/ percentage 100)))

  (defun paycheck_net (hours rate ot_thresh ot_mult fed_tax_pct state_tax_pct fixed_deduction)
    (def gross_pay (calculate_gross hours rate ot_thresh ot_mult))
    (def fed_tax (calculate_percentage_deduction gross_pay fed_tax_pct))
    (def state_tax (calculate_percentage_deduction gross_pay state_tax_pct))
    (def total_deductions (+ fed_tax state_tax fixed_deduction))
    (def net_pre_round (- gross_pay total_deductions))
    (round_currency net_pre_round))

  (test no_overtime_no_deductions
    (near? (paycheck_net 30 20 40 1.5 0 0 0) 600.00 0.01))

  (test standard_pay_with_deductions
    (near? (paycheck_net 40 25 40 1.5 10 5 25) 825.00 0.01))

  (test overtime_pay_with_deductions
    (near? (paycheck_net 50 20 40 1.5 10 5 25) 910.00 0.01))

  (test zero_hours_with_fixed_deduction
    (near? (paycheck_net 0 20 40 1.5 10 5 25) -25.00 0.01))

  (test fractional_hours_and_rates_with_deductions
    (near? (paycheck_net 35 15.5 40 1.5 10 5 10.50) 450.63 0.01))

  (test complex_overtime_and_taxes
    (near? (paycheck_net 45.5 30.75 40 1.5 12.5 6.0 75.0) 1134.21 0.01))

  (test exactly_at_overtime_threshold
    (near? (paycheck_net 40 30 40 1.5 10 5 50) 970.00 0.01)))

objective: Write a program that calculates the total monthly rent, including fixed utility costs and variable utility costs fetched from environment variables. The program should sum a base rent, specified fixed electricity and water costs, and dynamically retrieve gas and internet costs from environment variables to compute the final monthly expense.
solution:
(module MonthlyRentCalculator
  (extern defun get_env_var (name) @capability "env.get")

  (defun parse_number (s)
    (if (str_eq s "")
      0.0
      (str_to_num s)))

  (defun calculate_total_utilities (fixed_electricity_cost fixed_water_cost)
    (def gas_str (get_env_var "GAS_COST"))
    (def internet_str (get_env_var "INTERNET_COST"))
    (def gas_cost (parse_number gas_str))
    (def internet_cost (parse_number internet_str))
    (+ fixed_electricity_cost fixed_water_cost gas_cost internet_cost))

  (defun calculate_monthly_rent (base_rent fixed_electricity_cost fixed_water_cost)
    (def total_utilities (calculate_total_utilities fixed_electricity_cost fixed_water_cost))
    (+ base_rent total_utilities))

  (test full_utilities
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "GAS_COST" "50.0")
      ("env.get" "INTERNET_COST" "60.0"))
    (eq? (calculate_monthly_rent 1000.0 75.0 40.0) 1225.0))

  (test missing_env_utilities
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "GAS_COST" "") ; Missing gas cost
      ("env.get" "INTERNET_COST" "80.0"))
    (eq? (calculate_monthly_rent 800.0 60.0 30.0) 970.0))

  (test all_fixed_only
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "GAS_COST" "") ; Missing gas cost
      ("env.get" "INTERNET_COST" "")) ; Missing internet cost
    (eq? (calculate_monthly_rent 1500.0 100.0 50.0) 1650.0))

  (test zero_costs
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "GAS_COST" "0.0")
      ("env.get" "INTERNET_COST" "0.0"))
    (eq? (calculate_monthly_rent 900.0 0.0 0.0) 900.0))

  (test decimal_costs
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "GAS_COST" "45.75")
      ("env.get" "INTERNET_COST" "72.25"))
    (eq? (calculate_monthly_rent 1100.0 85.50 35.50) 1339.0))
)

objective: The objective is to write a function that calculates the sum of the digits of a given non-negative integer. For example, the digit sum of 123 is 1 + 2 + 3 = 6.
solution:
(module DigitSum
  (defun calculate_digit_sum (n)
    (def total_sum 0)
    (if (= n 0) (return 0)) ; Handle the case for 0 explicitly

    (while (> n 0)
      (set total_sum (+ total_sum (math_mod n 10)))
      (set n (math_floor (/ n 10))))
    total_sum)

  (test zero_input (eq? (calculate_digit_sum 0) 0))
  (test single_digit (eq? (calculate_digit_sum 7) 7))
  (test two_digits (eq? (calculate_digit_sum 25) 7)) ; 2 + 5 = 7
  (test three_digits (eq? (calculate_digit_sum 123) 6)) ; 1 + 2 + 3 = 6
  (test large_number (eq? (calculate_digit_sum 9876) 30)) ; 9 + 8 + 7 + 6 = 30
  (test number_with_zeros (eq? (calculate_digit_sum 505) 10)) ; 5 + 0 + 5 = 10
  (test power_of_ten (eq? (calculate_digit_sum 1000) 1))) ; 1 + 0 + 0 + 0 = 1

objective: Implement functions to convert temperatures between Celsius, Fahrenheit, and Kelvin scales. Your module should include functions for converting from Celsius to Kelvin, Fahrenheit to Kelvin, Kelvin to Celsius, and Kelvin to Fahrenheit.
solution:
(module KelvinConversions
  (defun celsius_to_kelvin (celsius)
    (+ celsius 273.15))

  (defun fahrenheit_to_kelvin (fahrenheit)
    (+ (* (- fahrenheit 32.0) (/ 5.0 9.0)) 273.15))

  (defun kelvin_to_celsius (kelvin)
    (- kelvin 273.15))

  (defun kelvin_to_fahrenheit (kelvin)
    (+ (* (- kelvin 273.15) (/ 9.0 5.0)) 32.0))

  (test celsius_zero_to_kelvin
    (near? (celsius_to_kelvin 0.0) 273.15 0.001))
  (test celsius_absolute_zero_to_kelvin
    (near? (celsius_to_kelvin -273.15) 0.0 0.001))

  (test fahrenheit_freezing_to_kelvin
    (near? (fahrenheit_to_kelvin 32.0) 273.15 0.001))
  (test fahrenheit_absolute_zero_to_kelvin
    (near? (fahrenheit_to_kelvin -459.67) 0.0 0.001))

  (test kelvin_to_celsius_freezing
    (near? (kelvin_to_celsius 273.15) 0.0 0.001))

  (test kelvin_to_fahrenheit_boiling
    (near? (kelvin_to_fahrenheit 373.15) 212.0 0.001))
  (test kelvin_to_fahrenheit_absolute_zero
    (near? (kelvin_to_fahrenheit 0.0) -459.67 0.001)))

objective: Calculate the total fuel cost for a trip given the distance, the vehicle's fuel efficiency in miles per gallon, and the current price of fuel per gallon.
solution:
(module FuelTripCost
  (defun calculate_gallons_needed (distance mpg)
    (/ distance mpg))

  (defun calculate_total_cost (gallons price_per_gallon)
    (* gallons price_per_gallon))

  (defun calculate_trip_fuel_cost (distance mpg price_per_gallon)
    (def gallons_required (calculate_gallons_needed distance mpg))
    (calculate_total_cost gallons_required price_per_gallon))

  (test standard_trip

topic: read file and count char occurrences
objective: Implement a function that reads the content of a file and counts the total occurrences of a specified character within that file. The character matching should be case-sensitive.
solution:
(module CharacterCounter
  (extern defun file_read (path) @capability "file.read")

  (defun count_char_in_file (filepath target_char)
    (def content (file_read filepath))
    (def n (str_length content))
    (def i 0)
    (def count 0)
    (while (< i n)
      (def current_char (str_substring content i 1))
      (if (str_eq current_char target_char)
        (set count (+ count 1)))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    count)

  (test empty_file
    (mocks (file.read "/empty.txt" ""))
    (eq? (count_char_in_file "/empty.txt" "a") 0))

  (test no_occurrences
    (mocks (file.read "/text.txt" "hello world"))
    (eq? (count_char_in_file "/text.txt" "x") 0))

  (test single_char_multiple_occurrences
    (mocks (file.read "/data.txt" "banana"))
    (eq? (count_char_in_file "/data.txt" "a") 3))

  (test case_sensitive_count
    (mocks (file.read "/document.txt" "Apple Pie"))
    (eq? (count_char_in_file "/document.txt" "p") 2))

  (test special_char_count
    (mocks (file.read "/symbols.txt" "hello, world! How are you?"))
    (eq? (count_char_in_file "/symbols.txt" "?") 1))

  (test target_char_at_edges
    (mocks (file.read "/edges.txt" "ababab"))
    (eq? (count_char_in_file "/edges.txt" "a") 3)))

objective: Write a function `repeat_string` that takes a string `s` and a non-negative integer `n`. The function should return a new string that consists of `s` concatenated with itself `n` times. If `n` is 0, an empty string should be returned.
solution:
(module StringRepeater
  (defun repeat_string (s n)
    (def result "")
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i n)
      (set result (str_concat result s))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    result)

  (test empty_string_zero_repeats (eq? (repeat_string "" 0) ""))
  (test hello_one_repeat (eq? (repeat_string "hello" 1) "hello"))
  (test ab_three_repeats (eq? (repeat_string "ab" 3) "ababab"))
  (test empty_string_five_repeats (eq? (repeat_string "" 5) ""))
  (test xyz_zero_repeats (eq? (repeat_string "xyz" 0) ""))
  (test single_char_two_repeats (eq? (repeat_string "a" 2) "aa"))
)

objective: Write a program that calculates the total monthly cost for a rental property. The cost includes base rent, a utility charge (which can be a fixed amount or a percentage of the base rent, with fixed taking precedence), and optional pet and parking fees.
solution:
(module MonthlyRentalCost
  ;; Helper function to determine the utility cost.
  ;; If fixed_utility is positive, it's used. Otherwise, utility_pct of base_rent is used.
  (defun calculate_utility_cost (base_rent fixed_utility utility_pct)
    (if (> fixed_utility 0)
      fixed_utility
      (* base_rent (/ utility_pct 100.0))))

  ;; Main function to calculate the total monthly rental cost.
  ;; All fees should be passed as 0 if not applicable.
  (defun calculate_total_monthly_cost (base_rent fixed_utility utility_pct pet_fee parking_fee)
    (def utilities (calculate_utility_cost base_rent fixed_utility utility_pct))
    (def total_fees (+ pet_fee parking_fee))
    (+ base_rent utilities total_fees))

  (test base_rent_only
    (eq? (calculate_total_monthly_cost 1000 0 0 0 0) 1000))

  (test fixed_utilities_only
    (eq? (calculate_total_monthly_cost 1000 150 0 0 0) 1150))

  (test percentage_utilities_only
    (eq? (calculate_total_monthly_cost 1000 0 10 0 0) 1100))

  (test fixed_utilities_precedence
    (eq? (calculate_total_monthly_cost 1000 200 15 0 0) 1200)) ; Fixed utility (200) takes precedence over 15% of 1000 (150)

  (test with_all_fees
    (eq? (calculate_total_monthly_cost 1200 0 12 50 75) 1469)) ; 1200 + (1200 * 0.12) + 50 + 75 = 1200 + 144 + 50 + 75 = 1469

  (test mixed_fixed_and_optional_fees
    (eq? (calculate_total_monthly_cost 1500 250 8 100 0) 1850)) ; 1500 + 250 (fixed) + 100 (pet) + 0 (parking) = 1850

  (test zero_percent_utilities_and_fees
    (eq? (calculate_total_monthly_cost 900 0 0 0 0) 900))
)

objective: Implement a function `get_env_flag` that reads an environment variable and interprets its string value as a boolean. The function should return `true` for "true", "1", or "yes" (case-insensitive), and `false` for any other value, including "false", "0", "no", or unrecognized strings. If the environment variable is not set (empty string), a provided default boolean value should be returned.
solution:
(module EnvFlag
  (extern defun env_read (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun is_truthy_string (s)
    (def lower_s (str_lower s))
    (if (str_eq lower_s "true") (return true))
    (if (str_eq lower_s "1") (return true))
    (if (str_eq lower_s "yes") (return true))
    false)

  (defun get_env_flag (key default_value)
    (def val (env_read key))
    (if (str_eq val "")
      (return default_value))
    (is_truthy_string val))

  (test present_true
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_ENABLED" "true"))
    (eq? (get_env_flag "FEATURE_ENABLED" false) true))

  (test present_yes_case_insensitive
    (mocks (env.get "DEBUG_MODE" "YeS"))
    (eq? (get_env_flag "DEBUG_MODE" false) true))

  (test present_false
    (mocks (env.get "ANALYTICS_ON" "false"))
    (eq? (get_env_flag "ANALYTICS_ON" true) false))

  (test present_zero
    (mocks (env.get "VERBOSE_LOGS" "0"))
    (eq? (get_env_flag "VERBOSE_LOGS" true) false))

  (test present_other_string
    (mocks (env.get "BETA_TESTER" "maybe"))
    (eq? (get_env_flag "BETA_TESTER" true) false))

  (test missing_uses_default_true
    (mocks (env.get "MAINTENANCE_MODE" ""))
    (eq? (get_env_flag "MAINTENANCE_MODE" true) true))

  (test missing_uses_default_false
    (mocks (env.get "DEV_SERVER" ""))
    (eq? (get_env_flag "DEV_SERVER" false) false)))

topic: shipping cost by weight and zone
objective: Implement a shipping cost calculator that determines the total cost based on the package weight and the destination zone. The calculation involves different base rates and per-kilogram rates for each zone, with one zone featuring tiered pricing for weight.
solution:
(module ShippingCostCalculator
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))

  (defun calculate_zone_base_cost (zone)
    (if (str_eq zone "A") (return 2.0))
    (if (str_eq zone "B") (return 3.0))
    (if (str_eq zone "C") (return 5.0))
    0.0) ; Default for unknown zones

  (defun calculate_zone_weight_cost (weight zone)
    (def cost 0.0)
    (if (str_eq zone "A")
      (set cost (* weight 0.5)))
    (if (str_eq zone "B")
      (if (<= weight 10.0)
        (set cost (* weight 0.75))
        (set cost (+ (* 10.0 0.75) (* (- weight 10.0) 0.60)))))
    (if (str_eq zone "C")
      (set cost (* weight 1.0)))
    cost)

  (defun calculate_shipping_cost (weight zone)
    (def base_c (calculate_zone_base_cost zone))
    (def weight_c (calculate_zone_weight_cost weight zone))
    (round2 (+ base_c weight_c)))

  (test zone_A_light
    (near? (calculate_shipping_cost 5 "A") 4.5 0.01)) ; 2.0 + (5 * 0.5) = 4.5

  (test zone_B_under_tier
    (near? (calculate_shipping_cost 8 "B") 9.0 0.01)) ; 3.0 + (8 * 0.75) = 9.0

  (test zone_B_over_tier
    (near? (calculate_shipping_cost 12 "B") 11.7 0.01)) ; 3.0 + (10 * 0.75) + (2 * 0.60) = 3.0 + 7.5 + 1.2 = 11.7

  (test zone_C_heavy
    (near? (calculate_shipping_cost 20 "C") 25.0 0.01)) ; 5.0 + (20 * 1.0) = 25.0

  (test zone_A_zero_weight
    (near? (calculate_shipping_cost 0 "A") 2.0 0.01)) ; 2.0 + (0 * 0.5) = 2.0 (base cost applied)

  (test unknown_zone
    (near? (calculate_shipping_cost 5 "D") 0.0 0.01))) ; Unknown zone returns 0.0
)

objective: Write an AGC function that calculates the length of a given string without using the `str_length` standard library function. The function should count characters by iteratively extracting single-character substrings until an empty string is returned, indicating the end of the input string.
solution:
(module StringLengthManual
  (defun string_length_manual (s)
    (def len 0)
    (def current_char_substring (str_substring s len 1))
    (while (not (str_eq current_char_substring ""))
      (set len (+ len 1))
      (set current_char_substring (str_substring s len 1)))
    len)

  (test empty_string (eq? (string_length_manual "") 0))
  (test single_char (eq? (string_length_manual "a") 1))

objective: Write a function `calculate_percentage_change` that computes the percentage change from an `old_value` to a `new_value`. If both values are zero, the percentage change is 0.0. If only the `old_value` is zero, the function should return 999999.0 to indicate an undefined/infinite change. Otherwise, the calculation is `((new_value - old_value) / old_value) * 100`.
solution:
(module PercentageChange
  (defun calculate_percentage_change (old_value new_value)
    (if (= old_value 0.0)
      (if (= new_value 0.0)
        (return 0.0)
        (return 999999.0))) ; Indicative large value for infinite change
    (* (/ (- new_value old_value) old_value) 100.0))

  (test increase
    (near? (calculate_percentage_change 10.0 12.0) 20.0 0.001))

  (test decrease
    (near? (calculate_percentage_change 100.0 75.0) -25.0 0.001))

  (test no_change
    (near? (calculate_percentage_change 50.0 50.0) 0.0 0.001))

  (test zero_to_zero
    (near? (calculate_percentage_change 0.0 0.0) 0.0 0.001))

  (test zero_to_non_zero
    (near? (calculate_percentage_change 0.0 10.0) 999999.0 0.001))

  (test negative_to_less_negative
    (near? (calculate_percentage_change -10.0 -5.0) -50.0 0.001))

  (test negative_to_positive
    (near? (calculate_percentage_change -10.0 10.0) -200.0 0.001)))

objective: Write a function that calculates the sum of all even numbers present in a given array of integers.
solution:
(module EvenArraySum
  (defun sum_even_numbers (numbers)
    (def total 0)
    (def i 0)
    (def n (arr_length numbers))
    (while (< i n)
      (def current_num (arr_get numbers i))
      (if (= (math_mod current_num 2) 0)
        (set total (+ total current_num)))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    total)

  (test empty_array
    (eq? (sum_even_numbers (arr_new 0)) 0))

  (test all_odd
    (def arr (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set arr 0 1)
    (arr_set arr 1 3)
    (arr_set arr 2 5)
    (eq? (sum_even_numbers arr) 0))

  (test all_even
    (def arr (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set arr 0 2)
    (arr_set arr 1 4)
    (arr_set arr 2 6)
    (eq? (sum_even_numbers arr) 12))

  (test mixed_numbers
    (def arr (arr_new 5))
    (arr_set arr 0 1)
    (arr_set arr 1 2)
    (arr_set arr 2 3)
    (arr_set arr 3 4)
    (arr_set arr 4 5)
    (eq? (sum_even_numbers arr) 6))

  (test negative_evens
    (def arr (arr_new 4))
    (arr_set arr 0 -2)
    (arr_set arr 1 1)
    (arr_set arr 2 -4)
    (arr_set arr 3 3)
    (eq? (sum_even_numbers arr) -6))

  (test includes_zero
    (def arr (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set arr 0 0)
    (arr_set arr 1 10)
    (arr_set arr 2 5)
    (eq? (sum_even_numbers arr) 10)))

objective: Write an AGC module to calculate a person's Body Mass Index (BMI) and classify it into categories like Underweight, Normal weight, Overweight, or Obesity. The module should take weight in kilograms and height in centimeters, then return the appropriate classification string.
solution:
(module BmiClassifier
  (defun calculate_bmi (weight_kg height_cm)
    (def height_m (/ height_cm 100.0))
    (/ weight_kg (* height_m height_m)))

  (defun classify_bmi (bmi)
    (if (< bmi 18.5) (return "Underweight"))
    (if (< bmi 25.0) (return "Normal weight"))
    (if (< bmi 30.0) (return "Overweight"))
    "Obesity")

  (defun get_bmi_classification (weight_kg height_cm)
    (def bmi_val (calculate_bmi weight_kg height_cm))
    (classify_bmi bmi_val))

  (test underweight
    (eq? (get_bmi_classification 50 170) "Underweight")) ; BMI approx 17.3

  (test normal_weight
    (eq? (get_bmi_classification 70 175) "Normal weight")) ; BMI approx 22.86

  (test overweight
    (eq? (get_bmi_classification 85 170) "Overweight")) ; BMI approx 29.41

  (test obesity
    (eq? (get_bmi_classification 100 170) "Obesity")) ; BMI approx 34.6

  (test edge_normal_lower
    (eq? (get_bmi_classification 53.465 170) "Normal weight")) ; BMI = 18.5 exactly

  (test edge_overweight_lower
    (eq? (get_bmi_classification 72.25 170) "Overweight")) ; BMI = 25.0 exactly

  (test edge_obesity_lower
    (eq? (get_bmi_classification 86.7 170) "Obesity")) ; BMI = 30.0 exactly
)

objective: Implement a function that takes an array of numbers and a constant value. The function should return a new array where each element is the result of subtracting the constant from the corresponding element in the original array.
solution:
(module ArraySubtractConstant
  (defun subtract_constant_from_array (numbers constant)
    (def n (arr_length numbers))
    (def result (arr_new n))
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i n)
      (arr_set result i (- (arr_get numbers i) constant))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    result)

  ; Helper functions to create arrays for tests
  (defun make_arr_1 (a)
    (def xs (arr_new 1))
    (arr_set xs 0 a)
    xs)

  (defun make_arr_2 (a b)
    (def xs (arr_new 2))
    (arr_set xs 0 a)
    (arr_set xs 1 b)
    xs)

  (defun make_arr_3 (a b c)
    (def xs (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set xs 0 a)
    (arr_set xs 1 b)
    (arr_set xs 2 c)
    xs)

  (test empty_array
    (eq?
      (subtract_constant_from_array (arr_new 0) 5)
      (arr_new 0)))

  (test positive_numbers_positive_constant
    (eq?
      (subtract_constant_from_array (make_arr_3 10 20 30) 5)
      (make_arr_3 5 15 25)))

  (test negative_numbers_positive_constant
    (eq?
      (subtract_constant_from_array (make_arr_2 -10 -20) 5)
      (make_arr_2 -15 -25)))

  (test mixed_numbers_negative_constant
    (eq?
      (subtract_constant_from_array (make_arr_3 10 -5 0) -2)
      (make_arr_3 12 -3 2)))

  (test zero_constant
    (eq?
      (subtract_constant_from_array (make_arr_2 1 2) 0)
      (make_arr_2 1 2)))

  (test single_element
    (eq?
      (subtract_constant_from_array (make_arr_1 7) 3)
      (make_arr_1 4)))
)

objective: Implement a function that calculates the digital root of a non-negative integer. The digital root is the single-digit value obtained by an iterative process of summing digits, where each iteration uses the result from the previous iteration, until a single-digit number is reached.
solution:
(module DigitalRoot
  (defun sum_digits (n)
    (def acc 0)
    (def cur n)
    (while (> cur 0)
      (set acc (+ acc (math_mod cur 10)))
      (set cur (math_floor (/ cur 10))))
    acc)

  (defun digital_root (n)
    (def current_num n)
    (while (> current_num 9)
      (set current_num (sum_digits current_num)))
    current_num)

  (test zero      (eq? (digital_root 0) 0))
  (test single_digit (eq? (digital_root 7) 7))
  (test two_digits_once (eq? (digital_root 16) 7)) ; 1 + 6 = 7
  (test two_steps   (eq? (digital_root 942) 6))   ; 9 + 4 + 2 = 15; 1 + 5 = 6
  (test large_number (eq? (digital_root 12345) 6)) ; 1+2+3+4+5 = 15; 1+5 = 6
  (test very_large_number (eq? (digital_root 987654321) 9)) ; 9+8+7+6+5+4+3+2+1 = 45; 4+5 = 9
)

objective: Calculate the digital root of a non-negative integer. The digital root is the single-digit value obtained by an iterative process of summing digits, on each iteration using the result from the previous iteration as the input to the next, until a single-digit number is produced.
solution:
(module DigitalRoot
  (defun digit_sum (n)
    (def sum 0)
    (def current_num n)
    (while (> current_num 0)
      (set sum (+ sum (math_mod current_num 10)))
      (set current_num (math_floor (/ current_num 10))))
    sum)

  (defun digital_root (n)
    (def root_val n)
    (while (> root_val 9)
      (set root_val (digit_sum root_val)))
    root_val)

  (test zero       (eq? (digital_root 0) 0))
  (test single_digit (eq? (digital_root 7) 7))
  (test ten        (eq? (digital_root 10) 1))
  (test forty_two  (eq? (digital_root 42) 6))
  (test large_num  (eq? (digital_root 12345) 6))
  (test another_large_num (eq? (digital_root 987654321) 9)))

objective: Calculate the total monthly phone bill for a customer, including a base plan cost and any overage charges for minutes used beyond the plan's included allowance. The final bill should be rounded to two decimal places.

(module PhonePlanCost
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))

  (defun calculate_overage_minutes (used_minutes included_minutes)
    (if (> used_minutes included_minutes)
      (- used_minutes included_minutes)
      0))

  (defun calculate_overage_cost (overage_minutes rate_per_minute)
    (* overage_minutes rate_per_minute))

  (defun calculate_total_bill (base_plan_cost included_minutes overage_rate_per_minute actual_minutes_used)

    (round2 (+ base_plan_cost overage_c)))

objective: Write a function `reverse_array` that takes an array of numbers and returns a new array with the elements in reverse order. The original array should not be modified.
solution:
(module ReverseArray
  (defun reverse_array (arr)
    (def len (arr_length arr))
    (def reversed_arr (arr_new len))
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i len)
      (def original_val (arr_get arr i))
      (arr_set reversed_arr (- (- len 1) i) original_val)
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    reversed_arr)

  (test empty_array
    (eq? (reverse_array (arr_new 0)) (arr_new 0)))

  (test single_element
    (eq? (reverse_array (arr_new 1 5)) (arr_new 1 5)))

  (test two_elements
    (eq? (reverse_array (arr_new 2 10 20)) (arr_new 2 20 10)))

  (test multiple_elements
    (eq? (reverse_array (arr_new 4 1 2 3 4)) (arr_new 4 4 3 2 1)))

  (test mixed_numbers
    (eq? (reverse_array (arr_new 5 0 -1 100 50 -5)) (arr_new 5 -5 50 100 -1 0)))

  (test larger_array
    (eq? (reverse_array (arr_new 6 11 22 33 44 55 66)) (arr_new 6 66 55 44 33 22 11)))
)

objective: Implement a function to calculate the factorial of a given non-negative integer. The factorial of a non-negative integer `n`, denoted by `n!`, is the product of all positive integers less than or equal to `n`, with `0!` defined as 1.
solution:
(module Factorial
  (defun factorial (n)
    (if (< n 0) (return 0)) ; Factorial for negative numbers is undefined, returning 0 as an error indicator.
    (if (<= n 1) (return 1)) ; Base cases: 0! = 1, 1! = 1

    (def result 1)
    (def i 2)
    (while (<= i n)
      (set result (* result i))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    result)

  (test factorial_of_0 (eq? (factorial 0) 1))
  (test factorial_of_1 (eq? (factorial 1) 1))
  (test factorial_of_2 (eq? (factorial 2) 2))
  (test factorial_of_3 (eq? (factorial 3) 6))
  (test factorial_of_5 (eq? (factorial 5) 120))
  (test factorial_of_7 (eq? (factorial 7) 5040))
  (test factorial_of_negative (eq? (factorial -1) 0)))

objective: Implement a function to calculate the minimum payment due on a credit card balance. The minimum payment is defined as the greater of a specified percentage of the outstanding balance or a fixed minimum dollar amount, but it can never exceed the total outstanding balance itself.
solution:
(module CreditCardMinimumPayment
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))

  (defun calculate_percentage_payment (balance percentage_rate)
    (* balance (/ percentage_rate 100.0)))

  (defun calculate_minimum_payment (balance percentage_rate fixed_minimum)
    (def percent_payment (calculate_percentage_payment balance percentage_rate))
    (def initial_min_payment (math_max percent_payment fixed_minimum))
    (round2 (math_min initial_min_payment balance)))

  (test case_1_percent_is_higher
    (near? (calculate_minimum_payment 1000 5 25) 50.00 0.01)) ; 1000 * 0.05 = 50. Max(50, 25) = 50. Min(50, 1000) = 50.

    (near? (calculate_minimum_payment 1000 2 25) 25.00 0.01)) ; 1000 * 0.02 = 20. Max(20, 25) = 25. Min(25, 1000) = 25.

  (test case_3_balance_is_less_than_fixed
    (near? (calculate_minimum_payment 10 2 25) 10.00 0.01)) ; 10 * 0.02 = 0.2. Max(0.2, 25) = 25. Min(25, 10) = 10.

  (test case_4_balance_is_less_than_percentage
    (near? (calculate_minimum_payment 40 10 2) 4.00 0.01)) ; 40 * 0.10 = 4. Max(4, 2) = 4. Min(4, 40) = 4.

  (test case_5_zero_balance
    (near? (calculate_minimum_payment 0 5 25) 0.00 0.01)) ; 0 * 0.05 = 0. Max(0, 25) = 25. Min(25, 0) = 0.

  (test case_6_large_balance_and_fixed
    (near? (calculate_minimum_payment 5000 1 50) 50.00 0.01)) ; 5000 * 0.01 = 50. Max(50, 50) = 50. Min(50, 5000) = 50.
)

objective: Implement a function that calculates the power of an integer base raised to a non-negative integer exponent. The function should correctly handle positive, negative, and zero bases, as well as zero and positive exponents.
solution:
(module IntegerPower
  (defun integer_power (base exponent)
    ; Handle negative exponents by returning 0, as this problem focuses on non-negative integer powers.
    (if (< exponent 0) (return 0))

    ; Special case for 0^0, typically defined as 1.
    (if (and (= base 0) (= exponent 0))
        (return 1))

    ; Special case for 0 raised to any positive power, which is 0.
    (if (= base 0)
        (return 0))

    ; Any non-zero base raised to the power of 0 is 1.
    (if (= exponent 0)
        (return 1))

    ; General case for positive exponents
    (def result 1)
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i exponent)
      (set result (* result base))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    result)

  (test two_to_the_third (eq? (integer_power 2 3) 8))
  (test five_to_the_zero (eq? (integer_power 5 0) 1))
  (test zero_to_the_fourth (eq? (integer_power 0 4) 0))
  (test zero_to_the_zero (eq? (integer_power 0 0) 1))
  (test negative_two_to_the_third (eq? (integer_power -2 3) -8))
  (test negative_three_to_the_second (eq? (integer_power -3 2) 9))
  (test ten_to_the_first (eq? (integer_power 10 1) 10)))

objective: Implement a function that finds the median of three given numbers. The median is the middle value when the numbers are sorted in ascending order.

    (def max_val (math_max a (math_max b c)))
    (- (+ a b c) min_val max_val))

  (test reverse_sorted (eq? (median_of_three 3 2 1) 2))

  (test duplicates (eq? (median_of_three 5 1 5) 5))
  (test all_same (eq? (median_of_three 7 7 7) 7))
  (test negatives (eq? (median_of_three -1 -5 -3) -3))
  (test zero_and_pos_neg (eq? (median_of_three 0 5 -2) 0)))

objective: Implement a function that retrieves an environment variable. It first attempts to find the variable using the provided key. If the value is an empty string (indicating not found or unset), it tries again using the uppercased version of the key. If neither attempt yields a non-empty value, a specified default value is returned.
solution:
(module EnvVarCaseInsensitive
  (extern defun env_read (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun get_env_case_insensitive (key default_value)
    (def val (env_read key))
    (if (str_eq val "")
      (set val (env_read (str_upper key))))
    (if (str_eq val "")
      (return default_value))
    val)

  (test original_key_present
    (mocks (env.get "app_name" "MyWebApp"))
    (eq? (get_env_case_insensitive "app_name" "DefaultApp") "MyWebApp"))

  (test uppercased_key_present
    (mocks (env.get "app_name" "")
           (env.get "APP_NAME" "MyWebApp"))
    (eq? (get_env_case_insensitive "app_name" "DefaultApp") "MyWebApp"))

  (test both_keys_missing
    (mocks (env.get "app_name" "")
           (env.get "APP_NAME" ""))
    (eq? (get_env_case_insensitive "app_name" "DefaultApp") "DefaultApp"))

  (test already_uppercase_key_present
    (mocks (env.get "PORT" "8080"))
    (eq? (get_env_case_insensitive "PORT" "3000") "8080"))

  (test already_uppercase_key_missing
    (mocks (env.get "PORT" "")
           (env.get "PORT" ""))
    (eq? (get_env_case_insensitive "PORT" "3000") "3000"))

  (test original_key_empty_value_uppercased_present
    (mocks (env.get "LOG_LEVEL" "")
           (env.get "LOG_LEVEL" "DEBUG"))
    (eq? (get_env_case_insensitive "LOG_LEVEL" "INFO") "DEBUG"))

  (test original_key_and_uppercased_key_empty_values
    (mocks (env.get "LOG_LEVEL" "")
           (env.get "LOG_LEVEL" ""))
    (eq? (get_env_case_insensitive "LOG_LEVEL" "INFO") "INFO"))
)

objective: Calculate the factorial of a given non-negative integer. For negative inputs, the function should return 0.

(module FactorialCalculator
  (defun calculate_factorial (n)
    (if (< n 0) (return 0)) ; Factorial is not defined for negative numbers, return 0.
    (if (= n 0) (return 1)) ; Base case: 0! = 1

    (def result 1)
    (def i 1)
    (while (<= i n)
      (set result (* result i))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    result)

  (test factorial_zero (eq? (calculate_factorial 0) 1))

  (test factorial_medium_positive (eq? (calculate_factorial 5) 120))
  (test factorial_larger_positive (eq? (calculate_factorial 7) 5040)))

objective: Implement a function that performs a binary search on a sorted array of numbers. The function should take the array and a target value as input, returning the index of the target if found, or -1 if the target is not present in the array.
solution:
(module BinarySearchArray
  (defun binary_search (arr target)
    (def low 0)
    (def high (- (arr_length arr) 1))

    (while (<= low high)
      (def mid (math_floor (/ (+ low high) 2)))
      (def val_at_mid (arr_get arr mid))

      (if (= val_at_mid target)
        (return mid))
      
      (if (< val_at_mid target)
        (set low (+ mid 1))
        (set high (- mid 1))))
    
    -1)

  (test found_middle
    (def arr (arr_new 5))
    (arr_set arr 0 10)
    (arr_set arr 1 20)
    (arr_set arr 2 30)
    (arr_set arr 3 40)
    (arr_set arr 4 50)
    (eq? (binary_search arr 30) 2))

  (test found_start
    (def arr (arr_new 5))
    (arr_set arr 0 10)
    (arr_set arr 1 20)
    (arr_set arr 2 30)
    (arr_set arr 3 40)
    (arr_set arr 4 50)
    (eq? (binary_search arr 10) 0))

  (test found_end
    (def arr (arr_new 5))
    (arr_set arr 0 10)
    (arr_set arr 1 20)
    (arr_set arr 2 30)
    (arr_set arr 3 40)
    (arr_set arr 4 50)
    (eq? (binary_search arr 50) 4))

  (test not_found_too_small
    (def arr (arr_new 5))
    (arr_set arr 0 10)
    (arr_set arr 1 20)
    (arr_set arr 2 30)
    (arr_set arr 3 40)
    (arr_set arr 4 50)
    (eq? (binary_search arr 5) -1))

  (test not_found_in_between
    (def arr (arr_new 4))
    (arr_set arr 0 10)
    (arr_set arr 1 20)
    (arr_set arr 2 40)
    (arr_set arr 3 50)
    (eq? (binary_search arr 30) -1))

  (test empty_array
    (def arr (arr_new 0))
    (eq? (binary_search arr 10) -1))

  (test single_element_found
    (def arr (arr_new 1))
    (arr_set arr 0 7)
    (eq? (binary_search arr 7) 0))

  (test single_element_not_found
    (def arr (arr_new 1))
    (arr_set arr 0 7)
    (eq? (binary_search arr 8) -1))
)

topic: read file and count char occurrences
objective: Create a function that reads the content of a specified file and counts the occurrences of a given character within that content, returning the total count. The character comparison should be case-sensitive.
solution:
(module CharCounter
  (extern defun file_read_content (filename) @capability "file.read")

  (defun count_char_in_file (filename target_char)
    (def content (file_read_content filename))
    (def count 0)
    (def i 0)
    (def len (str_length content))
    (while (< i len)
      (def current_char (str_substring content i 1))
      (if (str_eq current_char target_char)
        (set count (+ count 1)))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    count)

  (test basic_count
    (mocks (file.read "test.txt" "hello world"))
    (eq? (count_char_in_file "test.txt" "l") 3))

  (test no_occurrence
    (mocks (file.read "data.txt" "programming"))
    (eq? (count_char_in_file "data.txt" "x") 0))

  (test case_sensitive
    (mocks (file.read "document.txt" "Apple Pie"))
    (eq? (count_char_in_file "document.txt" "p") 2)) ; lowercase 'p' only

  (test empty_file
    (mocks (file.read "empty.txt" ""))
    (eq? (count_char_in_file "empty.txt" "a") 0))

  (test single_char_file
    (mocks (file.read "char.txt" "Z"))
    (eq? (count_char_in_file "char.txt" "Z") 1))
)

objective: Calculate the area of a triangle given its three side lengths using Heron's formula. The function should take three numbers representing the side lengths and return the calculated area.
solution:
(module TriangleArea
  (defun triangle_area_heron (a b c)
    (def s (/ (+ a b c) 2.0))
    (def term_a (- s a))
    (def term_b (- s b))
    (def term_c (- s c))
    (math_sqrt (* s term_a term_b term_c)))

  (test right_triangle
    (near? (triangle_area_heron 3 4 5) 6.0 0.0001))

  (test equilateral_triangle
    (near? (triangle_area_heron 3 3 3) 3.897114317 0.0001))

  (test isosceles_triangle

topic: read two env vars and concat
objective: Generate a function that reads two environment variables, `GREETING_PREFIX` and `GREETING_SUFFIX`, and concatenates their values. If `GREETING_PREFIX` is empty, use "Hi" as a default. If `GREETING_SUFFIX` is empty, use " everyone!" as a default.
solution:
(module GreetingGenerator
  (extern defun env_read (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun get_greeting ()
    (def prefix_val (env_read "GREETING_PREFIX"))
    (def suffix_val (env_read "GREETING_SUFFIX"))

    (def actual_prefix
      (if (str_eq prefix_val "")
          "Hi"
          prefix_val))

    (def actual_suffix
      (if (str_eq suffix_val "")
          " everyone!"
          suffix_val))

    (str_concat actual_prefix actual_suffix))

  (test both_present
    (mocks (env.get "GREETING_PREFIX" "Hello")
           (env.get "GREETING_SUFFIX" " World!"))
    (eq? (get_greeting) "Hello World!"))

  (test prefix_missing
    (mocks (env.get "GREETING_PREFIX" "")
           (env.get "GREETING_SUFFIX" " there!"))
    (eq? (get_greeting) "Hi there!"))

  (test suffix_missing
    (mocks (env.get "GREETING_PREFIX" "Good morning")
           (env.get "GREETING_SUFFIX" ""))
    (eq? (get_greeting) "Good morning everyone!"))

  (test both_missing
    (mocks (env.get "GREETING_PREFIX" "")
           (env.get "GREETING_SUFFIX" ""))
    (eq? (get_greeting) "Hi everyone!"))

  (test custom_prefix_empty_suffix
    (mocks (env.get "GREETING_PREFIX" "Greetings")
           (env.get "GREETING_SUFFIX" ""))
    (eq? (get_greeting) "Greetings everyone!"))

  (test custom_suffix_empty_prefix
    (mocks (env.get "GREETING_PREFIX" "")
           (env.get "GREETING_SUFFIX" " folks!"))
    (eq? (get_greeting) "Hi folks!"))

  (test all_empty_strings
    (mocks (env.get "GREETING_PREFIX" "")
           (env.get "GREETING_SUFFIX" ""))
    (eq? (get_greeting) "Hi everyone!")))

objective: Implement a function `sign` that takes a number `n` and returns `1` if `n` is positive, `-1` if `n` is negative, and `0` if `n` is zero.
solution:
(module SignFunction
  (defun sign (n)
    (if (> n 0)
      (return 1))
    (if (< n 0)
      (return -1))
    0) ; If n is not > 0 and not < 0, it must be 0.

  (test positive_five (eq? (sign 5) 1))
  (test negative_ten (eq? (sign -10) -1))
  (test zero (eq? (sign 0) 0))
  (test one (eq? (sign 1) 1))
  (test negative_one (eq? (sign -1) -1))
  (test float_positive (eq? (sign 3.14) 1))
  (test float_negative (eq? (sign -0.001) -1)))

objective: Implement a function `get_http_status_message` that takes a URL, fetches its HTTP status code using an external capability, and returns a human-readable message based on the status. It should recognize "200" for success, "404" for not found, "500" for internal server error, and provide a generic message for other codes.
solution:
(module HttpStatusChecker
  (extern defun http_fetch_status (url) @capability "http.fetch")

  (defun get_http_status_message (url)
    (def status_str (http_fetch_status url))
    (if (str_eq status_str "200") (return "OK"))
    (if (str_eq status_str "404") (return "Not Found"))
    (if (str_eq status_str "500") (return "Internal Server Error"))
    (str_concat "Unexpected Status: " status_str))

  (test success_status
    (mocks (http.fetch "http://example.com/data" "200"))
    (eq? (get_http_status_message "http://example.com/data") "OK"))

  (test not_found_status
    (mocks (http.fetch "http://example.com/missing" "404"))
    (eq? (get_http_status_message "http://example.com/missing") "Not Found"))

  (test server_error_status
    (mocks (http.fetch "http://example.com/api" "500"))
    (eq? (get_http_status_message "http://example.com/api") "Internal Server Error"))

  (test forbidden_status
    (mocks (http.fetch "http://example.com/secure" "403"))
    (eq? (get_http_status_message "http://example.com/secure") "Unexpected Status: 403"))

  (test redirect_status
    (mocks (http.fetch "http://example.com/old-page" "301"))
    (eq? (get_http_status_message "http://example.com/old-page") "Unexpected Status: 301")))

objective: Develop a program that calculates the total monthly housing cost. This includes a base rent amount plus variable utility expenses for electricity, water, and internet, which are retrieved from environment variables.
solution:
(module MonthlyRentCalculator
  (extern defun get_env (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun parse_utility_cost (key)
    (def val_str (get_env key))
    (if (str_eq val_str "")
      0.0 ; Return 0.0 for missing or empty utility value
      (str_to_num val_str)))

  (defun calculate_total_rent (base_rent)
    (def electricity_cost (parse_utility_cost "ELECTRICITY_COST"))
    (def water_cost (parse_utility_cost "WATER_COST"))
    (def internet_cost (parse_utility_cost "INTERNET_COST"))
    (+ base_rent electricity_cost water_cost internet_cost))

  (test full_utilities_present
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "ELECTRICITY_COST" "120.50")
      ("env.get" "WATER_COST" "45.00")
      ("env.get" "INTERNET_COST" "60.00"))
    (near? (calculate_total_rent 1000.00) 1225.50 0.01))

  (test some_utilities_missing
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "ELECTRICITY_COST" "") ; Missing electricity
      ("env.get" "WATER_COST" "30.00")
      ("env.get" "INTERNET_COST" "75.00"))
    (near? (calculate_total_rent 800.00) 905.00 0.01))

  (test all_utilities_missing
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "ELECTRICITY_COST" "")
      ("env.get" "WATER_COST" "")
      ("env.get" "INTERNET_COST" ""))
    (near? (calculate_total_rent 1500.00) 1500.00 0.01))

  (test zero_base_rent_only_utilities
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "ELECTRICITY_COST" "50.00")
      ("env.get" "WATER_COST" "20.00")
      ("env.get" "INTERNET_COST" "40.00"))
    (near? (calculate_total_rent 0.00) 110.00 0.01))

  (test all_utility_costs_are_zero
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "ELECTRICITY_COST" "0.00")
      ("env.get" "WATER_COST" "0.00")
      ("env.get" "INTERNET_COST" "0.00"))
    (near? (calculate_total_rent 900.00) 900.00 0.01))
)

objective: Create a module that scales ingredient quantities for a recipe based on a change in the number of servings. The module should calculate the new quantity and round it to two decimal places for practical use.
solution:
(module RecipeScaler
  ;; Helper function to round a number to a specified number of decimal places.
  ;; For example, (round_to_precision 3.14159 2) returns 3.14.
  (defun round_to_precision (value precision)
    (def factor (math_pow 10 precision))
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* value factor) 0.5)) factor))

  ;; Scales an ingredient quantity from an original number of servings
  ;; to a target number of servings.
  (defun scale_ingredient (original_quantity original_servings target_servings)
    (def ratio (/ target_servings original_servings))
    (def scaled_raw (* original_quantity ratio))
    (round_to_precision scaled_raw 2))

  ;; Test cases
  (test scale_up_simple
    (near? (scale_ingredient 2.0 4 8) 4.0 0.01)) ; 2 cups for 4 servings -> 4 cups for 8 servings

  (test scale_down_simple
    (near? (scale_ingredient 3.0 6 3) 1.5 0.01)) ; 3 cups for 6 servings -> 1.5 cups for 3 servings

  (test scale_fractional_up
    (near? (scale_ingredient 1.0 3 5) 1.67 0.01)) ; 1 cup for 3 servings -> 1.67 cups for 5 servings (1/3 * 5 = 1.666...)

  (test scale_fractional_down
    (near? (scale_ingredient 5.0 10 7) 3.5 0.01)) ; 5 cups for 10 servings -> 3.5 cups for 7 servings (5/10 * 7 = 3.5)

  (test scale_no_change
    (near? (scale_ingredient 2.75 4 4) 2.75 0.01)) ; 2.75 cups for 4 servings -> 2.75 cups for 4 servings

  (test scale_decimal_quantity
    (near? (scale_ingredient 0.75 2 3) 1.13 0.01)) ; 0.75 cups for 2 servings -> 1.125 -> 1.13 for 3 servings
)

objective: Create a program to calculate a student's final course grade. The final grade is determined by the weighted sum of average scores from quizzes, assignments, a midterm exam, and a final exam, with each component having a specified percentage weight.
solution:
(module CourseGradeCalculator
  (defun round_to_two_decimals (num)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* num 100.0) 0.5)) 100.0))

  (defun calculate_weighted_component (score weight_pct)
    (/ (* score weight_pct) 100.0))

  (defun calculate_final_grade (quiz_avg assignment_avg midterm_score final_score quiz_w assignment_w midterm_w final_w)
    (def weighted_quiz (calculate_weighted_component quiz_avg quiz_w))
    (def weighted_assignment (calculate_weighted_component assignment_avg assignment_w))
    (def weighted_midterm (calculate_weighted_component midterm_score midterm_w))
    (def weighted_final (calculate_weighted_component final_score final_w))
    (round_to_two_decimals (+ weighted_quiz weighted_assignment weighted_midterm weighted_final)))

  (test perfect_score
    (near? (calculate_final_grade 100 100 100 100 20 40 20 20) 100.0 0.01))

  (test average_student
    (near? (calculate_final_grade 80 90 75 85 20 40 20 20) 84.0 0.01))

  (test low_quiz_high_assignment
    (near? (calculate_final_grade 60 95 80 70 10 50 20 20) 83.5 0.01))

  (test different_weights
    (near? (calculate_final_grade 90 85 70 95 15 35 25 25) 84.5 0.01))

  (test all_zero_weights
    (near? (calculate_final_grade 90 85 70 95 0 0 0 0) 0.0 0.01))

  (test one_component_only
    (near? (calculate_final_grade 90 85 70 95 0 100 0 0) 85.0 0.01))

  (test fractional_scores
    (near? (calculate_final_grade 88.5 92.1 77.8 81.3 20 30 25 25) 85.11 0.01))

  (test another_scenario
    (near? (calculate_final_grade 70 80 90 60 25 25 25 25) 75.0 0.01)))

(module RideFareCalculator

)

objective: Implement a heart rate zone classifier that calculates a person's maximum heart rate based on age and then determines the appropriate training zone (Rest, Warm-up, Fat Burn, Aerobic, Anaerobic, Peak) for a given current heart rate.
solution:
(module HeartRateZoneClassifier
  (defun calculate_max_hr (age)
    (- 220 age))

  (defun get_zone_name (hr_percentage)
    (if (< hr_percentage 50) "Rest"
      (if (< hr_percentage 60) "Warm-up"
        (if (< hr_percentage 70) "Fat Burn"
          (if (< hr_percentage 80) "Aerobic"
            (if (< hr_percentage 90) "Anaerobic"
              "Peak"))))))

  (defun classify_heart_rate (current_hr age)
    (def max_hr (calculate_max_hr age))
    (if (<= max_hr 0) (return "Invalid Age"))
    (if (< current_hr 0) (return "Invalid HR")) ; Current HR cannot be negative

    (def hr_percentage (* (/ current_hr max_hr) 100))
    (get_zone_name hr_percentage))

  (test young_rest
    (eq? (classify_heart_rate 80 30) "Rest")) ; Max HR: 190, 80/190 = 42.1%
  
  (test young_fat_burn
    (eq? (classify_heart_rate 125 30) "Fat Burn")) ; Max HR: 190, 125/190 = 65.8%

  (test young_peak
    (eq? (classify_heart_rate 175 30) "Peak")) ; Max HR: 190, 175/190 = 92.1%

  (test older_warmup
    (eq? (classify_heart_rate 90 60) "Warm-up")) ; Max HR: 160, 90/160 = 56.25%

  (test older_anaerobic
    (eq? (classify_heart_rate 130 60) "Anaerobic")) ; Max HR: 160, 130/160 = 81.25%

  (test invalid_age
    (eq? (classify_heart_rate 100 250) "Invalid Age")) ; Max HR: -30

  (test invalid_hr
    (eq? (classify_heart_rate -10 30) "Invalid HR"))
)

objective: Create a program that calculates the final amount after exchanging an initial sum from a source currency to a target currency, applying a percentage-based transaction fee to the converted amount. All currency rates are provided as their equivalent value in a common base currency (e.g., USD).
solution:
(module CurrencyExchange
  ;; Helper to round numbers to two decimal places
  (defun round_to_2_decimals (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))

  ;; Converts an amount from source currency to target currency using base currency rates
  (defun convert_amount (amount source_rate_to_base target_rate_to_base)
    ;; amount_in_base = amount * source_rate_to_base
    ;; amount_in_target = amount_in_base / target_rate_to_base
    (/ (* amount source_rate_to_base) target_rate_to_base))

  ;; Applies a percentage fee to a given amount
  (defun apply_exchange_fee (converted_amount fee_percentage)
    (* converted_amount (- 1 (/ fee_percentage 100))))

  ;; Main function to perform currency exchange with fee
  (defun calculate_exchange (initial_amount source_rate_to_base target_rate_to_base fee_percentage)
    (def converted_val (convert_amount initial_amount source_rate_to_base target_rate_to_base))
    (def final_val_before_round (apply_exchange_fee converted_val fee_percentage))
    (round_to_2_decimals final_val_before_round))

  ;; Tests
  ;; Test 1: Simple conversion, no fee (EUR to GBP)
  ;; 100 EUR -> USD (rate 1.1) = 110 USD
  ;; 110 USD -> GBP (rate 1.3) = 84.61538 GBP
  ;; No fee, rounded = 84.62
  (test simple_no_fee
    (near? (calculate_exchange 100 1.1 1.3 0) 84.62 0.001))

  ;; Test 2: Conversion with 2% fee (EUR to GBP)
  ;; 100 EUR -> USD (rate 1.1) = 110 USD
  ;; 110 USD -> GBP (rate 1.3) = 84.61538 GBP
  ;; Apply 2% fee: 84.61538 * 0.98 = 82.92307 GBP
  ;; Rounded = 82.92
  (test eur_to_gbp_with_fee
    (near? (calculate_exchange 100 1.1 1.3 2) 82.92 0.001))

  ;; Test 3: Conversion from JPY to USD with 1.5% fee
  ;; 5000 JPY -> USD (rate 0.008) = 40 USD
  ;; 40 USD -> USD (rate 1.0) = 40 USD
  ;; Apply 1.5% fee: 40 * (1 - 0.015) = 40 * 0.985 = 39.4 USD
  ;; Rounded = 39.40
  (test jpy_to_usd_with_fee
    (near? (calculate_exchange 5000 0.008 1.0 1.5) 39.40 0.001))

  ;; Test 4: Conversion from USD to CAD with 0.5% fee
  ;; 250 USD -> USD (rate 1.0) = 250 USD
  ;; 250 USD -> CAD (rate 0.75) = 333.3333 CAD
  ;; Apply 0.5% fee: 333.3333 * (1 - 0.005) = 333.3333 * 0.995 = 331.6666 CAD
  ;; Rounded = 331.67
  (test usd_to_cad_with_fee
    (near? (calculate_exchange 250 1.0 0.75 0.5) 331.67 0.001))

  ;; Test 5: Conversion with large amount and a significant fee
  ;; 10000 AUD -> USD (rate 0.68) = 6800 USD
  ;; 6800 USD -> NZD (rate 0.62) = 10967.7419 NZD
  ;; Apply 5% fee: 10967.7419 * 0.95 = 10419.3548 NZD
  ;; Rounded = 10419.35
  (test large_amount_high_fee
    (near? (calculate_exchange 10000 0.68 0.62 5) 10419.35 0.001))
)

objective: Calculate the total cost of a gym membership, including a one-time joining fee and a monthly fee that can be subject to a percentage discount over a specified number of months.
solution:
(module GymMembershipCost
  (defun apply_monthly_discount (monthly_fee discount_pct)
    (- monthly_fee (/ (* monthly_fee discount_pct) 100)))

  (defun calculate_total_monthly_fees (monthly_fee discount_pct num_months)
    (* (apply_monthly_discount monthly_fee discount_pct) num_months))

  (defun calculate_total_membership_cost (base_monthly_fee base_joining_fee discount_pct num_months)
    (+ base_joining_fee (calculate_total_monthly_fees base_monthly_fee discount_pct num_months)))

  (test basic_one_month_no_discount
    (near? (calculate_total_membership_cost 50 100 0 1) 150 0.01))

  (test six_months_ten_percent_discount
    (near? (calculate_total_membership_cost 50 100 10 6) 370 0.01))

  (test twelve_months_no_discount
    (near? (calculate_total_membership_cost 50 100 0 12) 700 0.01))

  (test zero_months_only_joining_fee
    (near? (calculate_total_membership_cost 50 100 0 0) 100 0.01))

  (test zero_joining_fee_three_months_five_percent_discount
    (near? (calculate_total_membership_cost 50 0 5 3) 142.5 0.01)))

topic: read two env vars and concat
objective: Implement a function that constructs a unique identifier for an application by reading its `APP_NAME` and `APP_VERSION` from environment variables. The function should concatenate these two values with a hyphen in between. If an environment variable is not set (returns an empty string), use "UnknownApp" for `APP_NAME` or "UnknownVersion" for `APP_VERSION` as a default.
solution:
(module AppIdGenerator
  (extern defun env_read (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun get_app_identifier ()
    (def app_name (env_read "APP_NAME"))
    (def app_version (env_read "APP_VERSION"))

    (def final_app_name (if (str_eq app_name "") "UnknownApp" app_name))
    (def final_app_version (if (str_eq app_version "") "UnknownVersion" app_version))

    (str_concat final_app_name (str_concat "-" final_app_version)))

  (test both_present
    (mocks
      (env.get "APP_NAME" "MyService")
      (env.get "APP_VERSION" "1.0.5"))
    (eq? (get_app_identifier) "MyService-1.0.5"))

  (test app_name_missing
    (mocks
      (env.get "APP_NAME" "")
      (env.get "APP_VERSION" "2.1.0"))
    (eq? (get_app_identifier) "UnknownApp-2.1.0"))

  (test app_version_missing
    (mocks
      (env.get "APP_NAME" "DataProcessor")
      (env.get "APP_VERSION" ""))
    (eq? (get_app_identifier) "DataProcessor-UnknownVersion"))

  (test both_missing
    (mocks
      (env.get "APP_NAME" "")
      (env.get "APP_VERSION" ""))
    (eq? (get_app_identifier) "UnknownApp-UnknownVersion"))

  (test app_name_with_spaces
    (mocks
      (env.get "APP_NAME" "  My App  ")
      (env.get "APP_VERSION" "3.0"))
    (eq? (get_app_identifier) "  My App  -3.0"))
)
