objective: Write a function that fetches content from a given URL and returns the first space-separated word from the HTTP response body. If the body is empty or contains only spaces, an empty string should be returned.
solution:
(module HttpFirstWord
  (extern defun http_fetch (url) @capability "http.fetch")

  ;; Helper function to extract the first non-empty word from a string
  (defun get_first_word (s)
    (def parts (str_split s " "))
    (def i 0)
    (def n (arr_length parts))
    (while (< i n)
      (def part (arr_get parts i))
      (if (not (str_eq part "")) (return part))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    "") ; Return empty string if no non-empty word is found

  ;; Fetches content from a URL and returns the first word
  (defun get_first_word_from_url (url)
    (def body (http_fetch url))
    (get_first_word body))

  (test empty-body
    (mocks ("http.fetch" "http://example.com/api/empty" ""))
    (eq? (get_first_word_from_url "http://example.com/api/empty") ""))

  (test spaces-only
    (mocks ("http.fetch" "http://example.com/api/spaces" "   "))
    (eq? (get_first_word_from_url "http://example.com/api/spaces") ""))

  (test single-word
    (mocks ("http.fetch" "http://example.com/api/hello" "Hello"))
    (eq? (get_first_word_from_url "http://example.com/api/hello") "Hello"))

  (test multiple-words
    (mocks ("http.fetch" "http://example.com/api/sentence" "This is a sentence."))
    (eq? (get_first_word_from_url "http://example.com/api/sentence") "This"))

  (test leading-trailing-spaces
    (mocks ("http.fetch" "http://example.com/api/padded" "  Padded content here  "))
    (eq? (get_first_word_from_url "http://example.com/api/padded") "Padded"))

  (test special-characters
    (mocks ("http.fetch" "http://example.com/api/item" "Item-123/ABC is available"))
    (eq? (get_first_word_from_url "http://example.com/api/item") "Item-123/ABC"))

  (test multiple-spaces-between-words
    (mocks ("http.fetch" "http://example.com/api/gaps" "Word1   Word2"))
    (eq? (get_first_word_from_url "http://example.com/api/gaps") "Word1"))
)

objective: Write a function that parses a Roman numeral string and converts it to its corresponding integer value. The function should correctly handle both additive and subtractive notation for valid Roman numerals. Assume the input string contains only standard Roman numeral characters and represents a number between 1 and 3999.
solution:
(module RomanNumeralParser
  ; Helper function to convert a single Roman numeral character to its integer value.
  (defun roman_char_to_int (c)
    (if (str_eq c "I") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq c "V") (return 5))
    (if (str_eq c "X") (return 10))
    (if (str_eq c "L") (return 50))
    (if (str_eq c "C") (return 100))
    (if (str_eq c "D") (return 500))
    (if (str_eq c "M") (return 1000))
    0) ; Should not be reached with valid Roman numeral characters.

  ; Main function to parse a Roman numeral string and convert it to an integer.
  ; It processes the string from right to left to handle subtractive notation easily.
  (defun parse_roman (s)
    (def total 0)
    (def prev_val 0)
    (def len (str_length s))
    (if (= len 0) (return 0)) ; Handle empty string explicitly

    (def i (- len 1)) ; Start from the last character of the string

    (while (>= i 0)
      (def char_str (str_substring s i 1)) ; Extract the single character at index i
      (def current_val (roman_char_to_int char_str))

      (if (< current_val prev_val)
        (set total (- total current_val)) ; If current value is smaller than previous (e.g., I before V), subtract
        (set total (+ total current_val))) ; Otherwise, add

      (set prev_val current_val) ; Update previous value for the next iteration
      (set i (- i 1))) ; Move to the previous character

    total)

  (test simple_three (eq? (parse_roman "III") 3))
  (test four (eq? (parse_roman "IV") 4))
  (test nine (eq? (parse_roman "IX") 9))
  (test fifty_eight (eq? (parse_roman "LVIII") 58))
  (test one_thousand_nine_hundred_ninety_four (eq? (parse_roman "MCMXCIV") 1994))
  (test three_thousand_nine_hundred_ninety_nine (eq? (parse_roman "MMMCMXCIX") 3999))
  (test empty_string (eq? (parse_roman "") 0)))

objective: Calculate the minimum payment required for a credit card. The minimum payment is determined by the greater of a fixed percentage of the outstanding balance or a set fixed minimum amount, with a special rule for zero or negative balances.
solution:
(module CreditCardPayment
  ; Helper function to round a number to two decimal places (cents).
  (defun round_to_cents (amount)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* amount 100) 0.5)) 100))

  ; Calculates the payment amount based solely on a percentage of the balance.
  (defun calculate_percentage_payment (balance min_percentage)
    (* balance min_percentage))

  ; Calculates the total minimum payment for a credit card.
  ; If the balance is zero or negative, the minimum payment is 0.
  ; Otherwise, it's the greater of a percentage of the balance or a fixed minimum amount,
  ; rounded to two decimal places.
  (defun calculate_minimum_payment (balance min_percentage fixed_min_amount)
    (if (<= balance 0)
        (return 0))
    (def percent_payment (calculate_percentage_payment balance min_percentage))
    (def raw_min_payment (math_max percent_payment fixed_min_amount))
    (round_to_cents raw_min_payment))

  ; Test cases
  (test payment_above_fixed_min
    ; Balance $2000, 2% is $40. Fixed min is $25. Max is $40.
    (near? (calculate_minimum_payment 2000 0.02 25) 40.00 0.01))

  (test payment_at_fixed_min
    ; Balance $1000, 2% is $20. Fixed min is $25. Max is $25.
    (near? (calculate_minimum_payment 1000 0.02 25) 25.00 0.01))

  (test payment_with_fractional_percentage
    ; Balance $1234.56, 2.5% is $30.864. Fixed min is $25. Max is $30.864, rounded to $30.86.
    (near? (calculate_minimum_payment 1234.56 0.025 25) 30.86 0.01))

  (test zero_balance
    ; Zero balance should result in a $0 payment.
    (near? (calculate_minimum_payment 0 0.02 25) 0.00 0.01))

  (test negative_balance
    ; Negative balance should also result in a $0 payment.
    (near? (calculate_minimum_payment -100 0.02 25) 0.00 0.01))

  (test small_balance_triggers_fixed_min
    ; Balance $500, 3% is $15. Fixed min is $20. Max is $20.
    (near? (calculate_minimum_payment 500 0.03 20) 20.00 0.01))

  (test large_balance_high_percentage
    ; Balance $5000, 1.5% is $75. Fixed min is $35. Max is $75.
    (near? (calculate_minimum_payment 5000 0.015 35) 75.00 0.01))
)

objective: Implement a function `manual_str_lower` that converts an input string to its lowercase equivalent without using the built-in `str_lower` function. The conversion should be done by iterating through the string character by character and building a new string.
solution:
(module ManualLowercase
  (def upper_alphabet "ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ")
  (def lower_alphabet "abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz")

  (defun char_to_lower (c)
    "Converts a single uppercase character string to its lowercase equivalent.
     Returns the original character if it's not an uppercase letter."
    (def idx (str_index_of upper_alphabet c))
    (if (>= idx 0)
      (str_substring lower_alphabet idx 1)
      c))

  (defun manual_str_lower (s)
    "Converts the entire string s to lowercase using a manual loop and character-by-character conversion."
    (def i 0)
    (def len (str_length s))
    (def result "")
    (while (< i len)
      (def current_char (str_substring s i 1))
      (def lower_char (char_to_lower current_char))
      (set result (str_concat result lower_char))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    result)

  (test empty_string
    (eq? (manual_str_lower "") ""))
  (test all_uppercase
    (eq? (manual_str_lower "HELLO WORLD") "hello world"))
  (test all_lowercase
    (eq? (manual_str_lower "agc programming") "agc programming"))
  (test mixed_case
    (eq? (manual_str_lower "This Is A MiXeD CaSe StRiNg") "this is a mixed case string"))
  (test numbers_and_symbols
    (eq? (manual_str_lower "123!@#ABCxyz-+=_") "123!@#abcxyz-+=_"))
  (test leading_trailing_spaces
    (eq? (manual_str_lower "  TESTING  SPACES  ") "  testing  spaces  "))
  (test single_char
    (eq? (manual_str_lower "X") "x")))

(module ParkingCost

    (if (<= total_hours 1.0)
        0.0
        (math_ceil (- total_hours 1.0))))

  (defun calculate_cost (chargeable_hours hourly_rate)
    (* chargeable_hours hourly_rate))

  (defun get_parking_cost (total_hours hourly_rate)

    (calculate_cost chargeable hourly_rate))

  (test "less than 1 hour, free"

  (test "exactly 1 hour, free"

topic: discounted price after coupon and bulk-buy threshold
objective: Write a module to calculate the final price of an order. The calculation should account for a unit price, item quantity, and apply two potential discounts: a fixed percentage off if a specific coupon code ("SAVE10") is provided, and an additional percentage off if the total quantity exceeds a bulk-buy threshold.
solution:
(module PriceCalculator

  ;; Helper to calculate the initial total price based on unit price and quantity.
  (defun calc_base_total (unit_price quantity)
    (* unit_price quantity))

  ;; Applies a 10% discount if the coupon code is "SAVE10".
  (defun apply_coupon (total coupon_code)
    (if (str_eq coupon_code "SAVE10")
      (* total 0.90) ; 10% off
      total))

  ;; Applies a percentage discount if the quantity exceeds the bulk threshold.
  (defun apply_bulk (total quantity bulk_threshold bulk_discount_rate)
    (if (> quantity bulk_threshold)
      (* total (- 1 bulk_discount_rate))
      total))

  ;; Calculates the final price by applying coupon and bulk discounts sequentially.
  ;; coupon_code: The coupon string (e.g., "SAVE10" or "NONE").
  ;; bulk_threshold: The quantity above which the bulk_discount_rate applies.
  ;; bulk_discount_rate: The decimal rate for bulk discount (e.g., 0.05 for 5%).
  (defun calculate_final_price (unit_price quantity coupon_code bulk_threshold bulk_discount_rate)
    (def base (calc_base_total unit_price quantity))
    (def after_coupon (apply_coupon base coupon_code))
    (apply_bulk after_coupon quantity bulk_threshold bulk_discount_rate))

  ;; Test 1: No discounts applied.
  (test no_discounts
    (near? (calculate_final_price 10.0 5 "NONE" 10 0.05) 50.0 0.001))

  ;; Test 2: Only coupon discount applies.
  (test only_coupon
    (near? (calculate_final_price 10.0 5 "SAVE10" 10 0.05) 45.0 0.001)) ; 50 * 0.90 = 45

  ;; Test 3: Only bulk discount applies.
  (test only_bulk
    (near? (calculate_final_price 10.0 12 "NONE" 10 0.05) 114.0 0.001)) ; 120 * (1 - 0.05) = 120 * 0.95 = 114

  ;; Test 4: Both coupon and bulk discounts apply. Coupon first, then bulk.
  (test both_discounts
    (near? (calculate_final_price 10.0 12 "SAVE10" 10 0.05) 102.6 0.001)) ; 120 * 0.90 = 108; 108 * 0.95 = 102.6

  ;; Test 5: Coupon code is not "SAVE10", no coupon discount. Bulk threshold not met.
  (test wrong_coupon_no_bulk
    (near? (calculate_final_price 10.0 5 "DISCOUNT20" 10 0.05) 50.0 0.001))

  ;; Test 6: Bulk threshold met, but bulk discount rate is zero.
  (test bulk_zero_rate
    (near? (calculate_final_price 10.0 12 "NONE" 10 0.00) 120.0 0.001))
)

objective: Implement a function that calculates the dot product of two numerical arrays (vectors). Assume the input arrays will always be of the same length. The dot product is the sum of the products of their corresponding elements.
solution:
(module VectorDotProduct
  (defun make_vec3 (a b c)
    (def vec (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set vec 0 a)
    (arr_set vec 1 b)
    (arr_set vec 2 c)
    vec)

  (defun make_vec1 (a)
    (def vec (arr_new 1))
    (arr_set vec 0 a)
    vec)

  (defun dot_product (v1 v2)
    (def len (arr_length v1))
    (def i 0)
    (def sum_prod 0)
    (while (< i len)
      (def val1 (arr_get v1 i))
      (def val2 (arr_get v2 i))
      (set sum_prod (+ sum_prod (* val1 val2)))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    sum_prod)

  (test zero_vectors (eq? (dot_product (make_vec3 0 0 0) (make_vec3 0 0 0)) 0))
  (test simple_pos (eq? (dot_product (make_vec3 1 2 3) (make_vec3 4 5 6)) (+ (* 1 4) (* 2 5) (* 3 6)))) ; 4 + 10 + 18 = 32
  (test mixed_signs (eq? (dot_product (make_vec3 1 -2 3) (make_vec3 -4 5 -6)) (+ (* 1 -4) (* -2 5) (* 3 -6)))) ; -4 - 10 - 18 = -32
  (test one_zero_vec (eq? (dot_product (make_vec3 1 2 3) (make_vec3 0 0 0)) 0))
  (test empty_vectors (eq? (dot_product (arr_new 0) (arr_new 0)) 0))
  (test single_element_val (eq? (dot_product (make_vec1 7) (make_vec1 8)) 56))
  (test float_values (near? (dot_product (make_vec3 0.5 2.0 1.5) (make_vec3 1.0 0.5 2.0)) (+ (* 0.5 1.0) (* 2.0 0.5) (* 1.5 2.0)) 0.001)) ; 0.5 + 1.0 + 3.0 = 4.5
)

topic: monthly subscription cost with annual discount
objective: Calculate the total cost for a subscription, applying a monthly rate over a given number of months. If the subscription duration is 12 months or longer, apply a specified annual discount percentage to the total cost.

(module SubscriptionCalculator
  (defun calculate_base_cost (monthly_rate num_months)
    (* monthly_rate num_months))

  (defun apply_discount (total_cost discount_pct)
    (- total_cost (* total_cost (/ discount_pct 100.0))))

  (defun get_subscription_cost (monthly_rate num_months annual_discount_pct)
    (def base_total (calculate_base_cost monthly_rate num_months))
    (if (>= num_months 12)

    base_total)

  (test less_than_year_no_discount

  (test no_discount_percentage_more_than_year

)

objective: Calculate the nth Fibonacci number using an iterative approach. The sequence starts with F(0) = 0, F(1) = 1, and F(n) = F(n-1) + F(n-2) for n > 1.
solution:
(module FibonacciIterative
  (defun fib_iterative (n)
    (if (< n 0) (return 0)) ; Handle negative input, though problem typically assumes non-negative.
    (if (= n 0) (return 0))
    (if (= n 1) (return 1))

    (def a 0) ; Represents F(i-2)
    (def b 1) ; Represents F(i-1)
    (def i 2)

    (while (<= i n)
      (def next_fib (+ a b))
      (set a b)
      (set b next_fib)
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    b)

  (test fib_zero    (eq? (fib_iterative 0) 0))
  (test fib_one     (eq? (fib_iterative 1) 1))
  (test fib_two     (eq? (fib_iterative 2) 1))
  (test fib_three   (eq? (fib_iterative 3) 2))
  (test fib_five    (eq? (fib_iterative 5) 5))
  (test fib_ten     (eq? (fib_iterative 10) 55))
  (test fib_thirteen (eq? (fib_iterative 13) 233)))

objective: Create a function that takes a string and returns a new string with its first character capitalized, leaving the rest of the string unchanged. If the input string is empty, it should return an empty string.
solution:
(module CapitalizeFirstLetter
  (defun capitalize_first (s)
    (def len (str_length s))
    (if (= len 0) (return ""))
    (def first_char (str_substring s 0 1))
    (def rest_of_string (str_substring s 1 len))
    (def capitalized_first (str_upper first_char))
    (str_concat capitalized_first rest_of_string))

  (test empty_string (eq? (capitalize_first "") ""))
  (test single_lowercase (eq? (capitalize_first "a") "A"))
  (test single_uppercase (eq? (capitalize_first "B") "B"))
  (test regular_word (eq? (capitalize_first "hello") "Hello"))
  (test already_capitalized (eq? (capitalize_first "World") "World"))
  (test sentence (eq? (capitalize_first "this is a test") "This is a test"))
  (test leading_digit (eq? (capitalize_first "123go") "123go"))
  (test leading_space (eq? (capitalize_first " test string") " test string")))

objective: Calculate the total monthly phone bill based on a base plan cost, included data, overage charges for exceeding the data limit, and a sales tax percentage. The final bill should be rounded to two decimal places.

(module PhoneBillCalculator
  (defun round_to_cents (amount)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* amount 100) 0.5)) 100))

  (defun calculate_overage_charge (used_gb included_gb overage_rate_per_gb)
    (if (> used_gb included_gb)
      (* (- used_gb included_gb) overage_rate_per_gb)
      0))

  (defun apply_tax (amount tax_rate_pct)
    (* amount (+ 1 (/ tax_rate_pct 100))))

  (defun calculate_monthly_bill (base_cost included_data_gb overage_rate_per_gb used_data_gb tax_rate_pct)
    (def overage_cost (calculate_overage_charge used_data_gb included_data_gb overage_rate_per_gb))
    (def subtotal (+ base_cost overage_cost))
    (def taxed_amount (apply_tax subtotal tax_rate_pct))
    (round_to_cents taxed_amount))

    (near? (calculate_monthly_bill 50 10 5 12 0) 60 0.01))

    (near? (calculate_monthly_bill 30 5 10 5 15) 34.50 0.01))

(module TicketPricing
  (defun calculate_ticket_price (age)
    (if (< age 5)
      0 ; Free for under 5
      (if (<= age 12)
        10 ; Child price for 5-12
        (if (<= age 64)
          20 ; Adult price for 13-64
          15 ; Senior price for 65+
        )
      )
    )
  )

  (test free_baby (eq? (calculate_ticket_price 0) 0))
  (test free_toddler (eq? (calculate_ticket_price 4) 0))

)

objective: Calculate the discriminant of a quadratic equation `ax^2 + bx + c = 0` and determine the number of distinct real roots it possesses.
solution:
(module QuadraticDiscriminant
  (defun calculate_discriminant (a b c)
    (def b_squared (* b b))
    (def four_ac (* 4 a c))
    (- b_squared four_ac))

  (defun get_num_real_roots (a b c)
    (def discriminant (calculate_discriminant a b c))
    (if (> discriminant 0) (return 2)) ; Two distinct real roots
    (if (= discriminant 0) (return 1)) ; Exactly one real root
    0) ; No real roots (discriminant < 0)

  (test two_distinct_real_roots
    ; x^2 + 5x + 6 = 0 => D = 5^2 - 4*1*6 = 25 - 24 = 1
    (eq? (get_num_real_roots 1 5 6) 2))

  (test one_real_root
    ; x^2 + 4x + 4 = 0 => D = 4^2 - 4*1*4 = 16 - 16 = 0
    (eq? (get_num_real_roots 1 4 4) 1))

  (test no_real_roots
    ; x^2 + x + 1 = 0 => D = 1^2 - 4*1*1 = 1 - 4 = -3
    (eq? (get_num_real_roots 1 1 1) 0))

  (test fractional_coefficients
    ; 0.5x^2 - 3x + 4 = 0 => D = (-3)^2 - 4*0.5*4 = 9 - 8 = 1
    (eq? (get_num_real_roots 0.5 -3 4) 2))

  (test another_one_real_root
    ; 2x^2 - 4x + 2 = 0 => D = (-4)^2 - 4*2*2 = 16 - 16 = 0
    (eq? (get_num_real_roots 2 -4 2) 1))

  (test negative_a
    ; -x^2 + 2x - 1 = 0 => D = 2^2 - 4*(-1)*(-1) = 4 - 4 = 0
    (eq? (get_num_real_roots -1 2 -1) 1))
)

topic: tip calculation split among diners with rounding
objective: Implement a function that calculates the total amount to pay, including a tip, and then splits this total evenly among a specified number of diners. Each diner's share must be rounded *up* to the nearest cent.
solution:
(module TipSplitter
  ;; Helper function to round a number up to two decimal places (nearest cent)
  (defun round_up_to_cents (amount)
    (/ (math_ceil (* amount 100)) 100))

  ;; Calculates the tip amount based on the bill and percentage
  (defun calculate_tip (bill_amount tip_percentage)
    (* bill_amount (/ tip_percentage 100)))

  ;; Calculates the total bill including the tip
  (defun calculate_total (bill_amount tip_amount)
    (+ bill_amount tip_amount))

  ;; Main function to calculate and split the bill per diner
  ;; bill_amount: The original cost of the meal
  ;; tip_percentage: The tip percentage (e.g., 15 for 15%)
  ;; num_diners: The number of people splitting the bill
  (defun split_bill (bill_amount tip_percentage num_diners)
    (def tip_amt (calculate_tip bill_amount tip_percentage))
    (def total_amt (calculate_total bill_amount tip_amt))
    (def raw_share (/ total_amt num_diners))
    (round_up_to_cents raw_share))

  (test simple_split_even
    (near? (split_bill 100 15 2) 57.50 0.001)) ; 100 + 15 = 115. 115 / 2 = 57.50

  (test forced_round_up
    (near? (split_bill 10 15 3) 3.84 0.001)) ; 10 + 1.5 = 11.5. 11.5 / 3 = 3.833... rounded up to 3.84

  (test another_round_up
    (near? (split_bill 25.50 17 3) 9.95 0.001)) ; 25.50 + (25.50 * 0.17) = 25.50 + 4.335 = 29.835. 29.835 / 3 = 9.945 rounded up to 9.95

  (test single_diner
    (near? (split_bill 50 10 1) 55.00 0.001)) ; 50 + 5 = 55. 55 / 1 = 55.00

  (test zero_tip
    (near? (split_bill 75 0 4) 18.75 0.001)) ; 75 + 0 = 75. 75 / 4 = 18.75

  (test high_percentage_many_diners
    (near? (split_bill 123.45 25 7) 22.05 0.001)) ; 123.45 + (123.45 * 0.25) = 123.45 + 30.8625 = 154.3125. 154.3125 / 7 = 22.0446... rounded up to 22.05
)

objective: Calculate the total cost of a hotel stay, considering a base daily rate, the number of weekday nights, and the number of weekend nights. A percentage-based surcharge is applied only to weekend nights.
solution:
(module HotelStayCalculator
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))

  (defun get_surcharged_rate (base_rate surcharge_pct)
    (round2 (* base_rate (+ 1 (/ surcharge_pct 100))))
  )

  (defun calculate_total_weekend_cost (base_rate num_weekend_nights surcharge_pct)
    (def surcharged_rate (get_surcharged_rate base_rate surcharge_pct))
    (round2 (* surcharged_rate num_weekend_nights))
  )

  (defun calculate_total_weekday_cost (base_rate num_weekday_nights)
    (round2 (* base_rate num_weekday_nights))
  )

  (defun calculate_total_stay_cost (base_rate num_weekday_nights num_weekend_nights surcharge_pct)
    (def weekday_cost (calculate_total_weekday_cost base_rate num_weekday_nights))
    (def weekend_cost (calculate_total_weekend_cost base_rate num_weekend_nights surcharge_pct))
    (round2 (+ weekday_cost weekend_cost))
  )

  (test all_weekdays_no_surcharge
    (near? (calculate_total_stay_cost 100 3 0 0) 300.00 0.01))

  (test all_weekends_with_surcharge
    (near? (calculate_total_stay_cost 100 0 2 20) 240.00 0.01))

  (test mixed_stay_with_surcharge
    (near? (calculate_total_stay_cost 100 3 2 20) 540.00 0.01))

  (test zero_nights
    (near? (calculate_total_stay_cost 100 0 0 15) 0.00 0.01))

  (test zero_surcharge
    (near? (calculate_total_stay_cost 100 2 1 0) 300.00 0.01))

  (test fractional_rate_and_surcharge
    (near? (calculate_total_stay_cost 75.50 1 1 10) 158.55 0.01))

  (test complex_rounding
    (near? (calculate_total_stay_cost 99.999 1 1 10) 210.00 0.01))
)

objective: Calculate an individual's Body Mass Index (BMI) given their weight in kilograms and height in meters, then classify the BMI into categories: "Underweight", "Normal weight", "Overweight", or "Obese" based on standard ranges.

(module BmiCalculator

    (/ weight_kg (* height_m height_m)))

  (defun classify_bmi (bmi_val)
    (if (< bmi_val 18.5) (return "Underweight"))
    (if (< bmi_val 25.0) (return "Normal weight"))
    (if (< bmi_val 30.0) (return "Overweight"))
    "Obese")

objective: Implement a function `string_contains_char` that takes two string arguments: a main string and a single-character string. The function should return 1 if the main string contains the character, and 0 otherwise.
solution:
(module StringContainsChar
  (defun string_contains_char (main_str char_to_find)
    (def len (str_length main_str))
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i len)
      (def current_char (str_substring main_str i 1))
      (if (str_eq current_char char_to_find)
        (return 1))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    0)

  (test found_middle

    (eq? (string_contains_char "" "a") 0))

  (test multiple_occurrences
    (eq? (string_contains_char "programming" "g") 1))

  (test space_character
    (eq? (string_contains_char "hello world" " ") 1)))

objective: Implement a system to calculate sales commission based on tiered total sales volume. The commission rate is determined by the highest sales tier achieved, and that rate is applied to the entire sales amount.
solution:
(module SalesCommission
  (defun get_commission_rate (sales)
    "Determines the commission rate based on total sales volume."
    (if (<= sales 10000)
        (return 0.05)) ; 5% for sales up to $10,000
    (if (and (> sales 10000) (<= sales 50000))
        (return 0.075)) ; 7.5% for sales between $10,001 and $50,000
    ; If sales are greater than $50,000
    (return 0.10)) ; 10% for sales above $50,000

  (defun calculate_commission (sales)
    "Calculates the total commission for a given sales amount using tiered rates."
    (def rate (get_commission_rate sales))
    (* sales rate))

  (test tier1_low
    (near? (calculate_commission 5000) 250.0 0.01))

  (test tier1_boundary
    (near? (calculate_commission 10000) 500.0 0.01))

  (test tier2_mid
    (near? (calculate_commission 30000) 2250.0 0.01))

  (test tier2_boundary
    (near? (calculate_commission 50000) 3750.0 0.01))

  (test tier3_high
    (near? (calculate_commission 75000) 7500.0 0.01))

  (test zero_sales
    (near? (calculate_commission 0) 0.0 0.01))
)

  (defun calculate_excess_surcharge (actual_weight weight_limit surcharge_per_excess_kg)

      (* (- actual_weight weight_limit) surcharge_per_excess_kg)
      0))

topic: sales tax inclusive vs exclusive price
objective: Create a function that converts a product's price between its tax-inclusive and tax-exclusive forms. Given a price, a tax rate, and an indication of whether the input price already includes tax, the function should calculate and return the corresponding price in the alternate tax status, rounded to two decimal places.
solution:
(module TaxPriceConverter
  ; Helper to round a floating-point number to two decimal places (cents).
  (defun round_to_cents (amount)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* amount 100.0) 0.5)) 100.0))

  ; Calculates the price excluding tax, given an inclusive price and tax rate.
  ; tax_rate_pct is the tax rate as a percentage (e.g., 10 for 10%).
  (defun get_exclusive_from_inclusive (inclusive_price tax_rate_pct)
    ; exclusive = inclusive / (1 + tax_rate_decimal)
    (/ inclusive_price (+ 1.0 (/ tax_rate_pct 100.0))))

  ; Calculates the price including tax, given an exclusive price and tax rate.
  ; tax_rate_pct is the tax rate as a percentage (e.g., 10 for 10%).
  (defun get_inclusive_from_exclusive (exclusive_price tax_rate_pct)
    ; inclusive = exclusive * (1 + tax_rate_decimal)
    (* exclusive_price (+ 1.0 (/ tax_rate_pct 100.0))))

  ; Converts a price between tax-inclusive and tax-exclusive based on the input status.
  ; Parameters:
  ;   price: The input price.
  ;   tax_rate_pct: The tax rate as a percentage (e.g., 5 for 5%).
  ;   is_inclusive_input: A boolean, true if the input 'price' is tax-inclusive, false if tax-exclusive.
  ; Returns: The converted price (exclusive if input was inclusive, inclusive if input was exclusive),
  ;          rounded to two decimal places.
  (defun convert_price_tax_status (price tax_rate_pct is_inclusive_input)
    (if is_inclusive_input
      (round_to_cents (get_exclusive_from_inclusive price tax_rate_pct))
      (round_to_cents (get_inclusive_from_exclusive price tax_rate_pct))))

  (test "Exclusive to Inclusive - whole numbers"
    (near? (convert_price_tax_status 100.0 10.0 false) 110.0 0.001))

  (test "Inclusive to Exclusive - whole numbers"
    (near? (convert_price_tax_status 110.0 10.0 true) 100.0 0.001))

  (test "Exclusive to Inclusive - with cents and non-standard tax"
    (near? (convert_price_tax_status 99.99 7.5 false) 107.49 0.001)) ; 99.99 * 1.075 = 107.48925 -> 107.49

  (test "Inclusive to Exclusive - with cents and non-standard tax"
    (near? (convert_price_tax_status 107.49 7.5 true) 99.99 0.001)) ; 107.49 / 1.075 = 99.99069767... -> 99.99

  (test "Zero tax - exclusive input"
    (near? (convert_price_tax_status 50.0 0.0 false) 50.0 0.001))

  (test "Zero tax - inclusive input"
    (near? (convert_price_tax_status 50.0 0.0 true) 50.0 0.001))

  (test "High tax rate - exclusive to inclusive"
    (near? (convert_price_tax_status 200.0 100.0 false) 400.0 0.001)) ; 200 * (1 + 100/100) = 200 * 2 = 400
)

objective: Write a function `string_contains_char` that determines if a given string (`haystack`) contains a specific single character (`needle`). The function should return 1 if the `needle` character is found anywhere within the `haystack` string, and 0 otherwise. Assume `needle` will always be a string of length 1.
solution:
(module StringContainsChar
  (defun string_contains_char (haystack needle)
    (def i 0)
    (def len (str_length haystack))
    (while (< i len)
      (def current_char (str_substring haystack i 1))
      (if (str_eq current_char needle)
        (return 1))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    0)

  (test char_found_middle (eq? (string_contains_char "hello world" "o") 1))
  (test char_not_found   (eq? (string_contains_char "abcde" "x") 0))
  (test char_found_start (eq? (string_contains_char "apple" "a") 1))
  (test char_found_end   (eq? (string_contains_char "banana" "a") 1))
  (test empty_haystack   (eq? (string_contains_char "" "z") 0))
  (test single_char_match (eq? (string_contains_char "x" "x") 1))
  (test single_char_nomatch (eq? (string_contains_char "y" "x") 0)))

objective: Write a function `get_http_body_first_word` that fetches content from a given URL using the `http.fetch` capability. It should then extract the body of the HTTP response and return its first non-empty word. If the body is empty or contains only spaces, an empty string should be returned.
solution:
(module HttpFirstWord
  (extern defun http_fetch (url) @capability "http.fetch")

  ;; Helper function to find the first non-empty string in an array
  (defun get_first_non_empty_string (arr)
    (def i 0)
    (def len (arr_length arr))
    (while (< i len)
      (def item (arr_get arr i))
      (if (not (str_eq item ""))
        (return item))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    "") ; Return empty string if no non-empty items are found

  ;; Main function to get the first word from an HTTP body
  (defun get_http_body_first_word (url)
    (def body (http_fetch url))
    (if (str_eq body "") (return ""))
    (def words (str_split body " "))
    (get_first_non_empty_string words))

  (test simple_case
    (mocks
      (http.fetch "http://example.com/page1" "Hello World!"))
    (eq? (get_http_body_first_word "http://example.com/page1") "Hello"))

  (test leading_trailing_spaces
    (mocks
      (http.fetch "http://example.com/page2" "  First Second  "))
    (eq? (get_http_body_first_word "http://example.com/page2") "First"))

  (test multiple_internal_spaces
    (mocks
      (http.fetch "http://example.com/page3" "One   Two Three"))
    (eq? (get_http_body_first_word "http://example.com/page3") "One"))

  (test single_word_body
    (mocks
      (http.fetch "http://example.com/page4" "JustOne"))
    (eq? (get_http_body_first_word "http://example.com/page4") "JustOne"))

  (test empty_body
    (mocks
      (http.fetch "http://example.com/page5" ""))
    (eq? (get_http_body_first_word "http://example.com/page5") ""))

  (test body_only_spaces
    (mocks
      (http.fetch "http://example.com/page6" "   "))
    (eq? (get_http_body_first_word "http://example.com/page6") ""))

  (test body_with_line_breaks_and_tabs
    (mocks
      (http.fetch "http://example.com/page7" "\n\tWord\tWith\nBreaks"))
    (eq? (get_http_body_first_word "http://example.com/page7") "\n\tWord\tWith\nBreaks")) ; Note: str_split " " doesn't split on newlines/tabs by default.
)

objective: Implement a function that determines the length of a given string. This function must count the number of characters in the string without using the built-in `str_length` function.
solution:
(module StringLengthCalculator
  (defun calculate_string_length (s)
    (def current_index 0)
    (while 1
      ; Attempt to extract a single character at the current index.
      ; If the result is an empty string, it means we've passed the end of the string.
      (if (str_eq (str_substring s current_index 1) "")
        (return current_index))
      ; If a character was found, increment the index and continue.
      (set current_index (+ current_index 1))))

  (test empty_string
    (eq? (calculate_string_length "") 0))

  (test single_char_string
    (eq? (calculate_string_length "x") 1))

  (test short_string
    (eq? (calculate_string_length "hello") 5))

  (test long_string
    (eq? (calculate_string_length "pneumonoultramicroscopicsilicovolcanoconiosis") 45))

  (test string_with_spaces
    (eq? (calculate_string_length "  leading and trailing spaces  ") 31))

  (test string_with_numbers
    (eq? (calculate_string_length "1234567890") 10)))

topic: ticket pricing tiered by age group
objective: Implement a function that calculates the ticket price for an event based on the patron's age. Prices are tiered: infants (0-2 years) are free, children (3-12 years) pay $10, adults (13-64 years) pay $25, and seniors (65+ years) pay $18. Negative ages should result in a $0 price.
solution:
(module TicketPricing
  (defun calculate_ticket_price (age)
    (if (< age 0)
      (return 0)) ; Handle negative ages as free
    (if (<= age 2)
      (return 0)) ; Infant: 0-2 years
    (if (<= age 12)
      (return 10)) ; Child: 3-12 years
    (if (<= age 64)
      (return 25)) ; Adult: 13-64 years
    ; Otherwise, it must be 65+
    18) ; Senior: 65+ years

  (test infant_age_0 (eq? (calculate_ticket_price 0) 0))
  (test infant_age_2 (eq? (calculate_ticket_price 2) 0))
  (test child_age_3 (eq? (calculate_ticket_price 3) 10))
  (test child_age_12 (eq? (calculate_ticket_price 12) 10))
  (test adult_age_13 (eq? (calculate_ticket_price 13) 25))
  (test senior_age_65 (eq? (calculate_ticket_price 65) 18))
  (test invalid_age_negative (eq? (calculate_ticket_price -5) 0))
)

objective: Calculate the future value of an investment using the compound interest formula, assuming interest is compounded quarterly. The function should take the principal amount, annual interest rate (as a percentage), and the number of years, returning the total amount rounded to two decimal places.
solution:
(module QuarterlyCompoundInterest
  (defun rate_to_decimal (rate_percent)
    (/ rate_percent 100.0))

  (defun round_to_cents (amount)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* amount 100.0) 0.5)) 100.0))

  (defun calculate_quarterly_compound_interest (principal annual_rate_percent years)
    (def r (rate_to_decimal annual_rate_percent))
    (def n 4) ; Compounded quarterly
    (def base (+ 1 (/ r n)))
    (def exponent (* n years))
    (def future_value (* principal (math_pow base exponent)))
    (round_to_cents future_value))

  (test simple_one_year
    (near? (calculate_quarterly_compound_interest 1000 4 1) 1040.60 0.01))

  (test five_years_higher_rate
    (near? (calculate_quarterly_compound_interest 5000 6 5) 6734.28 0.01))

  (test half_year_low_rate
    (near? (calculate_quarterly_compound_interest 250 2 0.5) 252.51 0.01))

  (test zero_rate
    (near? (calculate_quarterly_compound_interest 100 0 10) 100.00 0.01))

  (test longer_term
    (near? (calculate_quarterly_compound_interest 10000 5 10) 16436.19 0.01)))

topic: shipping cost by weight and zone
objective: Implement a shipping cost calculator that determines the total cost based on the package's weight and destination zone. The calculation involves a zone-specific base rate and a weight-based surcharge.
solution:
(module ShippingCostCalculator

  ;; Helper function to determine the base rate for a given zone.
  ;; Zone "A" has a base rate of 5.00.
  ;; Zone "B" has a base rate of 8.00.
  ;; Zone "C" has a base rate of 12.00.
  ;; Any other zone defaults to a base rate of 15.00.
  (defun get_zone_base_rate (zone)
    (if (str_eq zone "A")
      (return 5.0))
    (if (str_eq zone "B")
      (return 8.0))
    (if (str_eq zone "C")
      (return 12.0))
    15.0) ; Default rate for unknown zones

  ;; Helper function to calculate the weight surcharge.
  ;; Surcharge is 0.50 per unit of weight.
  (defun get_weight_surcharge (weight)
    (* weight 0.50))

  ;; Calculates the total shipping cost for a given weight and zone.
  ;; The total cost is the sum of the zone's base rate and the weight surcharge.
  (defun calculate_shipping_cost (weight zone)
    (def base_rate (get_zone_base_rate zone))
    (def surcharge (get_weight_surcharge weight))
    (+ base_rate surcharge))

  (test zone_a_light_package
    (near? (calculate_shipping_cost 2.5 "A") 6.25 0.01)) ; 5.0 (A) + (2.5 * 0.5) = 5.0 + 1.25 = 6.25

  (test zone_b_medium_package
    (near? (calculate_shipping_cost 10.0 "B") 13.0 0.01)) ; 8.0 (B) + (10.0 * 0.5) = 8.0 + 5.0 = 13.0

  (test zone_c_heavy_package
    (near? (calculate_shipping_cost 20.0 "C") 22.0 0.01)) ; 12.0 (C) + (20.0 * 0.5) = 12.0 + 10.0 = 22.0

  (test unknown_zone_package
    (near? (calculate_shipping_cost 5.0 "X") 17.5 0.01)) ; 15.0 (default) + (5.0 * 0.5) = 15.0 + 2.5 = 17.5

  (test zero_weight_package
    (near? (calculate_shipping_cost 0.0 "A") 5.0 0.01)) ; 5.0 (A) + (0.0 * 0.5) = 5.0 + 0.0 = 5.0

  (test zone_b_decimal_weight
    (near? (calculate_shipping_cost 7.75 "B") 11.875 0.01)) ; 8.0 (B) + (7.75 * 0.5) = 8.0 + 3.875 = 11.875
)

objective: Given a URL, implement a function that uses the `http.fetch` capability to retrieve content and extracts only the HTTP status line (e.g., "200 OK", "404 Not Found") from the response. Assume the `http.fetch` capability returns a string where the first line is the HTTP status.
solution:
(module HttpStatusExtractor
  (extern defun http_fetch (url) @capability "http.fetch")

  (defun get_http_status (url)
    (def response_body (http_fetch url))
    (def parts (str_split response_body "\n"))
    (arr_get parts 0))

  (test success_200
    (mocks
      (http.fetch "http://example.com/api/data" "200 OK\nContent-Type: application/json\nContent-Length: 20\n\n{\"key\":\"value\"}"))

objective: Implement a function that performs linear interpolation (LERP) between two numbers. Given a start value `a`, an end value `b`, and an interpolation factor `t` (typically between 0.0 and 1.0), the function should return the value that is `t` percent of the way from `a` to `b`.
solution:
(module Lerp
  (defun lerp (a b t)
    (+ a (* (- b a) t)))

  (test lerp_at_start

  (test lerp_extrapolation_above

topic: discounted price after coupon and bulk-buy threshold
objective: Calculate the final price of an order, applying a bulk purchase discount if the quantity exceeds a specified threshold, and then applying a fixed-amount coupon discount if a valid coupon code is provided. The final price cannot be negative.
solution:
(module DiscountCalculator
  (defun round_to_2_decimals (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))

  (defun calculate_item_total (unit_price quantity)
    (* unit_price quantity))

  (defun apply_bulk_discount (total quantity threshold discount_rate)
    (if (>= quantity threshold)
        (* total (- 1 discount_rate))
        total))

  (defun apply_coupon (current_total user_coupon expected_coupon coupon_value)
    (if (str_eq user_coupon expected_coupon)
        (math_max 0 (- current_total coupon_value)) ; Ensure total doesn't go below zero
        current_total))

  (defun calculate_final_price (unit_price quantity bulk_threshold bulk_rate user_coupon expected_coupon coupon_value)
    (def item_total (calculate_item_total unit_price quantity))
    (def after_bulk (apply_bulk_discount item_total quantity bulk_threshold bulk_rate))
    (def final_price (apply_coupon after_bulk user_coupon expected_coupon coupon_value))
    (round_to_2_decimals final_price))

  (test no_discounts
    (near? (calculate_final_price 10.00 5 10 0.10 "NONE" "SAVE10" 5.00) 50.00 0.01))

  (test bulk_only
    (near? (calculate_final_price 10.00 12 10 0.10 "NONE" "SAVE10" 5.00) 108.00 0.01))

  (test coupon_only
    (near? (calculate_final_price 10.00 5 10 0.10 "SAVE10" "SAVE10" 5.00) 45.00 0.01))

  (test bulk_and_coupon
    (near? (calculate_final_price 10.00 12 10 0.10 "SAVE10" "SAVE10" 5.00) 103.00 0.01))

  (test bulk_and_invalid_coupon
    (near? (calculate_final_price 10.00 12 10 0.10 "WRONG" "SAVE10" 5.00) 108.00 0.01))

  (test price_floor_zero_coupon_exceeds
    (near? (calculate_final_price 1.00 1 10 0.10 "SAVE10" "SAVE10" 5.00) 0.00 0.01))

  (test price_floor_zero_with_bulk_and_coupon
    (near? (calculate_final_price 5.00 2 1 0.50 "SAVE10" "SAVE10" 10.00) 0.00 0.01))
)

objective: Create a program that reads the content of two specified files and returns their concatenated content. If a file is empty or does not exist, its content should be treated as an empty string for concatenation.
solution:
(module FileConcatenator

objective: Implement a function that calculates the factorial of a given non-negative integer. The factorial of a non-negative integer `n` is the product of all positive integers less than or equal to `n`. The factorial of 0 is defined as 1.
solution:
(module Factorial
  (defun factorial (n)
    (if (< n 0)
      (return 0)) ; Factorial is not defined for negative numbers, returning 0
    (if (<= n 1)
      (return 1)) ; Handles 0! and 1!

    (def result 1)
    (def i 2)
    (while (<= i n)
      (set result (* result i))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    result)

  (test zero_factorial (eq? (factorial 0) 1))
  (test one_factorial (eq? (factorial 1) 1))
  (test two_factorial (eq? (factorial 2) 2))
  (test three_factorial (eq? (factorial 3) 6))
  (test five_factorial (eq? (factorial 5) 120))
  (test ten_factorial (eq? (factorial 10) 3628800))
  (test negative_factorial (eq? (factorial -5) 0)))

objective: Implement a function that calculates the area of a triangle given its three side lengths using Heron's formula. The function should return 0.0 if the given side lengths cannot form a valid triangle (e.g., one side is too long, or sides are zero).
solution:
(module TriangleArea
  (defun calculate_heron_area (a b c)
    ; Calculate the semi-perimeter
    (def s (/ (+ a b c) 2.0))

    ; Calculate the value inside the square root
    (def term_under_sqrt (* s (- s a) (- s b) (- s c)))

    ; If the term under the square root is negative, the sides do not form a valid triangle.
    ; Return 0.0 in such cases.
    (if (< term_under_sqrt 0.0)
      (return 0.0))

    ; Otherwise, calculate the square root to find the area
    (math_sqrt term_under_sqrt))

  (test equilateral_triangle
    (near? (calculate_heron_area 3 3 3) 3.897114317 0.000001))

  (test right_triangle_3_4_5
    (near? (calculate_heron_area 3 4 5) 6.0 0.000001))

  (test scalene_triangle_7_8_9
    (near? (calculate_heron_area 7 8 9) 26.832815729 0.000001))

  (test degenerate_triangle
    (near? (calculate_heron_area 1 2 3) 0.0 0.000001))

  (test invalid_triangle_one_side_too_long
    (near? (calculate_heron_area 1 2 10) 0.0 0.000001))

  (test all_sides_zero
    (near? (calculate_heron_area 0 0 0) 0.0 0.000001))

  (test two_sides_zero
    (near? (calculate_heron_area 5 0 0) 0.0 0.000001)))

objective: Implement a function `get_file_size` that reads a file and returns its size as a number. If the file does not exist, or if its content is empty or explicitly indicates non-numeric data (`"ERR_NON_NUMERIC"`), the function should return 0.
solution:
(module FileSizeReader
  (extern defun file_read (path) @capability "file.read")

  ; Helper to safely parse a size string, returning 0 for empty or error strings.
  (defun parse_numeric_string (s)
    (if (str_eq s "") (return 0))
    (if (str_eq s "ERR_NON_NUMERIC") (return 0))
    ; At this point, 's' is guaranteed to be a valid numeric string by the problem's capability contract and test mocks.
    (str_to_num s))

  (defun get_file_size (filepath)
    (def raw_content (file_read filepath))
    (parse_numeric_string raw_content))

  (test existing_file_valid_size
    (mocks (file.read "/path/to/data.bin" "1024"))
    (eq? (get_file_size "/path/to/data.bin") 1024))

  (test non_existent_file
    (mocks (file.read "/path/to/nonexistent.txt" ""))
    (eq? (get_file_size "/path/to/nonexistent.txt") 0))

  (test empty_content_file
    (mocks (file.read "/path/to/empty.log" ""))
    (eq? (get_file_size "/path/to/empty.log") 0))

  (test file_with_zero_size
    (mocks (file.read "/path/to/small.txt" "0"))
    (eq? (get_file_size "/path/to/small.txt") 0))

  (test file_with_non_numeric_content
    (mocks (file.read "/path/to/corrupt.cfg" "ERR_NON_NUMERIC"))
    (eq? (get_file_size "/path/to/corrupt.cfg") 0))

  (test another_valid_size
    (mocks (file.read "/var/log/app.log" "204800"))
    (eq? (get_file_size "/var/log/app.log") 204800))
)

objective: Implement a function that reads an environment variable and interprets its string value as a boolean. The function should return true if the environment variable's value is "true", "1", or "yes" (case-insensitive). For any other value, including "false", "0", "no", or an empty string, the function should return false.
solution:
(module EnvFlag
  (extern defun env_read (key) @capability "env.get")

  ;; Helper function to parse a string into a boolean
  (defun parse_bool_string (s)
    (def lower_s (str_lower s))
    (if (str_eq lower_s "true") (return true))
    (if (str_eq lower_s "1") (return true))
    (if (str_eq lower_s "yes") (return true))
    false) ; Default to false for "false", "0", "no", empty string, or any other arbitrary string

  ;; Reads an environment variable and returns its boolean interpretation
  (defun is_feature_enabled (feature_key)
    (def env_val (env_read feature_key))
    (parse_bool_string env_val))

  (test feature_enabled_true_case
    (mocks (env.get "ENABLE_ALPHA" "TRUE"))
    (eq? (is_feature_enabled "ENABLE_ALPHA") true))

  (test feature_enabled_one
    (mocks (env.get "ENABLE_BETA" "1"))
    (eq? (is_feature_enabled "ENABLE_BETA") true))

  (test feature_enabled_yes_mixed_case
    (mocks (env.get "ENABLE_GAMMA" "Yes"))
    (eq? (is_feature_enabled "ENABLE_GAMMA") true))

  (test feature_disabled_false_case
    (mocks (env.get "ENABLE_DELTA" "FALSE"))
    (eq? (is_feature_enabled "ENABLE_DELTA") false))

  (test feature_disabled_zero
    (mocks (env.get "ENABLE_EPSILON" "0"))
    (eq? (is_feature_enabled "ENABLE_EPSILON") false))

  (test feature_disabled_missing_env_var
    (mocks (env.get "ENABLE_ZETA" "")) ; env.get returns empty string if not found
    (eq? (is_feature_enabled "ENABLE_ZETA") false))

  (test feature_disabled_arbitrary_string
    (mocks (env.get "ENABLE_ETA" "some_other_value"))
    (eq? (is_feature_enabled "ENABLE_ETA") false)))

topic: env var split on comma into count
objective: Write a function that reads an environment variable, splits its value by a comma, and returns the total count of items. An empty environment variable (or one set to an empty string) should result in a count of zero.
solution:
(module EnvListCount
  (extern defun env_read (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun get_env_list_count (key)
    (def val (env_read key))
    (if (str_eq val "")
      (return 0))
    (def items (str_split val ","))
    (arr_length items))

  (test empty_env_var
    (mocks (env.get "CONFIG_LIST" ""))
    (eq? (get_env_list_count "CONFIG_LIST") 0))

  (test single_item
    (mocks (env.get "CONFIG_LIST" "server1"))
    (eq? (get_env_list_count "CONFIG_LIST") 1))

  (test multiple_items
    (mocks (env.get "CONFIG_LIST" "server1,server2,server3"))
    (eq? (get_env_list_count "CONFIG_LIST") 3))

  (test items_with_trailing_comma
    (mocks (env.get "CONFIG_LIST" "itemA,itemB,"))
    (eq? (get_env_list_count "CONFIG_LIST") 3))

  (test items_with_leading_comma
    (mocks (env.get "CONFIG_LIST" ",itemA,itemB"))
    (eq? (get_env_list_count "CONFIG_LIST") 3))

  (test items_with_empty_middle
    (mocks (env.get "CONFIG_LIST" "itemA,,itemB"))
    (eq? (get_env_list_count "CONFIG_LIST") 3))

  (test only_commas
    (mocks (env.get "CONFIG_LIST" ",,"))
    (eq? (get_env_list_count "CONFIG_LIST") 3))
)

objective: Create a function that reads an environment variable specified by a key. It should convert the variable's string value to an integer, returning a provided default integer if the environment variable is missing (empty string) or explicitly set to "NaN".
solution:
(module EnvVarInteger

  (defun get_int_env (key default_value)
    (def raw_value (env_get key))
    (if (str_eq raw_value "")
      (return default_value))
    (if (str_eq raw_value "NaN") ; Explicitly handle a common non-numeric string
      (return default_value))
    (str_to_num raw_value))

  (test missing_var

    (eq? (get_int_env "PORT" 8080) 8080))

  (test explicit_nan
    (mocks (env.get "TIMEOUT" "NaN"))
    (eq? (get_int_env "TIMEOUT" 1000) 1000))

  (test valid_positive

    (eq? (get_int_env "COUNT" 0) 123))

  (test valid_negative
    (mocks (env.get "OFFSET" "-50"))
    (eq? (get_int_env "OFFSET" 0) -50))

  (test valid_zero
    (mocks (env.get "RETRIES" "0"))
    (eq? (get_int_env "RETRIES" 3) 0)))

objective: Implement a function `sign` that takes a number `n` and returns its sign: 1 if `n` is positive, -1 if `n` is negative, and 0 if `n` is zero.
solution:
(module SignFunction
  (defun sign (n)
    (if (> n 0)
      1
      (if (< n 0)
        -1
        0)))

  (test positive_five (eq? (sign 5) 1))
  (test negative_ten (eq? (sign -10) -1))
  (test zero (eq? (sign 0) 0))
  (test small_positive (eq? (sign 0.001) 1))
  (test small_negative (eq? (sign -0.001) -1))
  (test large_positive (eq? (sign 1234567) 1))
  (test large_negative (eq? (sign -987654) -1)))

objective: Implement a function that calculates a student's final course grade based on their scores in multiple components (e.g., assignments, midterms, final exam) and their respective percentage weights.
solution:
(module CourseGrade
  (defun calculate_weighted_component_score (score weight_percentage)
    (* score (/ weight_percentage 100.0)))

  (defun calculate_final_grade (assignment_score assignment_weight
                                midterm_score midterm_weight
                                final_exam_score final_exam_weight)
    (def weighted_assignment (calculate_weighted_component_score assignment_score assignment_weight))
    (def weighted_midterm (calculate_weighted_component_score midterm_score midterm_weight))
    (def weighted_final_exam (calculate_weighted_component_score final_exam_score final_exam_weight))
    (+ weighted_assignment weighted_midterm weighted_final_exam))

  (test basic_grade_calculation
    (near? (calculate_final_grade 90 20 80 30 75 50) 79.5 0.001)) ; (90*0.2) + (80*0.3) + (75*0.5) = 18 + 24 + 37.5 = 79.5

  (test perfect_score
    (near? (calculate_final_grade 100 25 100 25 100 50) 100.0 0.001)) ; (100*0.25) + (100*0.25) + (100*0.5) = 25 + 25 + 50 = 100.0

  (test failing_score
    (near? (calculate_final_grade 40 10 50 40 30 50) 39.0 0.001)) ; (40*0.1) + (50*0.4) + (30*0.5) = 4 + 20 + 15 = 39.0

  (test uneven_weights
    (near? (calculate_final_grade 85 10 70 60 95 30) 79.0 0.001)) ; (85*0.1) + (70*0.6) + (95*0.3) = 8.5 + 42.0 + 28.5 = 79.0

  (test single_component_dominant
    (near? (calculate_final_grade 60 0 70 0 80 100) 80.0 0.001)) ; (60*0) + (70*0) + (80*1) = 0 + 0 + 80 = 80.0
)

topic: read two env vars and concat
objective: Implement a function that constructs a user agent string by combining a base agent and an application version. These values should be read from environment variables `USER_AGENT_BASE` and `APP_VERSION`, respectively. If an environment variable is not set or empty, a default value should be used before concatenation.
solution:
(module UserAgentBuilder
  (extern defun env_read (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun get_user_agent ()
    (def base_agent_val (env_read "USER_AGENT_BASE"))
    (def app_version_val (env_read "APP_VERSION"))

    (def final_base_agent
      (if (str_eq base_agent_val "")
          "MyClient"
          base_agent_val))

    (def final_app_version
      (if (str_eq app_version_val "")
          "1.0.0"
          app_version_val))

    (str_concat final_base_agent (str_concat "/" final_app_version)))

  (test both_present
    (mocks (env.get "USER_AGENT_BASE" "CustomApp")
           (env.get "APP_VERSION" "2.1.0"))
    (eq? (get_user_agent) "CustomApp/2.1.0"))

  (test base_missing
    (mocks (env.get "USER_AGENT_BASE" "")
           (env.get "APP_VERSION" "1.5.0"))
    (eq? (get_user_agent) "MyClient/1.5.0"))

  (test version_missing
    (mocks (env.get "USER_AGENT_BASE" "MyApp")
           (env.get "APP_VERSION" ""))
    (eq? (get_user_agent) "MyApp/1.0.0"))

  (test both_missing
    (mocks (env.get "USER_AGENT_BASE" "")
           (env.get "APP_VERSION" ""))
    (eq? (get_user_agent) "MyClient/1.0.0"))

  (test with_complex_names
    (mocks (env.get "USER_AGENT_BASE" "Browser/v2")
           (env.get "APP_VERSION" "Alpha Build 3.0"))
    (eq? (get_user_agent) "Browser/v2/Alpha Build 3.0")))

objective: Create a function that reads a specified file and returns its first line. If the file is empty or starts with a newline, the function should return an empty string.
solution:
(module FirstLineReader
  (extern defun file_read (path) @capability "file.read")

  (defun get_first_line (filename)

  (test multiple_lines

    (eq? (get_first_line "multi.txt") "First line"))

  (test single_line

    (eq? (get_first_line "single.txt") "This is the only line"))

  (test empty_file

    (eq? (get_first_line "empty.txt") ""))

  (test file_with_only_newline

    (eq? (get_first_line "just_nl.txt") ""))

  (test file_starts_with_newline

    (eq? (get_first_line "starts_nl.txt") ""))

  (test file_ends_with_newline

    (eq? (get_first_line "ends_nl.txt") "Content line"))
)

objective: Implement a function that checks if a given string contains a specific character. The function should return 1 if the character is found in the string, and 0 otherwise.

(module StringContainsChar

      0

  (test found_at_start (eq? (string_contains_char "hello" "h") 1))
  (test found_in_middle (eq? (string_contains_char "world" "r") 1))
  (test found_at_end (eq? (string_contains_char "test" "t") 1)) ; str_index_of finds first 't' at index 0, still returns 1
  (test not_found (eq? (string_contains_char "example" "z") 0))
  (test empty_string (eq? (string_contains_char "" "a") 0))
  (test single_char_string_match (eq? (string_contains_char "b" "b") 1))

objective: Calculate the length of a given string without using the built-in `str_length` function. The function should iterate through the string, counting characters until it reaches the end.
solution:
(module StringLengthManual
  (defun string_length_manual (s)
    (def len 0)
    (while (not (str_eq (str_substring s len 1) ""))
      (set len (+ len 1)))
    len)

  (test empty_string
    (eq? (string_length_manual "") 0))

  (test single_char
    (eq? (string_length_manual "a") 1))

  (test normal_string
    (eq? (string_length_manual "hello") 5))

  (test string_with_spaces
    (eq? (string_length_manual "hello world") 11))

  (test unicode_chars
    (eq? (string_length_manual "你好世界") 4)))

objective: Calculate the total monthly phone bill based on a base plan cost, included data limit, actual data usage, an overage rate per MB, and a tax rate. The bill should account for any data used beyond the plan's limit, applying the specified overage charge, and then calculate the total including tax.
solution:
(module PhoneBillCalculator
  (defun calculate_overage_cost (actual_usage limit overage_rate)
    (def overage_units (- actual_usage limit))
    (if (> overage_units 0)
      (* overage_units overage_rate)
      0))

  (defun apply_tax (amount tax_percent)
    (* amount (+ 1 (/ tax_percent 100.0))))

  (defun calculate_phone_bill (base_cost data_limit actual_data_usage overage_rate_per_mb tax_rate_percent)
    (def overage_charge (calculate_overage_cost actual_data_usage data_limit overage_rate_per_mb))
    (def subtotal (+ base_cost overage_charge))
    (apply_tax subtotal tax_rate_percent))

  (test no_overage_no_tax (near? (calculate_phone_bill 50 1000 800 0.05 0) 50.0 0.001))
  (test with_overage_no_tax (near? (calculate_phone_bill 50 1000 1200 0.05 0) 60.0 0.001))
  (test with_overage_with_tax (near? (calculate_phone_bill 50 1000 1200 0.05 10) 66.0 0.001))
  (test no_overage_with_tax (near? (calculate_phone_bill 50 1000 900 0.05 10) 55.0 0.001))
  (test exact_limit_with_tax (near? (calculate_phone_bill 50 1000 1000 0.05 10) 55.0 0.001))
  (test higher_base_lower_usage (near? (calculate_phone_bill 75 2000 1500 0.03 5) 78.75 0.001))
  (test zero_overage_rate (near? (calculate_phone_bill 50 1000 1200 0 10) 55.0 0.001))
)

objective: Create a function that fetches the content of a given URL and returns the very first word found in the HTTP response body. If the body is empty or contains only whitespace, an empty string should be returned.
solution:
(module HttpFirstWord
  (extern defun http_fetch (url) @capability "http.fetch")

  ; Helper function to find the first non-empty string in an array of strings.
  ; Returns an empty string if no non-empty strings are found.
  (defun get_first_non_empty (str_array)
    (def i 0)
    (def n (arr_length str_array))
    (while (< i n)
      (def current_str (arr_get str_array i))
      (if (not (str_eq current_str ""))
        (return current_str))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    "")
  
  (defun get_first_word_from_url (url)
    (def body (http_fetch url))
    ; If the body is empty, there's no first word.
    (if (str_eq body "") (return "")) 
    
    ; Split the body by space to get potential words.
    (def words (str_split body " "))
    ; Use the helper to find the first actual word, ignoring empty strings from multiple spaces.
    (get_first_non_empty words))

  (test basic_fetch
    (mocks
      ("http.fetch" "http://example.com/hello" "Hello world! This is a test."))
    (eq? (get_first_word_from_url "http://example.com/hello") "Hello"))

  (test empty_body
    (mocks
      ("http.fetch" "http://example.com/empty" ""))
    (eq? (get_first_word_from_url "http://example.com/empty") ""))

  (test only_spaces
    (mocks
      ("http.fetch" "http://example.com/spaces" "   "))
    (eq? (get_first_word_from_url "http://example.com/spaces") ""))

  (test leading_trailing_spaces
    (mocks
      ("http.fetch" "http://example.com/padded" "  First word here "))
    (eq? (get_first_word_from_url "http://example.com/padded") "First"))

  (test multiple_internal_spaces
    (mocks
      ("http.fetch" "http://example.com/multi-space" "One   Two Three"))
    (eq? (get_first_word_from_url "http://example.com/multi-space") "One"))
  
  (test no_spaces_single_word
    (mocks
      ("http.fetch" "http://example.com/single-word" "JustOneWord"))
    (eq? (get_first_word_from_url "http://example.com/single-word") "JustOneWord"))

  (test complex_punctuation
    (mocks
      ("http.fetch" "http://example.com/punct" "Hello,world!")) ; str_split by space won't split this
    (eq? (get_first_word_from_url "http://example.com/punct") "Hello,world!"))
)

objective: Calculate the total parking cost based on the duration in minutes and an hourly rate, with the first 60 minutes of parking provided for free.
solution:
(module ParkingCost
  (defun get_chargeable_minutes (minutes_parked)
    (math_max 0 (- minutes_parked 60)))

  (defun minutes_to_hours (minutes)
    (/ minutes 60.0))

  (defun calculate_parking_cost (minutes_parked hourly_rate)
    (def chargeable_minutes (get_chargeable_minutes minutes_parked))
    (def chargeable_hours (minutes_to_hours chargeable_minutes))
    (* chargeable_hours hourly_rate))

  (test free_under_an_hour
    (eq? (calculate_parking_cost 30 5.0) 0.0))

  (test exactly_an_hour_free
    (eq? (calculate_parking_cost 60 7.5) 0.0))

  (test one_and_a_half_hours
    (eq? (calculate_parking_cost 90 10.0) 5.0))

  (test two_hours
    (eq? (calculate_parking_cost 120 15.0) 15.0))

  (test partial_hour_after_free
    (near? (calculate_parking_cost 75 12.0) 3.0 0.001))

  (test long_duration
    (near? (calculate_parking_cost 300 8.0) 32.0 0.001)))

objective: Implement a function for linear interpolation (LERP). Given a starting value, an ending value, and an interpolation factor `t`, the function should return the value proportionally between start and end. The factor `t` must be clamped between 0 and 1, inclusive.
solution:
(module Lerp
  (defun lerp (start end t)
    (def clamped_t (math_max 0 (math_min 1 t)))
    (+ (* start (- 1 clamped_t)) (* end clamped_t)))

  (test lerp_at_start (near? (lerp 10.0 20.0 0.0) 10.0 0.001))
  (test lerp_at_end (near? (lerp 10.0 20.0 1.0) 20.0 0.001))
  (test lerp_midpoint (near? (lerp 10.0 20.0 0.5) 15.0 0.001))
  (test lerp_quarter (near? (lerp 0.0 100.0 0.25) 25.0 0.001))
  (test lerp_three_quarter (near? (lerp 0.0 100.0 0.75) 75.0 0.001))
  (test lerp_clamp_negative (near? (lerp 5.0 15.0 -0.1) 5.0 0.001))
  (test lerp_clamp_positive (near? (lerp 5.0 15.0 1.1) 15.0 0.001))
  (test lerp_negative_range (near? (lerp -10.0 -20.0 0.5) -15.0 0.001))
  (test lerp_mixed_range (near? (lerp -10.0 10.0 0.5) 0.0 0.001)))

topic: env var split on comma into count
objective: Implement a function `get_list_item_count` that reads an environment variable specified by name. The function should parse the variable's value as a comma-separated list and return the count of non-empty items in that list. If the environment variable is empty or not found, it should return 0.
solution:
(module EnvListCount
  (extern defun env_read (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun get_list_item_count (env_var_name)
    (def raw_value (env_read env_var_name))
    (if (str_eq raw_value "") (return 0))

    (def parts (str_split raw_value ","))
    (def count 0)
    (def i 0)
    (def n (arr_length parts))

    (while (< i n)
      (def part (arr_get parts i))
      (if (not (str_eq part ""))
        (set count (+ count 1)))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    count)

  (test empty_var
    (mocks (env.get "MY_LIST" ""))
    (eq? (get_list_item_count "MY_LIST") 0))

  (test single_item
    (mocks (env.get "MY_LIST" "item1"))
    (eq? (get_list_item_count "MY_LIST") 1))

  (test multiple_items
    (mocks (env.get "MY_LIST" "item1,item2,item3"))
    (eq? (get_list_item_count "MY_LIST") 3))

  (test trailing_comma
    (mocks (env.get "MY_LIST" "item1,item2,"))
    (eq? (get_list_item_count "MY_LIST") 2))

  (test leading_comma
    (mocks (env.get "MY_LIST" ",item1,item2"))
    (eq? (get_list_item_count "MY_LIST") 2))

  (test only_commas
    (mocks (env.get "MY_LIST" ",,"))
    (eq? (get_list_item_count "MY_LIST") 0))

  (test non_existent_var_returns_empty
    (mocks (env.get "NON_EXISTENT_VAR" "")) ; env_read returns "" if not found
    (eq? (get_list_item_count "NON_EXISTENT_VAR") 0))
)

objective: Implement a function that counts the number of occurrences of the integer zero within a given array of numbers. The function should return this count.
solution:
(module CountZerosInArray
  (defun count_zeros (numbers)
    (def count 0)
    (def i 0)
    (def len (arr_length numbers))
    (while (< i len)
      (def current_num (arr_get numbers i))
      (if (= current_num 0)
        (set count (+ count 1)))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    count)

  (defun make_array_of_five (a b c d e)
    (def arr (arr_new 5))
    (arr_set arr 0 a)
    (arr_set arr 1 b)
    (arr_set arr 2 c)
    (arr_set arr 3 d)
    (arr_set arr 4 e)
    arr)

  (test empty_array
    (eq? (count_zeros (arr_new 0)) 0))

  (test mixed_zeros
    (eq? (count_zeros (make_array_of_five 1 0 3 0 5)) 2))

  (test no_zeros
    (eq? (count_zeros (make_array_of_five 1 2 3 4 5)) 0))

  (test all_zeros
    (eq? (count_zeros (make_array_of_five 0 0 0 0 0)) 5))

  (test negatives_and_zeros
    (eq? (count_zeros (make_array_of_five -1 0 -2 0 -3)) 2))

  (test single_zero_at_start
    (eq? (count_zeros (make_array_of_five 0 10 20 30 40)) 1))
)

topic: paycheck net from hours and rate
objective: Create a module to calculate an employee's net pay. The calculation should account for regular hours, overtime hours (at 1.5x the rate for anything over 40 hours), a percentage-based federal tax deduction, and a fixed benefit deduction. The final net pay should be rounded to two decimal places.
solution:
(module PaycheckCalculator
  ; Helper to round a number to two decimal places
  (defun round_to_cents (amount)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* amount 100) 0.5)) 100))

  ; Calculates gross pay, including overtime at 1.5x for hours over 40
  (defun calculate_gross_pay (hours rate)
    (def regular_hours (math_min hours 40))
    (def overtime_hours (math_max 0 (- hours 40)))
    (def regular_pay (* regular_hours rate))
    (def overtime_pay (* overtime_hours rate 1.5))
    (+ regular_pay overtime_pay))

  ; Calculates the tax amount based on gross pay and a tax percentage
  (defun calculate_tax_amount (gross_pay tax_percentage)
    (* gross_pay tax_percentage))

  ; Calculates the final net pay after all deductions
  (defun calculate_net_pay (hours rate federal_tax_pct benefit_deduction)
    (def gross (calculate_gross_pay hours rate))
    (def federal_tax (calculate_tax_amount gross federal_tax_pct))
    (def net_unrounded (- gross federal_tax benefit_deduction))
    (round_to_cents net_unrounded))

  (test forty_hours_no_deductions
    ; 40 hours @ $20/hr, 0% tax, $0 benefit
    ; Gross: 40 * 20 = 800
    ; Net: 800
    (near? (calculate_net_pay 40 20 0 0) 800 0.01))

  (test overtime_and_deductions
    ; 50 hours @ $20/hr, 15% tax, $50 benefit
    ; Gross: (40 * 20) + (10 * 20 * 1.5) = 800 + 300 = 1100
    ; Fed Tax: 1100 * 0.15 = 165
    ; Net: 1100 - 165 - 50 = 885
    (near? (calculate_net_pay 50 20 0.15 50) 885 0.01))

  (test under_forty_hours_some_deductions
    ; 30 hours @ $25/hr, 10% tax, $25 benefit
    ; Gross: 30 * 25 = 750
    ; Fed Tax: 750 * 0.10 = 75
    ; Net: 750 - 75 - 25 = 650
    (near? (calculate_net_pay 30 25 0.10 25) 650 0.01))

  (test zero_hours_with_deductions
    ; 0 hours @ $20/hr, 10% tax, $10 benefit
    ; Gross: 0
    ; Fed Tax: 0
    ; Net: 0 - 0 - 10 = -10
    (near? (calculate_net_pay 0 20 0.10 10) -10 0.01))

  (test fractional_hours_and_rates
    ; 45.5 hours @ $22.50/hr, 12% tax, $30.50 benefit
    ; Regular pay: 40 * 22.50 = 900.00
    ; Overtime hours: 5.5
    ; Overtime pay: 5.5 * 22.50 * 1.5 = 185.625
    ; Gross: 900.00 + 185.625 = 1085.625
    ; Fed Tax: 1085.625 * 0.12 = 130.275
    ; Net (unrounded): 1085.625 - 130.275 - 30.50 = 924.85
    (near? (calculate_net_pay 45.5 22.50 0.12 30.50) 924.85 0.01))
)

objective: Your task is to implement the classic FizzBuzz problem. Given an integer `n`, return "FizzBuzz" if `n` is divisible by both 3 and 5, "Fizz" if divisible by 3, "Buzz" if divisible by 5, or the number itself as a string otherwise.
solution:
(module FizzBuzz
  (defun fizzbuzz (n)
    (def is_div_by_3 (= (math_mod n 3) 0))
    (def is_div_by_5 (= (math_mod n 5) 0))

    (if is_div_by_3
      (if is_div_by_5
        (return "FizzBuzz")))

    (if is_div_by_3 (return "Fizz"))
    (if is_div_by_5 (return "Buzz"))

    (str_from_num n))

  (test one         (eq? (fizzbuzz 1) "1"))
  (test three       (eq? (fizzbuzz 3) "Fizz"))
  (test five        (eq? (fizzbuzz 5) "Buzz"))
  (test fifteen     (eq? (fizzbuzz 15) "FizzBuzz"))
  (test seven       (eq? (fizzbuzz 7) "7"))
  (test nine        (eq? (fizzbuzz 9) "Fizz"))
  (test ten         (eq? (fizzbuzz 10) "Buzz")))

objective: Create a function that converts a given temperature value from either Celsius or Fahrenheit to Kelvin. The function should accept the numerical temperature and a string indicating the original unit, returning the temperature in Kelvin.
solution:
(module TemperatureConverter
  (defun to_kelvin (value unit_str)
    (def kelvin_val 0.0)
    (if (str_eq unit_str "celsius")
      (set kelvin_val (+ value 273.15)))
    (if (str_eq unit_str "fahrenheit")
      (set kelvin_val (+ (* (- value 32.0) (/ 5.0 9.0)) 273.15)))
    kelvin_val)

  (test celsius_zero (near? (to_kelvin 0.0 "celsius") 273.15 0.001))
  (test celsius_hundred (near? (to_kelvin 100.0 "celsius") 373.15 0.001))
  (test celsius_absolute_zero (near? (to_kelvin -273.15 "celsius") 0.0 0.001))
  (test fahrenheit_freezing (near? (to_kelvin 32.0 "fahrenheit") 273.15 0.001))
  (test fahrenheit_boiling (near? (to_kelvin 212.0 "fahrenheit") 373.15 0.001))
  (test fahrenheit_absolute_zero (near? (to_kelvin -459.67 "fahrenheit") 0.0 0.01))
  (test unknown_unit (eq? (to_kelvin 10.0 "invalid_unit") 0.0)))

objective: Develop a program to calculate a user's recommended daily water intake in ounces. The calculation should be based on their body weight in pounds, an activity level (none, light, moderate, high), and whether the weather is considered hot.
solution:
(module WaterIntakeGoal
  (defun calculate_base_ounces (weight_lbs)
    (/ weight_lbs 2))

  (defun get_activity_bonus (activity_level_str)
    (if (str_eq activity_level_str "none")
        (return 0))
    (if (str_eq activity_level_str "light")
        (return 8))
    (if (str_eq activity_level_str "moderate")
        (return 16))
    (if (str_eq activity_level_str "high")
        (return 24))
    0) ; Default for unrecognized activity, though inputs should be controlled

  (defun get_temperature_bonus (is_hot_str)
    (if (str_eq is_hot_str "true")
        (return 10))
    0)

  (defun calculate_daily_water_goal (weight_lbs activity_level_str is_hot_str)
    (def base (calculate_base_ounces weight_lbs))
    (def activity_bonus (get_activity_bonus activity_level_str))
    (def temp_bonus (get_temperature_bonus is_hot_str))
    (+ base activity_bonus temp_bonus))

  (test standard_case
    (eq? (calculate_daily_water_goal 150 "moderate" "false") 91)) ; 75 (base) + 16 (moderate) + 0 (not hot) = 91

  (test light_activity_no_heat
    (eq? (calculate_daily_water_goal 120 "light" "false") 68)) ; 60 (base) + 8 (light) + 0 (not hot) = 68

  (test high_activity_with_heat
    (eq? (calculate_daily_water_goal 180 "high" "true") 124)) ; 90 (base) + 24 (high) + 10 (hot) = 124

  (test no_activity_no_heat
    (eq? (calculate_daily_water_goal 160 "none" "false") 80)) ; 80 (base) + 0 (none) + 0 (not hot) = 80

  (test minimum_weight_high_activity_hot
    (eq? (calculate_daily_water_goal 100 "high" "true") 84)) ; 50 (base) + 24 (high) + 10 (hot) = 84

  (test moderate_activity_with_heat
    (eq? (calculate_daily_water_goal 140 "moderate" "true") 96))) ; 70 (base) + 16 (moderate) + 10 (hot) = 96

(module HttpBodyLength

objective: Compute the digital root of a non-negative integer. The digital root is the single-digit value obtained by an iterative process of summing digits, on each iteration using the result from the previous iteration as the input to the digit-sum function, until a single-digit number is produced.
solution:
(module DigitalRoot
  (defun digit_sum (n)
    (def acc 0)
    (def cur n)
    (while (> cur 0)
      (set acc (+ acc (math_mod cur 10)))
      (set cur (math_floor (/ cur 10))))
    acc)

  (defun digital_root (n)
    (def current_val n)
    (while (> current_val 9)
      (set current_val (digit_sum current_val)))
    current_val)

  (test zero             (eq? (digital_root 0) 0))
  (test single_digit     (eq? (digital_root 7) 7))
  (test two_digits       (eq? (digital_root 42) 6)) ; 4 + 2 = 6
  (test three_digits     (eq? (digital_root 123) 6)) ; 1 + 2 + 3 = 6
  (test multiple_steps   (eq? (digital_root 999) 9)) ; 9 + 9 + 9 = 27, then 2 + 7 = 9
  (test large_number     (eq? (digital_root 123456789) 9)) ; Sum is 45, then 4 + 5 = 9
  (test another_large    (eq? (digital_root 789123) 3)) ; Sum is 30, then 3 + 0 = 3
)

objective: Implement a function that calculates the area of a triangle given its three side lengths using Heron's formula. The function should return 0 if the provided side lengths are non-positive or do not satisfy the triangle inequality.
solution:
(module TriangleArea
  (defun calculate_area (a b c)
    ;; Check for non-positive sides
    (if (or (<= a 0) (<= b 0) (<= c 0))
      (return 0))

    ;; Check triangle inequality (sum of any two sides must be greater than the third)
    ;; This also implicitly handles degenerate triangles where sum of two sides equals the third,
    ;; making (s - x) term zero and thus area zero.
    (if (or (<= (+ a b) c) (<= (+ a c) b) (<= (+ b c) a))
      (return 0))

    ;; Calculate semi-perimeter
    (def s (/ (+ a b c) 2.0))

    ;; Calculate the value under the square root
    (def area_squared_term (* s (- s a) (- s b) (- s c)))

    ;; Return the square root of the term
    (math_sqrt area_squared_term))

  ;; Test Cases

  ;; Right triangle (3, 4, 5) - Area = 6.0
  (test right_triangle
    (near? (calculate_area 3 4 5) 6.0 0.001))

  ;; Equilateral triangle (2, 2, 2) - Area = sqrt(3) ~ 1.73205
  (test equilateral_triangle
    (near? (calculate_area 2 2 2) 1.73205 0.001))

  ;; Scalene triangle (7, 8, 9) - Area = sqrt(720) ~ 26.83281
  (test scalene_triangle
    (near? (calculate_area 7 8 9) 26.83281 0.001))

  ;; Degenerate triangle (3, 4, 7) - Area should be 0
  (test degenerate_triangle
    (near? (calculate_area 3 4 7) 0.0 0.001))

  ;; Invalid triangle (violates inequality: 1, 2, 5) - Area should be 0
  (test invalid_inequality
    (near? (calculate_area 1 2 5) 0.0 0.001))

  ;; Invalid triangle (negative side: -3, 4, 5) - Area should be 0
  (test negative_side
    (near? (calculate_area -3 4 5) 0.0 0.001))

  ;; Invalid triangle (zero side: 0, 4, 5) - Area should be 0
  (test zero_side
    (near? (calculate_area 0 4 5) 0.0 0.001)))

objective: Create a function that calculates a server's final listening port. It should read a base port from the environment variable "BASE_PORT", defaulting to 8000 if not found or empty. It should also read an optional "PORT_OFFSET" from the environment, defaulting to 0, and add this offset to the base port to determine the final port.
solution:
(module ServerPortCalculator
  (extern defun env_read (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun _read_int_env (key default_value)
    (def val (env_read key))
    (if (str_eq val "") (return default_value))
    (str_to_num val))

  (defun calculate_server_port ()
    (def base_port (_read_int_env "BASE_PORT" 8000))
    (def port_offset (_read_int_env "PORT_OFFSET" 0))
    (+ base_port port_offset))

  (test both_present
    (mocks
      (env.get "BASE_PORT" "9000")
      (env.get "PORT_OFFSET" "100"))
    (eq? (calculate_server_port) 9100))

  (test base_present_offset_missing
    (mocks
      (env.get "BASE_PORT" "9000")
      (env.get "PORT_OFFSET" ""))
    (eq? (calculate_server_port) 9000))

  (test base_missing_offset_present
    (mocks
      (env.get "BASE_PORT" "")
      (env.get "PORT_OFFSET" "50"))
    (eq? (calculate_server_port) 8050))

  (test both_missing
    (mocks
      (env.get "BASE_PORT" "")
      (env.get "PORT_OFFSET" ""))
    (eq? (calculate_server_port) 8000))

  (test base_present_offset_zero
    (mocks
      (env.get "BASE_PORT" "7000")
      (env.get "PORT_OFFSET" "0"))
    (eq? (calculate_server_port) 7000))

  (test negative_offset
    (mocks
      (env.get "BASE_PORT" "8000")
      (env.get "PORT_OFFSET" "-50"))
    (eq? (calculate_server_port) 7950)))

objective: Create a function that takes two file paths, reads the content of each file using a capability, and returns a single string that is the concatenation of their contents.
solution:
(module ConcatenateFiles
  (extern defun file_read (path) @capability "file.read")

  (defun concat_two_files (path1 path2)
    (def content1 (file_read path1))
    (def content2 (file_read path2))
    (str_concat content1 content2))

  (test both_have_content
    (mocks
      (file.read "file1.txt" "Hello, ")
      (file.read "file2.txt" "World!"))
    (eq? (concat_two_files "file1.txt" "file2.txt") "Hello, World!"))

  (test first_empty
    (mocks
      (file.read "empty.txt" "")
      (file.read "file2.txt" "Second part."))
    (eq? (concat_two_files "empty.txt" "file2.txt") "Second part."))

  (test second_empty
    (mocks
      (file.read "file1.txt" "First part.")
      (file.read "empty.txt" ""))
    (eq? (concat_two_files "file1.txt" "empty.txt") "First part."))

  (test both_empty
    (mocks
      (file.read "empty1.txt" "")
      (file.read "empty2.txt" ""))
    (eq? (concat_two_files "empty1.txt" "empty2.txt") ""))

  (test multiline_content
    (mocks
      (file.read "partA.txt" "Line 1\nLine 2")
      (file.read "partB.txt" "\nLine 3"))
    (eq? (concat_two_files "partA.txt" "partB.txt") "Line 1\nLine 2\nLine 3")))

objective: Calculate the perimeter of a rectangle given its width and height. The function should accept two numbers and return their combined perimeter.
solution:
(module RectanglePerimeter
  (defun calculate_perimeter (width height)

objective: Implement the classic FizzBuzz logic for a single given integer. The function should return "FizzBuzz" if the number is divisible by both 3 and 5, "Fizz" if divisible by 3, "Buzz" if divisible by 5, and the number itself as a string otherwise.
solution:
(module FizzBuzz
  (defun fizzbuzz_single (n)
    (def div_by_3 (= (math_mod n 3) 0))
    (def div_by_5 (= (math_mod n 5) 0))
    (if (and div_by_3 div_by_5)
      (return "FizzBuzz"))
    (if div_by_3
      (return "Fizz"))
    (if div_by_5
      (return "Buzz"))
    (str_from_num n))

  (test normal_number (eq? (fizzbuzz_single 1) "1"))
  (test three         (eq? (fizzbuzz_single 3) "Fizz"))
  (test five          (eq? (fizzbuzz_single 5) "Buzz"))
  (test fifteen       (eq? (fizzbuzz_single 15) "FizzBuzz"))
  (test seven         (eq? (fizzbuzz_single 7) "7"))
  (test nine          (eq? (fizzbuzz_single 9) "Fizz"))
  (test ten           (eq? (fizzbuzz_single 10) "Buzz")))

objective: Write a function `get_first_char` that takes a filename as input. It should read the file's content using the `file.read` capability and return the first character of the file. If the file is empty or the read operation returns an empty string, it should also return an empty string.
solution:
(module FileFirstChar

  (test different_file

    (eq? (get_first_char "another.txt") "X")))
