objective: Implement the Euclidean algorithm to find the greatest common divisor (GCD) of two non-negative integers. The function should return the largest positive integer that divides both numbers without a remainder.
solution:
(module EuclideanGCD
  (defun gcd (a b)
    (while (> b 0)
      (def temp b)
      (set b (math_mod a b))
      (set a temp))
    a)

  (test gcd_of_zero_and_positive (eq? (gcd 0 5) 5))
  (test gcd_of_positive_and_zero (eq? (gcd 7 0) 7))
  (test gcd_simple_case (eq? (gcd 10 15) 5))
  (test gcd_one_is_multiple (eq? (gcd 14 28) 14))
  (test gcd_coprime (eq? (gcd 17 13) 1))
  (test gcd_larger_numbers (eq? (gcd 99 66) 33))
  (test gcd_prime_pair (eq? (gcd 101 103) 1)))

topic: monthly subscription cost with annual discount
objective: This module calculates the total cost of a subscription for a given number of months. It applies an annual discount if the subscription duration covers one or more full years, otherwise, the standard monthly rate is used.
solution:
(module SubscriptionCalculator
  (defun calculate_annual_discounted_cost (monthly_rate annual_discount_pct)
    (* monthly_rate 12 (- 1 (/ annual_discount_pct 100))))

  (defun calculate_total_subscription_cost (monthly_rate num_months annual_discount_pct)
    (def full_years (math_floor (/ num_months 12)))
    (def remaining_months (math_mod num_months 12))
    (def cost_for_full_years (* full_years (calculate_annual_discounted_cost monthly_rate annual_discount_pct)))
    (def cost_for_remaining_months (* remaining_months monthly_rate))
    (+ cost_for_full_years cost_for_remaining_months))

  (test five_months_no_discount
    (eq? (calculate_total_subscription_cost 10 5 10) 50))

  (test twelve_months_annual_discount
    (eq? (calculate_total_subscription_cost 10 12 10) 108))

  (test fifteen_months_mixed_discount
    (eq? (calculate_total_subscription_cost 10 15 10) 138))

  (test twenty_four_months_double_annual_discount
    (eq? (calculate_total_subscription_cost 20 24 5) 456))

  (test zero_discount_any_months
    (eq? (calculate_total_subscription_cost 50 7 0) 350))

  (test zero_months_cost
    (eq? (calculate_total_subscription_cost 100 0 15) 0)))

objective: Implement a function to calculate a sales agent's commission based on a tiered structure. Sales up to $1000 earn 5%, sales between $1001 and $5000 earn 7.5%, and sales above $5000 earn 10%. All commission amounts should be rounded to two decimal places.
solution:
(module SalesCommission
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))

  (defun get_tier_rate (sales_amount)
    (if (> sales_amount 5000)
      (return 0.10))
    (if (> sales_amount 1000)
      (return 0.075))
    0.05)

  (defun calculate_commission (sales_amount)
    (def rate (get_tier_rate sales_amount))
    (def raw_commission (* sales_amount rate))
    (round2 raw_commission))

  (test tier1_below_limit (near? (calculate_commission 500) 25.00 0.01))
  (test tier1_at_limit (near? (calculate_commission 1000) 50.00 0.01))
  (test tier2_just_above_limit (near? (calculate_commission 1001) 75.08 0.01)) ; 1001 * 0.075 = 75.075
  (test tier2_mid_range (near? (calculate_commission 3000) 225.00 0.01))
  (test tier2_at_upper_limit (near? (calculate_commission 5000) 375.00 0.01)) ; 5000 * 0.075 = 375
  (test tier3_just_above_limit (near? (calculate_commission 5001) 500.10 0.01)) ; 5001 * 0.10 = 500.1
  (test tier3_high_sales (near? (calculate_commission 12500) 1250.00 0.01)))

topic: depreciation over n years straight line
objective: Calculate the annual depreciation, the accumulated depreciation up to a specified year, and the book value of an asset at that year, using the straight-line depreciation method. The function should return an array containing these three values in order: `[annual_depreciation, accumulated_depreciation, book_value]`.
solution:
(module StraightLineDepreciation
  ;; Calculates the annual depreciation expense
  (defun calculate_annual_depreciation (cost salvage_value useful_life_years)
    (/ (- cost salvage_value) useful_life_years))

  ;; Calculates the accumulated depreciation up to a given year
  (defun calculate_accumulated_depreciation (annual_depreciation useful_life_years current_year)
    (def effective_years (math_min current_year useful_life_years))
    (* annual_depreciation effective_years))

  ;; Calculates the book value of the asset at a given point
  (defun calculate_book_value (cost accumulated_depreciation)
    (- cost accumulated_depreciation))

  ;; Main function to compute all depreciation information
  (defun calculate_depreciation_info (cost salvage_value useful_life_years current_year)
    (def annual_dep (calculate_annual_depreciation cost salvage_value useful_life_years))
    (def accum_dep (calculate_accumulated_depreciation annual_dep useful_life_years current_year))
    (def book_val (calculate_book_value cost accum_dep))
    
    (def result_arr (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set result_arr 0 annual_dep)
    (arr_set result_arr 1 accum_dep)
    (arr_set result_arr 2 book_val)
    result_arr)

  ;; Test Cases
  (test basic_scenario
    (def result (calculate_depreciation_info 10000 1000 5 3))
    (near? (arr_get result 0) 1800 0.01)
    (near? (arr_get result 1) 5400 0.01)
    (near? (arr_get result 2) 4600 0.01))

  (test fully_depreciated
    (def result (calculate_depreciation_info 10000 1000 5 6))
    (near? (arr_get result 0) 1800 0.01)
    (near? (arr_get result 1) 9000 0.01)
    (near? (arr_get result 2) 1000 0.01))

  (test first_year
    (def result (calculate_depreciation_info 10000 1000 5 1))
    (near? (arr_get result 0) 1800 0.01)
    (near? (arr_get result 1) 1800 0.01)
    (near? (arr_get result 2) 8200 0.01))

  (test no_depreciation
    (def result (calculate_depreciation_info 5000 5000 10 5))
    (near? (arr_get result 0) 0 0.01)
    (near? (arr_get result 1) 0 0.01)
    (near? (arr_get result 2) 5000 0.01))

  (test fractional_depreciation
    (def result (calculate_depreciation_info 10000 0 3 2))
    (near? (arr_get result 0) 3333.33 0.01)
    (near? (arr_get result 1) 6666.66 0.01)
    (near? (arr_get result 2) 3333.34 0.01))
)

objective: Implement an iterative function to calculate the Nth Fibonacci number. The function should take a non-negative integer `n` as input and return the `n`-th Fibonacci number, where F(0) = 0 and F(1) = 1.
solution:
(module FibonacciIterative
  (defun fib (n)
    (if (= n 0) (return 0))
    (if (= n 1) (return 1))

    (def a 0) ; Represents F(k-2)
    (def b 1) ; Represents F(k-1)
    (def k 2) ; Loop counter, starting from F(2)

    (while (<= k n)
      (def next_fib (+ a b)) ; Calculate F(k)
      (set a b)             ; Update F(k-2) to F(k-1)
      (set b next_fib)      ; Update F(k-1) to F(k)
      (set k (+ k 1)))
    b)

  (test fib_0 (eq? (fib 0) 0))
  (test fib_1 (eq? (fib 1) 1))
  (test fib_2 (eq? (fib 2) 1))
  (test fib_3 (eq? (fib 3) 2))
  (test fib_5 (eq? (fib 5) 5))
  (test fib_10 (eq? (fib 10) 55))
  (test fib_15 (eq? (fib 15) 610)))

If the variable is not set (i.e., `env.get` returns an empty string), it should return a default timeout of 60 seconds. Otherwise, it should parse the variable's value as a number and return it.
solution:
(module ConfigReader

topic: discounted price after coupon and bulk-buy threshold
objective: Calculate the final price of an item given its unit price, quantity, a bulk-buy discount threshold, the percentage for the bulk discount, and a fixed coupon amount. The bulk discount applies if the quantity exceeds the threshold, and the fixed coupon is applied afterwards, ensuring the price does not go below zero.
solution:
(module DiscountedPriceCalculator
  ;; Helper function to apply a percentage discount to an amount.
  (defun apply_percentage_discount (amount pct)
    (- amount (/ (* amount pct) 100)))

  ;; Helper function to apply a fixed discount to an amount,
  ;; ensuring the result does not fall below zero.
  (defun apply_fixed_discount (amount fixed_amount)
    (math_max 0 (- amount fixed_amount)))

  ;; Calculates the final price of items after potentially applying a bulk discount
  ;; and then a fixed coupon discount.
  (defun calculate_final_price (unit_price quantity bulk_threshold bulk_discount_pct coupon_amount)
    (def base_cost (* unit_price quantity))
    (def cost_after_bulk_discount base_cost)

    ;; Check if the quantity qualifies for a bulk discount
    (if (> quantity bulk_threshold)
      (set cost_after_bulk_discount (apply_percentage_discount base_cost bulk_discount_pct)))

    ;; Apply the fixed coupon discount to the price (which may or may not have had a bulk discount)
    (apply_fixed_discount cost_after_bulk_discount coupon_amount))

  ;; --- Tests ---

  ;; Test case 1: No discounts applied (quantity below threshold, no coupon)
  (test no_discounts
    (eq? (calculate_final_price 10 2 5 10 0) 20)) ; Base: 10 * 2 = 20. No bulk (2 < 5), no coupon.

  ;; Test case 2: Only bulk discount applied (quantity above threshold, no coupon)
  (test only_bulk_discount
    (eq? (calculate_final_price 10 6 5 10 0) 54)) ; Base: 10 * 6 = 60. Bulk (6 > 5): 60 - (60 * 10/100) = 54. No coupon.

  ;; Test case 3: Only coupon discount applied (quantity below threshold, with coupon)
  (test only_coupon_discount
    (eq? (calculate_final_price 10 2 5 10 3) 17)) ; Base: 10 * 2 = 20. No bulk (2 < 5). Coupon: 20 - 3 = 17.

  ;; Test case 4: Both bulk and coupon discounts applied
  (test both_discounts
    (eq? (calculate_final_price 10 6 5 10 3) 51)) ; Base: 10 * 6 = 60. Bulk (6 > 5): 60 - 6 = 54. Coupon: 54 - 3 = 51.

  ;; Test case 5: Coupon discount makes the price go below zero, should be capped at 0
  (test coupon_overshoot
    (eq? (calculate_final_price 5 1 0 0 10) 0)) ; Base: 5 * 1 = 5. Bulk (1 > 0): 5 - (5 * 0/100) = 5. Coupon: 5 - 10 = -5, capped to 0.

  ;; Test case 6: Zero quantity
  (test zero_quantity
    (eq? (calculate_final_price 10 0 5 10 0) 0)) ; Base: 10 * 0 = 0. No bulk, no coupon.

  ;; Test case 7: Larger numbers with both discounts
  (test large_numbers
    (eq? (calculate_final_price 100 100 50 20 500) 7500)) ; Base: 100 * 100 = 10000. Bulk (100 > 50): 10000 - (10000 * 20/100) = 8000. Coupon: 8000 - 500 = 7500.
)

topic: env var split on comma into count
objective: Write a function that reads a comma-separated list of values from an environment variable, then counts the number of non-empty items in that list. An empty environment variable should result in a count of zero.
solution:
(module EnvListCounter
  (extern defun env_read (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun count_non_empty_elements (arr)
    (def count 0)
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i (arr_length arr))
      (def item (arr_get arr i))
      (if (not (str_eq item ""))
        (set count (+ count 1)))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    count)

  (defun get_env_list_count (env_key)
    (def val (env_read env_key))
    (if (str_eq val "") (return 0))
    (def parts (str_split val ","))
    (count_non_empty_elements parts))

  (test empty_env_var
    (mocks (env.get "MY_ITEMS" ""))
    (eq? (get_env_list_count "MY_ITEMS") 0))

  (test single_item
    (mocks (env.get "MY_ITEMS" "apple"))
    (eq? (get_env_list_count "MY_ITEMS") 1))

  (test multiple_items
    (mocks (env.get "MY_ITEMS" "apple,banana,cherry"))
    (eq? (get_env_list_count "MY_ITEMS") 3))

  (test items_with_leading_trailing_commas
    (mocks (env.get "MY_ITEMS" ",apple,banana,"))
    (eq? (get_env_list_count "MY_ITEMS") 2))

  (test items_with_empty_in_middle
    (mocks (env.get "MY_ITEMS" "apple,,banana"))
    (eq? (get_env_list_count "MY_ITEMS") 2))

  (test only_commas
    (mocks (env.get "MY_ITEMS" ",,,,"))
    (eq? (get_env_list_count "MY_ITEMS") 0))

  (test mixed_empty_and_valid
    (mocks (env.get "MY_ITEMS" ",item1,,item2,item3,"))
    (eq? (get_env_list_count "MY_ITEMS") 3))
)

topic: reverse a string character by character
objective: Implement a function that takes a string as input and returns a new string with the characters in reverse order. For example, "hello" should become "olleh".
solution:
(module StringReverse
  (defun reverse_string (s)
    (def len (str_length s))
    (def reversed_str "")
    (def i (- len 1))
    (while (>= i 0)
      (def current_char (str_substring s i 1))
      (set reversed_str (str_concat reversed_str current_char))
      (set i (- i 1)))
    reversed_str)

  (test empty_string
    (eq? (reverse_string "") ""))

  (test single_char_string
    (eq? (reverse_string "a") "a"))

  (test simple_word
    (eq? (reverse_string "hello") "olleh"))

  (test palindrome
    (eq? (reverse_string "madam") "madam"))

  (test string_with_spaces
    (eq? (reverse_string "hello world") "dlrow olleh"))

  (test numbers_as_string
    (eq? (reverse_string "12345") "54321"))

  (test mixed_chars
    (eq? (reverse_string "!@#abc") "cba#@!")))

objective: Write a function that calculates the maximum depth of balanced parentheses within a given string. The depth is the highest number of open parentheses encountered before a matching closing parenthesis.
solution:
(module ParenthesesDepth
  (defun max_paren_depth (s)
    (def max_d 0)
    (def current_d 0)
    (def i 0)
    (def len (str_length s))
    (while (< i len)
      (def char (str_substring s i 1))
      (if (str_eq char "(")
        (set current_d (+ current_d 1)))
      (if (str_eq char ")")
        (set current_d (- current_d 1)))
      (set max_d (math_max max_d current_d))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    max_d)

  (test empty_string (eq? (max_paren_depth "") 0))
  (test no_parentheses (eq? (max_paren_depth "abcde") 0))
  (test simple_pair (eq? (max_paren_depth "()") 1))
  (test nested_triple (eq? (max_paren_depth "((()))") 3))
  (test multiple_levels (eq? (max_paren_depth "()(())") 2))
  (test mixed_content (eq? (max_paren_depth "a(b(c)d)e") 2))
  (test complex_nesting (eq? (max_paren_depth "((()()))") 3)))

objective: Implement a function to calculate the total fare for a ride. The fare comprises a fixed base amount, an additional cost determined by the distance traveled and a per-unit rate, and must always meet a specified minimum fare.
solution:
(module RideFareCalculator
  ;; Calculates the cost solely based on distance and rate per unit.
  (defun calculate_distance_cost (distance rate_per_unit)
    (* distance rate_per_unit))

  ;; Determines the final ride fare considering base fare, distance-based cost, and a minimum fare.
  (defun calculate_total_fare (base_fare distance rate_per_unit min_fare)
    (def distance_cost (calculate_distance_cost distance rate_per_unit))
    (def total_before_min (+ base_fare distance_cost))
    (math_max total_before_min min_fare))

  ;; Test cases
  (test normal_ride_fare
    (near? (calculate_total_fare 2.50 10 1.50 5.00) 17.50 0.01)) ; Base 2.50 + (10 * 1.50) = 17.50. Max(17.50, 5.00) = 17.50

  (test short_ride_min_fare_applies
    (near? (calculate_total_fare 2.50 1 1.50 5.00) 5.00 0.01))   ; Base 2.50 + (1 * 1.50) = 4.00. Max(4.00, 5.00) = 5.00

  (test zero_distance_min_fare_applies
    (near? (calculate_total_fare 2.50 0 1.50 5.00) 5.00 0.01))   ; Base 2.50 + (0 * 1.50) = 2.50. Max(2.50, 5.00) = 5.00

  (test different_rates_and_min
    (near? (calculate_total_fare 3.00 5 2.00 10.00) 13.00 0.01)) ; Base 3.00 + (5 * 2.00) = 13.00. Max(13.00, 10.00) = 13.00

  (test zero_rate_per_unit
    (near? (calculate_total_fare 5.00 10 0.00 8.00) 8.00 0.01))   ; Base 5.00 + (10 * 0.00) = 5.00. Max(5.00, 8.00) = 8.00

  (test min_fare_exactly_met
    (near? (calculate_total_fare 3.00 1 2.00 5.00) 5.00 0.01))   ; Base 3.00 + (1 * 2.00) = 5.00. Max(5.00, 5.00) = 5.00
)

topic: freelance billable hours with overtime multiplier
objective: Calculate the total bill for a freelancer based on hours worked, an hourly rate, an overtime threshold, and an overtime multiplier. Hours beyond the threshold are billed at the multiplied rate.
solution:
(module FreelanceBilling
  ;; A helper function to calculate the cost for a given number of hours at a specific rate.
  (defun calculate_cost (hours rate)
    (* hours rate))

  ;; Calculates the total bill considering regular hours and overtime hours with a multiplier.
  ;; total_hours: The total number of hours worked.
  ;; hourly_rate: The standard rate per hour.
  ;; overtime_threshold: The number of hours after which overtime rates apply.
  ;; overtime_multiplier: The factor by which the hourly rate is increased for overtime hours.
  (defun calculate_total_bill (total_hours hourly_rate overtime_threshold overtime_multiplier)
    (def regular_hours (math_min total_hours overtime_threshold))
    (def overtime_hours (math_max 0 (- total_hours overtime_threshold)))
    (def regular_cost (calculate_cost regular_hours hourly_rate))
    (def overtime_rate (* hourly_rate overtime_multiplier))
    (def overtime_cost (calculate_cost overtime_hours overtime_rate))
    (+ regular_cost overtime_cost))

  ;; Test Case 1: No overtime hours worked.
  (test no_overtime_scenario
    (eq? (calculate_total_bill 30 50 40 1.5) 1500)) ; 30 hours * $50/hr = $1500

  ;; Test Case 2: Exactly at the overtime threshold.
  (test at_threshold_scenario
    (eq? (calculate_total_bill 40 50 40 1.5) 2000)) ; 40 hours * $50/hr = $2000

  ;; Test Case 3: With overtime hours.
  ;; Regular: 40 hours * $50/hr = $2000
  ;; Overtime: (50 - 40) hours * ($50/hr * 1.5) = 10 hours * $75/hr = $750
  ;; Total: $2000 + $750 = $2750
  (test with_overtime_scenario
    (eq? (calculate_total_bill 50 50 40 1.5) 2750))

  ;; Test Case 4: Different rates and thresholds with overtime.
  ;; Regular: 35 hours * $100/hr = $3500
  ;; Overtime: (45 - 35) hours * ($100/hr * 2.0) = 10 hours * $200/hr = $2000
  ;; Total: $3500 + $2000 = $5500
  (test custom_rates_overtime
    (eq? (calculate_total_bill 45 100 35 2.0) 5500))

  ;; Test Case 5: Zero hours worked.
  (test zero_hours_scenario
    (eq? (calculate_total_bill 0 60 40 1.5) 0))

  ;; Test Case 6: Overtime threshold is zero (all hours are overtime).
  ;; Regular: 0 hours
  ;; Overtime: 10 hours * ($75/hr * 1.5) = 10 hours * $112.5 = $1125
  (test zero_threshold_all_overtime
    (eq? (calculate_total_bill 10 75 0 1.5) 1125))
)

topic: freelance billable hours with overtime multiplier
objective: Calculate the total bill for a freelancer based on hours worked, distinguishing between standard hours and overtime hours. Hours worked beyond a standard threshold are billed at a specified overtime multiplier of the base hourly rate.
solution:
(module FreelanceBilling
  (defun calculate_regular_pay (hours_worked base_rate standard_hours)
    (def regular_hours (math_min hours_worked standard_hours))
    (* regular_hours base_rate))

  (defun calculate_overtime_pay (hours_worked base_rate standard_hours overtime_multiplier)
    (def overtime_hours (math_max 0 (- hours_worked standard_hours)))
    (def overtime_rate (* base_rate overtime_multiplier))
    (* overtime_hours overtime_rate))

  (defun calculate_total_bill (hours_worked base_rate standard_hours overtime_multiplier)
    (def regular_bill (calculate_regular_pay hours_worked base_rate standard_hours))
    (def overtime_bill (calculate_overtime_pay hours_worked base_rate standard_hours overtime_multiplier))
    (+ regular_bill overtime_bill))

  (test no_overtime_full_standard
    (near? (calculate_total_bill 40 50 40 1.5) 2000.0 0.001))

  (test no_overtime_below_standard
    (near? (calculate_total_bill 30 50 40 1.5) 1500.0 0.001))

  (test with_overtime_50_hours
    (near? (calculate_total_bill 50 50 40 1.5) 2750.0 0.001)) ; 40*50 + (50-40)*50*1.5 = 2000 + 10*75 = 2000 + 750 = 2750

  (test with_overtime_fractional_hours
    (near? (calculate_total_bill 45.5 60 40 2.0) 3060.0 0.001)) ; 40*60 + (45.5-40)*60*2.0 = 2400 + 5.5*120 = 2400 + 660 = 3060

  (test zero_hours
    (near? (calculate_total_bill 0 75 40 1.5) 0.0 0.001))

  (test high_overtime_hours
    (near? (calculate_total_bill 80 100 40 1.75) 11000.0 0.001)) ; 40*100 + (80-40)*100*1.75 = 4000 + 40*175 = 4000 + 7000 = 11000
)

objective: Implement a function that takes a file path, reads its content, and returns the number of characters in the file. If the file does not exist or is empty, the function should report a size of 0.
solution:
(module FileSize
  (extern defun file_read (path) @capability "file.read")

  (defun get_file_size (filepath)
    (def content (file_read filepath))
    (str_length content))

  (test existing_file
    (mocks
      ("file.read" "data.txt" "Hello, World!"))
    (eq? (get_file_size "data.txt") 13))

  (test empty_file
    (mocks
      ("file.read" "empty.txt" ""))
    (eq? (get_file_size "empty.txt") 0))

  (test non_existent_file
    (mocks
      ("file.read" "nonexistent.txt" "")) ; Capability returns empty string for non-existent files
    (eq? (get_file_size "nonexistent.txt") 0))

  (test file_with_special_chars
    (mocks
      ("file.read" "special.txt" "你好世界"))
    (eq? (get_file_size "special.txt") 4))

  (test file_with_newlines
    (mocks
      ("file.read" "multiline.log" "Line 1\nLine 2"))
    (eq? (get_file_size "multiline.log") 13))

  (test another_short_file
    (mocks
      ("file.read" "abc.txt" "abc"))
    (eq? (get_file_size "abc.txt") 3)))

objective: Implement a function that reads the content of two specified files using a capability and concatenates their contents into a single string. If a file does not exist or is empty, it should contribute an empty string to the concatenation.
solution:
(module ConcatFileContent
  (extern defun file_read (path) @capability "file.read")

  (defun concatenate_files (path1 path2)
    (def content1 (file_read path1))
    (def content2 (file_read path2))
    (str_concat content1 content2))

  (test both_present
    (mocks (file.read "file1.txt" "First part: ")
           (file.read "file2.txt" "Second part."))
    (eq? (concatenate_files "file1.txt" "file2.txt") "First part: Second part."))

  (test first_empty
    (mocks (file.read "file_a.txt" "")
           (file.read "file_b.txt" "Only this part."))
    (eq? (concatenate_files "file_a.txt" "file_b.txt") "Only this part."))

  (test second_empty
    (mocks (file.read "file_x.txt" "This part only")
           (file.read "file_y.txt" ""))
    (eq? (concatenate_files "file_x.txt" "file_y.txt") "This part only"))

  (test both_empty
    (mocks (file.read "empty1.txt" "")
           (file.read "empty2.txt" ""))
    (eq? (concatenate_files "empty1.txt" "empty2.txt") ""))

  (test first_non_existent
    (mocks (file.read "nonexistent.log" "") ; file.read returns "" for non-existent
           (file.read "existing.log" "Found log entry."))
    (eq? (concatenate_files "nonexistent.log" "existing.log") "Found log entry."))

  (test second_non_existent
    (mocks (file.read "existing_data.txt" "Data is here. ")
           (file.read "missing_data.txt" "")) ; file.read returns "" for non-existent
    (eq? (concatenate_files "existing_data.txt" "missing_data.txt") "Data is here. "))
)

objective: Write a function that calculates the discriminant of a quadratic equation in the form `ax^2 + bx + c = 0`. The discriminant, often denoted by the Greek letter delta (Δ), is computed using the formula `b^2 - 4ac`.
solution:
(module QuadraticDiscriminant
  (defun calculate_discriminant (a b c)
    (- (* b b) (* 4 a c)))

  (test positive_discriminant
    (eq? (calculate_discriminant 1 5 6) 1)) ; x^2 + 5x + 6 = 0 -> D = 5^2 - 4*1*6 = 25 - 24 = 1

  (test zero_discriminant
    (eq? (calculate_discriminant 1 4 4) 0)) ; x^2 + 4x + 4 = 0 -> D = 4^2 - 4*1*4 = 16 - 16 = 0

  (test negative_discriminant
    (eq? (calculate_discriminant 1 2 3) -8)) ; x^2 + 2x + 3 = 0 -> D = 2^2 - 4*1*3 = 4 - 12 = -8

  (test complex_coefficients
    (eq? (calculate_discriminant 2 -3 1) 1)) ; 2x^2 - 3x + 1 = 0 -> D = (-3)^2 - 4*2*1 = 9 - 8 = 1

  (test zero_a
    (eq? (calculate_discriminant 0 5 6) 25)) ; 0x^2 + 5x + 6 = 0 (linear) -> D = 5^2 - 4*0*6 = 25

  (test zero_b
    (eq? (calculate_discriminant 1 0 -4) 16)) ; x^2 - 4 = 0 -> D = 0^2 - 4*1*(-4) = 16

  (test zero_c
    (eq? (calculate_discriminant 2 4 0) 16))) ; 2x^2 + 4x = 0 -> D = 4^2 - 4*2*0 = 16

objective: Implement a function that counts how many times the number zero appears in a given array of numbers. The function should return the total count of zeros.
solution:
(module ArrayZeroCounter
  (defun count_zeros (xs)
    (def i 0)
    (def n (arr_length xs))
    (def zero_count 0)
    (while (< i n)
      (def current_val (arr_get xs i))
      (if (= current_val 0)
        (set zero_count (+ zero_count 1)))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    zero_count)

  (test empty_array
    (eq? (count_zeros (arr_new 0)) 0))

  (test no_zeros
    (def arr (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set arr 0 1)
    (arr_set arr 1 2)
    (arr_set arr 2 3)
    (eq? (count_zeros arr) 0))

  (test some_zeros
    (def arr (arr_new 5))
    (arr_set arr 0 0)
    (arr_set arr 1 10)
    (arr_set arr 2 0)
    (arr_set arr 3 -5)
    (arr_set arr 4 0)
    (eq? (count_zeros arr) 3))

  (test all_zeros
    (def arr (arr_new 4))
    (arr_set arr 0 0)
    (arr_set arr 1 0)
    (arr_set arr 2 0)
    (arr_set arr 3 0)
    (eq? (count_zeros arr) 4))

  (test single_zero_element
    (def arr (arr_new 1))
    (arr_set arr 0 0)
    (eq? (count_zeros arr) 1))

  (test single_non_zero_element
    (def arr (arr_new 1))
    (arr_set arr 0 7)
    (eq? (count_zeros arr) 0))

  (test negative_numbers_no_zeros
    (def arr (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set arr 0 -1)
    (arr_set arr 1 -2)
    (arr_set arr 2 -3)
    (eq? (count_zeros arr) 0))
)

topic: read file and count char occurrences
objective: Implement a function that reads the entire content of a file and determines how many times a specific single character appears within it. The character count should be case-sensitive.
solution:
(module CharCounter
  (extern defun file_read (path) @capability "file.read")

  (defun count_char_occurrences (path char_to_find)
    (def content (file_read path))
    (def n (str_length content))
    (def count 0)
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i n)
      (def current_char (str_substring content i 1))
      (if (str_eq current_char char_to_find)
        (set count (+ count 1)))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    count)

  (test empty_file
    (mocks (file.read "/empty.txt" ""))
    (eq? (count_char_occurrences "/empty.txt" "a") 0))

  (test no_occurrence
    (mocks (file.read "/text1.txt" "hello world"))
    (eq? (count_char_occurrences "/text1.txt" "x") 0))

  (test single_occurrence
    (mocks (file.read "/text2.txt" "apple"))
    (eq? (count_char_occurrences "/text2.txt" "a") 1))

  (test multiple_occurrences
    (mocks (file.read "/text3.txt" "banana republic"))
    (eq? (count_char_occurrences "/text3.txt" "a") 3))

  (test case_sensitivity
    (mocks (file.read "/text4.txt" "Apple Pie"))
    (eq? (count_char_occurrences "/text4.txt" "p") 2))

  (test case_sensitivity_different_case
    (mocks (file.read "/text4.txt" "Apple Pie"))
    (eq? (count_char_occurrences "/text4.txt" "P") 1))

  (test with_newlines
    (mocks (file.read "/text5.txt" "line1\nline2\nline1"))
    (eq? (count_char_occurrences "/text5.txt" "1") 2)))

topic: discounted price after coupon and bulk-buy threshold
objective: Calculate the final price of items given a unit price, quantity, a bulk-purchase threshold for a percentage discount, and an optional coupon code for another percentage discount. Discounts are applied sequentially: first bulk, then coupon. The result should be rounded to two decimal places.
solution:
(module DiscountCalculator
  (defun calculate_subtotal (unit_price qty)
    (* unit_price qty))

  (defun apply_percentage_discount (amount percentage)
    (- amount (/ (* amount percentage) 100)))

  (defun round_to_cents (amount)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* amount 100) 0.5)) 100))

  (defun calculate_final_price (unit_price quantity bulk_threshold bulk_discount_pct coupon_code valid_coupon coupon_discount_pct)
    (def subtotal (calculate_subtotal unit_price quantity))
    (def price_after_bulk subtotal)
    (if (>= quantity bulk_threshold)
      (set price_after_bulk (apply_percentage_discount subtotal bulk_discount_pct)))

    (def final_price price_after_bulk)
    (if (str_eq coupon_code valid_coupon)
      (set final_price (apply_percentage_discount price_after_bulk coupon_discount_pct)))

    (round_to_cents final_price))

  (test no_discounts
    (near? (calculate_final_price 10 5 10 10 "NONE" "SAVE10" 10) 50.00 0.01))

  (test only_bulk_discount
    (near? (calculate_final_price 10 12 10 10 "NONE" "SAVE10" 10) 108.00 0.01))

  (test only_coupon_discount
    (near? (calculate_final_price 10 5 10 10 "SAVE10" "SAVE10" 10) 45.00 0.01))

  (test both_discounts
    (near? (calculate_final_price 10 12 10 10 "SAVE10" "SAVE10" 10) 97.20 0.01))

  (test at_threshold_no_coupon
    (near? (calculate_final_price 10 10 10 15 "INVALID" "SAVE20" 20) 85.00 0.01))

  (test zero_quantity
    (near? (calculate_final_price 10 0 10 10 "SAVE10" "SAVE10" 10) 0.00 0.01))

  (test different_percentages_and_values
    (near? (calculate_final_price 25 5 4 20 "GIVEME5" "GIVEME5" 5) 95.00 0.01))
)

objective: Implement a function to calculate the minimum payment for a credit card. The minimum payment is determined by taking the greater of a specified percentage of the outstanding balance or a fixed minimum dollar amount, but will never exceed the total outstanding balance itself.
solution:
(module CreditCardMinPayment

  ;; Helper to round a number to two decimal places
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))

  ;; Calculates the minimum payment for a credit card
  ;; balance: Current outstanding balance
  ;; pct_rate: Minimum payment percentage (e.g., 0.02 for 2%)
  ;; fixed_min_amount: Fixed minimum dollar amount
  (defun calculate_minimum_payment (balance pct_rate fixed_min_amount)
    (def percentage_portion (* balance pct_rate))
    (def max_of_percentage_or_fixed (math_max percentage_portion fixed_min_amount))
    (def final_min_payment (math_min max_of_percentage_or_fixed balance))
    (round2 final_min_payment))

  ;; Tests
  ;; Test case 1: Percentage portion is greater than fixed minimum
  (test pct_greater
    (near? (calculate_minimum_payment 2000 0.02 25) 40.00 0.01))

  ;; Test case 2: Fixed minimum is greater than percentage portion
  (test fixed_greater
    (near? (calculate_minimum_payment 1000 0.02 25) 25.00 0.01))

  ;; Test case 3: Balance is less than the calculated minimum
  (test balance_less_than_min
    (near? (calculate_minimum_payment 15 0.02 25) 15.00 0.01))

  ;; Test case 4: Balance is exactly the fixed minimum
  (test balance_equals_fixed_min
    (near? (calculate_minimum_payment 25 0.02 25) 25.00 0.01))

  ;; Test case 5: Balance is exactly the percentage calculation (e.g., 1250 * 0.02 = 25)
  (test balance_equals_pct_min
    (near? (calculate_minimum_payment 1250 0.02 25) 25.00 0.01))

  ;; Test case 6: Larger balance, higher percentage
  (test higher_balance_pct
    (near? (calculate_minimum_payment 5000 0.03 35) 150.00 0.01))

  ;; Test case 7: Zero balance
  (test zero_balance
    (near? (calculate_minimum_payment 0 0.02 25) 0.00 0.01)))

objective: Implement a function that takes an array of numbers and a scalar value, then returns a new array where each element from the original array has been multiplied by the scalar.
solution:
(module ScalarMultiply
  ;; Helper function to create a 3-element array for testing convenience.
  (defun make_array_3 (a b c)
    (def xs (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set xs 0 a)
    (arr_set xs 1 b)
    (arr_set xs 2 c)
    xs)

  ;; Multiplies each element of an array by a given scalar.
  ;; Returns a new array with the results.
  (defun scalar_multiply (arr scalar)
    (def len (arr_length arr))
    (def result_arr (arr_new len))
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i len)
      (def element (arr_get arr i))
      (def multiplied_element (* element scalar))
      (arr_set result_arr i multiplied_element)
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    result_arr)

  (test empty_array
    (eq?
      (scalar_multiply (arr_new 0) 5)
      (arr_new 0)))

  (test positive_numbers
    (eq?
      (scalar_multiply (make_array_3 1 2 3) 2)
      (make_array_3 2 4 6)))

  (test negative_scalar
    (eq?
      (scalar_multiply (make_array_3 1 -2 3) -2)
      (make_array_3 -2 4 -6)))

  (test zero_scalar
    (eq?
      (scalar_multiply (make_array_3 10 20 30) 0)
      (make_array_3 0 0 0)))

  (test fractional_scalar
    (eq?
      (scalar_multiply (make_array_3 2 4 8) 0.5)
      (make_array_3 1 2 4)))

  (test mixed_values_and_one_scalar
    (eq?
      (scalar_multiply (make_array_3 -5 0 5) 1)
      (make_array_3 -5 0 5)))
)

objective: Calculate the total parking cost based on a given duration in minutes and an hourly rate. The first hour of parking is free, and any time after the first hour is charged per full or partial hour.
solution:
(module ParkingCostCalculator
  ;; Helper function to determine the number of hours to charge for,
  ;; considering the first hour is free.
  ;; If total_minutes is 60 or less, 0 hours are charged.
  ;; Otherwise, the remaining minutes are rounded up to the nearest hour.
  (defun get_chargeable_hours (total_minutes)
    (if (<= total_minutes 60)
        (return 0))
    (def remaining_minutes (- total_minutes 60))
    ;; Ensure float division for math_ceil to work correctly for fractional hours.
    (math_ceil (/ remaining_minutes 60.0)))

  ;; Calculates the total parking cost given the duration in minutes
  ;; and the hourly rate. The first hour of parking is free.
  ;; Subsequent hours (or any part thereof) are charged at the full hourly rate.
  (defun calculate_parking_cost (duration_minutes hourly_rate)
    (def actual_charge_hours (get_chargeable_hours duration_minutes))
    (* actual_charge_hours hourly_rate))

  ;; Tests
  (test zero_duration
    (eq? (calculate_parking_cost 0 10.0) 0.0))

  (test under_hour_free
    (eq? (calculate_parking_cost 30 10.0) 0.0))

  (test exactly_hour_free
    (eq? (calculate_parking_cost 60 10.0) 0.0))

  (test one_minute_over_hour
    (eq? (calculate_parking_cost 61 10.0) 10.0))

  (test exactly_two_hours
    (eq? (calculate_parking_cost 120 10.0) 10.0))

  (test two_hours_one_minute
    (eq? (calculate_parking_cost 121 10.0) 20.0))

  (test longer_duration_decimal_rate
    ;; 150 minutes = 2 hours 30 minutes. First hour free, remaining 90 minutes.
    ;; 90 minutes rounds up to 2 chargeable hours. 2 * 7.5 = 15.0
    (near? (calculate_parking_cost 150 7.5) 15.0 0.01))
)

objective: Calculate the total monthly phone bill for a customer. The bill consists of a base monthly fee and an additional charge for data usage exceeding a specified included amount, at a given overage rate per gigabyte.
solution:
(module PhoneBillCalculator

    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))

  (defun calculate_overage_cost (actual_usage_gb included_data_gb overage_rate_per_gb)
    (def overage_gb (math_max 0 (- actual_usage_gb included_data_gb)))
    (* overage_gb overage_rate_per_gb))

  (defun calculate_total_bill (base_fee included_data_gb overage_rate_per_gb actual_usage_gb)
    (def overage_charge (calculate_overage_cost actual_usage_gb included_data_gb overage_rate_per_gb))
    (round2 (+ base_fee overage_charge)))

  (test no_overage
    (eq? (calculate_total_bill 30 10 5 8) 30.00))

  (test exact_limit
    (eq? (calculate_total_bill 30 10 5 10) 30.00))

  (test some_overage
    (eq? (calculate_total_bill 30 10 5 12) 40.00)) ; 30 + (2 * 5) = 40

  (test significant_overage
    (eq? (calculate_total_bill 45 20 7.5 30) 120.00)) ; 45 + (10 * 7.5) = 45 + 75 = 120

  (test fractional_overage
    (near? (calculate_total_bill 25 5 4.5 7.3) 35.35 0.001)) ; 25 + (2.3 * 4.5) = 25 + 10.35 = 35.35

  (test zero_overage_rate
    (eq? (calculate_total_bill 50 15 0 20) 50.00)))

objective: Write a function `calculate_percentage_change` that computes the percentage change between an `old_value` and a `new_value`. If both values are zero, the change is 0%. If `old_value` is zero and `new_value` is non-zero, it signifies an infinite change, and the function should return 1.0e9.
solution:
(module PercentageChange
  (defun calculate_percentage_change (old_value new_value)
    (if (and (= old_value 0.0) (= new_value 0.0))
      (return 0.0)) ; 0% change if both are zero
    (if (= old_value 0.0)
      (return 1.0e9)) ; Represents infinite change from zero to non-zero
    (* (/ (- new_value old_value) old_value) 100.0))

  (test increase
    (near? (calculate_percentage_change 100.0 120.0) 20.0 0.001))
  (test decrease
    (near? (calculate_percentage_change 100.0 80.0) -20.0 0.001))
  (test no_change
    (near? (calculate_percentage_change 50.0 50.0) 0.0 0.001))
  (test zero_to_zero
    (near? (calculate_percentage_change 0.0 0.0) 0.0 0.001))
  (test zero_to_positive
    (near? (calculate_percentage_change 0.0 10.0) 1.0e9 0.001)) ; Infinite increase
  (test positive_to_zero
    (near? (calculate_percentage_change 10.0 0.0) -100.0 0.001))
  (test negative_base_change
    (near? (calculate_percentage_change -10.0 -20.0) 100.0 0.001)) ; (-20 - -10) / -10 = -10 / -10 = 1 => 100%
)

objective: Implement a function to calculate the digital root of an integer. The digital root is the single-digit value obtained by an iterative process of summing digits, repeating the process with the sum until a single-digit number is reached. For negative inputs, calculate the digital root of its absolute value.
solution:
(module DigitalRoot
  (defun sum_digits (n)
    (def total 0)
    (while (> n 0)
      (set total (+ total (math_mod n 10)))
      (set n (math_floor (/ n 10))))
    total)

  (defun calculate_digital_root (n)
    (if (< n 0) (set n (math_abs n))) ; Take absolute value for negative inputs
    (while (>= n 10)
      (set n (sum_digits n)))
    n)

  (test zero (eq? (calculate_digital_root 0) 0))
  (test single_digit (eq? (calculate_digital_root 7) 7))
  (test two_digits_one_step (eq? (calculate_digital_root 18) 9)) ; 1+8 = 9
  (test multi_digits_two_steps (eq? (calculate_digital_root 12345) 6)) ; 1+2+3+4+5 = 15, then 1+5 = 6
  (test large_number (eq? (calculate_digital_root 987654321) 9)) ; Sums to 45, then 4+5 = 9
  (test negative_input (eq? (calculate_digital_root -42) 6)) ; abs(-42) = 42, then 4+2 = 6
)

objective: Implement a function `to_lower_case` that takes a string and returns its lowercase equivalent. The function must process the input string character by character using an explicit loop, building the result iteratively without using any built-in whole-string lowercase conversion.
solution:
(module ManualLower
  (defun to_lower_case (s)
    (def result "")
    (def i 0)
    (def len (str_length s))
    (while (< i len)
      (def current_char (str_substring s i 1))
      (def lower_char (str_lower current_char)) ; str_lower applied per character
      (set result (str_concat result lower_char))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    result)

  (test empty_string (eq? (to_lower_case "") ""))
  (test all_uppercase (eq? (to_lower_case "HELLO WORLD") "hello world"))
  (test mixed_case (eq? (to_lower_case "AgC PrOgRaMmInG") "agc programming"))
  (test all_lowercase (eq? (to_lower_case "already lowercase") "already lowercase"))
  (test with_numbers_symbols (eq? (to_lower_case "123 Test!@#") "123 test!@#"))
  (test single_char_upper (eq? (to_lower_case "Z") "z"))
  (test single_char_lower (eq? (to_lower_case "a") "a")))

objective: Create a function that reads the content of a specified file and returns only its first line. If the file is empty or cannot be read, an empty string should be returned.
solution:
(module FirstLineReader
  (extern defun file_read (path) @capability "file.read")

  (defun get_first_line (file_path)
    (def content (file_read file_path))
    ; If content is empty (e.g., file doesn't exist, is truly empty, or read failed), return empty string.
    ; This also handles str_split behavior where splitting "" might yield [""] or [].
    (if (str_eq content "") (return ""))
    (def lines (str_split content "\n"))
    ; After splitting, the first element of the array is the first line.
    (arr_get lines 0))

  (test multi_line_file
    (mocks (file.read "data.txt" "First Line\nSecond Line\nThird Line"))
    (eq? (get_first_line "data.txt") "First Line"))

  (test single_line_no_newline
    (mocks (file.read "single.txt" "This is the only line."))
    (eq? (get_first_line "single.txt") "This is the only line."))

  (test single_line_with_trailing_newline
    (mocks (file.read "trailing_nl.txt" "Last line ends with newline\n"))
    (eq? (get_first_line "trailing_nl.txt") "Last line ends with newline"))

  (test empty_file
    (mocks (file.read "empty.txt" ""))
    (eq? (get_first_line "empty.txt") ""))

  (test file_with_only_newlines
    (mocks (file.read "just_nls.txt" "\n\n\n"))
    (eq? (get_first_line "just_nls.txt") ""))

  (test non_existent_file
    ; Mocking file.read to return an empty string for a non-existent file
    (mocks (file.read "nonexistent.txt" ""))
    (eq? (get_first_line "nonexistent.txt") ""))
)

objective: Read an environment variable representing a timeout in seconds. If the variable is not set or is an empty string, return a default timeout of 30 seconds. The value should always be a non-negative integer. If the environment variable contains a negative number, return 0.
solution:
(module GetTimeoutSeconds
  (extern defun env_read (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun get_timeout (key)
    (def default_timeout 30)
    (def env_val (env_read key))
    (if (str_eq env_val "") (return default_timeout))
    (def num_val (str_to_num env_val))
    (if (< num_val 0) (return 0))
    num_val)

  (test missing_var (mocks (env.get "APP_TIMEOUT" ""))
    (eq? (get_timeout "APP_TIMEOUT") 30))

  (test present_valid (mocks (env.get "APP_TIMEOUT" "60"))
    (eq? (get_timeout "APP_TIMEOUT") 60))

  (test present_zero (mocks (env.get "APP_TIMEOUT" "0"))
    (eq? (get_timeout "APP_TIMEOUT") 0))

  (test present_negative (mocks (env.get "APP_TIMEOUT" "-10"))
    (eq? (get_timeout "APP_TIMEOUT") 0))

  (test another_key (mocks (env.get "DB_TIMEOUT" "120"))
    (eq? (get_timeout "DB_TIMEOUT") 120)))

objective: Calculate the total fuel cost for a trip given the total distance, the vehicle's fuel efficiency, and the price of fuel per unit. The calculation should handle various trip lengths and fuel costs accurately.
solution:
(module TripFuelCost

  (defun calculate_fuel_needed (distance efficiency)
    ;; Calculates the total amount of fuel (e.g., gallons or liters) required for the trip.
    ;; distance: total distance to travel (e.g., miles or km)
    ;; efficiency: fuel efficiency of the vehicle (e.g., MPG or km/L)
    (/ distance efficiency))

  (defun calculate_total_cost (fuel_amount price_per_unit)
    ;; Calculates the total cost of the fuel required.
    ;; fuel_amount: total units of fuel needed
    ;; price_per_unit: cost per unit of fuel
    (* fuel_amount price_per_unit))

  (defun trip_fuel_cost (distance efficiency price_per_unit)
    ;; Orchestrates the calculation of the total fuel cost for a trip.
    ;; distance: total distance to travel
    ;; efficiency: fuel efficiency of the vehicle
    ;; price_per_unit: cost per unit of fuel
    (def fuel_required (calculate_fuel_needed distance efficiency))
    (calculate_total_cost fuel_required price_per_unit))

  (test basic_trip_cost
    ;; 100 miles, 25 MPG, $3.50/gallon -> 4 gallons * $3.50 = $14.00
    (near? (trip_fuel_cost 100 25 3.50) 14.00 0.01))

  (test long_distance_trip
    ;; 500 miles, 30 MPG, $4.25/gallon -> 16.666... gallons * $4.25 = $70.833...
    (near? (trip_fuel_cost 500 30 4.25) 70.83 0.01))

  (test short_commute
    ;; 10 miles, 50 MPG, $3.00/gallon -> 0.2 gallons * $3.00 = $0.60
    (near? (trip_fuel_cost 10 50 3.00) 0.60 0.01))

  (test zero_distance_trip
    ;; 0 miles, 20 MPG, $4.00/gallon -> 0 gallons * $4.00 = $0.00
    (near? (trip_fuel_cost 0 20 4.00) 0.00 0.01))

  (test zero_price_fuel
    ;; 200 miles, 25 MPG, $0.00/gallon -> 8 gallons * $0.00 = $0.00
    (near? (trip_fuel_cost 200 25 0.00) 0.00 0.01))

  (test high_efficiency_vehicle
    ;; 300 miles, 60 MPG, $4.10/gallon -> 5 gallons * $4.10 = $20.50
    (near? (trip_fuel_cost 300 60 4.10) 20.50 0.01))
)

topic: discounted price after coupon and bulk-buy threshold
objective: Calculate the final price of an order, applying a bulk-purchase discount if the quantity meets a specified threshold, and then applying an additional coupon discount to the resulting amount.
solution:
(module ProductDiscount

  (defun calculate_subtotal (unit_price quantity)
    (* unit_price quantity))

  (defun apply_bulk_discount (amount quantity threshold bulk_discount_percent)
    (if (>= quantity threshold)
        (* amount (- 1 (/ bulk_discount_percent 100)))
        amount))

  (defun apply_coupon (amount coupon_percent)
    (* amount (- 1 (/ coupon_percent 100))))

  (defun calculate_final_price (unit_price quantity bulk_threshold bulk_discount_percent coupon_percent)
    (def subtotal_amount (calculate_subtotal unit_price quantity))
    (def after_bulk (apply_bulk_discount subtotal_amount quantity bulk_threshold bulk_discount_percent))
    (apply_coupon after_bulk coupon_percent))

  (test no_discounts
    (near? (calculate_final_price 10 5 10 10 0) 50.0 0.01))

  (test only_bulk
    (near? (calculate_final_price 10 12 10 10 0) 108.0 0.01))

  (test only_coupon
    (near? (calculate_final_price 10 5 10 0 5) 47.5 0.01))

  (test both_discounts
    (near? (calculate_final_price 10 12 10 10 5) 102.6 0.01))

  (test zero_quantity
    (near? (calculate_final_price 10 0 10 10 5) 0.0 0.01))

  (test large_order
    (near? (calculate_final_price 25.5 200 100 15 10) 3901.5 0.01))
)

objective: Given a URL, perform an HTTP GET request and return the HTTP status code as a string. The HTTP fetch capability returns a string in the format "STATUS_CODE BODY_CONTENT".
solution:

  (extern defun http_fetch (url) @capability "http.fetch")

  (defun get_http_status_code (url)
    (def response_str (http_fetch url))
    (def parts (str_split response_str " "))
    (arr_get parts 0))

  (test success_200
    (mocks (http.fetch "http://example.com/resource" "200 OK All good"))
    (eq? (get_http_status_code "http://example.com/resource") "200"))

  (test not_found_404

    (eq? (get_http_status_code "http://example.com/missing") "404"))

  (test server_error_500

    (eq? (get_http_status_code "http://example.com/fail") "500"))

  (test redirect_301
    (mocks (http.fetch "http://old.com" "301 Moved Permanently https://new.com/"))
    (eq? (get_http_status_code "http://old.com") "301"))

  (test no_body_200

  (test another_success_200

    (eq? (get_http_status_code "http://api.service/data") "200"))
)

objective: Implement a function `is_vowel` that determines if a given single-character string is a vowel (a, e, i, o, u), ignoring case. The function should return 1 if it is a vowel, and 0 otherwise.
solution:
(module VowelChecker
  (defun is_vowel (char_str)
    (def lower_char (str_lower char_str))
    (if (str_eq lower_char "a") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq lower_char "e") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq lower_char "i") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq lower_char "o") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq lower_char "u") (return 1))
    0)

  (test lowercase_a (eq? (is_vowel "a") 1))
  (test uppercase_E (eq? (is_vowel "E") 1))
  (test lowercase_b (eq? (is_vowel "b") 0))
  (test uppercase_Z (eq? (is_vowel "Z") 0))
  (test another_vowel (eq? (is_vowel "I") 1))
  (test non_alpha_char (eq? (is_vowel "$") 0))
  (test num_char (eq? (is_vowel "7") 0)))

objective: Calculate the parking fee for a given duration and hourly rate, where the first hour of parking is free. Any duration beyond the first free hour is rounded up to the nearest whole hour for billing purposes.
solution:
(module ParkingCostCalculator

  ; Helper to determine how many hours are billable after the first free hour.
  ; Rounds up to the nearest whole hour for billing.
  (defun calculate_billable_hours (actual_hours)
    (if (<= actual_hours 1)
      0
      (math_ceil (- actual_hours 1)))) ; Subtract 1 for the free hour, then ceil the remainder

  ; Calculates the total parking cost based on actual parked hours and an hourly rate.
  ; The first hour of parking is free. Subsequent hours are rounded up for billing.
  (defun calculate_parking_cost (actual_hours hourly_rate)
    (def billable_hours (calculate_billable_hours actual_hours))
    (* billable_hours hourly_rate))

  ; Test Cases
  (test less_than_one_hour_free (eq? (calculate_parking_cost 0.5 10) 0))
  (test exactly_one_hour_free (eq? (calculate_parking_cost 1.0 15) 0))
  (test just_over_one_hour_rounded_up (eq? (calculate_parking_cost 1.1 10) 10)) ; 0.1 becomes 1 billable hour
  (test two_full_hours (eq? (calculate_parking_cost 2.0 20) 20)) ; 1.0 becomes 1 billable hour
  (test two_and_half_hours_rounded_up (eq? (calculate_parking_cost 2.5 10) 20)) ; 1.5 becomes 2 billable hours
  (test multiple_hours_fractional (eq? (calculate_parking_cost 3.7 12) 36)) ; 2.7 becomes 3 billable hours
  (test zero_hours (eq? (calculate_parking_cost 0 5) 0)))

objective: Create a function that reads an environment variable and interprets its string value as a boolean. It should return `true` for "true" (case-insensitive) and `false` for "false" (case-insensitive). If the environment variable is not set (empty string), it should return a provided default boolean. Any other string value should also resolve to `false`.
solution:
(module EnvVarToBool
  (extern defun env_read (key) @capability "env.get")

  ;; Helper to convert a string to a boolean value.
  ;; "true" (case-insensitive) -> true
  ;; "false" (case-insensitive) -> false
  ;; Any other string -> false
  (defun string_to_bool (s)
    (def lower_s (str_lower s))
    (if (str_eq lower_s "true") (return true))
    (if (str_eq lower_s "false") (return false))
    false)

  ;; Reads an environment variable and returns its boolean interpretation.
  ;; If the variable is not set (empty string), `default_value` is returned.
  ;; Otherwise, the string is converted to a boolean using `string_to_bool`.
  (defun get_env_bool (key default_value)
    (def val (env_read key))
    (if (str_eq val "") (return default_value))
    (string_to_bool val))

  (test default_true_missing
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_X" ""))
    (eq? (get_env_bool "FEATURE_X" true) true))

  (test default_false_missing
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_Y" ""))
    (eq? (get_env_bool "FEATURE_Y" false) false))

  (test present_true_lowercase
    (mocks (env.get "DEBUG_MODE" "true"))
    (eq? (get_env_bool "DEBUG_MODE" false) true))

  (test present_true_uppercase
    (mocks (env.get "DEBUG_MODE" "TRUE"))
    (eq? (get_env_bool "DEBUG_MODE" false) true))

  (test present_false_lowercase
    (mocks (env.get "LOGGING" "false"))
    (eq? (get_env_bool "LOGGING" true) false))

  (test present_false_uppercase
    (mocks (env.get "LOGGING" "FALSE"))
    (eq? (get_env_bool "LOGGING" true) false))

  (test present_invalid_string
    (mocks (env.get "ANALYTICS_ENABLED" "yes"))
    (eq? (get_env_bool "ANALYTICS_ENABLED" true) false)))

topic: count occurrences of a character in a string
objective: Write a function `count_char_occurrences` that takes a string and a single-character string (the character to count) as input. It should return the total number of times the specified character appears in the input string, performing a case-sensitive count.
solution:
(module CharCounter
  (defun count_char_occurrences (text char_to_find)
    (def count 0)
    (def index 0)
    (def text_length (str_length text))

    (while (< index text_length)
      (def current_char (str_substring text index 1))
      (if (str_eq current_char char_to_find)
        (set count (+ count 1)))
      (set index (+ index 1)))
    count)

  (test basic_count
    (eq? (count_char_occurrences "hello world" "o") 2))

  (test no_occurrences
    (eq? (count_char_occurrences "programming" "x") 0))

  (test empty_string
    (eq? (count_char_occurrences "" "a") 0))

  (test single_occurrence
    (eq? (count_char_occurrences "apple" "p") 2))

  (test case_sensitive
    (eq? (count_char_occurrences "Apple" "a") 0))

  (test multiple_same_char
    (eq? (count_char_occurrences "aaaaa" "a") 5))

  (test char_at_ends
    (eq? (count_char_occurrences "banana" "b") 1)))

objective: Write an AGC function `digital_root` that computes the digital root of a non-negative integer. The digital root is the single-digit value obtained by repeatedly summing the digits of a number until a single-digit result is achieved.
solution:
(module DigitalRoot
  (defun sum_digits_of_num (n)
    (def acc 0)
    (def cur n)
    (while (> cur 0)
      (set acc (+ acc (math_mod cur 10)))
      (set cur (math_floor (/ cur 10))))
    acc)

  (defun digital_root (n)
    (def current_val n)
    (while (>= current_val 10)
      (set current_val (sum_digits_of_num current_val)))
    current_val)

  (test zero       (eq? (digital_root 0) 0))
  (test single_digit (eq? (digital_root 7) 7))
  (test two_digits   (eq? (digital_root 42) 6)) ; 4 + 2 = 6
  (test three_digits (eq? (digital_root 123) 6)) ; 1 + 2 + 3 = 6
  (test multi_step  (eq? (digital_root 12345) 6)) ; 1+2+3+4+5=15, 1+5=6
  (test nines        (eq? (digital_root 999) 9)) ; 9+9+9=27, 2+7=9
  (test large_number (eq? (digital_root 789123) 3))) ; 7+8+9+1+2+3=30, 3+0=3

objective: Create a function that counts the number of lines in a file by reading its content. The function should correctly handle empty files, files with a single line (with or without a trailing newline), and files with multiple lines, accurately reflecting the number of logical lines.
solution:
(module LineCounterSplit
  (extern defun file_read (path) @capability "file.read")

  (defun count_lines_in_file (path)
    (def content (file_read path))
    (if (str_eq content "") (return 0))
    (def lines (str_split content "\n"))
    (def num_segments (arr_length lines))
    (if (and (> num_segments 0) (str_eq (arr_get lines (- num_segments 1)) ""))
      (return (- num_segments 1)))
    num_segments)

  (test empty_file
    (mocks (file.read "/path/to/empty.txt" ""))
    (eq? (count_lines_in_file "/path/to/empty.txt") 0))

  (test single_line_no_newline
    (mocks (file.read "/path/to/single.txt" "Hello World"))
    (eq? (count_lines_in_file "/path/to/single.txt") 1))

  (test single_line_with_newline
    (mocks (file.read "/path/to/single_nl.txt" "First line\n"))
    (eq? (count_lines_in_file "/path/to/single_nl.txt") 1))

  (test multi_line_no_trailing_newline
    (mocks (file.read "/path/to/multi.txt" "Line one\nLine two\nLine three"))
    (eq? (count_lines_in_file "/path/to/multi.txt") 3))

  (test multi_line_with_trailing_newline
    (mocks (file.read "/path/to/multi_nl.txt" "Apple\nBanana\nCherry\n"))
    (eq? (count_lines_in_file "/path/to/multi_nl.txt") 3))

  (test file_with_only_newlines
    (mocks (file.read "/path/to/nls.txt" "\n\n\n"))
    (eq? (count_lines_in_file "/path/to/nls.txt") 3))

  (test file_with_initial_newline
    (mocks (file.read "/path/to/initial_nl.txt" "\nFirst line"))
    (eq? (count_lines_in_file "/path/to/initial_nl.txt") 2)))

objective: Find the index of the first character in a string that is not a space. If the string is empty or contains only spaces, return -1.
solution:
(module FindFirstNonSpace
  (defun find_first_non_space_index (s)
    (def len (str_length s))
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i len)
      (def char (str_substring s i 1))
      (if (str_eq char " ")
        (set i (+ i 1))
        (return i)))
    -1)

  (test simple_start (eq? (find_first_non_space_index "hello world") 0))
  (test leading_spaces (eq? (find_first_non_space_index "  hello") 2))
  (test all_spaces (eq? (find_first_non_space_index "   ") -1))
  (test empty_string (eq? (find_first_non_space_index "") -1))
  (test single_char (eq? (find_first_non_space_index "x") 0))
  (test trailing_spaces (eq? (find_first_non_space_index "  x  ") 2))
  (test no_spaces (eq? (find_first_non_space_index "abc") 0)))

objective: Implement a function that calculates the percentage change between an old value and a new value. The function should handle the case where the old value is zero: if both are zero, the change is 0%; if only the old value is zero, it represents an effectively infinite change.
solution:
(module PercentageChangeCalculator
  (defun calculate_percentage_change (old_value new_value)
    (if (= old_value 0.0)
      (if (= new_value 0.0)
        (return 0.0)
        (return 999999999.0))) ; Effectively infinite change
    (* (/ (- new_value old_value) old_value) 100.0))

  (test increase
    (near? (calculate_percentage_change 100.0 120.0) 20.0 0.001))
  (test decrease
    (near? (calculate_percentage_change 100.0 80.0) -20.0 0.001))
  (test no_change
    (near? (calculate_percentage_change 50.0 50.0) 0.0 0.001))
  (test large_increase
    (near? (calculate_percentage_change 10.0 100.0) 900.0 0.001))
  (test large_decrease
    (near? (calculate_percentage_change 100.0 10.0) -90.0 0.001))
  (test decimal_values
    (near? (calculate_percentage_change 10.5 11.0) 4.7619047619 0.001))
  (test zero_to_zero
    (near? (calculate_percentage_change 0.0 0.0) 0.0 0.001))
  (test zero_to_nonzero
    (near? (calculate_percentage_change 0.0 50.0) 999999999.0 0.001)))

objective: Implement a function `sign` that takes a single number `x` and returns `1` if `x` is positive, `-1` if `x` is negative, and `0` if `x` is zero.
solution:
(module SignFunction
  (defun sign (x)
    (if (> x 0)
      (return 1))
    (if (< x 0)
      (return -1))
    0)

  (test positive_integer (eq? (sign 10) 1))
  (test negative_integer (eq? (sign -5) -1))
  (test zero_value (eq? (sign 0) 0))
  (test positive_float (eq? (sign 3.14) 1))
  (test negative_float (eq? (sign -0.001) -1))
  (test large_positive (eq? (sign 987654) 1))
  (test large_negative (eq? (sign -123456) -1)))

topic: read file and count char occurrences
objective: Write a function that reads the content of a file and counts the occurrences of a specified character within that file. The character count should be case-sensitive.
solution:
(module FileCharCounter
  (extern defun file_read (path) @capability "file.read")

  (defun count_char_in_file (file_path char_to_find)
    (def content (file_read file_path))
    (def content_length (str_length content))
    (def count 0)
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i content_length)
      (def current_char (str_substring content i 1))
      (if (str_eq current_char char_to_find)
        (set count (+ count 1)))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    count)

  (test empty_file_no_char
    (mocks (file.read "/path/empty.txt" ""))
    (eq? (count_char_in_file "/path/empty.txt" "a") 0))

  (test basic_count
    (mocks (file.read "/path/doc1.txt" "hello world"))
    (eq? (count_char_in_file "/path/doc1.txt" "o") 2))

  (test multiple_occurrences
    (mocks (file.read "/path/fruits.txt" "banana"))
    (eq? (count_char_in_file "/path/fruits.txt" "a") 3))

  (test no_occurrences
    (mocks (file.read "/path/test.txt" "xyz123"))
    (eq? (count_char_in_file "/path/test.txt" "a") 0))

  (test case_sensitive
    (mocks (file.read "/path/mixed.txt" "AppleSauce"))
    (eq? (count_char_in_file "/path/mixed.txt" "a") 1))

  (test special_char
    (mocks (file.read "/path/symbols.txt" "a!b@c#d$"))
    (eq? (count_char_in_file "/path/symbols.txt" "#") 1))

  (test long_string_and_char
    (mocks (file.read "/path/lorem.txt" "Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit."))
    (eq? (count_char_in_file "/path/lorem.txt" "i") 6)))

objective: Implement a function that reads an environment variable and trims any leading or trailing space characters from its value. The function should return the cleaned string, handling cases where the variable is empty or consists solely of spaces.
solution:
(module TrimmedEnvReader
  (extern defun env_read (key) @capability "env.get")

  ;; Helper function to remove leading space characters from a string.
  (defun trim_left_spaces (s)
    (def len (str_length s))
    (def start_idx 0)
    (while (< start_idx len)
      (if (str_eq (str_substring s start_idx 1) " ")
        (set start_idx (+ start_idx 1))
        (return (str_substring s start_idx (- len start_idx)))))
    ; If all characters were spaces or string was empty, return empty string.
    "")

  ;; Helper function to remove trailing space characters from a string.
  (defun trim_right_spaces (s)
    (def len (str_length s))
    (def end_idx (- len 1))
    (while (>= end_idx 0)
      (if (str_eq (str_substring s end_idx 1) " ")
        (set end_idx (- end_idx 1))
        (return (str_substring s 0 (+ end_idx 1)))))
    ; If all characters were spaces or string was empty, return empty string.
    "")

  ;; Combines left and right trimming for space characters.
  (defun trim_spaces (s)
    (trim_right_spaces (trim_left_spaces s)))

  ;; Reads an environment variable using env_read and returns its value
  ;; with all leading and trailing space characters removed.
  ;; If the variable is not set or empty, it returns an empty string.
  (defun get_trimmed_env_var (key)
    (def raw_val (env_read key))
    (trim_spaces raw_val))

  (test val_with_leading_spaces
    (mocks (env.get "APP_NAME" "  My App"))
    (eq? (get_trimmed_env_var "APP_NAME") "My App"))

  (test val_with_trailing_spaces
    (mocks (env.get "APP_NAME" "My App  "))
    (eq? (get_trimmed_env_var "APP_NAME") "My App"))

  (test val_with_both_leading_and_trailing_spaces
    (mocks (env.get "APP_NAME" "  My App  "))
    (eq? (get_trimmed_env_var "APP_NAME") "My App"))

  (test val_with_no_spaces
    (mocks (env.get "APP_NAME" "MyApp"))
    (eq? (get_trimmed_env_var "APP_NAME") "MyApp"))

  (test val_is_all_spaces
    (mocks (env.get "APP_NAME" "   "))
    (eq? (get_trimmed_env_var "APP_NAME") ""))

  (test val_is_empty
    (mocks (env.get "APP_NAME" ""))
    (eq? (get_trimmed_env_var "APP_NAME") ""))

  (test val_with_internal_spaces_preserved
    (mocks (env.get "MESSAGE" "  Hello World!  "))
    (eq? (get_trimmed_env_var "MESSAGE") "Hello World!"))
)

objective: Write a program that calculates the minimum payment due on a credit card. The minimum payment is determined by taking the greater of a fixed minimum amount or a specified percentage of the outstanding balance, and then rounding the result to two decimal places.
solution:
(module CreditCardPayment
  (defun round_to_cents (amount)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* amount 100) 0.5)) 100))

  (defun calculate_percentage_portion (balance percentage_rate)
    (* balance percentage_rate))

  (defun calculate_minimum_payment (balance percentage_rate fixed_min_amount)
    (def percentage_payment (calculate_percentage_portion balance percentage_rate))
    (def raw_min_payment (math_max percentage_payment fixed_min_amount))
    (round_to_cents raw_min_payment))

  (test low_balance_fixed_min
    (near? (calculate_minimum_payment 1000 0.01 25.00) 25.00 0.001))
  (test high_balance_percentage_min
    (near? (calculate_minimum_payment 5000 0.02 25.00) 100.00 0.001))
  (test edge_case_equal
    (near? (calculate_minimum_payment 2500 0.01 25.00) 25.00 0.001))
  (test zero_balance
    (near? (calculate_minimum_payment 0 0.01 25.00) 25.00 0.001))
  (test fractional_percentage_result
    (near? (calculate_minimum_payment 1234.56 0.03 30.00) 37.04 0.001))
  (test large_balance_high_percentage
    (near? (calculate_minimum_payment 15000 0.05 50.00) 750.00 0.001))
)

objective: Implement a function `string_to_lower` that converts an input string to its lowercase equivalent. The function must perform this conversion manually by iterating through each character, identifying uppercase English letters ('A' through 'Z'), and replacing them with their lowercase forms, while leaving all other characters unchanged.
solution:
(module ManualStringToLower
  ;; Helper function to convert a single character to lowercase
  ;; Only converts 'A'-'Z', returns original character for others.
  (defun char_to_lower (c)
    (if (str_eq c "A") (return "a"))
    (if (str_eq c "B") (return "b"))
    (if (str_eq c "C") (return "c"))
    (if (str_eq c "D") (return "d"))
    (if (str_eq c "E") (return "e"))
    (if (str_eq c "F") (return "f"))
    (if (str_eq c "G") (return "g"))
    (if (str_eq c "H") (return "h"))
    (if (str_eq c "I") (return "i"))
    (if (str_eq c "J") (return "j"))
    (if (str_eq c "K") (return "k"))
    (if (str_eq c "L") (return "l"))
    (if (str_eq c "M") (return "m"))
    (if (str_eq c "N") (return "n"))
    (if (str_eq c "O") (return "o"))
    (if (str_eq c "P") (return "p"))
    (if (str_eq c "Q") (return "q"))
    (if (str_eq c "R") (return "r"))
    (if (str_eq c "S") (return "s"))
    (if (str_eq c "T") (return "t"))
    (if (str_eq c "U") (return "u"))
    (if (str_eq c "V") (return "v"))
    (if (str_eq c "W") (return "w"))
    (if (str_eq c "X") (return "x"))
    (if (str_eq c "Y") (return "y"))
    (if (str_eq c "Z") (return "z"))
    c)

  ;; Main function to convert an entire string to lowercase manually
  (defun string_to_lower (input_str)
    (def i 0)
    (def len (str_length input_str))
    (def result_str "")
    (while (< i len)
      (def current_char (str_substring input_str i 1))
      (def lower_char (char_to_lower current_char))
      (set result_str (str_concat result_str lower_char))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    result_str)

  (test empty_string
    (eq? (string_to_lower "") ""))

  (test all_uppercase
    (eq? (string_to_lower "HELLO WORLD") "hello world"))

  (test all_lowercase
    (eq? (string_to_lower "hello world") "hello world"))

  (test mixed_case
    (eq? (string_to_lower "AgC PrOgRaMmInG") "agc programming"))

  (test numbers_and_symbols
    (eq? (string_to_lower "123!@#$ ABC def") "123!@#$ abc def"))

  (test single_uppercase
    (eq? (string_to_lower "X") "x"))

  (test single_lowercase
    (eq? (string_to_lower "y") "y")))

objective: Write a function that counts the number of lines in a given file. A line is defined as a sequence of characters ending with a newline character, or the final sequence of characters if the file does not end with a newline. An empty file contains zero lines.
solution:
(module FileLineCounter
  (extern defun file_read (path) @capability "file.read")

  (defun count_lines_split (path)
    (def content (file_read path))
    (if (str_eq content "")
      (return 0))

    (def lines_arr (str_split content "\n"))
    (def num_segments (arr_length lines_arr))

    ; Determine if the original content ends with a newline character.
    ; This is used to adjust the count from str_split, which may produce
    ; an extra empty string segment if the content ends with a delimiter.
    (def content_length (str_length content))
    (def last_char_index (- content_length 1))
    (def ends_with_newline (and (> content_length 0) (str_eq (str_substring content last_char_index 1) "\n")))

    (if ends_with_newline
      ; If content ends with newline (e.g., "a\n" -> ["a", ""]),
      ; str_split yields one extra segment, so subtract 1.
      (- num_segments 1)
      ; Otherwise (e.g., "a" -> ["a"]), the segment count is correct.
      num_segments))

  (test empty_file
    (mocks (file.read "/empty.txt" ""))
    (eq? (count_lines_split "/empty.txt") 0))

  (test single_line_no_newline
    (mocks (file.read "/single.txt" "hello world"))
    (eq? (count_lines_split "/single.txt") 1))

  (test single_line_with_newline
    (mocks (file.read "/single_nl.txt" "hello\n"))
    (eq? (count_lines_split "/single_nl.txt") 1))

  (test multiple_lines_no_newline_end
    (mocks (file.read "/multi.txt" "line one\nline two\nline three"))
    (eq? (count_lines_split "/multi.txt") 3))

  (test multiple_lines_with_newline_end
    (mocks (file.read "/multi_nl.txt" "line A\nline B\nline C\n"))
    (eq? (count_lines_split "/multi_nl.txt") 3))

  (test only_newline
    (mocks (file.read "/only_nl.txt" "\n"))
    (eq? (count_lines_split "/only_nl.txt") 1))

  (test only_two_newlines
    (mocks (file.read "/only_two_nl.txt" "\n\n"))
    (eq? (count_lines_split "/only_two_nl.txt") 2)))

topic: read file and count char occurrences
objective: Write a function that reads the content of a specified file and counts the number of times a given character appears within it. The character comparison should be case-sensitive.
solution:
(module CharOccurrenceCounter

  (defun count_char_occurrences (filepath target_char)
    (def content (file_read filepath))
    (def content_length (str_length content))
    (def count 0)
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i content_length)
      (def current_char (str_substring content i 1))
      (if (str_eq current_char target_char)
        (set count (+ count 1)))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    count)

  (test empty_file_no_char
    (mocks (file.read "/tmp/empty.txt" ""))
    (eq? (count_char_occurrences "/tmp/empty.txt" "a") 0))

  (test basic_count

  (test no_occurrence

  (test case_sensitive

  (test single_char_file

  (test multiple_lines

)

objective: Develop a module that accurately scales ingredient quantities for a recipe. Given an original ingredient quantity, the original number of servings the recipe yields, and a target number of servings, the module should calculate and return the proportionally adjusted ingredient quantity.
solution:
(module RecipeScaler
  ; Calculates the factor by which to scale ingredients
  (defun calculate_scaling_factor (original_servings target_servings)
    (/ target_servings original_servings))

  ; Scales a single ingredient quantity by a given factor
  (defun scale_ingredient_quantity (original_qty scaling_factor)
    (* original_qty scaling_factor))

  ; Main function to scale an ingredient quantity for a recipe based on servings
  (defun scale_recipe_item (original_qty original_servings target_servings)
    (def factor (calculate_scaling_factor original_servings target_servings))
    (scale_ingredient_quantity original_qty factor))

  ; Tests
  (test scale_up_simple
    (near? (scale_recipe_item 2 4 8) 4.0 0.001)) ; 2 units for 4 servings, scale to 8 servings -> 4 units

  (test scale_down_simple
    (near? (scale_recipe_item 10 5 2) 4.0 0.001)) ; 10 units for 5 servings, scale to 2 servings -> 4 units

  (test scale_to_same_servings
    (near? (scale_recipe_item 5 4 4) 5.0 0.001)) ; Scale to same servings, quantity should be unchanged

  (test decimal_original_quantity
    (near? (scale_recipe_item 1.5 3 6) 3.0 0.001)) ; 1.5 units for 3 servings, scale to 6 servings -> 3 units

  (test decimal_result_quantity
    (near? (scale_recipe_item 7 2 3) 10.5 0.001)) ; 7 units for 2 servings, scale to 3 servings -> 10.5 units

  (test original_servings_is_one
    (near? (scale_recipe_item 3 1 5) 15.0 0.001)) ; 3 units for 1 serving, scale to 5 servings -> 15 units
)

objective: Implement a function that calculates the digital root of a non-negative integer. The digital root is the single-digit value obtained by an iterative process of summing digits, on each iteration using the result from the previous iteration as the input to the next, until a single-digit number is produced.
solution:
(module DigitalRoot
  (defun sum_digits (n)
    (def acc 0)
    (def cur n)
    (while (> cur 0)
      (set acc (+ acc (math_mod cur 10)))
      (set cur (math_floor (/ cur 10))))
    acc)

  (defun digital_root (n)
    (if (< n 0)
      (return 0)) ; Digital root is typically defined for non-negative integers.

    (def current_num n)
    (while (> current_num 9)
      (set current_num (sum_digits current_num)))
    current_num)

  (test zero_input (eq? (digital_root 0) 0))
  (test single_digit (eq? (digital_root 7) 7))
  (test two_digits_simple (eq? (digital_root 10) 1)) ; 1 + 0 = 1
  (test two_digits_sum (eq? (digital_root 42) 6)) ; 4 + 2 = 6
  (test multiple_iterations (eq? (digital_root 999) 9)) ; 9+9+9 = 27; 2+7 = 9
  (test larger_number (eq? (digital_root 1987) 7)) ; 1+9+8+7 = 25; 2+5 = 7
  (test large_number_multi_iter (eq? (digital_root 123456789) 9))) ; Sum = 45; 4+5 = 9

objective: Write a function that calculates the maximum nesting depth of balanced parentheses in a given string. The depth should be 0 if no parentheses are present.
solution:
(module ParenthesesDepth
  (defun max_depth (s)
    (def max_d 0)
    (def current_d 0)
    (def i 0)
    (def len (str_length s))
    (while (< i len)
      (def char (str_substring s i 1))
      (if (str_eq char "(")
        (set current_d (+ current_d 1))
        (if (> current_d max_d)
          (set max_d current_d)))
      (if (str_eq char ")")
        (set current_d (- current_d 1)))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    max_d)

  (test empty_string
    (eq? (max_depth "") 0))

  (test no_parentheses
    (eq? (max_depth "abcde") 0))

  (test simple_pair
    (eq? (max_depth "()") 1))

  (test nested_once
    (eq? (max_depth "(())") 2))

  (test multiple_at_same_level
    (eq? (max_depth "()()()") 1))

  (test complex_nesting
    (eq? (max_depth "((()())())") 3))

  (test deeper_complex_nesting
    (eq? (max_depth "(((())))") 4)))

topic: discounted price after coupon and bulk-buy threshold
objective: Calculate the final price of an order after applying a bulk-purchase discount (if quantity meets a threshold) and a coupon code discount (if a valid coupon is provided). Discounts are applied sequentially: bulk first, then coupon.
solution:
(module OrderPriceCalculator
  (defun calculate_item_total (price_per_item quantity)
    (* price_per_item quantity))

  (defun apply_percentage_discount (amount percentage)
    (* amount (- 1 (/ percentage 100.0))))

  (defun calculate_final_price (price_per_item quantity bulk_threshold bulk_discount_pct coupon_code valid_coupon_code coupon_discount_pct)
    (def current_total (calculate_item_total price_per_item quantity))

    ; Apply bulk discount if quantity meets the threshold
    (if (>= quantity bulk_threshold)
      (set current_total (apply_percentage_discount current_total bulk_discount_pct)))

    ; Apply coupon discount if coupon code is valid
    (if (str_eq coupon_code valid_coupon_code)
      (set current_total (apply_percentage_discount current_total coupon_discount_pct)))

    current_total)

  (test no_discounts
    (near? (calculate_final_price 10.0 5 10 10 "NOCPN" "SAVEBIG" 20) 50.0 0.001))

  (test only_bulk_discount
    (near? (calculate_final_price 10.0 15 10 10 "NOCPN" "SAVEBIG" 20) 135.0 0.001)) ; (10 * 15) * (1 - 0.10) = 150 * 0.9 = 135

  (test only_coupon_discount
    (near? (calculate_final_price 10.0 5 10 10 "SAVEBIG" "SAVEBIG" 20) 40.0 0.001)) ; (10 * 5) * (1 - 0.20) = 50 * 0.8 = 40

  (test both_discounts
    (near? (calculate_final_price 10.0 15 10 10 "SAVEBIG" "SAVEBIG" 20) 108.0 0.001)) ; ((10 * 15) * 0.9) * 0.8 = 135 * 0.8 = 108

  (test bulk_threshold_exact_and_coupon
    (near? (calculate_final_price 10.0 10 10 10 "SAVEBIG" "SAVEBIG" 20) 72.0 0.001)) ; ((10 * 10) * 0.9) * 0.8 = 90 * 0.8 = 72

  (test bulk_met_invalid_coupon
    (near? (calculate_final_price 10.0 12 10 10 "WRONG" "SAVEBIG" 20) 108.0 0.001)) ; (10 * 12) * 0.9 = 120 * 0.9 = 108
)

objective: Implement a function that calculates the minimum payment due on a credit card. The minimum payment is the greater of a fixed minimum amount or a percentage of the current balance, but it can never exceed the current balance itself.
solution:
(module CreditCardPayment
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))

  (defun calculate_percentage_amount (balance percentage_rate)
    (* balance (/ percentage_rate 100.0)))

  (defun determine_min_payment (balance percentage_rate fixed_minimum)
    (def pct_min (calculate_percentage_amount balance percentage_rate))
    (def greater_of_two (math_max pct_min fixed_minimum))
    (def final_payment (math_min greater_of_two balance))
    (round2 final_payment))

  (test case_fixed_min_applies
    (near? (determine_min_payment 1000 1 25) 25 0.01))

  (test case_percentage_min_applies
    (near? (determine_min_payment 1000 5 25) 50 0.01))

  (test case_balance_is_less_than_min
    (near? (determine_min_payment 15 1 25) 15 0.01))

  (test case_zero_balance
    (near? (determine_min_payment 0 2 25) 0 0.01))

  (test case_small_balance_percentage_applies
    (near? (determine_min_payment 300 10 25) 30 0.01))

  (test case_small_balance_fixed_applies
    (near? (determine_min_payment 100 1 25) 25 0.01))
)

topic: tip calculation split among diners with rounding
objective: Calculate the individual share of a restaurant bill, including a specified tip percentage, split among a given number of diners. Each diner's share must be rounded up to the nearest cent.
solution:
(module TipSplitter
  (defun round_up_to_cents (amount)
    (/ (math_ceil (* amount 100)) 100.0))

  (defun calculate_individual_share (bill_amount tip_percentage num_diners)
    (def tip_multiplier (/ tip_percentage 100.0))
    (def tip_amount (* bill_amount tip_multiplier))
    (def total_bill (+ bill_amount tip_amount))
    (def raw_per_person_share (/ total_bill num_diners))
    (round_up_to_cents raw_per_person_share))

  (test simple_case
