objective: Calculate the total monthly phone bill, including a base fee, data overage charges, minute overage charges, and a percentage-based tax. Data and minute overages are charged per unit only if the usage exceeds the included allowance.
solution:
(module PhoneBillCalculator
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))

  (defun calculate_data_overage_cost (used_data_mb included_data_mb rate_per_mb)
    (if (> used_data_mb included_data_mb)
      (* (- used_data_mb included_data_mb) rate_per_mb)
      0))

  (defun calculate_minute_overage_cost (used_minutes included_minutes rate_per_min)
    (if (> used_minutes included_minutes)
      (* (- used_minutes included_minutes) rate_per_min)
      0))

  (defun apply_tax (amount tax_rate_pct)
    (* amount (+ 1 (/ tax_rate_pct 100))))

  (defun calculate_monthly_bill (base_fee included_data_mb data_overage_rate_per_mb used_data_mb included_minutes minute_overage_rate_per_min used_minutes tax_rate_pct)
    (def data_overage_cost (calculate_data_overage_cost used_data_mb included_data_mb data_overage_rate_per_mb))
    (def minute_overage_cost (calculate_minute_overage_cost used_minutes included_minutes minute_overage_rate_per_min))
    (def subtotal (+ base_fee data_overage_cost minute_overage_cost))
    (round2 (apply_tax subtotal tax_rate_pct)))

  (test no_overages_no_tax
    (near? (calculate_monthly_bill 30 1000 0.05 500 500 0.10 200 0) 30.00 0.01))

  (test data_overage_only_with_tax
    (near? (calculate_monthly_bill 30 1000 0.05 1200 500 0.10 300 8) 43.20 0.01))
    ; base_fee=30, data_overage=(1200-1000)*0.05 = 200*0.05 = 10
    ; subtotal = 30+10 = 40
    ; tax = 40 * 0.08 = 3.2
    ; total = 40 + 3.2 = 43.2

  (test minute_overage_only_with_tax
    (near? (calculate_monthly_bill 30 1000 0.05 900 500 0.10 600 8) 43.20 0.01))
    ; base_fee=30, minute_overage=(600-500)*0.10 = 100*0.10 = 10
    ; subtotal = 30+10 = 40
    ; tax = 40 * 0.08 = 3.2
    ; total = 40 + 3.2 = 43.2

  (test both_overages_with_tax
    (near? (calculate_monthly_bill 30 1000 0.05 1500 500 0.10 700 8) 81.00 0.01))
    ; base_fee=30, data_overage=(1500-1000)*0.05 = 500*0.05 = 25
    ; minute_overage=(700-500)*0.10 = 200*0.10 = 20
    ; subtotal = 30+25+20 = 75
    ; tax = 75 * 0.08 = 6
    ; total = 75 + 6 = 81

  (test exactly_at_limits_with_tax
    (near? (calculate_monthly_bill 30 1000 0.05 1000 500 0.10 500 8) 32.40 0.01))
    ; base_fee=30, overages=0
    ; subtotal = 30
    ; tax = 30 * 0.08 = 2.4
    ; total = 30 + 2.4 = 32.4

  (test below_limits_with_tax
    (near? (calculate_monthly_bill 30 1000 0.05 999 500 0.10 499 8) 32.40 0.01))
    ; base_fee=30, overages=0
    ; subtotal = 30
    ; tax = 30 * 0.08 = 2.4
    ; total = 30 + 2.4 = 32.4
)

objective: Create an AGC function `count_lines_by_split` that reads a file and returns the number of lines it contains. A line is defined as a sequence of characters terminated by a newline character, or the final sequence of characters if the file does not end with a newline. An empty file has zero lines.
solution:
(module LineCounterSplit
  (extern defun file_read (path) @capability "file.read")

  (defun count_lines_by_split (path)
    (def content (file_read path))
    
    (if (str_eq content "") (return 0))

    (def parts (str_split content "\n"))
    (def num_parts (arr_length parts))

    (def last_char_index (- (str_length content) 1))
    (def last_char (str_substring content last_char_index 1))

    (if (str_eq last_char "\n")
        (- num_parts 1)
        num_parts))

  (test empty_file
    (mocks (file.read "/test/empty.txt" ""))
    (eq? (count_lines_by_split "/test/empty.txt") 0))

  (test single_line_no_newline
    (mocks (file.read "/test/single_no_nl.txt" "Hello World"))
    (eq? (count_lines_by_split "/test/single_no_nl.txt") 1))

  (test single_line_with_newline
    (mocks (file.read "/test/single_with_nl.txt" "First line\n"))
    (eq? (count_lines_by_split "/test/single_with_nl.txt") 1))

  (test multiple_lines_no_newline
    (mocks (file.read "/test/multi_no_nl.txt" "Line one\nLine two\nLine three"))
    (eq? (count_lines_by_split "/test/multi_no_nl.txt") 3))

  (test multiple_lines_with_newline
    (mocks (file.read "/test/multi_with_nl.txt" "Line one\nLine two\nLine three\n"))
    (eq? (count_lines_by_split "/test/multi_with_nl.txt") 3))
  
  (test only_newlines
    (mocks (file.read "/test/only_nl.txt" "\n\n"))
    (eq? (count_lines_by_split "/test/only_nl.txt") 2)))

topic: ticket pricing tiered by age group
objective: Write a function that calculates the ticket price for an individual based on their age and a base price. Infants (0-2 years old) are free, children (3-12 years old) pay 50% of the base price, seniors (65 years and older) pay 75% of the base price, and all others (adults 13-64 years old) pay the full base price.
solution:
(module TieredTicketPricing
  (defun calculate_ticket_price (age base_price)
    (if (< age 3)
      (return 0)) ; Infants (0-2) are free
    (if (<= age 12)
      (return (* base_price 0.5))) ; Children (3-12) get 50% off
    (if (>= age 65)
      (return (* base_price 0.75))) ; Seniors (65+) get 25% off
    ; Otherwise, adult price (13-64)
    base_price)

  (test infant_free (near? (calculate_ticket_price 1 50.0) 0 0.01))
  (test child_discount (near? (calculate_ticket_price 8 100.0) 50.0 0.01))
  (test adult_full_price (near? (calculate_ticket_price 35 120.0) 120.0 0.01))
  (test senior_discount (near? (calculate_ticket_price 70 80.0) 60.0 0.01))
  (test boundary_child_min (near? (calculate_ticket_price 3 90.0) 45.0 0.01))
  (test boundary_adult_min (near? (calculate_ticket_price 13 100.0) 100.0 0.01))
  (test boundary_adult_max (near? (calculate_ticket_price 64 100.0) 100.0 0.01))
)

objective: Implement a function that logs application status messages to a predefined file path. The message content varies based on the provided status (e.g., "success", "failure").
solution:
(module LogFileWriter
  (extern defun file_write_cap (path content) @capability "file.write")

  (defun write_status_log (status)
    (def log_file_path "/var/log/app_status.log")
    (def message_content "")

    (if (str_eq status "success")
      (set message_content "Application started successfully."))
    (if (str_eq status "failure")
      (set message_content "Application failed to start. Check dependencies."))
    (if (str_eq message_content "")
      (set message_content (str_concat "Unknown application status: " status)))

    (file_write_cap log_file_path message_content)
    log_file_path) ; Return the path written to for testing purposes

  (test write_success_status

    (eq? (write_status_log "success") "/var/log/app_status.log"))

    (eq? (write_status_log "warning") "/var/log/app_status.log"))

  (test write_empty_status

)

objective: Write a function `count_file_lines` that reads the content of a file and returns the number of lines. A line is defined as a sequence of characters terminated by a newline, or the last sequence of characters in the file if not newline-terminated. A trailing newline character at the end of the file does not count as an additional empty line.
solution:
(module FileLineCountSplit
  (extern defun file_read (path) @capability "file.read")

  (defun count_file_lines (path)
    (def content (file_read path))
    (if (str_eq content "") (return 0))
    (def lines_arr (str_split content "\n"))
    (def num_segments (arr_length lines_arr))
    (def final_count num_segments)
    (if (and (> num_segments 0) (str_eq (arr_get lines_arr (- num_segments 1)) ""))
      (set final_count (- final_count 1)))
    final_count)

  (test empty_file
    (mocks (file.read "/path/empty.txt" ""))
    (eq? (count_file_lines "/path/empty.txt") 0))

  (test single_line_no_newline
    (mocks (file.read "/path/single.txt" "Hello World"))
    (eq? (count_file_lines "/path/single.txt") 1))

  (test two_lines_with_newline
    (mocks (file.read "/path/two_lines.txt" "Line 1\nLine 2"))
    (eq? (count_file_lines "/path/two_lines.txt") 2))

  (test two_lines_trailing_newline
    (mocks (file.read "/path/two_trailing.txt" "Line 1\nLine 2\n"))
    (eq? (count_file_lines "/path/two_trailing.txt") 2))

  (test multiple_empty_lines
    (mocks (file.read "/path/empty_lines.txt" "\n\nLine 3\n"))
    (eq? (count_file_lines "/path/empty_lines.txt") 3))

  (test only_newline
    (mocks (file.read "/path/only_newline.txt" "\n"))
    (eq? (count_file_lines "/path/only_newline.txt") 1))

  (test multiple_newlines_only
    (mocks (file.read "/path/multiple_newlines.txt" "\n\n\n"))
    (eq? (count_file_lines "/path/multiple_newlines.txt") 3))
)

topic: discounted price after coupon and bulk-buy threshold
objective: Calculate the final price of an item given its unit price, quantity, and two types of discounts. The first discount is a bulk-buy discount applied if the quantity exceeds a specified threshold. The second is a flat coupon discount applied to the price after any bulk discount.
solution:
(module DiscountCalculator
  (defun apply_percentage_discount (amount percentage)
    (- amount (/ (* amount percentage) 100)))

  (defun calculate_final_price (unit_price quantity bulk_threshold bulk_discount_pct coupon_discount_pct)
    (def current_price (* unit_price quantity))
    (if (> quantity bulk_threshold)
      (set current_price (apply_percentage_discount current_price bulk_discount_pct)))
    (apply_percentage_discount current_price coupon_discount_pct))

  (test no_discounts
    (near? (calculate_final_price 10 5 10 15 0) 50.0 0.001))

  (test only_bulk_discount
    (near? (calculate_final_price 10 12 10 15 0) 102.0 0.001))

  (test only_coupon_discount
    (near? (calculate_final_price 10 5 10 0 10) 45.0 0.001))

  (test both_discounts
    (near? (calculate_final_price 10 12 10 15 10) 91.8 0.001))

  (test quantity_at_threshold
    (near? (calculate_final_price 10 10 10 15 5) 95.0 0.001))

  (test higher_prices_and_discounts
    (near? (calculate_final_price 25.5 20 15 20 7.5) 377.4 0.001)))

objective: Implement a currency exchange calculator that converts an amount from a source currency to a target currency, applying either a percentage-based or a fixed-value fee. The final exchanged amount should be rounded to two decimal places.
solution:
(module CurrencyExchange
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))

  (defun exchange_currency (source_amount exchange_rate fee_type fee_value)
    (def converted_no_fee (* source_amount exchange_rate))
    (def fee
      (if (str_eq fee_type "percentage")
        (* converted_no_fee fee_value)
        fee_value))
    (def final_amount (- converted_no_fee fee))
    (round2 final_amount))

  (test no_fee_exchange
    (near? (exchange_currency 100 0.9 "fixed" 0) 90.00 0.001))

  (test percentage_fee_exchange
    (near? (exchange_currency 100 0.9 "percentage" 0.02) 88.20 0.001))

  (test fixed_fee_exchange
    (near? (exchange_currency 100 0.9 "fixed" 5) 85.00 0.001))

  (test large_amount_percentage_fee
    (near? (exchange_currency 500 150 "percentage" 0.005) 74625.00 0.001))

objective: Implement a function that reads an environment variable and interprets its string value as a boolean. The function should return 1 for true and 0 for false, recognizing case-insensitive 'true', '1', 'yes', or 'on' as true, and all other values (including empty string) as false.
solution:
(module EnvVarToBool
  (extern defun env_read (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun get_env_bool (key)
    (def raw_val (env_read key))
    (def lower_val (str_lower raw_val))
    (if (str_eq lower_val "true") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq lower_val "1") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq lower_val "yes") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq lower_val "on") (return 1))
    0)

  (test true_string

    (eq? (get_env_bool "DEBUG_MODE") 0))

    (eq? (get_env_bool "ACCEPT_TERMS") 1))

  (test empty_string

    (eq? (get_env_bool "OPT_IN") 0))

objective: Create a module that calculates the total monthly rent bill, including a base rent, fixed utility charges, a variable utility charge calculated as a percentage of the base rent, and an optional late fee applied as a percentage of the total bill before the late fee.
solution:
(module MonthlyRentUtilities
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))

  (defun calculate_variable_utilities (base_rent utility_pct)
    (/ (* base_rent utility_pct) 100))

  (defun compute_monthly_bill (base_rent fixed_utilities variable_utility_pct is_late late_fee_pct)
    (def variable_cost (calculate_variable_utilities base_rent variable_utility_pct))
    (def subtotal (+ base_rent fixed_utilities variable_cost))
    (def late_fee 0)
    (if (> is_late 0)
      (set late_fee (/ (* subtotal late_fee_pct) 100)))
    (round2 (+ subtotal late_fee)))

  (test basic_no_utilities_no_late_fee
    (near? (compute_monthly_bill 1000 0 0 0 0) 1000.00 0.01))

  (test basic_with_fixed_utilities
    (near? (compute_monthly_bill 1000 50 0 0 0) 1050.00 0.01))

  (test with_variable_utilities_no_late_fee
    (near? (compute_monthly_bill 1000 50 10 0 0) 1150.00 0.01)) ; 1000 + 50 + (1000 * 0.10) = 1000 + 50 + 100 = 1150

  (test with_variable_and_late_fee
    (near? (compute_monthly_bill 1000 50 10 1 5) 1207.50 0.01)) ; Subtotal = 1150; Late fee = 1150 * 0.05 = 57.50; Total = 1150 + 57.50 = 1207.50

  (test different_values_no_late_fee
    (near? (compute_monthly_bill 1500 100 5 0 0) 1675.00 0.01)) ; 1500 + 100 + (1500 * 0.05) = 1600 + 75 = 1675

  (test different_values_with_late_fee
    (near? (compute_monthly_bill 1500 100 5 1 10) 1842.50 0.01)) ; Subtotal = 1675; Late fee = 1675 * 0.10 = 167.50; Total = 1675 + 167.50 = 1842.50

  (test zero_percentages
    (near? (compute_monthly_bill 800 25 0 1 0) 825.00 0.01))) ; 800 + 25 + 0; is_late is true, but late_fee_pct is 0, so no late fee added.
)

topic: count occurrences of a character in a string
objective: Implement a function that counts the number of times a specific character appears within a given string, returning the total count. The comparison should be case-sensitive.
solution:
(module CharCounter
  (defun count_char (s char)
    (def count 0)
    (def i 0)
    (def len (str_length s))
    (while (< i len)
      (if (str_eq (str_substring s i 1) char)
        (set count (+ count 1)))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    count)

  (test basic_match
    (eq? (count_char "hello world" "o") 2))

    (eq? (count_char "programming" "x") 0))

  (test case_sensitive_upper
    (eq? (count_char "Apple Pie" "P") 1))

    (eq? (count_char "aaaaabbbaaa" "a") 8)))

topic: split string on space then count words
objective: Write a function `count_words` that takes a string as input and returns the number of words in it. A word is defined as a sequence of non-space characters. The function should correctly handle leading, trailing, and multiple spaces between words, ensuring they do not contribute to the word count.
solution:
(module WordCounter
  (defun count_words (input_str)
    (def words (str_split input_str " "))
    (def word_count 0)
    (def i 0)
    (def len (arr_length words))
    (while (< i len)
      (def word (arr_get words i))
      ;; Only count non-empty strings as words
      (if (= (str_eq word "") 0)
        (set word_count (+ word_count 1)))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    word_count)

  (test simple_sentence (eq? (count_words "hello world") 2))
  (test leading_trailing_spaces (eq? (count_words "  hello world  ") 2))
  (test multiple_internal_spaces (eq? (count_words "hello   beautiful   world") 3))
  (test empty_string (eq? (count_words "") 0))
  (test only_spaces (eq? (count_words "     ") 0))
  (test single_word (eq? (count_words "programming") 1))
  (test sentence_with_punctuation (eq? (count_words "Hello, world! How are you?") 5)))

objective: Create a module to calculate the final amount of an investment with compound interest, specifically using quarterly compounding. The module should take the principal amount, annual interest rate (as a percentage), and the number of years, then return the total amount rounded to two decimal places.
solution:
(module CompoundInterestCalculator

  (defun round_to_2_decimals (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))

  (defun calculate_quarterly_compound_interest (principal annual_rate_percent years)
    (def r_decimal (/ annual_rate_percent 100.0))
    (def n 4) ; Quarterly compounding
    (def num_periods (* n years))
    (def rate_per_period (/ r_decimal n))
    (def base (+ 1 rate_per_period))
    (def power_term (math_pow base num_periods))
    (def final_amount (* principal power_term))
    (round_to_2_decimals final_amount))

  (test simple_investment
    (near? (calculate_quarterly_compound_interest 1000 4 1) 1040.60 0.01))

  (test longer_term_higher_rate
    (near? (calculate_quarterly_compound_interest 5000 6 5) 6734.28 0.01))

  (test fractional_years
    (near? (calculate_quarterly_compound_interest 2000 8 0.5) 2080.80 0.01))

  (test zero_interest
    (near? (calculate_quarterly_compound_interest 1500 0 10) 1500.00 0.01))

  (test larger_principal_longer_term
    (near? (calculate_quarterly_compound_interest 10000 5 10) 16436.19 0.01))
)

topic: tip calculation split among diners with rounding
objective: Calculate how much each diner should pay for a restaurant bill, including a tip, and ensure the amount is rounded to the nearest cent. The function should take the initial bill amount, the tip percentage, and the number of diners, then return the individual cost per diner.
solution:
(module SplitTip
  ;; Rounds a given amount to two decimal places (nearest cent).
  (defun round_to_cents (amount)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* amount 100) 0.5)) 100))

  ;; Calculates the total bill amount including the tip.
  (defun calculate_total_with_tip (bill_amount tip_percentage)
    (* bill_amount (+ 1 (/ tip_percentage 100))))

  ;; Divides the total bill (with tip) among the specified number of diners,
  ;; rounding each diner's share to the nearest cent.
  (defun split_bill (bill_amount tip_percentage num_diners)
    (def total_with_tip (calculate_total_with_tip bill_amount tip_percentage))
    (def amount_per_diner (/ total_with_tip num_diners))
    (round_to_cents amount_per_diner))

  (test simple_even_split
    (near? (split_bill 100 15 2) 57.50 0.001))

  (test rounding_up_example
    (near? (split_bill 33.33 15 3) 12.78 0.001)) ; (33.33 * 1.15) / 3 = 38.3295 / 3 = 12.7765 -> 12.78

  (test rounding_down_example
    (near? (split_bill 33.34 15 3) 12.78 0.001)) ; (33.34 * 1.15) / 3 = 38.341 / 3 = 12.78033 -> 12.78

  (test zero_tip_scenario
    (near? (split_bill 50 0 5) 10.00 0.001))

  (test single_diner_no_split
    (near? (split_bill 75.25 20 1) 90.30 0.001))

  (test complex_bill_many_diners
    (near? (split_bill 123.45 18 7) 20.81 0.001)) ; (123.45 * 1.18) / 7 = 145.671 / 7 = 20.81014... -> 20.81

  (test another_rounding_case
    (near? (split_bill 99.99 10 4) 27.50 0.001)) ; (99.99 * 1.10) / 4 = 109.989 / 4 = 27.49725 -> 27.50
)

objective: Write a function `is_leap_year` that takes an integer representing a year and returns 1 if it is a leap year, and 0 otherwise. A year is a leap year if it is divisible by 4, unless it is divisible by 100 but not by 400.
solution:
(module LeapYear
  (defun is_leap_year (year)
    (if (= (math_mod year 400) 0)
      1 ; Divisible by 400 is always a leap year
      (if (= (math_mod year 100) 0)
        0 ; Divisible by 100 but not 400 is NOT a leap year
        (if (= (math_mod year 4) 0)
          1 ; Divisible by 4 but not 100 IS a leap year
          0)))) ; Otherwise, not a leap year)

  (test year_2024_is_leap (eq? (is_leap_year 2024) 1))
  (test year_2000_is_leap (eq? (is_leap_year 2000) 1))
  (test year_1900_is_not_leap (eq? (is_leap_year 1900) 0))
  (test year_2023_is_not_leap (eq? (is_leap_year 2023) 0))
  (test year_1600_is_leap (eq? (is_leap_year 1600) 1))
  (test year_1804_is_leap (eq? (is_leap_year 1804) 1))
  (test year_1700_is_not_leap (eq? (is_leap_year 1700) 0)))

objective: Create a function that fetches content from a given URL using an HTTP capability, then returns the first non-empty word found in the response body. Words are delimited by spaces.
solution:
(module HttpFirstWord
  (extern defun http_fetch (url) @capability "http.fetch")

  (defun get_first_word_from_url (url)
    (def body (http_fetch url))
    (def parts (str_split body " "))
    (def i 0)
    (def n (arr_length parts))
    (while (< i n)
      (def current_part (arr_get parts i))
      (if (not (str_eq current_part ""))
        (return current_part)) ; Found the first non-empty word
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    "") ; No non-empty word found, return empty string

  (test empty_body
    (mocks (http.fetch "http://example.com/empty" ""))
    (eq? (get_first_word_from_url "http://example.com/empty") ""))

  (test single_word
    (mocks (http.fetch "http://example.com/single" "hello"))
    (eq? (get_first_word_from_url "http://example.com/single") "hello"))

  (test multiple_words
    (mocks (http.fetch "http://example.com/multi" "hello world agc"))
    (eq? (get_first_word_from_url "http://example.com/multi") "hello"))

  (test leading_spaces
    (mocks (http.fetch "http://example.com/leading" "   first second"))
    (eq? (get_first_word_from_url "http://example.com/leading") "first"))

  (test trailing_spaces
    (mocks (http.fetch "http://example.com/trailing" "first   "))
    (eq? (get_first_word_from_url "http://example.com/trailing") "first"))

  (test only_spaces
    (mocks (http.fetch "http://example.com/spaces" "     "))
    (eq? (get_first_word_from_url "http://example.com/spaces") ""))

  (test multiple_spaces_between
    (mocks (http.fetch "http://example.com/gaps" "one   two three"))
    (eq? (get_first_word_from_url "http://example.com/gaps") "one")))

topic: read file and count char occurrences
objective: Implement a function that reads the content of a file from a given path and returns the total number of occurrences of a specified single character within that file's content. This function must utilize an external file reading capability.
solution:
(module FileCharCounter
  (extern defun file_read (path) @capability "file.read")

  (defun count_char_occurrences (path target_char)
    (def content (file_read path))
    (def content_length (str_length content))
    (def count 0)
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i content_length)
      (def current_char (str_substring content i 1))
      (if (str_eq current_char target_char)
        (set count (+ count 1)))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    count)

  (test empty_file_no_char (mocks (file.read "/empty.txt" "")) (eq? (count_char_occurrences "/empty.txt" "a") 0))
  (test no_occurrence (mocks (file.read "/file1.txt" "hello world")) (eq? (count_char_occurrences "/file1.txt" "z") 0))
  (test single_occurrence (mocks (file.read "/file2.txt" "apple")) (eq? (count_char_occurrences "/file2.txt" "p") 2))
  (test multiple_occurrences (mocks (file.read "/file3.txt" "banana republic")) (eq? (count_char_occurrences "/file3.txt" "a") 3))
  (test case_sensitive (mocks (file.read "/file4.txt" "Apple")) (eq? (count_char_occurrences "/file4.txt" "a") 0))
  (test case_sensitive_match (mocks (file.read "/file5.txt" "Apple")) (eq? (count_char_occurrences "/file5.txt" "A") 1))
  (test special_char (mocks (file.read "/file6.txt" "hello, world!")) (eq? (count_char_occurrences "/file6.txt" " ") 1)))

objective: Calculate the total fuel cost for a trip given the distance to be traveled, the vehicle's fuel efficiency (e.g., miles per gallon), and the current price of fuel per unit. The final cost should be rounded to two decimal places.
solution:
(module TripFuelCost
  (defun round_to_decimal_places (num places)
    (def multiplier (math_pow 10 places))
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* num multiplier) 0.5)) multiplier))

  (defun calculate_fuel_needed (distance efficiency)
    (/ distance efficiency))

  (defun calculate_raw_cost (fuel_amount price_per_unit)
    (* fuel_amount price_per_unit))

  (defun estimate_trip_fuel_cost (distance efficiency price_per_unit)
    (def fuel_amount (calculate_fuel_needed distance efficiency))
    (def raw_total_cost (calculate_raw_cost fuel_amount price_per_unit))
    (round_to_decimal_places raw_total_cost 2))

  (test basic_trip

objective: Implement a function `is_power_of_two` that determines if a given non-negative integer is a power of two. The function should return `1` if the number is a power of two (e.g., 1, 2, 4, 8, etc.), and `0` otherwise.
solution:
(module PowerOfTwo
  (defun is_power_of_two (n)
    (if (<= n 0) (return 0)) ; Powers of two must be positive integers
    
    (while (= (math_mod n 2) 0)
      (set n (/ n 2)))
    
    ; After repeatedly dividing by 2, if n is 1, it was a power of two.
    ; Otherwise, it had an odd factor other than 1.
    (if (= n 1)
      1
      0))

  (test zero       (eq? (is_power_of_two 0) 0))
  (test one        (eq? (is_power_of_two 1) 1)) ; 2^0
  (test four       (eq? (is_power_of_two 4) 1)) ; 2^2
  (test three      (eq? (is_power_of_two 3) 0))
  (test six        (eq? (is_power_of_two 6) 0))
  (test large_power (eq? (is_power_of_two 1024) 1))) ; 2^10

topic: reverse a string character by character
objective: Reverse a given string character by character. For example, "hello" should become "olleh". The function should handle empty strings and single-character strings correctly.
solution:
(module StringReverse
  (defun reverse_string (s)
    (def len (str_length s))
    (def reversed_s "")
    (def i (- len 1)) ; Start from the last index

    (while (>= i 0)
      (def char (str_substring s i 1)) ; Extract one character at index i
      (set reversed_s (str_concat reversed_s char)) ; Append to the result
      (set i (- i 1)))
    reversed_s)

  (test empty_string
    (eq? (reverse_string "") ""))

  (test single_char
    (eq? (reverse_string "a") "a"))

  (test two_chars
    (eq? (reverse_string "ab") "ba"))

  (test simple_word
    (eq? (reverse_string "hello") "olleh"))

  (test sentence_with_spaces
    (eq? (reverse_string "hello world") "dlrow olleh"))

  (test numbers_and_symbols
    (eq? (reverse_string "123!@#") "#@!321")))

objective: Calculate the total price for a given quantity of an item, applying bulk discounts based on predefined tiers. The item has a base unit price, and discounts are applied if the quantity meets certain thresholds. The final price should be rounded to two decimal places.
solution:
(module BulkPriceCalculator
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100.0) 0.5)) 100.0))

  (defun get_discount_percentage (quantity)
    (if (>= quantity 50)
      (return 20.0))
    (if (>= quantity 10)
      (return 10.0))
    0.0)

  (defun calculate_discounted_unit_price (unit_price quantity)
    (def discount_pct (get_discount_percentage quantity))
    (def effective_multiplier (- 1.0 (/ discount_pct 100.0)))
    (* unit_price effective_multiplier))

  (defun calculate_total_bulk_price (unit_price quantity)
    (def discounted_unit_price (calculate_discounted_unit_price unit_price quantity))
    (def total (* discounted_unit_price quantity))
    (round2 total))

  (test no_discount_single_item
    (near? (calculate_total_bulk_price 25.00 1) 25.00 0.001))

  (test no_discount_small_qty
    (near? (calculate_total_bulk_price 10.00 5) 50.00 0.001))

  (test first_tier_discount_just_enough
    (near? (calculate_total_bulk_price 10.00 10) 90.00 0.001))

  (test first_tier_discount_mid_qty
    (near? (calculate_total_bulk_price 12.50 25) 281.25 0.001))

  (test first_tier_discount_just_below_next
    (near? (calculate_total_bulk_price 10.00 49) 441.00 0.001))

  (test second_tier_discount_just_enough
    (near? (calculate_total_bulk_price 10.00 50) 400.00 0.001))

  (test second_tier_discount_large_qty
    (near? (calculate_total_bulk_price 5.99 100) 479.20 0.001))
)

objective: Implement a function `integer_power` that calculates the result of raising an integer `base` to a non-negative integer `exponent`. The function should return 1 if the exponent is 0, and the `base` raised to the `exponent` otherwise.
solution:
(module IntegerPower
  (defun integer_power (base exponent)
    (if (< exponent 0)
      (return 0)) ; Or handle error, returning 0 for negative exponents as per problem constraint.
    (if (= exponent 0)
      (return 1))
    (def result 1)
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i exponent)
      (set result (* result base))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    result)

  (test base_two_exp_zero (eq? (integer_power 2 0) 1))
  (test base_five_exp_one (eq? (integer_power 5 1) 5))
  (test base_two_exp_three (eq? (integer_power 2 3) 8))
  (test base_three_exp_four (eq? (integer_power 3 4) 81))
  (test base_negative_two_exp_three (eq? (integer_power -2 3) -8))
  (test base_negative_two_exp_four (eq? (integer_power -2 4) 16))
  (test base_zero_exp_five (eq? (integer_power 0 5) 0)))

objective: Create a program that calculates how a restaurant bill, including a specified tip percentage, should be split evenly among a given number of diners. The final amount per person should be rounded to two decimal places.

(module TipCalculator

    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100.0) 0.5)) 100.0))

  (defun calculate_tip_amount (bill_amount tip_percentage)
    (* bill_amount (/ tip_percentage 100.0)))

  (defun calculate_total_bill (bill_amount tip_amount)
    (+ bill_amount tip_amount))

  (defun calculate_split_amount (total_bill num_diners)
    (/ total_bill num_diners))

  (defun calculate_bill_split (bill_amount tip_percentage num_diners)
    (def tip (calculate_tip_amount bill_amount tip_percentage))
    (def total (calculate_total_bill bill_amount tip))
    (def per_person (calculate_split_amount total num_diners))
    (round_two_decimals per_person))

)

(module PhoneBillCalculator

    (if (> usage_mb included_mb)
        (- usage_mb included_mb)
        0))

  (defun calculate_overage_charge (overage_mb rate_per_mb)
    (* overage_mb rate_per_mb))

  (defun get_monthly_bill (base_plan_cost included_data_mb overage_rate_per_mb actual_usage_mb)

  (test bill_with_overage_large
    (eq? (get_monthly_bill 45 1000 0.05 1500) 70)) ; 45 base + (1500-1000)*0.05 = 45 + 500*0.05 = 45 + 25 = 70

  (test bill_with_zero_overage_rate

objective: Create a function to calculate the minimum payment for a credit card statement. The minimum payment is the greater of a fixed percentage of the outstanding balance or a predefined fixed minimum amount, unless the balance is zero or negative, in which case the minimum payment is zero.
solution:
(module CreditCardMinPayment
  ;; Helper function to round a number to two decimal places.
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))

  ;; Calculates the minimum payment for a credit card.
  ;; If balance is 0 or negative, minimum payment is 0.
  ;; Otherwise, it's the maximum of (balance * min_pct_rate) and fixed_min_amount.
  (defun calculate_min_payment (balance min_pct_rate fixed_min_amount)
    (if (<= balance 0)
      (return 0))
    (def pct_payment (* balance min_pct_rate))
    (def raw_min_payment (math_max pct_payment fixed_min_amount))
    (round2 raw_min_payment))

  ;; Test cases
  (test low_balance_fixed_min_applies
    (near? (calculate_min_payment 1000 0.01 25) 25.00 0.01))

  (test high_balance_pct_applies
    (near? (calculate_min_payment 3000 0.01 25) 30.00 0.01))

  (test zero_balance
    (near? (calculate_min_payment 0 0.01 25) 0.00 0.01))

  (test negative_balance
    (near? (calculate_min_payment -50 0.01 25) 0.00 0.01))

  (test exact_boundary_case
    (near? (calculate_min_payment 2500 0.01 25) 25.00 0.01))

  (test slightly_above_boundary
    (near? (calculate_min_payment 2501 0.01 25) 25.01 0.01))

  (test different_fixed_amount
    (near? (calculate_min_payment 1000 0.02 50) 50.00 0.01))
)

topic: shipping cost by weight and zone
objective: Implement a shipping cost calculator that determines the total cost based on package weight and destination zone. The cost structure includes tiered pricing for weight and a multiplier for different shipping zones to reflect varying shipping complexities.
solution:
(module ShippingCalculator

  ;; Determines the base cost component based on package weight.
  ;; - Up to 5 kg: 5.0
  ;; - 5.01 kg to 10 kg: 10.0
  ;; - Over 10 kg: 15.0
  (defun get_base_weight_cost (weight)
    (if (<= weight 5.0)
      (return 5.0))
    (if (<= weight 10.0)
      (return 10.0))
    15.0)

  ;; Determines the per-kilogram rate based on package weight.
  ;; - Up to 5 kg: 1.50 per kg
  ;; - 5.01 kg to 10 kg: 1.20 per kg
  ;; - Over 10 kg: 1.00 per kg
  (defun get_per_kg_rate (weight)
    (if (<= weight 5.0)
      (return 1.5))
    (if (<= weight 10.0)
      (return 1.2))
    1.0)

  ;; Determines the zone multiplier.
  ;; - Zone "A": 1.0
  ;; - Zone "B": 1.2
  ;; - Zone "C": 1.5
  ;; - Any other zone: 2.0 (default/unknown zone penalty)
  (defun get_zone_multiplier (zone)
    (if (str_eq zone "A")
      (return 1.0))
    (if (str_eq zone "B")
      (return 1.2))
    (if (str_eq zone "C")
      (return 1.5))
    2.0)

  ;; Calculates the total shipping cost based on weight and zone.
  ;; Formula: (base_cost + (weight * per_kg_rate)) * zone_multiplier
  (defun calculate_shipping_cost (weight zone)
    (def base_cost (get_base_weight_cost weight))
    (def per_kg_rate (get_per_kg_rate weight))
    (def zone_multiplier (get_zone_multiplier zone))
    (* (+ base_cost (* weight per_kg_rate)) zone_multiplier))

  (test test_3kg_zoneA
    (near? (calculate_shipping_cost 3.0 "A") 9.5 0.01)) ; (5.0 + 3*1.5) * 1.0 = (5.0 + 4.5) * 1.0 = 9.5

  (test test_7kg_zoneB
    (near? (calculate_shipping_cost 7.0 "B") 22.08 0.01)) ; (10.0 + 7*1.2) * 1.2 = (10.0 + 8.4) * 1.2 = 18.4 * 1.2 = 22.08

  (test test_12kg_zoneC
    (near? (calculate_shipping_cost 12.0 "C") 40.5 0.01)) ; (15.0 + 12*1.0) * 1.5 = (15.0 + 12.0) * 1.5 = 27.0 * 1.5 = 40.5

  (test test_0kg_zoneA
    (near? (calculate_shipping_cost 0.0 "A") 5.0 0.01)) ; (5.0 + 0*1.5) * 1.0 = 5.0 * 1.0 = 5.0

  (test test_5kg_zoneB_edge
    (near? (calculate_shipping_cost 5.0 "B") 15.0 0.01)) ; (5.0 + 5*1.5) * 1.2 = (5.0 + 7.5) * 1.2 = 12.5 * 1.2 = 15.0

  (test test_10kg_zoneC_edge
    (near? (calculate_shipping_cost 10.0 "C") 33.0 0.01)) ; (10.0 + 10*1.2) * 1.5 = (10.0 + 12.0) * 1.5 = 22.0 * 1.5 = 33.0

  (test test_2kg_unknown_zone
    (near? (calculate_shipping_cost 2.0 "D") 16.0 0.01)) ; (5.0 + 2*1.5) * 2.0 = (5.0 + 3.0) * 2.0 = 8.0 * 2.0 = 16.0
)

(module BmiClassifier

    (if (< bmi 25.0) (return "Normal weight"))

  (test obesity_example

  (test boundary_normal_lower
    (eq? (get_bmi_classification 53.465 1.70) "Normal weight")) ; BMI = 18.50
  (test boundary_overweight_lower

objective: The objective is to create a function that reads an environment variable and interprets its value as a boolean. The function should return `true` if the variable is "true" (case-insensitive), `false` if it's "false" (case-insensitive), and a provided default boolean value for any other input, including an empty or missing variable.
solution:
(module EnvBoolParser
  (extern defun env_read (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun get_env_bool (key default_value)
    (def raw_val (env_read key))
    (if (str_eq raw_val "") (return default_value))
    (def lower_val (str_lower raw_val))
    (if (str_eq lower_val "true") (return true))

topic: freelance billable hours with overtime multiplier
objective: Calculate the total bill for freelance work, applying a standard hourly rate for hours up to a specified threshold and an overtime rate (base rate multiplied by a factor) for any hours exceeding that threshold.
solution:
(module FreelanceBilling
  (defun calculate_regular_hours (hours_worked overtime_threshold)
    (if (< hours_worked overtime_threshold)
      hours_worked
      overtime_threshold))

  (defun calculate_overtime_hours (hours_worked overtime_threshold)
    (if (> hours_worked overtime_threshold)
      (- hours_worked overtime_threshold)
      0))

  (defun calculate_total_bill (hours_worked hourly_rate overtime_threshold overtime_multiplier)
    (def regular_hours (calculate_regular_hours hours_worked overtime_threshold))
    (def overtime_hours (calculate_overtime_hours hours_worked overtime_threshold))
    (def regular_cost (* regular_hours hourly_rate))
    (def overtime_cost (* overtime_hours hourly_rate overtime_multiplier))
    (+ regular_cost overtime_cost))

  (test no_overtime
    (near? (calculate_total_bill 35 50 40 1.5) 1750 0.01))

  (test exactly_threshold
    (near? (calculate_total_bill 40 50 40 1.5) 2000 0.01))

  (test with_overtime
    (near? (calculate_total_bill 50 50 40 1.5) 2750 0.01))

  (test zero_hours
    (near? (calculate_total_bill 0 50 40 1.5) 0 0.01))

  (test high_overtime_multiplier
    (near? (calculate_total_bill 45 60 40 2.0) 3000 0.01)))

solution:
(module EuclideanGCD

objective: Implement a function that calculates the Nth Fibonacci number iteratively. The Fibonacci sequence starts with F(0) = 0 and F(1) = 1, and each subsequent number is the sum of the two preceding ones.
solution:
(module IterativeFibonacci
  (defun fibonacci (n)
    (if (= n 0) (return 0))
    (if (= n 1) (return 1))

    (def a 0) ; Represents F(i-2)
    (def b 1) ; Represents F(i-1)
    (def i 2)

    (while (<= i n)
      (def next_fib (+ a b))
      (set a b)
      (set b next_fib)
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    b)

  (test fib_0 (eq? (fibonacci 0) 0))
  (test fib_1 (eq? (fibonacci 1) 1))
  (test fib_2 (eq? (fibonacci 2) 1))
  (test fib_3 (eq? (fibonacci 3) 2))
  (test fib_5 (eq? (fibonacci 5) 5))
  (test fib_10 (eq? (fibonacci 10) 55))
  (test fib_15 (eq? (fibonacci 15) 610)))

objective: Write a function that takes an array of numbers and a constant value. The function should return a *new* array where each element is the corresponding element from the original array, with the given constant subtracted from it.
solution:
(module SubtractConstantFromArray
  (defun subtract_constant_from_array (arr constant)
    (def len (arr_length arr))
    (def result_arr (arr_new len))
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i len)
      (def original_val (arr_get arr i))
      (arr_set result_arr i (- original_val constant))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    result_arr)

  (test empty_array
    (eq? (subtract_constant_from_array (arr_new 0) 5) (arr_new 0)))

  (test positive_numbers_positive_constant
    (def input_arr (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set input_arr 0 10)
    (arr_set input_arr 1 20)
    (arr_set input_arr 2 30)
    (def expected_arr (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set expected_arr 0 5)
    (arr_set expected_arr 1 15)
    (arr_set expected_arr 2 25)
    (eq? (subtract_constant_from_array input_arr 5) expected_arr))

  (test negative_numbers_positive_constant
    (def input_arr (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set input_arr 0 -1)
    (arr_set input_arr 1 -5)
    (arr_set input_arr 2 -10)
    (def expected_arr (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set expected_arr 0 -6)   ; -1 - 5 = -6
    (arr_set expected_arr 1 -10)  ; -5 - 5 = -10
    (arr_set expected_arr 2 -15)  ; -10 - 5 = -15
    (eq? (subtract_constant_from_array input_arr 5) expected_arr))

  (test mixed_numbers_negative_constant
    (def input_arr (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set input_arr 0 10)
    (arr_set input_arr 1 -5)
    (arr_set input_arr 2 0)
    (def expected_arr (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set expected_arr 0 12)  ; 10 - (-2) = 12
    (arr_set expected_arr 1 -3)  ; -5 - (-2) = -3
    (arr_set expected_arr 2 2)   ; 0 - (-2) = 2
    (eq? (subtract_constant_from_array input_arr -2) expected_arr))

  (test single_element_array
    (def input_arr (arr_new 1))
    (arr_set input_arr 0 100)
    (def expected_arr (arr_new 1))
    (arr_set expected_arr 0 90)
    (eq? (subtract_constant_from_array input_arr 10) expected_arr))
)

objective: Create a function that reads the content of a file and accurately counts the number of lines it contains. The line count should consider an empty file as 0 lines, and a file with content (even if it doesn't end with a newline) as at least 1 line.
solution:
(module FileLineCounter
  (extern defun file_read (path) @capability "file.read")

  ;; Helper function to count newline characters in a string
  (defun count_newlines (s)
    (def count 0)
    (def i 0)
    (def len (str_length s))
    (while (< i len)
      (if (str_eq (str_substring s i 1) "\n")
        (set count (+ count 1)))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    count)

  ;; Main function to count lines in a file
  (defun count_lines_in_file (filepath)
    (def content (file_read filepath))
    ;; An empty file has 0 lines
    (if (str_eq content "") (return 0))
    
    (def newline_count (count_newlines content))
    
    ;; If the file content ends with a newline, the number of lines is simply the count of newlines.
    ;; Otherwise, it's the count of newlines plus one for the last line that doesn't end with a newline.
    (if (str_eq (str_substring content (- (str_length content) 1) 1) "\n")
      newline_count
      (+ newline_count 1)))

  (test empty_file
    (mocks (file.read "empty.txt" ""))
    (eq? (count_lines_in_file "empty.txt") 0))

  (test single_line_no_newline
    (mocks (file.read "single_line.txt" "Hello World"))
    (eq? (count_lines_in_file "single_line.txt") 1))

  (test single_line_with_newline
    (mocks (file.read "single_line_nl.txt" "Hello World\n"))
    (eq? (count_lines_in_file "single_line_nl.txt") 1))

  (test multiple_lines_no_trailing_newline
    (mocks (file.read "multi_line.txt" "Line 1\nLine 2\nLine 3"))
    (eq? (count_lines_in_file "multi_line.txt") 3))

  (test multiple_lines_with_trailing_newline
    (mocks (file.read "multi_line_nl.txt" "Line 1\nLine 2\nLine 3\n"))
    (eq? (count_lines_in_file "multi_line_nl.txt") 3))

  (test only_newline_char
    (mocks (file.read "only_nl.txt" "\n"))
    (eq? (count_lines_in_file "only_nl.txt") 1))

  (test multiple_newline_chars
    (mocks (file.read "multi_nl.txt" "\n\n\n"))
    (eq? (count_lines_in_file "multi_nl.txt") 3)))

topic: depreciation over n years straight line
objective: Calculate the annual depreciation amount for an asset using the straight-line method, and determine its book value at the end of a specified year, ensuring the book value does not fall below the salvage value.
solution:
(module DepreciationCalculator
  (defun calculate_annual_depreciation (cost salvage_value useful_life)
    (/ (- cost salvage_value) useful_life))

  (defun calculate_book_value (cost salvage_value useful_life current_year)
    (def annual_depreciation (calculate_annual_depreciation cost salvage_value useful_life))
    (def total_depreciable_amount (- cost salvage_value))
    (def accumulated_depreciation (* annual_depreciation current_year))
    (def actual_accumulated_depreciation (math_min accumulated_depreciation total_depreciable_amount))
    (- cost actual_accumulated_depreciation))

  (test annual_depreciation_simple
    (near? (calculate_annual_depreciation 10000 1000 5) 1800 0.01))

  (test annual_depreciation_zero_salvage
    (near? (calculate_annual_depreciation 5000 0 10) 500 0.01))

  (test book_value_year_one
    (near? (calculate_book_value 10000 1000 5 1) 8200 0.01))

  (test book_value_mid_life
    (near? (calculate_book_value 10000 1000 5 3) 4600 0.01))

  (test book_value_end_of_life
    (near? (calculate_book_value 10000 1000 5 5) 1000 0.01))

  (test book_value_after_end_of_life
    (near? (calculate_book_value 10000 1000 5 7) 1000 0.01))

  (test book_value_another_case
    (near? (calculate_book_value 25000 2500 8 4) 13750 0.01))
)

objective: Create a program to calculate the minimum monthly payment for a credit card. The minimum payment is determined by summing the monthly accrued interest and the greater of a fixed minimum dollar amount or a percentage of the outstanding balance.
solution:
(module CreditCardMinPayment
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))

  (defun calc_monthly_interest (balance annual_apr)
    (if (> balance 0)
        (round2 (* balance (/ annual_apr 12)))
        0))

  (defun calc_base_payment_component (balance min_balance_pct fixed_min_dollar)
    (if (> balance 0)
        (round2 (math_max fixed_min_dollar (* balance min_balance_pct)))
        0))

  (defun calc_total_min_payment (balance annual_apr min_balance_pct fixed_min_dollar)
    (def interest (calc_monthly_interest balance annual_apr))
    (def base_component (calc_base_payment_component balance min_balance_pct fixed_min_dollar))
    (round2 (+ interest base_component)))

  (test percentage_component_higher
    (near? (calc_total_min_payment 1000 0.18 0.025 25) 40.00 0.01))

  (test fixed_component_higher
    (near? (calc_total_min_payment 500 0.18 0.025 25) 32.50 0.01))

  (test zero_balance
    (near? (calc_total_min_payment 0 0.18 0.025 25) 0.00 0.01))

  (test small_balance_high_fixed_min
    (near? (calc_total_min_payment 10 0.24 0.03 30) 30.20 0.01))

  (test large_balance_low_apr
    (near? (calc_total_min_payment 5000 0.12 0.01 20) 100.00 0.01))
)

objective: Implement a function `string_length_manual` that calculates the length of a given string without using the `str_length` standard library function. Instead, determine the length by iteratively attempting to extract single characters.
solution:
(module StringLengthManual
  (defun string_length_manual (s)
    (def count 0)
    (def current_char "")
    (while (not (str_eq (set current_char (str_substring s count 1)) ""))
      (set count (+ count 1)))
    count)

  (test empty_string (eq? (string_length_manual "") 0))
  (test single_char (eq? (string_length_manual "a") 1))
  (test normal_string (eq? (string_length_manual "hello") 5))
  (test string_with_spaces (eq? (string_length_manual "hello world") 11))
  (test unicode_chars (eq? (string_length_manual "你好世界") 4))
  (test long_string (eq? (string_length_manual "abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz") 26)))

objective: Write a function that counts the number of consonants in a given string. A consonant is defined as any letter from the English alphabet that is not a vowel (a, e, i, o, u), ignoring case. Non-alphabetic characters like numbers, spaces, and symbols should not be counted.
solution:
(module ConsonantCounter
  (defun is_vowel_lower (char_lower)
    (if (str_eq char_lower "a") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq char_lower "e") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq char_lower "i") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq char_lower "o") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq char_lower "u") (return 1))
    0)

  (defun is_lower_letter (char_lower)
    (if (> (str_index_of "abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz" char_lower) -1)
        1
        0))

  (defun count_consonants (s)
    (def count 0)
    (def i 0)
    (def len (str_length s))
    (while (< i len)
      (def char_at_i (str_substring s i 1))
      (def char_lower (str_lower char_at_i))
      
      (if (and (= (is_lower_letter char_lower) 1)
               (= (is_vowel_lower char_lower) 0))
          (set count (+ count 1)))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    count)

  (test simple_word (eq? (count_consonants "hello") 3))
  (test mixed_case (eq? (count_consonants "World") 4))
  (test no_consonants (eq? (count_consonants "aeiouAEIOU") 0))
  (test with_spaces (eq? (count_consonants "test string") 8))
  (test empty_string (eq? (count_consonants "") 0))
  (test numbers_symbols (eq? (count_consonants "123!@#abc") 2))
  (test all_consonants (eq? (count_consonants "rhythm") 6)))

topic: package weight cost with surcharge per excess kilogram
objective: Calculate the total shipping cost for a package. The cost is based on a flat rate for a certain weight, plus a surcharge for every kilogram exceeding that weight limit.
solution:
(module PackageShippingCost
  (defun calculate_excess_weight (actual_weight limit)
    (if (> actual_weight limit)
      (- actual_weight limit)
      0))

  (defun calculate_excess_surcharge (excess_weight surcharge_per_kg)
    (* excess_weight surcharge_per_kg))

  (defun calculate_total_shipping_cost (actual_weight flat_rate weight_limit surcharge_per_kg)
    (def excess_w (calculate_excess_weight actual_weight weight_limit))
    (def surcharge (calculate_excess_surcharge excess_w surcharge_per_kg))
    (+ flat_rate surcharge))

  (test no_excess
    (eq? (calculate_total_shipping_cost 5 100 10 5) 100))

  (test some_excess
    (eq? (calculate_total_shipping_cost 12 100 10 5) 110)) ; 2kg excess * 5 = 10 surcharge

  (test large_excess
    (eq? (calculate_total_shipping_cost 20 150 10 10) 250)) ; 10kg excess * 10 = 100 surcharge

  (test zero_weight
    (eq? (calculate_total_shipping_cost 0 50 10 5) 50)) ; No excess, just flat rate

  (test exact_limit
    (eq? (calculate_total_shipping_cost 10 75 10 8) 75)))

topic: monthly subscription cost with annual discount
objective: Calculate the total cost for a subscription given its monthly price, an annual discount percentage, and the total number of months. The calculation should apply the annual discount for each full year of subscription and charge any remaining months at the regular monthly rate.
solution:
(module SubscriptionCalculator
  (defun round_to_two_decimals (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))

  (defun calculate_discounted_annual_price (monthly_price discount_pct)
    (def base_annual_price (* monthly_price 12))
    (def discount_multiplier (- 1 (/ discount_pct 100)))
    (* base_annual_price discount_multiplier))

  (defun calculate_total_subscription_cost (monthly_price annual_discount_pct num_months)
    (def num_years (math_floor (/ num_months 12)))
    (def remaining_months (math_mod num_months 12))
    (def cost_for_years (* num_years (calculate_discounted_annual_price monthly_price annual_discount_pct)))
    (def cost_for_remaining_months (* remaining_months monthly_price))
    (round_to_two_decimals (+ cost_for_years cost_for_remaining_months)))

  (test one_month_no_discount (near? (calculate_total_subscription_cost 10.0 10.0 1) 10.0 0.01))
  (test full_year_discount (near? (calculate_total_subscription_cost 10.0 10.0 12) 108.0 0.01))
  (test multiple_full_years (near? (calculate_total_subscription_cost 10.0 10.0 24) 216.0 0.01))
  (test mixed_year_and_month (near? (calculate_total_subscription_cost 10.0 10.0 13) 118.0 0.01))
  (test zero_months (near? (calculate_total_subscription_cost 10.0 10.0 0) 0.0 0.01))
  (test partial_year_no_discount (near? (calculate_total_subscription_cost 15.0 20.0 6) 90.0 0.01)))

objective: Implement a function that calculates the digital root of a non-negative integer. The digital root is the single-digit value obtained by an iterative process of summing digits, using the result from each iteration as the input to the next, until a single-digit number is reached.
solution:
(module DigitalRoot
  ;; Helper function to sum the digits of a number
  (defun sum_digits (n)
    (def total_sum 0)
    (while (> n 0)
      (set total_sum (+ total_sum (math_mod n 10)))
      (set n (math_floor (/ n 10))))
    total_sum)

  ;; Calculates the digital root of a non-negative integer
  (defun digital_root (n)
    (if (< n 0) (return -1)) ; Digital root is typically defined for non-negative integers. Return -1 for invalid input.
    (if (= n 0) (return 0))

    (while (> n 9)
      (set n (sum_digits n)))
    n)

  (test zero       (eq? (digital_root 0) 0))
  (test single_digit (eq? (digital_root 7) 7))
  (test two_digits   (eq? (digital_root 19) 1)) ; 1+9=10, 1+0=1
  (test multi_digits (eq? (digital_root 12345) 6)) ; 1+2+3+4+5=15, 1+5=6
  (test large_number (eq? (digital_root 987654321) 9)) ; Sum is 45, 4+5=9
  (test another_large (eq? (digital_root 1000000000) 1))
  (test negative_input (eq? (digital_root -5) -1)))

topic: discounted price after coupon and bulk-buy threshold
objective: Calculate the final price for an order, applying a percentage discount based on a provided coupon code first. If the quantity purchased meets or exceeds a specified bulk-buy threshold, an additional bulk discount percentage is then applied to the price.
solution:
(module DiscountCalculator
  (extern defun env_get (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun parse_discount (discount_str)
    (if (str_eq discount_str "")
      0
      (str_to_num discount_str)))

  (defun apply_percentage_discount (amount percent)
    (- amount (/ (* amount percent) 100)))

  (defun calculate_final_price (item_price quantity coupon_code bulk_threshold bulk_discount_percent)
    (def total_base_cost (* item_price quantity))
    (def coupon_pct_str (env_get coupon_code))
    (def coupon_pct (parse_discount coupon_pct_str))
    (def cost_after_coupon (apply_percentage_discount total_base_cost coupon_pct))

    (if (>= quantity bulk_threshold)
      (apply_percentage_discount cost_after_coupon bulk_discount_percent)
      cost_after_coupon))

  (test no_coupon_no_bulk
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "NONE" ""))
    (eq? (calculate_final_price 100 2 "NONE" 5 10) 200))

  (test with_coupon_no_bulk
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "SAVE10" "10"))
    (eq? (calculate_final_price 100 2 "SAVE10" 5 10) 180))

  (test no_coupon_with_bulk
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "NONE" ""))
    (eq? (calculate_final_price 100 5 "NONE" 5 10) 450))

  (test with_coupon_with_bulk
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "SAVE10" "10"))
    (eq? (calculate_final_price 100 5 "SAVE10" 5 10) 405))

  (test invalid_coupon_code
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "UNKNOWN" ""))
    (eq? (calculate_final_price 100 3 "UNKNOWN" 5 10) 300))

  (test bulk_threshold_exact
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "SAVE5" "5"))
    (eq? (calculate_final_price 100 5 "SAVE5" 5 10) 427.5))

  (test zero_quantity
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "SAVE10" "10"))
    (eq? (calculate_final_price 100 0 "SAVE10" 5 10) 0))
)

objective: Write a function `sum_positives` that takes an array of numbers and returns the sum of only the positive numbers within that array. Zero and negative numbers should be excluded from the sum.
solution:
(module PositiveArraySum
  (defun sum_positives (xs)
    (def i 0)
    (def n (arr_length xs))
    (def total 0)
    (while (< i n)
      (def current_val (arr_get xs i))
      (if (> current_val 0)
        (set total (+ total current_val)))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    total)

  (defun make_arr_3 (a b c)
    (def xs (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set xs 0 a)
    (arr_set xs 1 b)
    (arr_set xs 2 c)
    xs)

  (defun make_arr_5 (a b c d e)
    (def xs (arr_new 5))
    (arr_set xs 0 a)
    (arr_set xs 1 b)
    (arr_set xs 2 c)
    (arr_set xs 3 d)
    (arr_set xs 4 e)
    xs)

  (test empty_array (eq? (sum_positives (arr_new 0)) 0))
  (test all_positives (eq? (sum_positives (make_arr_3 1 2 3)) 6))
  (test mixed_pos_neg (eq? (sum_positives (make_arr_5 1 -2 3 -4 5)) 9))
  (test all_negatives (eq? (sum_positives (make_arr_3 -1 -2 -3)) 0))
  (test single_positive (eq? (sum_positives (make_arr_3 7 0 -1)) 7))
  (test zeros_only (eq? (sum_positives (make_arr_3 0 0 0)) 0))
  (test mixed_zeros (eq? (sum_positives (make_arr_5 0 1 -5 2 0)) 3))
)

topic: tip calculation split among diners with rounding
objective: Create a program that calculates the total cost of a meal, including a given tip percentage, and then splits that total cost evenly among a specified number of diners, rounding the final per-diner amount to two decimal places.
solution:
(module TipSplitter
  ; Helper function to round a number to two decimal places.
  (defun round_to_two_decimals (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100.0) 0.5)) 100.0))

  ; Calculates the tip amount based on the bill and percentage.
  (defun calculate_tip (bill_amount tip_percentage)
    (* bill_amount (/ tip_percentage 100.0)))

  ; Calculates the total amount including the tip.
  (defun calculate_total (bill_amount tip_amount)
    (+ bill_amount tip_amount))

  ; Main function: Calculates the amount each diner pays after tip and rounding.
  (defun split_meal_cost (bill_amount tip_percentage num_diners)
    (def tip_amt (calculate_tip bill_amount tip_percentage))
    (def total_amt (calculate_total bill_amount tip_amt))
    (def per_diner_amt (/ total_amt num_diners))
    (round_to_two_decimals per_diner_amt))

  (test basic_split
    (near? (split_meal_cost 100.00 15 2) 57.50 0.001)) ; (100 + 15% of 100) / 2 = 115 / 2 = 57.50

  (test odd_split_rounding_up
    (near? (split_meal_cost 75.50 18 3) 29.70 0.001)) ; (75.50 + 18% of 75.50) / 3 = (75.50 + 13.59) / 3 = 89.09 / 3 = 29.6966... -> 29.70

  (test zero_tip
    (near? (split_meal_cost 50.00 0 4) 12.50 0.001)) ; (50 + 0%) / 4 = 50 / 4 = 12.50

  (test large_group_rounding_down
    (near? (split_meal_cost 240.75 20 7) 41.27 0.001)) ; (240.75 + 20% of 240.75) / 7 = (240.75 + 48.15) / 7 = 288.90 / 7 = 41.2714... -> 41.27

  (test small_bill_one_person
    (near? (split_meal_cost 12.34 10 1) 13.57 0.001)) ; (12.34 + 10% of 12.34) / 1 = (12.34 + 1.234) = 13.574 -> 13.57

  (test small_bill_high_tip_multiple_people
    (near? (split_meal_cost 8.99 25 2) 5.62 0.001)) ; (8.99 + 25% of 8.99) / 2 = (8.99 + 2.2475) / 2 = 11.2375 / 2 = 5.61875 -> 5.62
)

objective: Calculate the digital root of a non-negative integer. The digital root is the single-digit value obtained by iteratively summing the digits of a number until a single-digit number is reached.
solution:
(module DigitalRootCalculator
  (defun sum_digits (n)
    (def total 0)
    (def current_n n)
    (while (> current_n 0)
      (set total (+ total (math_mod current_n 10)))
      (set current_n (math_floor (/ current_n 10))))
    total)

  (defun digital_root (n)
    (if (= n 0) (return 0))
    (def current_sum n)
    (while (> current_sum 9)
      (set current_sum (sum_digits current_sum)))
    current_sum)

  (test zero     (eq? (digital_root 0) 0))
  (test single   (eq? (digital_root 5) 5))
  (test tens     (eq? (digital_root 10) 1))
  (test basic    (eq? (digital_root 123) 6))
  (test larger   (eq? (digital_root 9876) 3)) ; 9+8+7+6 = 30; 3+0 = 3
  (test multi_iter (eq? (digital_root 49319) 8)) ; 4+9+3+1+9 = 26; 2+6 = 8
  (test nine     (eq? (digital_root 9999) 9))) ; 9+9+9+9 = 36; 3+6 = 9

objective: Implement functions to calculate a customer's monthly phone bill, accounting for a base plan cost, included data, and an overage charge for data usage exceeding the plan's limit. The final bill should be rounded to two decimal places.
solution:
(module PhoneBillCalculator

  ;; Helper to round a number to two decimal places for currency.
  (defun round_to_2_decimals (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))

  ;; Calculates the overage cost based on actual usage, included data, and overage rate.
  ;; Returns 0 if usage is within the included limit.
  (defun calculate_overage_cost (used_gb included_gb overage_rate_per_gb)
    (if (<= used_gb included_gb)
        0
        (* (- used_gb included_gb) overage_rate_per_gb)))

  ;; Calculates the total monthly phone bill including base cost and any data overage.
  (defun calculate_total_bill (base_cost used_gb included_gb overage_rate_per_gb)
    (def overage_charge (calculate_overage_cost used_gb included_gb overage_rate_per_gb))
    (round_to_2_decimals (+ base_cost overage_charge)))

  (test no_overage
    (near? (calculate_total_bill 30.00 8.0 10.0 5.50) 30.00 0.01))

  (test exact_usage
    (near? (calculate_total_bill 45.00 15.0 15.0 4.00) 45.00 0.01))

  (test some_overage
    (near? (calculate_total_bill 50.00 12.5 10.0 6.20) 65.50 0.01))
    ;; Calculation: 50.00 + (12.5 - 10.0) * 6.20 = 50.00 + 2.5 * 6.20 = 50.00 + 15.50 = 65.50

  (test large_overage
    (near? (calculate_total_bill 25.00 20.0 5.0 3.75) 81.25 0.01))
    ;; Calculation: 25.00 + (20.0 - 5.0) * 3.75 = 25.00 + 15.0 * 3.75 = 25.00 + 56.25 = 81.25

  (test zero_base_cost_overage_only
    (near? (calculate_total_bill 0.00 7.0 2.0 10.00) 50.00 0.01))
    ;; Calculation: 0.00 + (7.0 - 2.0) * 10.00 = 0.00 + 5.0 * 10.00 = 50.00

  (test fractional_overage_rate
    (near? (calculate_total_bill 60.00 22.1 20.0 5.00) 70.50 0.01))
    ;; Calculation: 60.00 + (22.1 - 20.0) * 5.00 = 60.00 + 2.1 * 5.00 = 60.00 + 10.50 = 70.50
)

topic: discounted price after coupon and bulk-buy threshold

(module DiscountedOrderPrice

    (* amount (- 1 (/ percentage 100))))

  (defun calculate_final_price (item_price quantity coupon_percentage bulk_threshold bulk_discount_percentage)

)

topic: tip calculation split among diners with rounding

(module TipSplitter

    (* bill_amount (/ tip_percentage 100.0)))

objective: Implement a function `get_request_timeout` that reads the "REQUEST_TIMEOUT_SECONDS" environment variable. If the variable is not set (returns an empty string), it should return a default timeout of 30 seconds. Otherwise, it should parse the variable's string value into an integer and return it.
solution:
(module RequestTimeout
  (extern defun env_get (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun get_request_timeout ()
    (def timeout_str (env_get "REQUEST_TIMEOUT_SECONDS"))
    (if (str_eq timeout_str "")
      (return 30)) ; Default timeout in seconds
    (str_to_num timeout_str))

  (test default_timeout_when_missing
    (mocks (env.get "REQUEST_TIMEOUT_SECONDS" ""))
    (eq? (get_request_timeout) 30))

  (test custom_timeout_set_to_60
    (mocks (env.get "REQUEST_TIMEOUT_SECONDS" "60"))
    (eq? (get_request_timeout) 60))

  (test custom_timeout_set_to_15
    (mocks (env.get "REQUEST_TIMEOUT_SECONDS" "15"))
    (eq? (get_request_timeout) 15))

  (test zero_timeout_is_valid
    (mocks (env.get "REQUEST_TIMEOUT_SECONDS" "0"))
    (eq? (get_request_timeout) 0))

objective: Calculate the total parking fee based on the duration parked and an hourly rate. The first 60 minutes of parking are free, and any subsequent partial hour is rounded up to a full hour for billing purposes.
solution:
(module ParkingFeeCalculator
  (defun get_billable_minutes (total_minutes)
    "Determines the number of minutes to be billed after the first free hour."
    (if (<= total_minutes 60)
        0
        (- total_minutes 60)))

  (defun ceil_to_billable_hours (billable_minutes)
    "Converts billable minutes into billable hours, rounding up any partial hour."
    (if (<= billable_minutes 0)
        0
        (math_ceil (/ billable_minutes 60.0))))

  (defun calculate_parking_fee (total_minutes hourly_rate)
    "Calculates the total parking fee, applying the first hour free and rounding up partial hours."
    (def effective_minutes (get_billable_minutes total_minutes))
    (def billable_hours (ceil_to_billable_hours effective_minutes))
    (* billable_hours hourly_rate))

  (test less_than_hour_free
    (near? (calculate_parking_fee 30 5.0) 0.0 0.01))

  (test exactly_one_hour_free
    (near? (calculate_parking_fee 60 5.0) 0.0 0.01))

  (test just_over_one_hour
    (near? (calculate_parking_fee 61 5.0) 5.0 0.01))

  (test one_hour_thirty_minutes
    (near? (calculate_parking_fee 90 5.0) 5.0 0.01))

  (test exactly_two_hours
    (near? (calculate_parking_fee 120 5.0) 5.0 0.01))

  (test two_hours_one_minute
    (near? (calculate_parking_fee 121 5.0) 10.0 0.01))

  (test three_hours_custom_rate
    (near? (calculate_parking_fee 180 7.5) 15.0 0.01)))

objective: Implement a function that calculates the maximum nesting depth of balanced parentheses within a given string. The function should iterate through the string, keeping track of the current depth, and return the highest depth reached.
solution:
(module ParenthesesDepth
  (defun max_parentheses_depth (s)
    (def current_depth 0)
    (def max_depth 0)
    (def i 0)
    (def len (str_length s))

    (while (< i len)
      (def char (str_substring s i 1)) ; Extract one character

      (if (str_eq char "(")
        (set current_depth (+ current_depth 1))
        ; Update max_depth if current_depth is greater
        (if (> current_depth max_depth)
          (set max_depth current_depth)))

      (if (str_eq char ")")
        ; Decrement depth, assuming balanced input
        (set current_depth (- current_depth 1)))
      
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    max_depth)

  (test empty_string
    (eq? (max_parentheses_depth "") 0))

  (test no_parentheses
    (eq? (max_parentheses_depth "hello world") 0))

  (test simple_nesting
    (eq? (max_parentheses_depth "()") 1))

  (test nested_structure
    (eq? (max_parentheses_depth "(())") 2))

  (test multiple_sequences
    (eq? (max_parentheses_depth "()()()") 1))

  (test mixed_content
    (eq? (max_parentheses_depth "a(b(c)d)e") 2))

  (test deep_nesting
    (eq? (max_parentheses_depth "((()))") 3)))

objective: Create a program that calculates a person's recommended daily water intake in liters, based on their body weight in pounds. The recommendation is half an ounce of water per pound of body weight, rounded to two decimal places.
solution:
(module WaterIntakeCalculator
  (defun calculate_daily_ounces (weight_lbs)
    "Calculates the recommended daily water intake in fluid ounces based on body weight in pounds."
    (* weight_lbs 0.5))

  (defun ounces_to_ml (ounces)
    "Converts fluid ounces to milliliters."
    (* ounces 29.5735)) ; 1 fluid ounce = 29.5735 mL

  (defun ml_to_liters (ml)
    "Converts milliliters to liters."
    (/ ml 1000))

  (defun round2 (num)
    "Rounds a number to two decimal places."
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* num 100) 0.5)) 100))

  (defun get_water_goal_liters (weight_lbs)
    "Calculates the daily water intake goal in liters for a given body weight in pounds."
    (def ounces (calculate_daily_ounces weight_lbs))
    (def ml (ounces_to_ml ounces))
    (def liters (ml_to_liters ml))
    (round2 liters))

  (test water_goal_150lbs
    (near? (get_water_goal_liters 150) 2.22 0.01)) ; 150 lbs -> 75 oz -> 2218.0125 mL -> 2.2180125 L -> 2.22 L

  (test water_goal_200lbs
    (near? (get_water_goal_liters 200) 2.96 0.01)) ; 200 lbs -> 100 oz -> 2957.35 mL -> 2.95735 L -> 2.96 L

  (test water_goal_120lbs
    (near? (get_water_goal_liters 120) 1.77 0.01)) ; 120 lbs -> 60 oz -> 1774.41 mL -> 1.77441 L -> 1.77 L

  (test water_goal_185_5lbs
    (near? (get_water_goal_liters 185.5) 2.75 0.01)) ; 185.5 lbs -> 92.75 oz -> 2747.017625 mL -> 2.747017625 L -> 2.75 L

  (test water_goal_min_weight
    (near? (get_water_goal_liters 1) 0.01 0.01)) ; 1 lb -> 0.5 oz -> 14.78675 mL -> 0.01478675 L -> 0.01 L
)

objective: Create a function `is_feature_enabled` that reads an environment variable specified by `key`. The function should interpret the variable's string value as a boolean, returning true for "true" or "1" (case-insensitive) and false for "false" or "0" (case-insensitive). If the environment variable is not set, or its value is not a recognized boolean string, it should return a provided `default_value`.
solution:
(module EnvVarBoolean
  (extern defun env_read (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun is_feature_enabled (key default_value)
    (def val (env_read key))
    (def lower_val (str_lower val))

    (if (str_eq lower_val "true") (return #true))
    (if (str_eq lower_val "1") (return #true))

    (if (str_eq lower_val "false") (return #false))
    (if (str_eq lower_val "0") (return #false))

    ; If none of the recognized boolean strings, return the default
    default_value)

  (test enabled_true
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_X" "true"))
    (eq? (is_feature_enabled "FEATURE_X" #false) #true))

  (test enabled_1
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_X" "1"))
    (eq? (is_feature_enabled "FEATURE_X" #false) #true))

  (test enabled_uppercase_true
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_X" "TRUE"))
    (eq? (is_feature_enabled "FEATURE_X" #false) #true))

  (test disabled_false
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_X" "false"))
    (eq? (is_feature_enabled "FEATURE_X" #true) #false))

  (test disabled_0
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_X" "0"))
    (eq? (is_feature_enabled "FEATURE_X" #true) #false))

  (test missing_uses_default
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_X" ""))
    (eq? (is_feature_enabled "FEATURE_X" #true) #true))

  (test invalid_string_uses_default
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_X" "yes"))
    (eq? (is_feature_enabled "FEATURE_X" #false) #false))
)

objective: Write a function that takes an array of numbers and a constant, then returns a *new* array where each element from the original array has the constant subtracted from it. The original array must not be modified.
solution:
(module ArraySubtractConstant
  (defun make_num_array (a b c)
    (def xs (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set xs 0 a)
    (arr_set xs 1 b)
    (arr_set xs 2 c)
    xs)

  (defun subtract_constant_from_array (numbers constant)
    (def len (arr_length numbers))
    (def result_array (arr_new len))
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i len)
      (def original_value (arr_get numbers i))
      (def new_value (- original_value constant))
      (arr_set result_array i new_value)
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    result_array)

  (test empty_array
    (eq? (subtract_constant_from_array (arr_new 0) 10)
         (arr_new 0)))

  (test positive_numbers_pos_constant
    (eq? (subtract_constant_from_array (make_num_array 10 20 30) 5)
         (make_num_array 5 15 25)))

  (test negative_numbers_pos_constant
    (eq? (subtract_constant_from_array (make_num_array -1 -5 -10) 2)
         (make_num_array -3 -7 -12)))

  (test mixed_numbers_neg_constant
    (eq? (subtract_constant_from_array (make_num_array 5 0 -5) -3) ; 5 - (-3) = 8
         (make_num_array 8 3 -2)))

  (test zero_constant
    (eq? (subtract_constant_from_array (make_num_array 1 2 3) 0)
         (make_num_array 1 2 3)))

  (test single_element
    (def single_arr (arr_new 1))
    (arr_set single_arr 0 100)
    (def expected_arr (arr_new 1))
    (arr_set expected_arr 0 90)
    (eq? (subtract_constant_from_array single_arr 10)
         expected_arr)))

objective: Implement a function `fetch_first_word` that takes a URL, performs an HTTP GET request to that URL using the `http.fetch` capability, and returns the first non-empty word from the response body. Words are separated by spaces. If the response body is empty or contains only spaces, return an empty string.
solution:
(module HttpFirstWord
  (extern defun http_get (url) @capability "http.fetch")

  ; Helper to find the first non-empty string in an array of strings
  (defun get_first_non_empty_part (parts)
    (def i 0)
    (def len (arr_length parts))
    (while (< i len)
      (def part (arr_get parts i))
      (if (not (str_eq part ""))
        (return part))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    "") ; Return empty string if no non-empty part found

  ; Fetches a URL and returns the first word of the response body
  (defun fetch_first_word (url)
    (def body (http_get url))
    (def parts (str_split body " "))
    (get_first_non_empty_part parts))

  (test standard_body
    (mocks (http.fetch "http://example.com/hello" "Hello World, this is a test."))
    (eq? (fetch_first_word "http://example.com/hello") "Hello"))

  (test single_word_body
    (mocks (http.fetch "http://example.com/single" "OnlyWord"))
    (eq? (fetch_first_word "http://example.com/single") "OnlyWord"))

  (test empty_body
    (mocks (http.fetch "http://example.com/empty" ""))
    (eq? (fetch_first_word "http://example.com/empty") ""))

  (test body_with_leading_spaces
    (mocks (http.fetch "http://example.com/leading" "   First second third"))
    (eq? (fetch_first_word "http://example.com/leading") "First"))

  (test body_with_only_spaces
    (mocks (http.fetch "http://example.com/spaces" "     "))
    (eq? (fetch_first_word "http://example.com/spaces") ""))

  (test body_with_multiple_spaces_between_words
    (mocks (http.fetch "http://example.com/multi-space" "One   Two    Three"))
    (eq? (fetch_first_word "http://example.com/multi-space") "One"))

  (test body_no_spaces_but_other_chars
    (mocks (http.fetch "http://example.com/no-space-other" "HelloWorld!"))
    (eq? (fetch_first_word "http://example.com/no-space-other") "HelloWorld!"))
)

objective: Write a function `get_first_line` that takes a file path, reads the content of the file using a capability, and returns the very first line. If the file is empty or does not exist, the function should return an empty string.
solution:
(module FirstLineReader
  (extern defun file_read (path) @capability "file.read")

  (defun get_first_line (file_path)
    (def file_content (file_read file_path))
    ; str_split of an empty string "" by "\n" results in [""]
    ; str_split of "hello" by "\n" results in ["hello"]
    ; str_split of "hello\nworld" by "\n" results in ["hello", "world"]
    (def lines (str_split file_content "\n"))
    ; Safely get the first element, which will be "" for empty or initial newline cases
    (arr_get lines 0))

  (test multi_line_file
    (mocks (file.read "data.txt" "First line of text\nSecond line\nThird line here"))
    (eq? (get_first_line "data.txt") "First line of text"))

  (test single_line_file
    (mocks (file.read "single.txt" "This is the only line in the file."))
    (eq? (get_first_line "single.txt") "This is the only line in the file."))

  (test empty_file
    (mocks (file.read "empty.txt" ""))
    (eq? (get_first_line "empty.txt") ""))

  (test file_not_found_mock
    (mocks (file.read "nonexistent.txt" "")) ; Mock returns empty string for non-existent files
    (eq? (get_first_line "nonexistent.txt") ""))

  (test file_starts_with_newline
    (mocks (file.read "starts_with_nl.txt" "\nSecond line\nThird line"))
    (eq? (get_first_line "starts_with_nl.txt") ""))

  (test file_only_newline
    (mocks (file.read "only_nl.txt" "\n"))
    (eq? (get_first_line "only_nl.txt") "")))

(module BondYieldToMaturity

    (* face_value coupon_rate))

  (defun get_annualized_capital_gain_loss (face_value current_price years_to_maturity)
    (/ (- face_value current_price) years_to_maturity))

  (defun get_average_bond_price (face_value current_price)
    (/ (+ face_value current_price) 2))

    (def annualized_gain_loss (get_annualized_capital_gain_loss face_value current_price years_to_maturity))

    (/ (+ annual_coupon annualized_gain_loss) avg_price))

    (mocks (file.read "empty1.txt" "")
           (file.read "empty2.txt" ""))
    (eq? (concat_two_files "empty1.txt" "empty2.txt") ""))

  (test longer_content
    (mocks (file.read "long1.txt" "This is the first part of a longer string. ")
           (file.read "long2.txt" "And this is the second part, completing the message."))
    (eq? (concat_two_files "long1.txt" "long2.txt") "This is the first part of a longer string. And this is the second part, completing the message.")))

topic: monthly subscription cost with annual discount
objective: Calculate the total cost of a subscription for a given number of months, factoring in a potential annual discount. If the subscription period includes full years (12-month blocks), those years will receive a specified annual discount, while any remaining partial months will be charged at the standard monthly rate.
solution:
(module SubscriptionCalculator
  (defun calculate_yearly_discounted_cost (monthly_rate discount_pct)
    (def base_annual_cost (* monthly_rate 12))
    (* base_annual_cost (- 1 (/ discount_pct 100))))

  (defun calculate_monthly_period_cost (monthly_rate num_months)
    (* monthly_rate num_months))

  (defun get_total_subscription_cost (monthly_rate num_months annual_discount_pct)
    (def num_full_years (math_floor (/ num_months 12)))
    (def remaining_months (math_mod num_months 12))
    (def yearly_discounted_cost (calculate_yearly_discounted_cost monthly_rate annual_discount_pct))
    (def cost_full_years (* num_full_years yearly_discounted_cost))
    (def cost_remaining_months (calculate_monthly_period_cost monthly_rate remaining_months))
    (+ cost_full_years cost_remaining_months))

  (test less_than_year_no_discount
    (near? (get_total_subscription_cost 10 5 10) 50 0.01))

  (test exactly_one_year_with_discount
    (near? (get_total_subscription_cost 10 12 10) 108 0.01))

  (test one_year_and_partial_months
    (near? (get_total_subscription_cost 10 15 10) 138 0.01)) ; 1 year (108) + 3 months (30)

  (test multiple_years_with_discount
    (near? (get_total_subscription_cost 10 24 10) 216 0.01)) ; 2 years (2 * 108)

  (test zero_discount_behaves_as_no_discount
    (near? (get_total_subscription_cost 10 12 0) 120 0.01))

  (test different_rate_and_discount
    (near? (get_total_subscription_cost 25 13 5) 310 0.01)) ; 1 year (25*12*(1-0.05)=285) + 1 month (25)

  (test just_one_month
    (near? (get_total_subscription_cost 50 1 20) 50 0.01)))

topic: read file and count char occurrences
objective: Write a function that reads the content of a file and counts the occurrences of a specified single character within that content. The function should take the file path and the character to find as arguments.
solution:
(module FileCharCounter
  (extern defun file_read (path) @capability "file.read")

  (defun count_char_in_file (path char_to_find)
    (def content (file_read path))
    (def content_len (str_length content))
    (def count 0)
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i content_len)
      (if (str_eq (str_substring content i 1) char_to_find)
        (set count (+ count 1)))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    count)

  (test empty_file
    (mocks (file.read "/empty.txt" ""))
    (eq? (count_char_in_file "/empty.txt" "a") 0))

  (test no_occurrences
    (mocks (file.read "/data.txt" "hello world"))
    (eq? (count_char_in_file "/data.txt" "z") 0))

  (test single_occurrence
    (mocks (file.read "/data.txt" "banana"))
    (eq? (count_char_in_file "/data.txt" "b") 1))

  (test multiple_occurrences
    (mocks (file.read "/data.txt" "banana"))
    (eq? (count_char_in_file "/data.txt" "a") 3))

  (test case_sensitive
    (mocks (file.read "/data.txt" "Apple"))
    (eq? (count_char_in_file "/data.txt" "a") 0))

  (test includes_whitespace_and_newline
    (mocks (file.read "/complex.txt" "one two\nthree"))
    (eq? (count_char_in_file "/complex.txt" "e") 3)))

objective: Write a function `product_of_array` that calculates the product of all numbers in an input array. If the array is empty, it should return 1.
solution:
(module ArrayProduct
  (defun product_of_array (xs)
    (def i 0)
    (def n (arr_length xs))
    (def total_product 1)

    (while (< i n)
      (set total_product (* total_product (arr_get xs i)))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    total_product)

  (test empty_array
    (eq? (product_of_array (arr_new 0)) 1))

  (test single_element
    (def arr (arr_new 1))
    (arr_set arr 0 5)
    (eq? (product_of_array arr) 5))

  (test positive_integers
    (def arr (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set arr 0 1)
    (arr_set arr 1 2)
    (arr_set arr 2 3)
    (eq? (product_of_array arr) 6))

  (test with_zero
    (def arr (arr_new 4))
    (arr_set arr 0 2)
    (arr_set arr 1 5)
    (arr_set arr 2 0)
    (arr_set arr 3 3)
    (eq? (product_of_array arr) 0))

  (test negative_numbers
    (def arr (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set arr 0 2)
    (arr_set arr 1 -3)
    (arr_set arr 2 4)
    (eq? (product_of_array arr) -24))

  (test mixed_numbers
    (def arr (arr_new 4))
    (arr_set arr 0 10)
    (arr_set arr 1 -1)
    (arr_set arr 2 0.5)
    (arr_set arr 3 -2)
    (eq? (product_of_array arr) 10)))

objective: Implement two pure functions that convert temperatures. One function should convert Celsius to Kelvin, and another should convert Fahrenheit to Kelvin.

(module TemperatureConverter

  (test c_to_k_freezing (near? (celsius_to_kelvin 0.0) 273.15 0.01))
