objective: Calculate the Nth Fibonacci number using an iterative approach. The function should take a non-negative integer `n` and return the Nth Fibonacci number, where F(0) = 0 and F(1) = 1. For negative inputs, return 0.
solution:
(module FibonacciIterative
  (defun fib_iterative (n)
    (if (< n 0) (return 0))
    (if (= n 0) (return 0))
    (if (= n 1) (return 1))

    (def a 0) ; Represents F(i-2)
    (def b 1) ; Represents F(i-1)
    (def i 2)

    (while (<= i n)
      (def next_fib (+ a b))
      (set a b)
      (set b next_fib)
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    b)

  (test fib_0 (eq? (fib_iterative 0) 0))
  (test fib_1 (eq? (fib_iterative 1) 1))
  (test fib_2 (eq? (fib_iterative 2) 1))
  (test fib_3 (eq? (fib_iterative 3) 2))
  (test fib_5 (eq? (fib_iterative 5) 5))
  (test fib_10 (eq? (fib_iterative 10) 55))
  (test fib_negative (eq? (fib_iterative -5) 0)))

topic: split string on space then count words
objective: Given a string, count the number of words it contains. A word is defined as any sequence of non-space characters. Multiple spaces between words or leading/trailing spaces should not result in empty words being counted.
solution:
(module WordCounter
  (defun count_words (text)
    (def words_arr (str_split text " "))
    (def word_count 0)
    (def i 0)
    (def n (arr_length words_arr))
    (while (< i n)
      (def current_word (arr_get words_arr i))
      (if (not (str_eq current_word ""))
        (set word_count (+ word_count 1)))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    word_count)

  (test empty_string
    (eq? (count_words "") 0))

  (test single_word
    (eq? (count_words "hello") 1))

  (test two_words_normal
    (eq? (count_words "hello world") 2))

  (test multiple_spaces
    (eq? (count_words "  this   has   extra   spaces  ") 4))

  (test punctuation_words
    (eq? (count_words "Hello, world! How are you?") 5))

  (test only_spaces
    (eq? (count_words "   ") 0)))

objective: Create a function that reads the content of a specified file and returns only its first line. The function should gracefully handle files that are empty, non-existent, or begin with a newline character, returning an empty string in such cases.
solution:
(module FirstLineReader
  (extern defun file_read (path) @capability "file.read")

  (defun get_first_line (file_path)
    (def content (file_read file_path))
    (def lines (str_split content "\n"))
    (arr_get lines 0))

  (test multiple_lines
    (mocks (file.read "multi.txt" "First line of text.\nSecond line.\nThird line."))
    (eq? (get_first_line "multi.txt") "First line of text."))

  (test single_line_no_newline
    (mocks (file.read "single.txt" "This is the only line."))
    (eq? (get_first_line "single.txt") "This is the only line."))

  (test empty_file
    (mocks (file.read "empty.txt" ""))
    (eq? (get_first_line "empty.txt") ""))

  (test non_existent_file
    (mocks (file.read "missing.txt" ""))
    (eq? (get_first_line "missing.txt") ""))

  (test starts_with_newline
    (mocks (file.read "starts_nl.txt" "\nActual first line.\nAnother line."))
    (eq? (get_first_line "starts_nl.txt") ""))

  (test ends_with_newline
    (mocks (file.read "ends_nl.txt" "Line with trailing newline.\n"))
    (eq? (get_first_line "ends_nl.txt") "Line with trailing newline."))

  (test only_newlines
    (mocks (file.read "only_nl.txt" "\n\n\n"))
    (eq? (get_first_line "only_nl.txt") ""))
)

topic: read two env vars and concat
objective: Create a function that reads the values of two specified environment variables. It should concatenate their values, treating any missing environment variable as an empty string.
solution:
(module EnvConcat
  (extern defun env_read (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun concat_env_vars (key1 key2)
    (def val1 (env_read key1))
    (def val2 (env_read key2))
    (str_concat val1 val2))

  (test both_present
    (mocks
      (env.get "PREFIX" "Hello, ")
      (env.get "SUFFIX" "World!"))
    (eq? (concat_env_vars "PREFIX" "SUFFIX") "Hello, World!"))

  (test first_missing
    (mocks
      (env.get "PREFIX" "")
      (env.get "SUFFIX" "World!"))
    (eq? (concat_env_vars "PREFIX" "SUFFIX") "World!"))

  (test second_missing
    (mocks
      (env.get "PREFIX" "Hello, ")
      (env.get "SUFFIX" ""))
    (eq? (concat_env_vars "PREFIX" "SUFFIX") "Hello, "))

  (test both_missing
    (mocks
      (env.get "PREFIX" "")
      (env.get "SUFFIX" ""))
    (eq? (concat_env_vars "PREFIX" "SUFFIX") ""))

  (test non_string_values
    (mocks
      (env.get "NUM1" "123")
      (env.get "NUM2" "456"))
    (eq? (concat_env_vars "NUM1" "NUM2") "123456")))

objective: Write a function `count_evens` that takes an array of numbers and returns the total count of even numbers within that array. A number is considered even if it is perfectly divisible by 2 (i.e., its remainder when divided by 2 is 0).
solution:
(module CountEvens
  (defun count_evens (numbers)
    (def i 0)
    (def count 0)
    (def n (arr_length numbers))
    (while (< i n)
      (def current_num (arr_get numbers i))
      (if (= (math_mod current_num 2) 0)
        (set count (+ count 1)))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    count)

  ; Helper functions for creating test arrays of specific lengths
  (defun make_list_0 ()
    (arr_new 0))

  (defun make_list_1 (a)
    (def xs (arr_new 1))
    (arr_set xs 0 a)
    xs)

  (defun make_list_3 (a b c)
    (def xs (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set xs 0 a)
    (arr_set xs 1 b)
    (arr_set xs 2 c)
    xs)

  (defun make_list_5 (a b c d e)
    (def xs (arr_new 5))
    (arr_set xs 0 a)
    (arr_set xs 1 b)
    (arr_set xs 2 c)
    (arr_set xs 3 d)
    (arr_set xs 4 e)
    xs)

  (test empty_array
    (eq? (count_evens (make_list_0)) 0))

  (test single_odd
    (eq? (count_evens (make_list_1 7)) 0))

  (test single_even
    (eq? (count_evens (make_list_1 4)) 1))

  (test all_odds
    (eq? (count_evens (make_list_3 1 3 5)) 0))

  (test mixed_numbers
    (eq? (count_evens (make_list_5 1 2 3 4 5)) 2))

  (test all_evens
    (eq? (count_evens (make_list_5 2 4 6 8 10)) 5))

  (test with_zero_and_negatives
    (eq? (count_evens (make_list_5 -2 -1 0 1 2)) 3))
)

topic: freelance billable hours with overtime multiplier
objective: Calculate the total billable cost for freelance work, accounting for a standard hourly rate up to a specified threshold and an overtime rate multiplier for any hours worked beyond that threshold.
solution:
(module FreelanceBilling
  (defun calculate_standard_billable_hours (total_hours threshold)
    (math_min total_hours threshold))

  (defun calculate_overtime_billable_hours (total_hours threshold)
    (math_max 0 (- total_hours threshold)))

  (defun calculate_total_bill (total_hours hourly_rate threshold overtime_multiplier)
    (def std_hours (calculate_standard_billable_hours total_hours threshold))
    (def ot_hours (calculate_overtime_billable_hours total_hours threshold))
    (def std_cost (* std_hours hourly_rate))
    (def ot_cost (* ot_hours hourly_rate overtime_multiplier))
    (+ std_cost ot_cost))

  (test no_overtime
    (eq? (calculate_total_bill 30 50 40 1.5) 1500)) ; 30 hours * $50/hour = $1500

  (test with_overtime
    (eq? (calculate_total_bill 45 50 40 1.5) 2375)) ; (40 * 50) + (5 * 50 * 1.5) = 2000 + 375 = 2375

  (test exact_threshold
    (eq? (calculate_total_bill 40 60 40 2.0) 2400)) ; 40 hours * $60/hour = $2400

  (test more_overtime
    (eq? (calculate_total_bill 50 75 40 2.0) 4500)) ; (40 * 75) + (10 * 75 * 2.0) = 3000 + 1500 = 4500

  (test zero_hours
    (eq? (calculate_total_bill 0 100 40 1.5) 0))

  (test high_overtime_rate
    (eq? (calculate_total_bill 42 100 40 3.0) 4600)) ; (40 * 100) + (2 * 100 * 3.0) = 4000 + 600 = 4600
)

objective: Write a function that fetches content from a given URL using an HTTP GET request and returns the very first word found in the response body. A "word" is defined as any sequence of non-space characters. Leading, trailing, and multiple internal spaces should be handled correctly.
solution:
(module HttpGetFirstWord
  (extern defun http_fetch (url) @capability "http.fetch")

  (defun first_non_empty_string (arr)
    (def i 0)
    (def len (arr_length arr))
    (while (< i len)
      (def current_str (arr_get arr i))
      (if (not (str_eq current_str ""))
        (return current_str))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    "") ; Return empty string if no non-empty string is found

  (defun get_first_word_from_url (url)
    (def body (http_fetch url))
    (def parts (str_split body " "))
    (first_non_empty_string parts))

  (test simple_case
    (mocks (http.fetch "http://example.com/hello" "Hello World! This is a test."))
    (eq? (get_first_word_from_url "http://example.com/hello") "Hello"))

  (test leading_trailing_spaces
    (mocks (http.fetch "http://example.com/spaced" "  Trimmed content starts here."))
    (eq? (get_first_word_from_url "http://example.com/spaced") "Trimmed"))

  (test empty_body
    (mocks (http.fetch "http://example.com/empty" ""))
    (eq? (get_first_word_from_url "http://example.com/empty") ""))

  (test body_only_spaces
    (mocks (http.fetch "http://example.com/justspaces" "     "))
    (eq? (get_first_word_from_url "http://example.com/justspaces") ""))

  (test single_word_body
    (mocks (http.fetch "http://example.com/single" "OnlyWord"))
    (eq? (get_first_word_from_url "http://example.com/single") "OnlyWord"))

  (test multiple_internal_spaces
    (mocks (http.fetch "http://example.com/multi-space" "First    Second Third"))
    (eq? (get_first_word_from_url "http://example.com/multi-space") "First"))

  (test punctuation_in_word
    (mocks (http.fetch "http://example.com/punctuation" "Hello,world! How are you?"))
    (eq? (get_first_word_from_url "http://example.com/punctuation") "Hello,world!"))
)

objective: Write a function that retrieves an environment variable by its key. If the environment variable is not set or its value is an empty string, the function should return a specified default integer. Otherwise, it should parse the environment variable's string value into an integer and return it.
solution:
(module EnvIntReader
  (extern defun env_read (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun get_env_int (key default_value)
    (def raw_val (env_read key))
    (if (str_eq raw_val "")
      (return default_value))
    (str_to_num raw_val))

  (test missing_port
    (mocks (env.get "APP_PORT" ""))
    (eq? (get_env_int "APP_PORT" 8080) 8080))

  (test explicit_port
    (mocks (env.get "APP_PORT" "9000"))
    (eq? (get_env_int "APP_PORT" 8080) 9000))

  (test timeout_value
    (mocks (env.get "REQUEST_TIMEOUT_SECONDS" "300"))
    (eq? (get_env_int "REQUEST_TIMEOUT_SECONDS" 60) 300))

  (test default_timeout
    (mocks (env.get "REQUEST_TIMEOUT_SECONDS" ""))
    (eq? (get_env_int "REQUEST_TIMEOUT_SECONDS" 120) 120))

  (test negative_offset
    (mocks (env.get "OFFSET_VALUE" "-10"))
    (eq? (get_env_int "OFFSET_VALUE" 0) -10))

  (test zero_value
    (mocks (env.get "MAX_RETRIES" "0"))
    (eq? (get_env_int "MAX_RETRIES" 3) 0)))

objective: Implement a function that takes an array of numbers and returns a new array with the elements in reverse order. The original array should remain unchanged.
solution:
(module ArrayReverser
  (defun reverse_numbers (arr)
    (def len (arr_length arr))
    (def reversed_arr (arr_new len))
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i len)
      (def original_val (arr_get arr i))
      (arr_set reversed_arr (- (- len 1) i) original_val)
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    reversed_arr)

  (test empty_array
    (eq? (reverse_numbers (arr_new 0)) (arr_new 0)))

  (test single_element_array
    (def arr1 (arr_new 1))
    (arr_set arr1 0 5)
    (def expected1 (arr_new 1))
    (arr_set expected1 0 5)
    (eq? (reverse_numbers arr1) expected1))

  (test even_length_array
    (def arr2 (arr_new 4))
    (arr_set arr2 0 1)
    (arr_set arr2 1 2)
    (arr_set arr2 2 3)
    (arr_set arr2 3 4)
    (def expected2 (arr_new 4))
    (arr_set expected2 0 4)
    (arr_set expected2 1 3)
    (arr_set expected2 2 2)
    (arr_set expected2 3 1)
    (eq? (reverse_numbers arr2) expected2))

  (test odd_length_array
    (def arr3 (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set arr3 0 10)
    (arr_set arr3 1 20)
    (arr_set arr3 2 30)
    (def expected3 (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set expected3 0 30)
    (arr_set expected3 1 20)
    (arr_set expected3 2 10)
    (eq? (reverse_numbers arr3) expected3))

  (test array_with_negatives
    (def arr4 (arr_new 5))
    (arr_set arr4 0 -1)
    (arr_set arr4 1 0)
    (arr_set arr4 2 2)
    (arr_set arr4 3 -3)
    (arr_set arr4 4 4)
    (def expected4 (arr_new 5))
    (arr_set expected4 0 4)
    (arr_set expected4 1 -3)
    (arr_set expected4 2 2)
    (arr_set expected4 3 0)
    (arr_set expected4 4 -1)
    (eq? (reverse_numbers arr4) expected4))

  (test original_array_unchanged
    (def original_arr (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set original_arr 0 1)
    (arr_set original_arr 1 2)
    (arr_set original_arr 2 3)
    (def _ (reverse_numbers original_arr)) ; Call to ensure it doesn't modify
    (def expected_original (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set expected_original 0 1)
    (arr_set expected_original 1 2)
    (arr_set expected_original 2 3)
    (eq? original_arr expected_original)))

objective: Create a function that reads a file's content, interpreting it as a numeric string representing a size. If the file is not found or empty (indicated by `file.read` returning an empty string), the function should return 0. Otherwise, it converts the file's content to a number and returns it.
solution:
(module ContentSizeReader
  (extern defun file_read (filepath) @capability "file.read")

  (defun get_content_size (filepath)
    (def content (file_read filepath))
    (if (str_eq content "") (return 0))
    (str_to_num content))

  (test reads_valid_numeric_content
    (mocks (file.read "/path/to/data.txt" "12345"))
    (eq? (get_content_size "/path/to/data.txt") 12345))

  (test returns_zero_for_empty_file
    (mocks (file.read "/path/to/empty.txt" ""))
    (eq? (get_content_size "/path/to/empty.txt") 0))

  (test returns_zero_for_non_existent_file
    (mocks (file.read "/path/to/nonexistent.txt" ""))
    (eq? (get_content_size "/path/to/nonexistent.txt") 0))

  (test handles_zero_as_content
    (mocks (file.read "/path/to/zero.txt" "0"))
    (eq? (get_content_size "/path/to/zero.txt") 0))

  (test handles_large_numbers
    (mocks (file.read "/path/to/large.txt" "987654321098765"))
    (eq? (get_content_size "/path/to/large.txt") 987654321098765))
)

topic: tip calculation split among diners with rounding
objective: Implement a function that calculates the amount each diner pays after splitting a restaurant bill, including a tip, and rounding the final per-person amount to two decimal places.
solution:
(module BillSplitter

  ; Helper function to round a number to two decimal places
  (defun round_to_two_decimals (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100.0) 0.5)) 100.0))

  ; Calculates the tip amount based on the bill and percentage
  (defun calculate_tip_amount (bill_amount tip_percentage)
    (* bill_amount (/ tip_percentage 100.0)))

  ; Calculates the total bill including the tip
  (defun calculate_total_with_tip (bill_amount tip_amount)
    (+ bill_amount tip_amount))

  ; Divides the total amount among the diners
  (defun divide_among_diners (total_amount num_diners)
    (/ total_amount num_diners))

  ; Main function to calculate the split bill per person with tip and rounding
  (defun calculate_split_bill (bill_amount tip_percentage num_diners)
    (def tip_amt (calculate_tip_amount bill_amount tip_percentage))
    (def total_with_tip (calculate_total_with_tip bill_amount tip_amt))
    (def per_person_raw (divide_among_diners total_with_tip num_diners))
    (round_to_two_decimals per_person_raw))

  ; Tests
  (test simple_split
    (near? (calculate_split_bill 100.00 15 2) 57.50 0.001))

  (test common_scenario
    (near? (calculate_split_bill 75.50 20 3) 30.20 0.001))

  (test rounding_up_example
    (near? (calculate_split_bill 45.67 18 4) 13.47 0.001))

  (test rounding_down_example
    (near? (calculate_split_bill 60.00 10 7) 9.43 0.001))

  (test zero_tip
    (near? (calculate_split_bill 50.00 0 5) 10.00 0.001))

  (test single_diner
    (near? (calculate_split_bill 82.35 25 1) 102.94 0.001))
)

objective: Calculate the future value of an investment using the compound interest formula, given the initial principal, annual interest rate (as a percentage), the number of times interest is compounded per year, and the total number of years.
solution:
(module CompoundInterestCalculator
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))

  (defun calculate_future_value (principal annual_rate_pct compound_frequency years)
    (def rate_decimal (/ annual_rate_pct 100))
    (def base (+ 1 (/ rate_decimal compound_frequency)))
    (def exponent (* compound_frequency years))
    (def factor (math_pow base exponent))
    (round2 (* principal factor)))

  (test simple_annual
    (near? (calculate_future_value 1000 5 1 1) 1050.00 0.01))

  (test quarterly_10_years
    (near? (calculate_future_value 1000 5 4 10) 1643.62 0.01))

  (test monthly_5_years
    (near? (calculate_future_value 5000 3 12 5) 5808.08 0.01))

  (test daily_1_year
    (near? (calculate_future_value 2000 4 365 1) 2081.61 0.01))

  (test zero_rate
    (near? (calculate_future_value 1000 0 12 5) 1000.00 0.01))

  (test high_frequency_short_term
    (near? (calculate_future_value 100 10 365 0.5) 105.13 0.01)))

objective: Count Zeros in Array
solution:
(module CountZerosInArray
  (defun count_zeros (arr)
    (def count 0)
    (def i 0)
    (def len (arr_length arr))
    (while (< i len)
      (if (= (arr_get arr i) 0)
        (set count (+ count 1)))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    count)

  (defun make_array (a b c d)
    (def xs (arr_new 4))
    (arr_set xs 0 a)
    (arr_set xs 1 b)
    (arr_set xs 2 c)
    (arr_set xs 3 d)
    xs)

  (test empty_array
    (eq? (count_zeros (arr_new 0)) 0))

  (test no_zeros
    (eq? (count_zeros (make_array 1 2 3 4)) 0))

  (test one_zero
    (eq? (count_zeros (make_array 1 0 3 4)) 1))

  (test multiple_zeros
    (eq? (count_zeros (make_array 0 2 0 4)) 2))

  (test all_zeros
    (eq? (count_zeros (make_array 0 0 0 0)) 4))

  (test negative_numbers
    (eq? (count_zeros (make_array -1 -2 0 -4)) 1))

  (test large_number_array
    (def large_arr (arr_new 5))
    (arr_set large_arr 0 100)
    (arr_set large_arr 1 0)
    (arr_set large_arr 2 200)
    (arr_set large_arr 3 0)
    (arr_set large_arr 4 300)
    (eq? (count_zeros large_arr) 2)))

objective: Implement a heart rate zone classifier that determines a person's training zone based on their age and current heart rate. The system should calculate maximum heart rate (MHR) using the formula 220 - age, and then classify the current heart rate into standard zones (e.g., Zone 1: 50-60% MHR, Zone 2: 60-70% MHR, etc.).
solution:
(module HeartRateZoneClassifier
  (defun calculate_max_heart_rate (age)
    (- 220 age))

  (defun classify_heart_rate (age current_hr)
    (def max_hr (calculate_max_heart_rate age))

    ; Define the lower bounds for each zone based on MHR percentages
    (def zone5_min (* max_hr 0.90)) ; 90-100% MHR (Maximum)
    (def zone4_min (* max_hr 0.80)) ; 80-90% MHR (Hard)
    (def zone3_min (* max_hr 0.70)) ; 70-80% MHR (Moderate)
    (def zone2_min (* max_hr 0.60)) ; 60-70% MHR (Light)
    (def zone1_min (* max_hr 0.50)) ; 50-60% MHR (Very Light)

    ; Classify from highest zone to lowest
    (if (>= current_hr zone5_min) (return "Zone 5 (Maximum)"))
    (if (>= current_hr zone4_min) (return "Zone 4 (Hard)"))
    (if (>= current_hr zone3_min) (return "Zone 3 (Moderate)"))
    (if (>= current_hr zone2_min) (return "Zone 2 (Light)"))
    (if (>= current_hr zone1_min) (return "Zone 1 (Very Light)"))
    
    ; If below all defined zones
    "Below Zone 1")

  (test young_person_zone2
    (eq? (classify_heart_rate 25 125) "Zone 2 (Light)")) ; MHR=195, Z2 >= 117

  (test young_person_zone5
    (eq? (classify_heart_rate 25 180) "Zone 5 (Maximum)")) ; MHR=195, Z5 >= 175.5

  (test young_person_below_zone1
    (eq? (classify_heart_rate 25 90) "Below Zone 1")) ; MHR=195, Z1 >= 97.5

  (test middle_aged_zone3
    (eq? (classify_heart_rate 40 138) "Zone 3 (Moderate)")) ; MHR=180, Z3 >= 126

  (test middle_aged_zone4_boundary
    (eq? (classify_heart_rate 40 144) "Zone 4 (Hard)")) ; MHR=180, Z4 >= 144

  (test older_person_zone1
    (eq? (classify_heart_rate 60 85) "Zone 1 (Very Light)")) ; MHR=160, Z1 >= 80

  (test older_person_zone3
    (eq? (classify_heart_rate 60 120) "Zone 3 (Moderate)")) ; MHR=160, Z3 >= 112
)

objective: Create a function that reads an environment variable by key and interprets its string value as a boolean. The function should return `true` if the variable's value (case-insensitively) is "true", "1", "yes", or "on"; otherwise, it should return `false`.
solution:
(module EnvVarBoolParser
  (extern defun env_read (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun is_feature_enabled (key)
    (def val_str (env_read key))
    (def lower_val (str_lower val_str))
    (if (str_eq lower_val "true") (return true))
    (if (str_eq lower_val "1") (return true))
    (if (str_eq lower_val "yes") (return true))
    (if (str_eq lower_val "on") (return true))
    false)

  (test enabled_true
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_X" "true"))
    (eq? (is_feature_enabled "FEATURE_X") true))

  (test enabled_1
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_Y" "1"))
    (eq? (is_feature_enabled "FEATURE_Y") true))

  (test enabled_yes_case_insensitive
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_Z" "YeS"))
    (eq? (is_feature_enabled "FEATURE_Z") true))

  (test enabled_on
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_A" "ON"))
    (eq? (is_feature_enabled "FEATURE_A") true))

  (test disabled_false
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_B" "false"))
    (eq? (is_feature_enabled "FEATURE_B") false))

  (test disabled_empty
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_C" ""))
    (eq? (is_feature_enabled "FEATURE_C") false))

  (test disabled_other_string
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_D" "unknown"))
    (eq? (is_feature_enabled "FEATURE_D") false)))

objective: Implement a function that converts a Roman numeral string into its corresponding integer value. The function should correctly handle both additive and subtractive notation (e.g., IV = 4, IX = 9).
solution:
(module RomanNumeralParser
  (defun char_to_val (c_str)
    (if (str_eq c_str "I") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq c_str "V") (return 5))
    (if (str_eq c_str "X") (return 10))
    (if (str_eq c_str "L") (return 50))
    (if (str_eq c_str "C") (return 100))
    (if (str_eq c_str "D") (return 500))
    (if (str_eq c_str "M") (return 1000))
    0) ; Should not be reached with valid Roman numeral characters

  (defun roman_to_int (s)
    (def total 0)
    (def i 0)
    (def n (str_length s))
    (while (< i n)
      (def current_val (char_to_val (str_substring s i 1)))
      (def next_val 0)
      ; Check if there's a next character to consider for subtractive notation
      (if (< (+ i 1) n)
        (set next_val (char_to_val (str_substring s (+ i 1) 1))))

      ; If current value is less than the next, it's a subtractive case (e.g., IV)
      ; otherwise, it's an additive case or the last character
      (if (< current_val next_val)
        (set total (- total current_val))
        (set total (+ total current_val)))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    total)

  (test simple_iii (eq? (roman_to_int "III") 3))
  (test simple_iv (eq? (roman_to_int "IV") 4))
  (test simple_ix (eq? (roman_to_int "IX") 9))
  (test example_lviii (eq? (roman_to_int "LVIII") 58))
  (test example_mcmxciv (eq? (roman_to_int "MCMXCIV") 1994))
  (test single_m (eq? (roman_to_int "M") 1000))
  (test empty_string (eq? (roman_to_int "") 0)))

objective: Write a function that takes three integer arguments and returns the largest among them.
solution:
(module MaxOfThree
  (defun max_of_three (a b c)
    (def current_max a)
    (if (> b current_max)
      (set current_max b))
    (if (> c current_max)
      (set current_max c))
    current_max)

  (test first_is_max    (eq? (max_of_three 10 5 3) 10))
  (test second_is_max   (eq? (max_of_three 1 20 7) 20))
  (test third_is_max    (eq? (max_of_three 4 6 15) 15))
  (test all_negative    (eq? (max_of_three -5 -1 -10) -1))
  (test mixed_values    (eq? (max_of_three -8 0 3) 3))
  (test equal_values    (eq? (max_of_three 7 7 7) 7))
  (test two_equal_max   (eq? (max_of_three 12 5 12) 12)))

objective: Implement a function that reads the content of two specified files and returns their concatenated content. If a file is not found or empty, it should be treated as an empty string for concatenation.
solution:
(module FileConcatenator
  (extern defun file_read (path) @capability "file.read")

  (defun concatenate_files (path1 path2)
    (def content1 (file_read path1))
    (def content2 (file_read path2))
    (str_concat content1 content2))

  (test both_have_content
    (mocks (file.read "/path/to/file1.txt" "First part. ")
           (file.read "/path/to/file2.txt" "Second part."))
    (eq? (concatenate_files "/path/to/file1.txt" "/path/to/file2.txt") "First part. Second part."))

  (test first_file_empty
    (mocks (file.read "/path/to/empty1.txt" "")

topic: paycheck net from hours and rate
objective: Calculate an employee's net paycheck. The calculation should account for regular hours (up to 40) paid at a standard rate, overtime hours (above 40) paid at 1.5 times the standard rate, a 15% federal tax on gross pay, and a fixed deduction amount. The final net pay should be rounded to two decimal places.
solution:
(module PaycheckCalculator
  ; Helper to round numbers to two decimal places
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))

  ; Calculates gross pay considering regular and overtime hours
  (defun calculate_gross_pay (hours rate)
    (if (> hours 40)
        (+ (* 40 rate) (* (- hours 40) (* rate 1.5)))
      (* hours rate)))

  ; Calculates federal income tax based on gross pay (15%)
  (defun calculate_federal_tax (gross_pay)
    (* gross_pay 0.15))

  ; Calculates the final net pay after taxes and a fixed deduction
  (defun calculate_net_pay (hours rate fixed_deduction)
    (def gross (calculate_gross_pay hours rate))
    (def tax (calculate_federal_tax gross))
    (round2 (- gross tax fixed_deduction)))

  (test regular_pay_no_deduction
    (near? (calculate_net_pay 30 10 0) 255.00 0.01))

  (test regular_pay_with_deduction
    (near? (calculate_net_pay 40 15 50) 460.00 0.01))

  (test overtime_pay_with_deduction
    (near? (calculate_net_pay 45 20 75) 732.50 0.01))

  (test low_hours_with_deduction
    (near? (calculate_net_pay 10 10 20) 65.00 0.01))

  (test max_regular_hours_no_deduction
    (near? (calculate_net_pay 40 25 0) 850.00 0.01))

  (test high_overtime_pay
    (near? (calculate_net_pay 60 30 100) 1685.00 0.01))

  (test zero_hours_with_deduction
    (near? (calculate_net_pay 0 20 10) -10.00 0.01))
)

objective: Write a program that calculates a mobile phone bill based on a base plan cost, included data and minutes, and overage rates for exceeding these limits. The function should take the plan details and actual usage, returning the total bill amount rounded to two decimal places.
solution:
(module PhoneBillCalculator
  ;; Helper to round a number to two decimal places, suitable for currency.
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))

  ;; Calculates the cost for data usage exceeding the plan limit.
  (defun calculate_data_overage_cost (data_limit_gb actual_data_gb data_rate_gb)
    (if (> actual_data_gb data_limit_gb)
        (* (- actual_data_gb data_limit_gb) data_rate_gb)
        0))

  ;; Calculates the cost for minute usage exceeding the plan limit.
  (defun calculate_minutes_overage_cost (min_limit actual_minutes min_rate_per_min)
    (if (> actual_minutes min_limit)
        (* (- actual_minutes min_limit) min_rate_per_min)
        0))

  ;; Calculates the total phone bill including base cost and any overage charges.
  (defun calculate_total_bill (base_cost data_limit_gb data_rate_gb min_limit min_rate_per_min actual_data_gb actual_minutes)
    (def data_overage_cost (calculate_data_overage_cost data_limit_gb actual_data_gb data_rate_gb))
    (def minutes_overage_cost (calculate_minutes_overage_cost min_limit actual_minutes min_rate_per_min))
    (round2 (+ base_cost data_overage_cost minutes_overage_cost)))

  (test no_overage
    (near? (calculate_total_bill 30.00 10 5.00 500 0.10 8 400) 30.00 0.01))

  (test data_overage_only
    (near? (calculate_total_bill 30.00 10 5.00 500 0.10 12 400) 40.00 0.01)) ; 30 + (2 GB * 5.00) = 40

  (test minutes_overage_only
    (near? (calculate_total_bill 30.00 10 5.00 500 0.10 8 550) 35.00 0.01)) ; 30 + (50 min * 0.10) = 35

  (test both_data_and_minutes_overage
    (near? (calculate_total_bill 30.00 10 5.00 500 0.10 12 550) 45.00 0.01)) ; 30 + (2 GB * 5.00) + (50 min * 0.10) = 45

  (test exact_limits_no_overage
    (near? (calculate_total_bill 30.00 10 5.00 500 0.10 10 500) 30.00 0.01))

  (test larger_overage_values
    (near? (calculate_total_bill 50.00 20 2.50 1000 0.05 25 1200) 72.50 0.01)) ; 50 + (5 GB * 2.50) + (200 min * 0.05) = 50 + 12.50 + 10 = 72.50

  (test zero_overage_rates
    (near? (calculate_total_bill 25.00 10 0.00 500 0.00 15 600) 25.00 0.01)) ; Overage rates are 0, so total is just base cost
)

topic: monthly subscription cost with annual discount
objective: Calculate the total cost for a subscription given a monthly base rate, an annual discount percentage, and the total number of months. Full years of subscription qualify for the annual discount, while any remaining partial months are charged at the standard monthly rate.
solution:
(module SubscriptionCostCalculator
  (defun calculate_effective_monthly_rate (monthly_base_cost annual_discount_pct)
    "Calculates the effective monthly cost if the subscription is paid annually."
    (* monthly_base_cost (- 1.0 (/ annual_discount_pct 100.0))))

  (defun calculate_total_subscription_cost (monthly_base_cost annual_discount_pct num_months)
    "Calculates the total cost for a subscription, applying annual discounts for full years."
    (def num_full_years (math_floor (/ num_months 12)))
    (def remaining_months (math_mod num_months 12))
    (def annual_discounted_rate_per_month (calculate_effective_monthly_rate monthly_base_cost annual_discount_pct))

    (def cost_for_full_years (* num_full_years 12 annual_discounted_rate_per_month))
    (def cost_for_remaining_months (* remaining_months monthly_base_cost))

    (+ cost_for_full_years cost_for_remaining_months))

  (test no_discount_short_term
    (eq? (calculate_total_subscription_cost 10 0 5) 50.0))

  (test no_discount_long_term
    (eq? (calculate_total_subscription_cost 10 0 15) 150.0))

  (test full_year_discount
    (near? (calculate_total_subscription_cost 10 20 12) 96.0 0.01)) ; 12 months * 10 * (1 - 0.20) = 96

  (test mixed_term_discount
    (near? (calculate_total_subscription_cost 10 20 15) 126.0 0.01)) ; 1 year discounted (96) + 3 months standard (30) = 126

  (test multiple_years_discount
    (near? (calculate_total_subscription_cost 10 20 24) 192.0 0.01)) ; 2 years discounted (2 * 96) = 192

  (test fractional_discount_percentage
    (near? (calculate_total_subscription_cost 25 10.5 7) 175.0 0.01)) ; No full years, 7 months * 25 = 175

  (test zero_months
    (eq? (calculate_total_subscription_cost 50 15 0) 0.0)))

topic: read file and count char occurrences
objective: Develop a module that reads the content of a specified file and counts the occurrences of a given character within that content, handling cases where the file is empty or not found.
solution:
(module FileCharCounter
  (extern defun file_read (filename) @capability "file.read")

  ; Helper function to count occurrences of a character within a string.
  (defun count_char_in_string (s char_to_find)
    (def count 0)
    (def i 0)
    (def s_length (str_length s))
    (while (< i s_length)
      (def current_char (str_substring s i 1))
      (if (str_eq current_char char_to_find)
        (set count (+ count 1)))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    count)

  ; Reads a file and counts the occurrences of a specified character.
  ; Returns 0 if the file is empty or not found.
  (defun count_char_occurrences (filename char_to_count)
    (def file_content (file_read filename))
    (if (str_eq file_content "")
      (return 0)) ; Return 0 for empty or non-existent files
    (count_char_in_string file_content char_to_count))

  (test counts_multiple_chars
    (mocks (file.read "data.txt" "hello world"))
    (eq? (count_char_occurrences "data.txt" "l") 3))

  (test counts_no_chars
    (mocks (file.read "data.txt" "hello world"))
    (eq? (count_char_occurrences "data.txt" "x") 0))

  (test handles_empty_file
    (mocks (file.read "empty.txt" ""))
    (eq? (count_char_occurrences "empty.txt" "a") 0))

  (test handles_missing_file
    (mocks (file.read "nonexistent.txt" ""))
    (eq? (count_char_occurrences "nonexistent.txt" "b") 0))

  (test case_sensitivity
    (mocks (file.read "text.txt" "Apple Pie"))
    (eq? (count_char_occurrences "text.txt" "p") 2))

  (test case_sensitivity_upper
    (mocks (file.read "text.txt" "Apple Pie"))
    (eq? (count_char_occurrences "text.txt" "P") 1))

  (test counts_spaces
    (mocks (file.read "sentence.txt" "one two three"))
    (eq? (count_char_occurrences "sentence.txt" " ") 2))
)

objective: Implement a function that removes leading and trailing whitespace characters (space, tab, newline, carriage return) from a given string. Internal whitespace should remain untouched.
solution:
(module StringTrimmer
  (defun is_whitespace (char_str)
    (if (str_eq char_str " ") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq char_str "\t") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq char_str "\n") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq char_str "\r") (return 1))
    0)

  (defun trim_whitespace (s)
    (def len (str_length s))
    (if (= len 0) (return ""))

    ; Find start_idx: first non-whitespace character
    (def start_idx 0)
    (def found_start_char 0) ; Flag to control loop exit
    (while (and (< start_idx len) (= found_start_char 0))
      (def char_at_start (str_substring s start_idx 1))
      (if (= (is_whitespace char_at_start) 0)
        (set found_start_char 1) ; Found non-whitespace, exit loop
        (set start_idx (+ start_idx 1))))

    ; If no non-whitespace character was found (string was all whitespace)
    (if (= found_start_char 0) (return ""))

    ; Find end_idx: last non-whitespace character
    (def end_idx (- len 1))
    (def found_end_char 0) ; Flag to control loop exit
    (while (and (>= end_idx start_idx) (= found_end_char 0))
      (def char_at_end (str_substring s end_idx 1))
      (if (= (is_whitespace char_at_end) 0)
        (set found_end_char 1) ; Found non-whitespace, exit loop
        (set end_idx (- end_idx 1))))

    ; Extract the substring from start_idx to end_idx (inclusive)
    (str_substring s start_idx (+ (- end_idx start_idx) 1)))

  (test basic_trim
    (eq? (trim_whitespace "  hello world  ") "hello world"))

  (test leading_only
    (eq? (trim_whitespace "\t\n  leading text") "leading text"))

  (test trailing_only
    (eq? (trim_whitespace "trailing text \r ") "trailing text"))

  (test no_whitespace
    (eq? (trim_whitespace "no-whitespace-here") "no-whitespace-here"))

  (test all_whitespace
    (eq? (trim_whitespace " \t\n\r   ") ""))

  (test empty_string
    (eq? (trim_whitespace "") ""))

  (test internal_whitespace
    (eq? (trim_whitespace "  multi \n line \t string  ") "multi \n line \t string"))
)

objective: Write a function that retrieves an environment variable's string value and interprets it as a boolean. The function should return `true` if the variable's value is "true", "1", or "yes" (case-insensitive), and `false` for any other value, including an empty string or unrecognized text.
solution:
(module EnvBoolReader
  (extern defun env_read (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun is_truthy_string (s)
    (def lower_s (str_lower s))
    (if (str_eq lower_s "true") (return true))
    (if (str_eq lower_s "1") (return true))
    (if (str_eq lower_s "yes") (return true))
    false)

  (defun get_env_bool (key)
    (def env_val (env_read key))
    (is_truthy_string env_val))

  (test present_true (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_X" "true")) (eq? (get_env_bool "FEATURE_X") true))
  (test present_TRUE_case (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_X" "TRUE")) (eq? (get_env_bool "FEATURE_X") true))
  (test present_1 (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_X" "1")) (eq? (get_env_bool "FEATURE_X") true))
  (test present_yes (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_X" "yes")) (eq? (get_env_bool "FEATURE_X") true))
  (test present_false (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_X" "false")) (eq? (get_env_bool "FEATURE_X") false))
  (test present_0 (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_X" "0")) (eq? (get_env_bool "FEATURE_X") false))
  (test present_no_case (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_X" "NO")) (eq? (get_env_bool "FEATURE_X") false))
  (test missing_env (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_X" "")) (eq? (get_env_bool "FEATURE_X") false))
  (test garbage_env (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_X" "random_text")) (eq? (get_env_bool "FEATURE_X") false)))

objective: Write a program that calculates the total fare for a ride based on a fixed base fare, a per-mile rate, and an optional minimum fare. The final fare should be the greater of the calculated fare or the specified minimum fare.
solution:
(module RideFareCalculator
  (defun calculate_distance_charge (distance_miles rate_per_mile)
    (* distance_miles rate_per_mile))

  (defun calculate_base_plus_distance (base_fare distance_charge)
    (+ base_fare distance_charge))

  (defun apply_minimum_fare (calculated_fare min_fare)
    (math_max calculated_fare min_fare))

  (defun calculate_ride_fare (base_fare distance_miles rate_per_mile min_fare)
    (def distance_charge (calculate_distance_charge distance_miles rate_per_mile))
    (def fare_before_min (calculate_base_plus_distance base_fare distance_charge))
    (apply_minimum_fare fare_before_min min_fare))

  (test basic_fare
    (near? (calculate_ride_fare 3.0 5.0 1.5 0.0) 10.5 0.01)) ; 3 + (5 * 1.5) = 10.5

  (test zero_distance

  (test longer_distance
    (near? (calculate_ride_fare 4.0 10.0 1.8 10.0) 22.0 0.01)) ; 4 + (10 * 1.8) = 22.0, min 10.0 doesn't apply
)

objective: Write a function `reverse_array` that takes an array of numbers and returns a new array with the elements in reverse order. The original array should not be modified.
solution:
(module ArrayReverser
  (defun reverse_array (xs)
    (def n (arr_length xs))
    (def reversed_xs (arr_new n))
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i n)
      (def val (arr_get xs i))
      (arr_set reversed_xs (- (- n 1) i) val)
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    reversed_xs)

  (test empty_array
    (eq? (reverse_array (arr_new 0)) (arr_new 0)))

  (test single_element_array
    (def input (arr_new 1))
    (arr_set input 0 42)
    (def expected (arr_new 1))
    (arr_set expected 0 42)
    (eq? (reverse_array input) expected))

  (test two_element_array
    (def input (arr_new 2))
    (arr_set input 0 10)
    (arr_set input 1 20)
    (def expected (arr_new 2))
    (arr_set expected 0 20)
    (arr_set expected 1 10)
    (eq? (reverse_array input) expected))

  (test three_element_array
    (def input (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set input 0 1)
    (arr_set input 1 2)
    (arr_set input 2 3)
    (def expected (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set expected 0 3)
    (arr_set expected 1 2)
    (arr_set expected 2 1)
    (eq? (reverse_array input) expected))

  (test four_element_array
    (def input (arr_new 4))
    (arr_set input 0 100)
    (arr_set input 1 200)
    (arr_set input 2 300)
    (arr_set input 3 400)
    (def expected (arr_new 4))
    (arr_set expected 0 400)
    (arr_set expected 1 300)
    (arr_set expected 2 200)
    (arr_set expected 3 100)
    (eq? (reverse_array input) expected))

  (test negative_numbers
    (def input (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set input 0 -1)
    (arr_set input 1 -2)
    (arr_set input 2 -3)
    (def expected (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set expected 0 -3)
    (arr_set expected 1 -2)
    (arr_set expected 2 -1)
    (eq? (reverse_array input) expected))

  (test mixed_numbers
    (def input (arr_new 5))
    (arr_set input 0 0)
    (arr_set input 1 -5)
    (arr_set input 2 10)
    (arr_set input 3 -15)
    (arr_set input 4 20)
    (def expected (arr_new 5))
    (arr_set expected 0 20)
    (arr_set expected 1 -15)
    (arr_set expected 2 10)
    (arr_set expected 3 -5)
    (arr_set expected 4 0)
    (eq? (reverse_array input) expected)))

(module FileSizeReader

  (defun get_file_size_or_default (filename default_size)
    (def content (file_read filename))

    (eq? (get_file_size_or_default "non_existent.txt" 750) 750))

    (mocks (file.read "zero.txt" "0"))
    (eq? (get_file_size_or_default "zero.txt" 999) 0))

  (test different_default
    (mocks (file.read "another.txt" ""))
    (eq? (get_file_size_or_default "another.txt" 2000) 2000))

  (test large_number
    (mocks (file.read "large.txt" "987654321"))
    (eq? (get_file_size_or_default "large.txt" 100) 987654321)))

objective: Implement a function that calculates the final price for a given quantity of items, applying bulk discounts based on the total quantity ordered. Discounts are tiered: 5% for quantities of 10 or more, 10% for quantities of 25 or more, and 15% for quantities of 50 or more. The final price should be rounded to two decimal places.
solution:
(module BulkPriceCalculator
  ;; Helper to round a number to two decimal places
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100.0))

  ;; Determines the applicable discount rate based on the quantity.
  ;; Returns 0.0 for no discount, or a decimal rate (e.g., 0.05 for 5%).
  (defun get_discount_rate (qty)
    (if (>= qty 50) (return 0.15))
    (if (>= qty 25) (return 0.10))
    (if (>= qty 10) (return 0.05))
    0.0) ; No discount for quantities less than 10

  ;; Calculates the subtotal price before applying any discounts.
  (defun calculate_subtotal (unit_price qty)
    (* unit_price qty))

  ;; Applies a given discount rate to an amount.
  (defun apply_discount (amount discount_rate)
    (- amount (* amount discount_rate)))

  ;; Calculates the final price for a given unit price and quantity,
  ;; taking into account bulk discounts and rounding to two decimal places.
  (defun calculate_final_price (unit_price qty)
    (def subtotal_price (calculate_subtotal unit_price qty))
    (def discount_rate (get_discount_rate qty))
    (def discounted_price (apply_discount subtotal_price discount_rate))
    (round2 discounted_price))

  ;; Tests
  (test no_discount_small_qty (near? (calculate_final_price 10.0 5) 50.0 0.01))
  (test no_discount_edge_qty (near? (calculate_final_price 10.0 9) 90.0 0.01))
  (test first_tier_discount_min (near? (calculate_final_price 10.0 10) 95.0 0.01)) ; 10 * 10 = 100, 5% off = 95
  (test second_tier_discount_min (near? (calculate_final_price 4.0 25) 90.0 0.01)) ; 4 * 25 = 100, 10% off = 90
  (test third_tier_discount_min (near? (calculate_final_price 2.0 50) 85.0 0.01)) ; 2 * 50 = 100, 15% off = 85
  (test third_tier_discount_large (near? (calculate_final_price 1.25 100) 106.25 0.01)) ; 1.25 * 100 = 125, 15% off = 106.25
  (test mixed_price_qty (near? (calculate_final_price 2.50 30) 67.50 0.01)) ; 2.50 * 30 = 75, 10% off = 67.50
)

topic: env var split on comma into count
objective: Implement a function that reads an environment variable named "ITEM_LIST", splits its value by commas, and returns the total count of resulting items. An empty environment variable should result in a count of 0.
solution:
(module EnvVarItemCounter
  (extern defun env_read (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun count_env_comma_items ()
    (def item_list_str (env_read "ITEM_LIST"))
    (if (str_eq item_list_str "")
      (return 0))
    (def items (str_split item_list_str ","))
    (arr_length items))

  (test empty_env_var
    (mocks (env.get "ITEM_LIST" ""))
    (eq? (count_env_comma_items) 0))

  (test single_item
    (mocks (env.get "ITEM_LIST" "apple"))
    (eq? (count_env_comma_items) 1))

  (test multiple_items
    (mocks (env.get "ITEM_LIST" "apple,banana,cherry"))
    (eq? (count_env_comma_items) 3))

  (test with_empty_segments
    (mocks (env.get "ITEM_LIST" "apple,,banana,")) ; Expected: ["apple", "", "banana", ""]
    (eq? (count_env_comma_items) 4))

  (test leading_trailing_commas
    (mocks (env.get "ITEM_LIST" ",orange,grape,")) ; Expected: ["", "orange", "grape", ""]
    (eq? (count_env_comma_items) 4))

  (test only_commas
    (mocks (env.get "ITEM_LIST" ",,")) ; Expected: ["", "", ""]
    (eq? (count_env_comma_items) 3))
)

topic: read two env vars and concat
objective: Write a function that constructs a complete file path for an application log. It should read a base directory from the environment variable `APP_HOME` and a log file name from `LOG_FILE_NAME`. If `APP_HOME` is empty, use `/var/log` as the default. If `LOG_FILE_NAME` is empty, use `app.log` as the default. The final path should concatenate the chosen base directory and file name with a `/` in between.
solution:
(module FilePathBuilder
  (extern defun env_read (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun get_full_log_path ()
    (def home_dir (env_read "APP_HOME"))
    (def file_name (env_read "LOG_FILE_NAME"))

    (def final_home_dir (if (str_eq home_dir "") "/var/log" home_dir))
    (def final_file_name (if (str_eq file_name "") "app.log" file_name))

    (str_concat final_home_dir (str_concat "/" final_file_name)))

  (test both_present
    (mocks
      (env.get "APP_HOME" "/usr/local/app")
      (env.get "LOG_FILE_NAME" "server.log"))
    (eq? (get_full_log_path) "/usr/local/app/server.log"))

  (test home_missing
    (mocks
      (env.get "APP_HOME" "")
      (env.get "LOG_FILE_NAME" "access.log"))
    (eq? (get_full_log_path) "/var/log/access.log"))

  (test file_name_missing
    (mocks
      (env.get "APP_HOME" "/opt/app")
      (env.get "LOG_FILE_NAME" ""))
    (eq? (get_full_log_path) "/opt/app/app.log"))

  (test both_missing
    (mocks
      (env.get "APP_HOME" "")
      (env.get "LOG_FILE_NAME" ""))
    (eq? (get_full_log_path) "/var/log/app.log"))

  (test custom_root
    (mocks
      (env.get "APP_HOME" "/mnt/data/logs")
      (env.get "LOG_FILE_NAME" "debug.log"))
    (eq? (get_full_log_path) "/mnt/data/logs/debug.log"))
)

topic: env var split on comma into count
objective: Write a function `count_env_items` that reads an environment variable specified by a key. It should parse the value as a comma-separated list and return the count of items in the list. If the environment variable is not set or its value is an empty string, the count should be 0.
solution:
(module EnvItemCount
  (extern defun env_read (key) @capability "env.get")

  (defun count_env_items (key)
    (def val (env_read key))
    (if (str_eq val "")
      (return 0))
    (def items (str_split val ","))
    (arr_length items))

  (test empty_or_missing
    (mocks (env.get "SERVICES" ""))
    (eq? (count_env_items "SERVICES") 0))

  (test single_item
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURES" "feature_a"))
    (eq? (count_env_items "FEATURES") 1))

  (test multiple_items
    (mocks (env.get "COMPONENTS" "core,plugin,ui,api"))
    (eq? (count_env_items "COMPONENTS") 4))

  (test trailing_comma
    (mocks (env.get "DEVICES" "dev1,dev2,"))
    (eq? (count_env_items "DEVICES") 3)) ; str_split "dev1,dev2," "," -> ["dev1", "dev2", ""]

  (test leading_comma
    (mocks (env.get "USERS" ",admin,guest"))
    (eq? (count_env_items "USERS") 3)) ; str_split ",admin,guest" "," -> ["", "admin", "guest"]

  (test only_commas
    (mocks (env.get "EMPTY_LIST" ",,"))
    (eq? (count_env_items "EMPTY_LIST") 3)) ; str_split ",," "," -> ["", "", ""]
)

objective: Calculate a person's recommended daily water intake based on their body weight in pounds. The general guideline is to consume 0.5 ounces of water per pound of body weight. The module should provide functions to calculate the goal in both ounces and rounded liters.
solution:
(module WaterIntakeGoal
  ;; Calculates the recommended daily water intake in ounces based on body weight in pounds.
  ;; The rule is 0.5 ounces per pound of body weight.
  (defun calculate_ounces_goal (weight_lbs)
    (* weight_lbs 0.5))

  ;; Converts a given amount of ounces to liters.
  ;; The conversion factor used is 1 liter = 33.814 ounces.
  (defun ounces_to_liters (ounces)
    (/ ounces 33.814))

  ;; Helper function to round a number to two decimal places.
  (defun round_to_2_decimals (value)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* value 100) 0.5)) 100))

  ;; Calculates the daily water intake goal in liters, rounded to two decimal places.
  ;; It first calculates ounces and then converts to liters.
  (defun get_daily_liters_goal (weight_lbs)
    (def ounces_goal (calculate_ounces_goal weight_lbs))
    (def liters_unrounded (ounces_to_liters ounces_goal))
    (round_to_2_decimals liters_unrounded))

  ;; Tests for calculate_ounces_goal
  (test ounces_goal_for_150lbs (eq? (calculate_ounces_goal 150) 75.0))
  (test ounces_goal_for_100lbs (eq? (calculate_ounces_goal 100) 50.0))
  (test ounces_goal_for_200lbs (eq? (calculate_ounces_goal 200) 100.0))

  ;; Tests for get_daily_liters_goal
  (test liters_goal_for_150lbs (near? (get_daily_liters_goal 150) 2.22 0.01))
  (test liters_goal_for_100lbs (near? (get_daily_liters_goal 100) 1.48 0.01))
  (test liters_goal_for_200lbs (near? (get_daily_liters_goal 200) 2.96 0.01))
  (test liters_goal_for_175lbs (near? (get_daily_liters_goal 175) 2.59 0.01))
)

objective: Implement a set of functions to calculate the estimated fuel cost for a given trip. The calculation should consider the total distance, the vehicle's fuel efficiency, and the price per unit of fuel, rounding the final cost to two decimal places.
solution:
(module TripFuelCost
  (defun round_to_cents (amount)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* amount 100) 0.5)) 100.0))

  (defun calculate_fuel_needed (distance efficiency)
    (if (<= efficiency 0)
        (return 0)) ; Prevent division by zero, return 0 fuel if efficiency is 0 or negative
    (/ distance efficiency))

  (defun calculate_total_fuel_cost (fuel_needed price_per_unit)
    (* fuel_needed price_per_unit))

  (defun estimate_trip_fuel_cost (distance efficiency price_per_unit)
    (def fuel (calculate_fuel_needed distance efficiency))
    (def raw_cost (calculate_total_fuel_cost fuel price_per_unit))
    (round_to_cents raw_cost))

  (test basic_trip
    (near? (estimate_trip_fuel_cost 100 25 3.00) 12.00 0.001))

  (test longer_trip_with_fractions
    (near? (estimate_trip_fuel_cost 350 30 3.55) 41.42 0.001))

  (test zero_distance
    (near? (estimate_trip_fuel_cost 0 20 4.00) 0.00 0.001))

  (test high_efficiency_low_price
    (near? (estimate_trip_fuel_cost 500 50 2.50) 25.00 0.001))

  (test large_numbers
    (near? (estimate_trip_fuel_cost 1200 28.5 4.15) 174.74 0.001))

  (test zero_efficiency
    (near? (estimate_trip_fuel_cost 100 0 3.00) 0.00 0.001))

  (test negative_efficiency
    (near? (estimate_trip_fuel_cost 100 -10 3.00) 0.00 0.001)))

objective: Calculate the perimeter of a rectangle given its length and width. Ensure both dimensions are non-negative.

objective: Develop a program that calculates the total cost of a gym membership. The calculation includes a one-time joining fee and a monthly fee. A percentage discount is applied to the monthly fee if the membership duration meets or exceeds a specified threshold in months.
solution:
(module GymMembershipCalculator
  ;; Calculates the effective monthly fee after applying a discount if the duration is long enough.
  (defun calculate_discounted_monthly_fee (monthly_fee duration_months discount_threshold_months discount_pct)
    (if (>= duration_months discount_threshold_months)
      (- monthly_fee (/ (* monthly_fee discount_pct) 100))
      monthly_fee)) ; Return original monthly_fee if no discount applies

  ;; Calculates the total cost of a gym membership, including joining fee and discounted monthly costs.
  (defun calculate_total_membership_cost (joining_fee monthly_fee duration_months discount_threshold_months discount_pct)
    (def effective_monthly_fee (calculate_discounted_monthly_fee monthly_fee duration_months discount_threshold_months discount_pct))
    (def total_monthly_cost (* effective_monthly_fee duration_months))
    (+ joining_fee total_monthly_cost))

  (test short_term_no_discount
    ;; 6 months, no discount (12-month threshold), 50 joining, 30 monthly
    ;; Expected: 50 + (30 * 6) = 50 + 180 = 230
    (eq? (calculate_total_membership_cost 50 30 6 12 10) 230))

  (test zero_duration_only_joining_fee
    ;; 0 months, no discount, 50 joining, 30 monthly
    ;; Expected: 50 + (30 * 0) = 50
    (eq? (calculate_total_membership_cost 50 30 0 12 10) 50))

  (test long_term_with_discount
    ;; 12 months, 10% discount (30 - 3 = 27 monthly), 50 joining, 30 monthly
    ;; Expected: 50 + (27 * 12) = 50 + 324 = 374
    (eq? (calculate_total_membership_cost 50 30 12 12 10) 374))

  (test extended_long_term_with_discount
    ;; 24 months, 10% discount (27 monthly), 0 joining, 30 monthly
    ;; Expected: 0 + (27 * 24) = 648
    (eq? (calculate_total_membership_cost 0 30 24 12 10) 648))

  (test no_joining_fee_short_term
    ;; 3 months, no discount, 0 joining, 30 monthly
    ;; Expected: 0 + (30 * 3) = 90
    (eq? (calculate_total_membership_cost 0 30 3 12 10) 90))

  (test higher_discount_percentage
    ;; 18 months, 20% discount (30 - 6 = 24 monthly), 100 joining, 30 monthly
    ;; Expected: 100 + (24 * 18) = 100 + 432 = 532
    (eq? (calculate_total_membership_cost 100 30 18 12 20) 532))
)

topic: package weight cost with surcharge per excess kilogram
objective: Calculate the total shipping cost for a package. The cost is based on a base rate per kilogram up to a certain limit, with an additional surcharge per kilogram for any weight exceeding that limit.
solution:
(module PackageShippingCost
  (defun calculate_base_cost (weight rate_per_kg)
    (* weight rate_per_kg))

  (defun calculate_excess_surcharge (weight limit surcharge_per_kg)
    (if (> weight limit)
      (* (- weight limit) surcharge_per_kg)
      0))

  (defun calculate_shipping_cost (weight base_rate_per_kg weight_limit excess_surcharge_per_kg)
    (def effective_weight (math_min weight weight_limit))
    (def base_cost (calculate_base_cost effective_weight base_rate_per_kg))
    (def excess_cost (calculate_excess_surcharge weight weight_limit excess_surcharge_per_kg))
    (+ base_cost excess_cost))

  (test under_limit
    (eq? (calculate_shipping_cost 5 10 10 20) 50)) ; 5kg * 10/kg = 50

  (test at_limit
    (eq? (calculate_shipping_cost 10 10 10 20) 100)) ; 10kg * 10/kg = 100

  (test over_limit_small_excess
    (eq? (calculate_shipping_cost 12 10 10 20) 140)) ; (10kg * 10/kg) + (2kg * 20/kg) = 100 + 40 = 140

  (test over_limit_large_excess
    (eq? (calculate_shipping_cost 15 10 10 20) 200)) ; (10kg * 10/kg) + (5kg * 20/kg) = 100 + 100 = 200

  (test zero_weight
    (eq? (calculate_shipping_cost 0 10 10 20) 0))

  (test different_rates
    (eq? (calculate_shipping_cost 8 5 10 15) 40)) ; 8kg * 5/kg = 40

  (test high_excess_rate
    (eq? (calculate_shipping_cost 11 10 10 100) 200))) ; (10kg * 10/kg) + (1kg * 100/kg) = 100 + 100 = 200
)

topic: shipping cost by weight and zone
objective: Implement a shipping cost calculator that determines the total cost based on an item's weight and a shipping zone code. Different zones have different rates per kilogram, and a default rate applies to unknown zones.
solution:
(module ShippingCostCalculator
  ; Returns the rate per kilogram for a given shipping zone code.
  ; Defaults to a standard rate if the zone is not recognized.
  (defun get_zone_rate (zone_code)
    (if (str_eq zone_code "A") (return 2.50))
    (if (str_eq zone_code "B") (return 3.25))
    (if (str_eq zone_code "C") (return 4.75))
    ; Default rate for unrecognized zones
    5.00)

  ; Calculates the cost based on weight and a rate per kilogram.
  ; Ensures weight is not negative.
  (defun calculate_weight_cost (weight rate_per_kg)
    (if (< weight 0) (return 0.0)) ; Treat negative weight as zero
    (* weight rate_per_kg))

  ; Calculates the total shipping cost for an item given its weight and zone code.
  (defun calculate_total_shipping_cost (weight zone_code)
    (def zone_rate (get_zone_rate zone_code))
    (calculate_weight_cost weight zone_rate))

  (test zone_A_light_item
    (near? (calculate_total_shipping_cost 2.0 "A") 5.00 0.01)) ; 2.0 kg * 2.50/kg = 5.00

  (test zone_B_medium_item
    (near? (calculate_total_shipping_cost 5.0 "B") 16.25 0.01)) ; 5.0 kg * 3.25/kg = 16.25

  (test zone_C_heavy_item
    (near? (calculate_total_shipping_cost 10.0 "C") 47.50 0.01)) ; 10.0 kg * 4.75/kg = 47.50

  (test unknown_zone
    (near? (calculate_total_shipping_cost 3.0 "X") 15.00 0.01)) ; 3.0 kg * 5.00/kg (default) = 15.00

  (test zero_weight
    (near? (calculate_total_shipping_cost 0.0 "A") 0.00 0.01)) ; 0.0 kg * 2.50/kg = 0.00

  (test negative_weight_handled
    (near? (calculate_total_shipping_cost -1.5 "B") 0.00 0.01)) ; Negative weight should result in 0 cost
)

objective: Implement a function that reads the content of a file and returns its very first character. If the file is empty or effectively non-existent (yielding empty content), the function should return an empty string.

(module FileFirstChar

objective: Implement a function that calculates the sum of all numbers in an array that are strictly greater than a specified threshold value.
solution:
(module SumGreaterThanThreshold
  (defun sum_greater_than_threshold (nums threshold)
    (def total 0)
    (def i 0)
    (def n (arr_length nums))
    (while (< i n)
      (def current_num (arr_get nums i))
      (if (> current_num threshold)
        (set total (+ total current_num)))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    total)

  (test empty_array
    (def nums (arr_new 0))
    (eq? (sum_greater_than_threshold nums 5) 0))

  (test all_below_threshold
    (def nums (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set nums 0 1)
    (arr_set nums 1 2)
    (arr_set nums 2 3)
    (eq? (sum_greater_than_threshold nums 5) 0))

  (test all_above_threshold
    (def nums (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set nums 0 6)
    (arr_set nums 1 7)
    (arr_set nums 2 8)
    (eq? (sum_greater_than_threshold nums 5) 21)) ; 6 + 7 + 8 = 21

  (test mixed_values
    (def nums (arr_new 5))
    (arr_set nums 0 10)
    (arr_set nums 1 2)
    (arr_set nums 2 15)
    (arr_set nums 3 5)
    (arr_set nums 4 8)
    (eq? (sum_greater_than_threshold nums 7) 33)) ; 10 + 15 + 8 = 33

  (test threshold_is_zero
    (def nums (arr_new 5))
    (arr_set nums 0 -1)
    (arr_set nums 1 0)
    (arr_set nums 2 5)
    (arr_set nums 3 10)
    (arr_set nums 4 -2)
    (eq? (sum_greater_than_threshold nums 0) 15)) ; 5 + 10 = 15

  (test negative_numbers_and_threshold
    (def nums (arr_new 4))
    (arr_set nums 0 -5)
    (arr_set nums 1 -2)
    (arr_set nums 2 0)
    (arr_set nums 3 3)
    (eq? (sum_greater_than_threshold nums -3) 1)) ; -2 + 0 + 3 = 1

  (test exact_match_not_included
    (def nums (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set nums 0 5)
    (arr_set nums 1 6)
    (arr_set nums 2 7)
    (eq? (sum_greater_than_threshold nums 5) 13)) ; 6 + 7 = 13
)

objective: Implement a function `clamp` that takes a numerical value, a minimum bound, and a maximum bound. The function should return the value itself if it falls within the inclusive range [minimum, maximum]. If the value is below the minimum, it should return the minimum bound; if it's above the maximum, it should return the maximum bound. Assume minimum is always less than or equal to maximum.
solution:
(module NumberClamp
  (defun clamp (value min max)
    (def clamped_value value)
    (if (< clamped_value min)
      (set clamped_value min))
    (if (> clamped_value max)
      (set clamped_value max))
    clamped_value)

  (test value_in_range
    (eq? (clamp 5 0 10) 5))

  (test value_below_min
    (eq? (clamp -2 0 10) 0))

  (test value_above_max
    (eq? (clamp 15 0 10) 10))

  (test value_is_min
    (eq? (clamp 0 0 10) 0))

  (test value_is_max
    (eq? (clamp 10 0 10) 10))

  (test negative_range_in_bounds
    (eq? (clamp -5 -10 -1) -5))

  (test tight_range
    (eq? (clamp 7 7 7) 7)))

objective: Calculate the approximate yield to maturity (YTM) for a bond using the simple YTM formula. This calculation requires the bond's face value, annual coupon rate, its current market price, and the number of years remaining until maturity.
solution:
(module BondYieldToMaturity
  (defun round_decimal_places (value places)
    (def multiplier (math_pow 10 places))
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* value multiplier) 0.5)) multiplier))

  (defun calculate_simple_ytm (face_value coupon_rate current_price years_to_maturity)
    ;; Calculate annual coupon payment
    (def annual_coupon_payment (* face_value coupon_rate))
    ;; Calculate capital gain or loss per year until maturity
    (def capital_gain_loss_per_year (/ (- face_value current_price) years_to_maturity))
    ;; Calculate the average price of the bond
    (def average_price (/ (+ face_value current_price) 2))
    ;; Calculate the numerator for the YTM formula
    (def ytm_numerator (+ annual_coupon_payment capital_gain_loss_per_year))
    ;; Calculate the simple YTM
    (def simple_ytm (/ ytm_numerator average_price))
    ;; Round the result to 4 decimal places for financial reporting
    (round_decimal_places simple_ytm 4))

  (test ytm_par_bond
    (near? (calculate_simple_ytm 1000 0.05 1000 5) 0.0500 0.0001))

  (test ytm_discount_bond
    (near? (calculate_simple_ytm 1000 0.05 950 5) 0.0615 0.0001))

  (test ytm_premium_bond
    (near? (calculate_simple_ytm 1000 0.05 1050 5) 0.0390 0.0001))

  (test ytm_short_term_discount

objective: Calculate the total sales commission for a given sales amount based on a tiered structure. The commission rates are 5% for sales up to $1000, 7% for sales between $1001 and $5000, and 10% for sales above $5000.
solution:
(module SalesCommission
  (def TIER1_SALES_MAX 1000.0)
  (def TIER2_SALES_MAX 5000.0)
  (def RATE_TIER1 0.05)
  (def RATE_TIER2 0.07)
  (def RATE_TIER3 0.10)

  (defun calculate_commission (total_sales)
    (def commission 0.0)

    (if (<= total_sales TIER1_SALES_MAX)
      (set commission (* total_sales RATE_TIER1)))

    (if (and (> total_sales TIER1_SALES_MAX) (<= total_sales TIER2_SALES_MAX))
      (set commission (+ (* TIER1_SALES_MAX RATE_TIER1)
                         (* (- total_sales TIER1_SALES_MAX) RATE_TIER2))))

    (if (> total_sales TIER2_SALES_MAX)
      (set commission (+ (* TIER1_SALES_MAX RATE_TIER1)
                         (* (- TIER2_SALES_MAX TIER1_SALES_MAX) RATE_TIER2)
                         (* (- total_sales TIER2_SALES_MAX) RATE_TIER3))))
    commission)

  (test zero_sales (near? (calculate_commission 0.0) 0.0 0.001))
  (test tier1_mid (near? (calculate_commission 500.0) 25.0 0.001))
  (test tier1_max (near? (calculate_commission 1000.0) 50.0 0.001))
  (test tier2_min_plus_one (near? (calculate_commission 1001.0) 50.07 0.001))
  (test tier2_mid (near? (calculate_commission 3000.0) 190.0 0.001))
  (test tier2_max (near? (calculate_commission 5000.0) 330.0 0.001))
  (test tier3_example (near? (calculate_commission 7500.0) 580.0 0.001)))

topic: split string on space then count words
objective: Implement a function that counts the number of words in a given string. A word is defined as any sequence of non-space characters. Multiple spaces, as well as leading or trailing spaces, should not result in extra word counts.
solution:
(module WordCount
  (defun count_words (s)
    (def parts (str_split s " "))
    (def count 0)
    (def i 0)
    (def n (arr_length parts))
    (while (< i n)
      (def part (arr_get parts i))
      (if (> (str_length part) 0)
        (set count (+ count 1)))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    count)

  (test empty_string
    (eq? (count_words "") 0))

  (test only_spaces
    (eq? (count_words "   ") 0))

  (test single_word
    (eq? (count_words "hello") 1))

  (test two_words
    (eq? (count_words "hello world") 2))

  (test leading_trailing_spaces
    (eq? (count_words "  hello world  ") 2))

  (test multiple_spaces_between
    (eq? (count_words "hello   beautiful   world") 3))

  (test complex_sentence
    (eq? (count_words "  This is a test sentence.  ") 5)))

topic: discounted price after coupon and bulk-buy threshold
objective: Calculate the final price of an order, applying a bulk-buy discount if the quantity exceeds a threshold, and then applying a coupon discount if a specific coupon code is provided. All discounts are percentages applied sequentially.
solution:
(module DiscountCalculator
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))

  (defun calculate_base_total (item_price quantity)
    (* item_price quantity))

  (defun apply_bulk_discount (total quantity bulk_threshold bulk_discount_pct)
    (if (>= quantity bulk_threshold)
      (* total (- 1 (/ bulk_discount_pct 100)))
      total))

  (defun apply_coupon_discount (total user_coupon_code required_coupon_code coupon_discount_pct)
    (if (str_eq user_coupon_code required_coupon_code)
      (* total (- 1 (/ coupon_discount_pct 100)))
      total))

  (defun get_final_price (item_price quantity user_coupon_code bulk_threshold bulk_discount_pct required_coupon_code coupon_discount_pct)
    (def base (calculate_base_total item_price quantity))
    (def after_bulk (apply_bulk_discount base quantity bulk_threshold bulk_discount_pct))
    (def final (apply_coupon_discount after_bulk user_coupon_code required_coupon_code coupon_discount_pct))
    (round2 final))

  (test no_discounts
    (near? (get_final_price 10.0 5 "" 10 15.0 "SAVE20" 20.0) 50.0 0.01))

  (test only_bulk_discount
    (near? (get_final_price 10.0 12 "" 10 15.0 "SAVE20" 20.0) 102.0 0.01))

  (test only_coupon_discount
    (near? (get_final_price 10.0 5 "SAVE20" 10 15.0 "SAVE20" 20.0) 40.0 0.01))

  (test both_discounts
    (near? (get_final_price 10.0 12 "SAVE20" 10 15.0 "SAVE20" 20.0) 81.6 0.01))

  (test different_coupon_code
    (near? (get_final_price 10.0 12 "SAVE10" 10 15.0 "SAVE20" 20.0) 102.0 0.01))

  (test zero_price
    (near? (get_final_price 0.0 10 "SAVE20" 5 10.0 "SAVE20" 20.0) 0.0 0.01))

  (test zero_quantity
    (near? (get_final_price 10.0 0 "SAVE20" 5 10.0 "SAVE20" 20.0) 0.0 0.01))
)

objective: Calculate a person's Body Mass Index (BMI) given their weight in kilograms and height in meters, then classify their BMI into categories: "Underweight", "Normal weight", "Overweight", or "Obese".
solution:
(module BmiClassifier
  (defun calculate_bmi (weight_kg height_m)
    (/ weight_kg (* height_m height_m)))

  (defun classify_bmi (bmi_value)
    (if (< bmi_value 18.5) (return "Underweight"))
    (if (< bmi_value 25.0) (return "Normal weight"))
    (if (< bmi_value 30.0) (return "Overweight"))
    "Obese")

  (defun get_bmi_classification (weight_kg height_m)
    (def bmi (calculate_bmi weight_kg height_m))
    (classify_bmi bmi))

  (test underweight_case
    (eq? (get_bmi_classification 45 1.70) "Underweight")) ; BMI ~15.57
  (test normal_case
    (eq? (get_bmi_classification 70 1.75) "Normal weight")) ; BMI ~22.86
  (test overweight_case
    (eq? (get_bmi_classification 85 1.70) "Overweight")) ; BMI ~29.41
  (test obese_case
    (eq? (get_bmi_classification 90 1.60) "Obese")) ; BMI ~35.15
  (test edge_case_underweight
    (eq? (get_bmi_classification 54.0 1.71) "Underweight")) ; BMI = 54 / (1.71 * 1.71) = 18.46... (just under 18.5)
  (test edge_case_normal
    (eq? (get_bmi_classification 54.0 1.70) "Normal weight")) ; BMI = 54 / (1.70 * 1.70) = 18.68... (just over 18.5)
  (test edge_case_overweight
    (eq? (get_bmi_classification 72.25 1.70) "Overweight")) ; BMI = 72.25 / (1.70 * 1.70) = 25.0 (exact boundary)
  (test edge_case_obese
    (eq? (get_bmi_classification 86.7 1.70) "Obese")) ; BMI = 86.7 / (1.70 * 1.70) = 30.0 (exact boundary)
)

objective: Write a function `is_power_of_two` that determines if a given positive integer is a power of two (i.e., `2^k` for some non-negative integer `k`). The function should return 1 if true, and 0 if false. Input values less than or equal to zero should return 0.
solution:
(module PowerOfTwoChecker
  (defun is_power_of_two (n)
    (if (<= n 0) (return 0))
    (def log_val (/ (math_log n) (math_log 2)))
    (if (= (math_floor log_val) (math_ceil log_val))
      1
      0))

  (test one (eq? (is_power_of_two 1) 1))
  (test two (eq? (is_power_of_two 2) 1))
  (test eight (eq? (is_power_of_two 8) 1))
  (test sixty_four (eq? (is_power_of_two 64) 1))
  (test zero (eq? (is_power_of_two 0) 0))
  (test negative (eq? (is_power_of_two -4) 0))
  (test three (eq? (is_power_of_two 3) 0))
  (test seven (eq? (is_power_of_two 7) 0))
  (test one_hundred (eq? (is_power_of_two 100) 0)))

objective: Write a function `find_max` that takes an array of numbers and returns the largest element within it. If the input array is empty, the function should return 0.
solution:
(module ArrayMax
  (defun find_max (xs)
    (def n (arr_length xs))
    (if (= n 0)
      (return 0)) ; Return 0 for an empty array

    (def max_val (arr_get xs 0)) ; Initialize max_val with the first element
    (def i 1)
    (while (< i n)
      (def current_val (arr_get xs i))
      (if (> current_val max_val)
        (set max_val current_val))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    max_val)

  ; Helper function to create an array with 3 elements for testing
  (defun make_arr_3 (a b c)
    (def xs (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set xs 0 a)
    (arr_set xs 1 b)
    (arr_set xs 2 c)
    xs)

  ; Helper function to create an array with 5 elements for testing
  (defun make_arr_5 (a b c d e)
    (def xs (arr_new 5))
    (arr_set xs 0 a)
    (arr_set xs 1 b)
    (arr_set xs 2 c)
    (arr_set xs 3 d)
    (arr_set xs 4 e)
    xs)

  (test empty_array (eq? (find_max (arr_new 0)) 0))
  (test single_element (eq? (find_max (make_arr_3 42 0 0)) 42)) ; Using make_arr_3 for convenience
  (test positive_elements (eq? (find_max (make_arr_5 10 2 8 15 5)) 15))
  (test negative_elements (eq? (find_max (make_arr_3 -10 -2 -8)) -2))
  (test mixed_elements (eq? (find_max (make_arr_5 -10 20 -5 15 0)) 20))
  (test duplicate_max (eq? (find_max (make_arr_3 7 2 7)) 7))
  (test first_is_max (eq? (find_max (make_arr_3 100 20 50)) 100))
)

objective: Write a function that reads a boolean value from an environment variable. The function should accept a default boolean value to return if the environment variable is not set. It should interpret strings like "true", "1", "yes", or "on" (case-insensitive) as `true`, and anything else (including "false", "0", "no", "off", or an empty string from the environment) as `false`.
solution:
(module EnvBooleanReader
  (extern defun env_read (key) @capability "env.get")

  ;; Helper to parse a string into a boolean
  ;; Returns true if s is "true", "1", "yes", or "on" (case-insensitive)
  ;; Returns false otherwise.
  (defun parse_bool_string (s)
    (def lower_s (str_lower s))
    (if (str_eq lower_s "true") (return true))
    (if (str_eq lower_s "1") (return true))
    (if (str_eq lower_s "yes") (return true))
    (if (str_eq lower_s "on") (return true))
    false)

  ;; Reads an environment variable and returns a boolean.
  ;; If the variable is not set (empty string), `default_value` is returned.
  ;; Otherwise, the string value is parsed using `parse_bool_string`.
  (defun read_env_bool_or (key default_value)
    (def val_str (env_read key))
    (if (str_eq val_str "") (return default_value))
    (parse_bool_string val_str))

  (test enabled_true_string
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_X" "true"))
    (eq? (read_env_bool_or "FEATURE_X" false) true))

  (test enabled_one_numeric
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_X" "1"))
    (eq? (read_env_bool_or "FEATURE_X" false) true))

  (test enabled_yes_case_insensitive
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_X" "Yes"))
    (eq? (read_env_bool_or "FEATURE_X" false) true))

  (test disabled_false_string
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_X" "false"))
    (eq? (read_env_bool_or "FEATURE_X" true) false))

  (test disabled_zero_numeric
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_X" "0"))
    (eq? (read_env_bool_or "FEATURE_X" true) false))

  (test disabled_garbage_string
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_X" "notabool"))
    (eq? (read_env_bool_or "FEATURE_X" true) false))

  (test missing_env_var_returns_default_true
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_X" ""))
    (eq? (read_env_bool_or "FEATURE_X" true) true))

  (test missing_env_var_returns_default_false
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_X" ""))
    (eq? (read_env_bool_or "FEATURE_X" false) false)))

objective: Calculate an electricity bill based on a tiered pricing structure and a fixed service charge. The bill factors in different rates for consumption within specified kilowatt-hour (kWh) ranges.
solution:
(module ElectricityBill
  ;; Helper function to determine kWh consumed in Tier 1 (0-50 kWh)
  (defun get_tier1_kwh (total_kwh)
    (math_min total_kwh 50))

  ;; Helper function to determine kWh consumed in Tier 2 (51-150 kWh)
  (defun get_tier2_kwh (total_kwh)
    (math_min (math_max 0 (- total_kwh 50)) 100))

  ;; Helper function to determine kWh consumed in Tier 3 (151+ kWh)
  (defun get_tier3_kwh (total_kwh)
    (math_max 0 (- total_kwh 150)))

  ;; Main function to calculate the total electricity bill
  ;; Tier 1: 0-50 kWh @ $0.10/kWh
  ;; Tier 2: 51-150 kWh @ $0.12/kWh
  ;; Tier 3: 151+ kWh @ $0.15/kWh
  (defun calculate_total_bill (kwh service_charge)
    (def rate1 0.10)
    (def rate2 0.12)
    (def rate3 0.15)

    (def kwh_in_tier1 (get_tier1_kwh kwh))
    (def kwh_in_tier2 (get_tier2_kwh kwh))
    (def kwh_in_tier3 (get_tier3_kwh kwh))

    (def cost_tier1 (* kwh_in_tier1 rate1))
    (def cost_tier2 (* kwh_in_tier2 rate2))
    (def cost_tier3 (* kwh_in_tier3 rate3))

    (+ service_charge cost_tier1 cost_tier2 cost_tier3))

  (test bill_zero_consumption
    (near? (calculate_total_bill 0 5.00) 5.00 0.001))

  (test bill_tier1_only
    (near? (calculate_total_bill 25 5.00) 7.50 0.001)) ; 5.00 + (25 * 0.10) = 7.50

  (test bill_exact_tier1_limit
    (near? (calculate_total_bill 50 5.00) 10.00 0.001)) ; 5.00 + (50 * 0.10) = 10.00

  (test bill_into_tier2
    (near? (calculate_total_bill 75 5.00) 13.00 0.001)) ; 5.00 + (50 * 0.10) + (25 * 0.12) = 5.00 + 5.00 + 3.00 = 13.00

  (test bill_exact_tier2_limit
    (near? (calculate_total_bill 150 5.00) 22.00 0.001)) ; 5.00 + (50 * 0.10) + (100 * 0.12) = 5.00 + 5.00 + 12.00 = 22.00

  (test bill_into_tier3
    (near? (calculate_total_bill 200 5.00) 29.50 0.001)) ; 5.00 + (50 * 0.10) + (100 * 0.12) + (50 * 0.15) = 5.00 + 5.00 + 12.00 + 7.50 = 29.50
)

objective: Create a program that calculates the average speed of a trip consisting of two distinct segments. The program should use helper functions to sum the total distance and total time, then compute the overall average speed.
solution:
(module AverageTripSpeed
  ; Calculates the total distance covered across multiple segments.
  (defun calculate_total_distance (d1 d2)
    (+ d1 d2))

  ; Calculates the total time taken across multiple segments.
  (defun calculate_total_time (t1 t2)
    (+ t1 t2))

  ; Calculates the average speed for a trip consisting of two segments.
  ; Parameters: d1 (distance of segment 1), t1 (time of segment 1),
  ;             d2 (distance of segment 2), t2 (time of segment 2).
  (defun calculate_average_trip_speed (d1 t1 d2 t2)
    (def total_dist (calculate_total_distance d1 d2))
    (def total_time_taken (calculate_total_time t1 t2))
    ; Average speed = Total Distance / Total Time
    (/ total_dist total_time_taken))

  (test simple_trip
    ; Segment 1: 100 distance in 2 time (speed 50)
    ; Segment 2: 150 distance in 3 time (speed 50)
    ; Total: 250 distance in 5 time. Average speed = 50.
    (near? (calculate_average_trip_speed 100 2 150 3) 50 0.01))

  (test varied_segments
    ; Segment 1: 60 distance in 1.5 time (speed 40)
    ; Segment 2: 90 distance in 2.5 time (speed 36)
    ; Total: 150 distance in 4 time. Average speed = 37.5.
    (near? (calculate_average_trip_speed 60 1.5 90 2.5) 37.5 0.01))

  (test one_stationary_segment
    ; Segment 1: 50 distance in 1 time (speed 50)
    ; Segment 2: 0 distance in 1 time (stationary)
    ; Total: 50 distance in 2 time. Average speed = 25.
    (near? (calculate_average_trip_speed 50 1 0 1) 25 0.01))

  (test longer_segments
    ; Segment 1: 200 distance in 4 time (speed 50)
    ; Segment 2: 300 distance in 6 time (speed 50)
    ; Total: 500 distance in 10 time. Average speed = 50.
    (near? (calculate_average_trip_speed 200 4 300 6) 50 0.01))
)

objective: Write a function that takes an array of numbers and returns the total count of elements within the array that are equal to zero. The function should iterate through the array, checking each element and incrementing a counter if the element is zero.
solution:
(module ArrayZeroCounter
  (defun count_zeros (arr)
    (def i 0)
    (def n (arr_length arr))
    (def zero_count 0)
    (while (< i n)
      (def current_element (arr_get arr i))
      (if (= current_element 0)
        (set zero_count (+ zero_count 1)))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    zero_count)

  (test empty_array
    (eq? (count_zeros (arr_new 0)) 0))

  (test no_zeros
    (def xs (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set xs 0 1)
    (arr_set xs 1 2)
    (arr_set xs 2 3)
    (eq? (count_zeros xs) 0))

  (test some_zeros
    (def xs (arr_new 5))
    (arr_set xs 0 10)
    (arr_set xs 1 0)
    (arr_set xs 2 -5)
    (arr_set xs 3 0)
    (arr_set xs 4 20)
    (eq? (count_zeros xs) 2))

  (test all_zeros
    (def xs (arr_new 4))
    (arr_set xs 0 0)
    (arr_set xs 1 0)
    (arr_set xs 2 0)
    (arr_set xs 3 0)
    (eq? (count_zeros xs) 4))

  (test mixed_numbers
